Exodus32:2 “Give me your gold earrings,“ Aaron replied." 
Exodus32:3 So they all did—men and women, boys and girls." 
Exodus32:4 Aaron melted the gold, then molded and tooled it into the form of a calf. The people exclaimed, “O Israel, this is the god that brought you out of Egypt!“" 
Exodus32:5 When Aaron saw how happy the people were about it, he built an altar before the calf and announced, “Tomorrow there will be a feast to Jehovah!“" 
Exodus32:6 So they were up early the next morning and began offering burnt offerings and peace offerings to the calf-idol; afterwards they sat down to feast and drink at a wild party, followed by sexual immorality." 
Exodus32:7 Then the Lord told Moses, “Quick! Go on down, for your people that you brought from Egypt have defiled themselves," 
Exodus32:8 and have quickly abandoned all my laws. They have molded themselves a calf, and worshiped it, and sacrificed to it, and said, 'This is your god, O Israel, that brought you out of Egypt.' “" 
Exodus32:9 Then the Lord said, “I have seen what a stubborn, rebellious lot these people are." 
Exodus32:10 Now let me alone and my anger shall blaze out against them and destroy them all; and I will make you, Moses, into a great nation instead of them.“" 
Exodus32:11 But Moses begged God not to do it. “Lord,“ he pleaded, “why is your anger so hot against your own people whom you brought from the land of Egypt with such great power and mighty miracles?" 
Exodus32:12 Do you want the Egyptians to say, 'God tricked them into coming to the mountains so that he could slay them, destroying them from off the face of the earth'? Turn back from your fierce wrath. Turn away from this terrible evil you are planning against your people!" 
Exodus32:13 Remember your promise to your servants—to Abraham, Isaac, and Israel. For you swore by your own self, 'I will multiply your posterity as the stars of heaven, and I will give them all of this land I have promised to your descendants, and they shall inherit it forever.' “" 
Exodus32:14 So the Lord changed his mind and spared them. 
Exodus32:15 Then Moses went down the mountain, holding in his hands the Ten Commandments written on both sides of two stone tablets." 
Exodus32:16 (God himself had written the commandments on the tablets.) 
Exodus32:17 When Joshua heard the noise below them, of all the people shouting, he exclaimed to Moses, “It sounds as if they are preparing for war!“" 
Exodus32:17 When Joshua heard the noise below them, of all the people shouting, he exclaimed to Moses, “It sounds as if they are preparing for war!“" 
Exodus32:18 But Moses replied, “No, it's not a cry of victory or defeat, but singing.“" 
Exodus32:19 When they came near the camp, Moses saw the calf and the dancing, and in terrible anger he threw the tablets to the ground, and they lay broken at the foot of the mountain." 
Exodus32:20 He took the calf and melted it in the fire, and when the metal cooled, he ground it into powder and spread it upon the water and made the people drink it." 
Exodus32:21 Then he turned to Aaron. “What in the world did the people do to you,“ he demanded, “to make you bring such a terrible sin upon them?“" 
Exodus32:22 “Don't get so upset,“ Aaron replied. “You know these people and what a wicked bunch they are." 
Exodus32:23 They said to me, 'Make us a god to lead us, for something has happened to this fellow Moses who led us out of Egypt.'" 
Exodus32:24 Well, I told them, 'Bring me your gold earrings.' So they brought them to me and I threw them into the fire, and. . . well. . . this calf came out!“" 
Exodus32:25 When Moses saw that the people had been committing adultery—at Aaron's encouragement, and much to the amusement of their enemies—" 
Exodus32:26 he stood at the camp entrance and shouted, “All of you who are on the Lord's side, come over here and join me.“ And all the Levites came." 
Exodus32:27 He told them, “Jehovah the God of Israel says, 'Get your swords and go back and forth from one end of the camp to the other and kill even your brothers, friends, and neighbors.' “" 
Exodus32:28 So they did, and about three thousand men died that day." 
Exodus32:29 Then Moses told the Levites, “Today you have ordained yourselves for the service of the Lord, for you obeyed him even though it meant killing your own sons and brothers; now he will give you a great blessing.“" 
Exodus32:30 The next day Moses said to the people, “You have sinned a great sin, but I will return to the Lord on the mountain—perhaps I will be able to obtain his forgiveness for you.“" 
Exodus32:31 So Moses returned to the Lord and said, “Oh, these people have sinned a great sin and have made themselves gods of gold." 
Exodus32:32 Yet now if you will only forgive their sin—and if not, then blot me out of the book you have written.“" 
Exodus32:33 And the Lord replied to Moses, “Whoever has sinned against me will be blotted out of my book." 
Exodus32:34 And now go, lead the people to the place I told you about, and I assure you that my Angel shall travel on ahead of you; however, when I come to visit these people, I will punish them for their sins.“" 
Exodus32:35 And the Lord sent a great plague upon the people because they had worshiped Aaron's calf. 
Exodus33:1 The Lord said to Moses, “Lead these people you brought from Egypt to the land I promised Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob; for I said, 'I will give this land to your descendants.'" 
Exodus33:2 I will send an Angel before you to drive out the Canaanites, Amorites, Hittites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites." 
Exodus33:3 It is a land 'flowing with milk and honey'; but I will not travel among you, for you are a stubborn, unruly people, and I would be tempted to destroy you along the way.“" 
Exodus33:4 When the people heard these stern words, they went into mourning and stripped themselves of their jewelry and ornaments." 
Exodus33:5 For the Lord had told Moses to tell them, “You are an unruly, stubborn people. If I were there among you for even a moment, I would exterminate you. Remove your jewelry and ornaments until I decide what to do with you.“" 
Exodus33:6 So, after that, they wore no jewelry." 
Exodus33:7 Moses always erected the sacred tent (the “Tent for Meeting with God,“ he called it) far outside the camp, and everyone who wanted to consult with Jehovah went out there." 
Exodus33:8 Whenever Moses went to the Tabernacle, all the people, when they saw it, stood and would rise and stand in their tent doors." 
Exodus33:9 As he entered, the pillar of cloud would come down and stand at the door while the Lord spoke with Moses." 
Exodus33:10 Then all the people worshiped from their tent doors, bowing low to the pillar of cloud." 
Exodus33:11 Inside the tent the Lord spoke to Moses face to face, as a man speaks to his friend. Afterwards Moses would return to the camp, but the young man who assisted him, Joshua (son of Nun), stayed behind in the Tabernacle." 
Exodus33:11 Inside the tent the Lord spoke to Moses face to face, as a man speaks to his friend. Afterwards Moses would return to the camp, but the young man who assisted him, Joshua (son of Nun), stayed behind in the Tabernacle." 
Exodus33:12 Moses talked there with the Lord and said to him, “You have been telling me, 'Take these people to the Promised Land,' but you haven't told me whom you will send with me. You say you are my friend, and that I have found favor before you;" 
Exodus33:13 please, if this is really so, guide me clearly along the way you want me to travel so that I will understand you and walk acceptably before you. For don't forget that this nation is your people.“" 
Exodus33:14 And the Lord replied, “I myself will go with you and give you success.“" 
Exodus33:15 For Moses had said, “If you aren't going with us, don't let us move a step from this place." 
Exodus33:16 If you don't go with us, who will ever know that I and my people have found favor with you, and that we are different from any other people upon the face of the earth?“" 
Exodus33:17 And the Lord had replied to Moses, “Yes, I will do what you have asked, for you have certainly found favor with me, and you are my friend.“" 
Exodus33:18 Then Moses asked to see God's glory. 
Exodus33:19 The Lord replied, “I will make my goodness pass before you, and I will announce to you the meaning of my name Jehovah, the Lord. I show kindness and mercy to anyone I want to." 
Exodus33:20 But you may not see the glory of my face, for man may not see me and live." 
Exodus33:21 However, stand here on this rock beside me." 
Exodus33:22 And when my glory goes by, I will put you in the cleft of the rock and cover you with my hand until I have passed." 
Exodus33:23 Then I will remove my hand, and you shall see my back but not my face.“" 
Exodus34:1 The Lord told Moses, “Prepare two stone tablets like the first ones, and I will write upon them the same commands that were on the tablets you broke." 
Exodus34:2 Be ready in the morning to come up into Mount Sinai and present yourself to me on the top of the mountain. 
Exodus34:3 No one shall come with you and no one must be anywhere on the mountain. Do not let the flocks or herds feed close to the mountain.“ 
Exodus34:4 So Moses took two tablets of stone like the first ones, and was up early and climbed Mount Sinai, as the Lord had told him to, taking the two stone tablets in his hands." 
Exodus34:5 Then the Lord descended in the form of a pillar of cloud and stood there with him, and passed in front of him and announced the meaning of his name." 
Exodus34:6 “I am Jehovah, the merciful and gracious God,“ he said, “slow to anger and rich in steadfast love and truth." 
Exodus34:7 I, Jehovah, show this steadfast love to many thousands by forgiving their sins; or else I refuse to clear the guilty, and require that a father's sins be punished in the sons and grandsons, and even later generations.“" 
Exodus34:8 Moses fell down before the Lord and worshiped. 
Exodus34:9 And he said, “If it is true that I have found favor in your sight, O Lord, then please go with us to the Promised Land; yes, it is an unruly, stubborn people, but pardon our iniquity and our sins, and accept us as your own.“" 
Exodus34:10 The Lord replied, “All right, this is the contract I am going to make with you. I will do miracles such as have never been done before anywhere in all the earth, and all the people of Israel shall see the power of the Lord—the terrible power I will display through you." 
Exodus34:11 Your part of the agreement is to obey all of my commandments; then I will drive out from before you the Amorites, Canaanites, Hittites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites." 
Exodus34:12 “Be very, very careful never to compromise with the people there in the land where you are going, for if you do, you will soon be following their evil ways." 
Exodus34:13 Instead, you must break down their heathen altars, smash the obelisks they worship, and cut down their shameful idols." 
Exodus34:14 For you must worship no other gods, but only Jehovah, for he is a God who claims absolute loyalty and exclusive devotion." 
Exodus34:15 “No, do not make a peace treaty of any kind with the people living in the land, for they are spiritual prostitutes, committing adultery against me by sacrificing to their gods. If you become friendly with them and one of them invites you to go with him an" 
Exodus34:16 And you would accept their daughters, who worship other gods, as wives for your sons—and then your sons would commit adultery against me by worshiping their wives' gods." 
Exodus34:17 You must have nothing to do with idols. 
Exodus34:18 “Be sure to celebrate the Feast of Unleavened Bread for seven days, just as I instructed you, at the dates appointed each year in March; that was the month you left Egypt." 
Exodus34:19 “Every firstborn male is mine—cattle, sheep, and goats." 
Exodus34:20 The firstborn colt of a donkey may be redeemed by giving a lamb in its place. If you decide not to redeem it, then its neck must be broken. But your sons must all be redeemed. And no one shall appear before me without a gift." 
Exodus34:21 “Even during plowing and harvest times, work only six days, and rest on the seventh." 
Exodus34:22 “And you must remember to celebrate these three annual religious festivals: the Festival of Weeks, the Festival of the First Wheat, and the Harvest Festival." 
Exodus34:23 On each of these three occasions all the men and boys of Israel shall appear before the Lord. 
Exodus34:24 No one will attack and conquer your land when you go up to appear before the Lord your God those three times each year. For I will drive out the nations from before you and enlarge your boundaries. 
Exodus34:25 “You must not use leavened bread with your sacrifices to me, and none of the meat of the Passover lamb may be kept over until the following morning." 
Exodus34:26 And you must bring the best of the first of each year's crop to the Tabernacle of the Lord your God. You must not cook a young goat in its mother's milk.“ 
Exodus34:27 And the Lord said to Moses, “Write down these laws that I have given you, for they represent the terms of my covenant with you and with Israel.“" 
Exodus34:28 Moses was up on the mountain with the Lord for forty days and forty nights, and in all that time he neither ate nor drank. At that time God wrote out the Covenant—the Ten Commandments—on the stone tablets." 
Exodus34:29 Moses didn't realize as he came back down the mountain with the tablets that his face glowed from being in the presence of God. 
Exodus34:30 Because of this radiance upon his face, Aaron and the people of Israel were afraid to come near him." 
Exodus34:31 But Moses called them over to him, and Aaron and the leaders of the congregation came and talked with him." 
Exodus34:32 Afterwards, all the people came to him, and he gave them the commandments the Lord had given him upon the mountain." 
Exodus34:33 When Moses had finished speaking with them, he put a veil over his face;" 
Exodus34:34 but whenever he went into the Tabernacle to speak with the Lord, he removed the veil until he came out again; then he would pass on to the people whatever instructions God had given him," 
Exodus34:35 and the people would see his face aglow. Afterwards he would put the veil on again until he returned to speak with God. 
Exodus35:1 Now Moses called a meeting of all the people and told them, “These are the laws of Jehovah you must obey." 
Exodus35:2 “Work six days only; the seventh day is a day of solemn rest, a holy day to be used to worship Jehovah; anyone working on that day must die." 
Exodus35:3 Don't even light the fires in your homes that day.“ 
Exodus35:4 Then Moses said to all the people, “This is what the Lord has commanded:" 
Exodus35:5 All of you who wish to, all those with generous hearts, may bring these offerings to Jehovah: Gold, silver, and bronze;" 
Exodus35:6 Blue, purple, and scarlet cloth, made of fine-twined linen or of goats' hair;" 
Exodus35:7 Tanned rams' skins and specially treated goatskins; Acacia wood; 
Exodus35:8 Olive oil for the lamps; Spices for the anointing oil and for the incense; 
Exodus35:9 Onyx stones and stones to be used for the ephod and chestpiece. 
Exodus35:10 “Come, all of you who are skilled craftsmen having special talents, and construct what God has commanded us:" 
Exodus35:11 The Tabernacle tent, and its coverings, clasps, frames, bars, pillars, and bases;" 
Exodus35:12 The Ark and its poles; The place of mercy; The curtain to enclose the Holy Place; 
Exodus35:13 The table, its carrying poles, and all of its utensils; The Bread of the Presence;" 
Exodus35:14 Lamp holders, with lamps and oil;" 
Exodus35:15 The incense altar and its carrying poles; The anointing oil and sweet incense; The curtain for the door of the Tabernacle; 
Exodus35:16 The altar for the burnt offerings; The bronze grating of the altar, and its carrying poles and utensils; The basin with its pedestal;" 
Exodus35:17 The drapes for the walls of the court; The pillars and their bases; Drapes for the entrance to the court; 
Exodus35:18 The posts of the Tabernacle court, and their cords;" 
Exodus35:19 The beautiful clothing for the priests, to be used when ministering in the Holy Place; The holy garments for Aaron the priest, and for his sons.“" 
Exodus35:20 So all the people went to their tents to prepare their gifts. 
Exodus35:21 Those whose hearts were stirred by God's Spirit returned with their offerings of materials for the Tabernacle, its equipment, and for the holy garments." 
Exodus35:22 Both men and women came, all who were willing-hearted. They brought to the Lord their offerings of gold, jewelry—earrings, rings from their fingers, necklaces—and gold objects of every kind." 
Exodus35:23 Others brought blue, purple, and scarlet cloth made from the fine-twined linen or goats' hair; and rams' skins dyed red, and specially treated goatskins." 
Exodus35:24 Others brought silver and bronze as their offering to the Lord; and some brought the acacia wood needed in the construction. 
Exodus35:25 The women skilled in sewing and spinning prepared blue, purple, and scarlet thread and cloth, and fine-twined linen, and brought them in." 
Exodus35:26 Some other women gladly used their special skill to spin the goats' hair into cloth. 
Exodus35:27 The leaders brought onyx stones to be used for the ephod and the chestpiece; 
Exodus35:28 and spices, and oil—for the light, and for compounding the anointing oil and the sweet incense." 
Exodus35:29 So the people of Israel—every man and woman who wanted to assist in the work given to them by the Lord's command to Moses—brought their freewill offerings to him. 
Exodus35:30 And Moses told them, “Jehovah has specifically appointed Bezalel (the son of Uri and" 
Exodus35:31 grandson of Hur of the tribe of Judah) as general superintendent of the project. 
Exodus35:32 He will be able to create beautiful workmanship from gold, silver, and bronze;" 
Exodus35:33 he can cut and set stones like a jeweler and can do beautiful carving; in fact, he has every needed skill." 
Exodus35:34 And God has made him and Oholiab gifted teachers of their skills to others. (Oholiab is the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan.)" 
Exodus35:35 God has filled them both with unusual skills as jewelers, carpenters, embroidery designers in blue, purple, and scarlet on linen backgrounds, and as weavers—they excel in all the crafts we will be needing in the work." 
Exodus36:1 “All the other craftsmen with God-given abilities are to assist Bezalel and Oholiab in constructing and furnishing the Tabernacle.“ 
Exodus36:2 So Moses told Bezalel and Oholiab and all others who felt called to the work to begin. 
Exodus36:3 Moses gave them the materials donated by the people and additional gifts were received each morning. 
Exodus36:4 But finally the workmen all left their task 
Exodus36:5 to meet with Moses and told him, “We have more than enough materials on hand now to complete the job!“" 
Exodus36:6 So Moses sent a message throughout the camp announcing that no more donations were needed. 
Exodus36:7 Then at last the people were restrained from bringing more! 
Exodus36:8 The skilled weavers first made ten sheets from fine linen, then embroidered into them blue, purple, and scarlet Guardian Angels." 
Exodus36:9 Each sheet was 42 feet long and 6 feet wide. 
Exodus36:10 Five of these sheets were attached end to end, then five others similarly attached, forming two long roofsheets." 
Exodus36:11 Fifty blue ribbons were looped along the edges of these two long 
Exodus36:12 sheets, each loop being opposite its mate on the other long sheet." 
Exodus36:13 Then fifty clasps of gold were made to connect the loops, thus tying the two long sheets together to form the ceiling of the Tabernacle." 
Exodus36:14 Above the ceiling was a second layer formed by eleven draperies 
Exodus36:15 made of goats' hair (uniformly 45 feet long and 6 feet wide). 
Exodus36:16 Bezalel coupled five of these draperies together to make one long piece, and six others to make another long piece." 
Exodus36:17 Then he made fifty loops along the end of each 
Exodus36:18 and fifty small bronze clasps to couple the loops so that the draperies were firmly attached to each other. 
Exodus36:19 The top layer of the roof was made of rams' skins, dyed red, and tanned goatskins." 
Exodus36:20 For the sides of the Tabernacle he used frames of acacia wood standing on end. 
Exodus36:21 The height of each frame was 15 feet and the width 2 1/4 feet. 
Exodus36:22 Each frame had two clasps joining it to the next. 
Exodus36:23 There were twenty frames on the south side," 
Exodus36:24 with the bottoms fitting into forty silver bases. Each frame was connected to its base by two clasps. 
Exodus36:25 There were also twenty frames on the north side of 
Exodus36:26 the Tabernacle, with forty silver bases, two for each frame." 
Exodus36:27 The west side of the Tabernacle, which was its rear, was made from six frames," 
Exodus36:28 plus another at each corner. 
Exodus36:29 These frames, including those at the corners, were linked to each other at both top and bottom by rings." 
Exodus36:30 So, on the west side, there were a total of eight frames with sixteen silver bases beneath them, two for each frame." 
Exodus36:31 Then he made five sets of bars from acacia wood to tie 
Exodus36:32 the frames together along the sides, five for each side of the Tabernacle." 
Exodus36:33 The middle bar of the five was halfway up the frames, along each side, running from one end to the other." 
Exodus36:34 The frames and bars were all overlaid with gold, and the rings were pure gold." 
Exodus36:35 The blue, purple, and scarlet inner curtain was made from woven linen, with Guardian Angels skillfully embroidered into it." 
Exodus36:36 The curtain was then attached to four gold hooks set into four posts of acacia wood, overlaid with gold and set into four silver bases." 
Exodus36:37 Then he made a drapery for the entrance to the Tabernacle; it was woven from finespun linen, embroidered with blue, purple, and scarlet." 
Exodus36:38 This drapery was connected by five hooks to five posts. The posts and their capitals and rods were overlaid with gold; their five bases were molded from bronze. 
Exodus37:1 Next Bezalel made the Ark. This was constructed of acacia wood and was 3 3/4 feet long, 2 1/4 feet wide, and 2 1/4 feet high." 
Exodus37:2 It was plated with pure gold inside and out, and had a molding of gold all the way around the sides." 
Exodus37:3 There were four gold rings fastened into its four feet, two rings at each end." 
Exodus37:4 Then he made poles from acacia wood, and overlaid them with gold," 
Exodus37:5 and put the poles into the rings at the sides of the Ark, to carry it." 
Exodus37:6 Then, from pure gold, he made a lid called “the place of mercy“; it was 3 3/4 feet long and 2 1/4 feet wide." 
Exodus37:7 He made two statues of Guardian Angels of beaten gold and placed them at the two ends of the gold lid. 
Exodus37:8 They were molded so that they were actually a part of the gold lid—it was all one piece. 
Exodus37:9 The Guardian Angels faced each other, with outstretched wings that overshadowed the place of mercy, looking down upon it." 
Exodus37:10 Then he made a table, using acacia wood, 3 feet long, 1 1/2 feet wide, and 2 1/4 feet high." 
Exodus37:11 It was overlaid with pure gold, with a gold molding all around the edge." 
Exodus37:12 A rim 4 inches high was constructed around the edges of the table, with a gold molding along the rim." 
Exodus37:13 Then he cast four rings of gold and placed them into the four table legs," 
Exodus37:14 close to the molding, to hold the carrying poles in place." 
Exodus37:15 He made the carrying poles of acacia wood covered with gold. 
Exodus37:16 Next, using pure gold, he made the bowls, flagons, dishes, and spoons to be placed upon this table." 
Exodus37:17 Then he made the lampstand, again using pure, beaten gold. Its base, shaft, lamp-holders, and decorations of almond flowers were all of one piece." 
Exodus37:18 The lampstand had six branches, three from each side." 
Exodus37:19 Each of the branches was decorated with identical carvings of blossoms. 
Exodus37:20 The main stem of the lampstand was similarly decorated with almond blossoms," 
Exodus37:21 a flower on the stem beneath each pair of branches; also a flower below the bottom pair and above the top pair, four in all." 
Exodus37:22 The decorations and branches were all one piece of pure, beaten gold." 
Exodus37:23 Then he made the seven lamps at the ends of the branches, the snuffers, and the ashtrays, all of pure gold." 
Exodus37:24 The entire lampstand weighed 107 pounds, all pure gold." 
Exodus37:25 The incense altar was made of acacia wood. It was 18 inches square and 3 feet high, with its corner-horns made as part of the altar so that it was all one piece." 
Exodus37:26 He overlaid it all with pure gold and ran a gold molding around the edge. 
Exodus37:27 Two gold rings were placed on each side, beneath this molding, to hold the carrying poles." 
Exodus37:28 The carrying poles were gold-plated acacia wood. 
Exodus37:29 Then, from sweet spices, he made the sacred oil for anointing the priests, and the pure incense, using the techniques of the most skilled perfumers." 
Exodus38:1 The burnt-offering altar was also constructed of acacia wood; it was 7 1/2 feet square at the top, and 4 1/2 feet high." 
Exodus38:2 There were four horns at the four corners, all of one piece with the rest. This altar was overlaid with bronze." 
Exodus38:3 Then he made bronze utensils to be used with the altar—the pots, shovels, basins, meat hooks, and fire pans." 
Exodus38:4 Next he made a bronze grating that rested upon a ledge about halfway up in the firebox. 
Exodus38:5 Four rings were cast for each side of the grating, to insert the carrying poles." 
Exodus38:6 The carrying poles themselves were made of acacia wood, overlaid with bronze." 
Exodus38:7 The carrying poles were inserted into the rings at the side of the altar. The altar was hollow, with plank siding." 
Exodus38:8 The bronze washbasin and its bronze pedestal were cast from the solid bronze mirrors donated by the women who assembled at the entrance to the Tabernacle. 
Exodus38:9 Then he constructed the courtyard. The south wall was 150 feet long; it consisted of drapes woven from fine-twined linen thread. 
Exodus38:10 There were twenty posts to hold drapes, with bases of bronze and with silver hooks and rods." 
Exodus38:11 The north wall was also 150 feet long, with twenty bronze posts and bases and with silver hooks and rods." 
Exodus38:12 The west side was 75 feet wide; the walls were made from drapes supported by ten posts and bases, and with silver hooks and rods." 
Exodus38:13 The east side was also 75 feet wide. 
Exodus38:14 The drapes at either side of the entrance were 22 
Exodus38:15 1/2 feet wide, each with three posts and three bases." 
Exodus38:16 All the drapes making up the walls of the court were woven of fine-twined linen. 
Exodus38:17 Each post had a bronze base, and all the hooks and rods were silver; the tops of the posts were overlaid with silver, and the rods to hold up the drapes were solid silver." 
Exodus38:18 The drapery covering the entrance to the court was made of fine-twined linen, beautifully embroidered with blue, purple, and scarlet thread. It was 30 feet long and 7 1/2 feet wide, just the same as the drapes composing the walls of the court." 
Exodus38:19 It was supported by four posts, with four bronze bases and with silver hooks and rods; the tops of the posts were also silver." 
Exodus38:20 All the nails used in constructing the Tabernacle and court were bronze. 
Exodus38:21 This summarizes the various steps in building the Tabernacle to house the Ark, so that the Levites could carry on their ministry. All was done in the order designated by Moses and was supervised by Ithamar, son of Aaron the priest." 
Exodus38:22 Bezalel (son of Uri and grandson of Hur, of the tribe of Judah) was the master craftsman," 
Exodus38:23 assisted by Oholiab (son of Ahisamach of the tribe of Dan); he too was a skilled craftsman and also an expert at engraving, weaving, and at embroidering blue, purple, and scarlet threads into fine linen cloth." 
Exodus38:24 The people brought gifts of 3,140 pounds of gold, all of which was used throughout the Tabernacle." 
Exodus38:25 The amount of silver used was 9,575 pounds, which came from the fifty-cent head tax collected from all those registered in the census" 
Exodus38:26 who were twenty years old or older, a total of 603,550 men." 
Exodus38:27 The bases for the frames of the sanctuary walls and for the posts supporting the veil required 9,500 pounds of silver, 95 pounds for each socket." 
Exodus38:28 The silver left over was used for the posts and to overlay their tops, and for the rods and hooks." 
Exodus38:29 The people brought 7,540 pounds of bronze," 
Exodus38:30 which was used for casting the bases for the posts at the entrance to the Tabernacle, and for the bronze altar, the bronze grating, the altar utensils," 
Exodus38:31 the bases for the posts supporting the drapes enclosing the court, and for all the nails used in the construction of the Tabernacle and the court." 
Exodus39:1 Then, for the priests, the people made beautiful garments of blue, purple, and scarlet cloth—garments to be used while ministering in the Holy Place. This same cloth was used for Aaron's sacred garments, in accordance with the Lord's instructions to Moses." 
Exodus39:2 The ephod was made from this cloth too, woven from fine-twined linen thread." 
Exodus39:3 Bezalel beat gold into thin plates and cut it into wire threads, to work into the blue, purple, and scarlet linen; it was a skillful and beautiful piece of workmanship when finished." 
Exodus39:4 The ephod was held together by shoulder straps at the top 
Exodus39:5 and was tied down by an elaborate one-piece woven sash made of the same gold, blue, purple, and scarlet cloth cut from fine-twined linen thread, just as God had directed Moses." 
Exodus39:6 The two onyx stones, attached to the two shoulder straps of the ephod, were set in gold, and the stones were engraved with the names of the tribes of Israel, just as initials are engraved upon a ring." 
Exodus39:7 These stones were reminders to Jehovah concerning the people of Israel; all this was done in accordance with the Lord's instructions to Moses. 
Exodus39:8 The chestpiece was a beautiful piece of work, just like the ephod, made from the finest gold, blue, purple, and scarlet linen." 
Exodus39:9 It was a piece nine inches square, doubled over to form a pouch;" 
Exodus39:10 there were four rows of stones across it. In the first row were a sardius, a topaz, and a carbuncle;" 
Exodus39:11 in the second row were an emerald, a sapphire, and a diamond." 
Exodus39:12 In the third row were a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst." 
Exodus39:13 In the fourth row, a beryl, an onyx, and a jasper—all set in gold filigree." 
Exodus39:14 The stones were engraved like a seal, with the names of the twelve tribes of Israel." 
Exodus39:15 To attach the chestpiece to the ephod, a gold ring was placed at the top of each shoulder strap of the ephod," 
Exodus39:16 and from these gold rings, two strands" 
Exodus39:17 of twined gold attached to gold clasps 
Exodus39:18 on the top corners of the chestpiece. 
Exodus39:19 Two gold rings were also set at the lower edge of the chestpiece, on the under side, next to the ephod." 
Exodus39:20 Two other gold rings were placed low on the shoulder straps of the ephod, close to where the ephod joined its beautifully woven sash." 
Exodus39:21 The chestpiece was held securely above the beautifully woven sash of the ephod by tying the rings of the chestpiece to the rings of the ephod with a blue ribbon. All this was commanded to Moses by the Lord. 
Exodus39:22 The main part of the ephod was woven, all of blue," 
Exodus39:23 and there was a hole at the center, just as in a coat of mail, for the head to go through, reinforced around the edge so that it would not tear." 
Exodus39:24 Pomegranates were attached to the bottom edge of the robe; these were made of linen cloth, embroidered with blue, purple, and scarlet." 
Exodus39:25 Bells of pure gold were placed between the pomegranates along the bottom edge of the skirt," 
Exodus39:26 with bells and pomegranates alternating all around the edge. This robe was worn when Aaron ministered to the Lord, just as the Lord had commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:27 Robes were now made for Aaron and his sons from fine-twined linen thread. 
Exodus39:28 The chestpiece, the beautiful turbans, and the caps and the underclothes were all made of this linen," 
Exodus39:29 and the linen belt was beautifully embroidered with blue, purple, and scarlet threads, just as Jehovah had commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:30 Finally, they made the holy plate of pure gold to wear on the front of the turban, engraved with the words, “Consecrated to Jehovah.“" 
Exodus39:31 It was tied to the turban with a blue cord, just as the Lord had instructed." 
Exodus39:32 And so at last the Tabernacle was finished, following all of the Lord's instructions to Moses." 
Exodus39:33 Then they brought the entire Tabernacle to Moses: Furniture; clasps; frames; bars; Posts; bases; layers of covering for the roof and sides— 
Exodus39:34 the rams' skins dyed red, the specially tanned goatskins, and the entrance drape;" 
Exodus39:35 the Ark with the Ten Commandments in it; The carrying poles; The place of mercy; 
Exodus39:36 The table and all its utensils; The Bread of the Presence; 
Exodus39:37 The pure gold lampstand with its lamps, utensils, and oil;" 
Exodus39:38 The gold altar; The anointing oil; The sweet incense; The curtain-door of the Tabernacle; 
Exodus39:39 The bronze altar; The bronze grating; The poles and the utensils; The washbasin and its base; 
Exodus39:40 The drapes for the walls of the court and the posts holding them up; The bases and the drapes at the gate of the court; The cords and nails; All the utensils used there in the work of the Tabernacle. 
Exodus39:41 They also brought for his inspection the beautifully tailored garments to be worn while ministering in the Holy Place and the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and those for his sons, to be worn when on duty." 
Exodus39:42 So the people of Israel followed all the Lord's instructions to Moses. 
Exodus39:43 And Moses inspected all their work and blessed them because it was all as the Lord had instructed him. 
Exodus40:1 The Lord now said to Moses," 
Exodus40:2 “Put together the Tabernacle on the first day of the first month. 
Exodus40:3 In it place the Ark containing the Ten Commandments; and install the veil to enclose the Ark within the Holy of Holies. 
Exodus40:4 Then bring in the table and place the utensils on it, and bring in the lampstand and light the lamps." 
Exodus40:5 “Place the gold altar for the incense in front of the Ark. Set up the drapes at the entrance of the Tabernacle," 
Exodus40:6 and place the altar for burnt offerings in front of the entrance. 
Exodus40:7 Set the washbasin between the Tabernacle-tent and the altar, and fill it with water." 
Exodus40:8 Then make the courtyard around the outside of the tent, and hang the curtain-door at the entrance to the courtyard." 
Exodus40:9 “Take the anointing oil and sprinkle it here and there upon the Tabernacle and everything in it, upon all of its utensils and parts, and all the furniture, to hallow it; and it shall become holy." 
Exodus40:10 Sprinkle the anointing oil upon the altar of burnt offering and its utensils, sanctifying it; for the altar shall then become most holy." 
Exodus40:11 Then anoint the washbasin and its pedestal, sanctifying it." 
Exodus40:12 “Now bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the Tabernacle and wash them with water; 
Exodus40:13 and clothe Aaron with the holy garments and anoint him, sanctifying him to minister to me as a priest." 
Exodus40:14 Then bring his sons and put their robes upon them," 
Exodus40:15 and anoint them as you did their father, that they may minister to me as priests; their anointing shall be permanent from generation to generation: all their children and children's children shall forever be my priests.“" 
Exodus40:16 So Moses proceeded to do all as the Lord had commanded him. 
Exodus40:17 On the first day of the first month, in the second year, the Tabernacle was put together." 
Exodus40:18 Moses erected it by setting its frames into their bases and attaching the bars. 
Exodus40:19 Then he spread the coverings over the framework and put on the top layers, just as the Lord had commanded him." 
Exodus40:20 Inside the Ark he placed the stones with the Ten Commandments engraved on them, and attached the carrying poles to the Ark and installed the gold lid, the place of mercy." 
Exodus40:21 Then he brought the Ark into the Tabernacle and set up the curtain to screen it, just as the Lord had commanded." 
Exodus40:22 Next he placed the table at the north side of the room outside the curtain 
Exodus40:23 and set the Bread of the Presence upon the table before the Lord, just as the Lord had commanded." 
Exodus40:24 And he placed the lampstand next to the table, on the south side of the Tabernacle." 
Exodus40:25 Then he lighted the lamps before the Lord, following all the instructions," 
Exodus40:26 and placed the gold altar in the Tabernacle next to the curtain," 
Exodus40:27 and burned upon it the incense made from sweet spices, just as the Lord had commanded." 
Exodus40:28 He attached the curtain at the entrance of the Tabernacle," 
Exodus40:29 and placed the outside altar for the burnt offerings near the entrance, and offered upon it a burnt offering and a meal offering, just as the Lord had commanded him." 
Exodus40:30 Next he placed the washbasin between the tent and the altar and filled it with water so that the priests could use it for washing. 
Exodus40:31 Moses and Aaron and Aaron's sons washed their hands and feet there. 
Exodus40:32 Whenever they walked past the altar to enter the Tabernacle, they stopped and washed, just as the Lord had commanded Moses." 
Exodus40:33 Then he erected the enclosure surrounding the tent and the altar, and set up the curtain-door at the entrance of the enclosure. So at last Moses finished the work." 
Exodus40:34 Then the cloud covered the Tabernacle and the glory of the Lord filled it. 
Exodus40:35 Moses was not able to enter because the cloud was standing there, and the glory of the Lord filled the Tabernacle." 
Exodus40:36 Whenever the cloud lifted and moved, the people of Israel journeyed onward, following it." 
Exodus40:37 But if the cloud stayed, they stayed until it moved." 
Exodus40:38 The cloud rested upon the Tabernacle during the daytime, and at night there was fire in the cloud so that all the people of Israel could see it. This continued throughout all their journeys." 
Leviticus1:1 The Lord now spoke to Moses from the Tabernacle," 
Leviticus1:2 and commanded him to give the following instructions to the people of Israel: “When you sacrifice to the Lord, use animals from your herds and flocks." 
Leviticus1:3 “If your sacrifice is to be an ox given as a burnt offering, use only a bull with no physical defects. Bring the animal to the entrance of the Tabernacle where the priests will accept your gift for the Lord." 
Leviticus1:4 The person bringing it is to lay his hand upon its head, and it then becomes his substitute: the death of the animal will be accepted by God instead of the death of the man who brings it, as the penalty for his sins." 
Leviticus1:5 The man shall then kill the animal there before the Lord, and Aaron's sons, the priests, will present the blood before the Lord, sprinkling it upon all sides of the altar at the entrance of the Tabernacle." 
Leviticus1:6 Then the priests will skin the animal and quarter 
Leviticus1:7 it, and build a wood fire upon the altar," 
Leviticus1:8 and put the sections of the animal and its head and fat upon the wood. 
Leviticus1:9 The internal organs and the legs are to be washed, then the priests will burn them upon the altar, and they will be an acceptable burnt offering with which the Lord is pleased." 
Leviticus1:10 “If the animal used as a burnt offering is a sheep or a goat, it too must be a male, and without any blemishes." 
Leviticus1:11 The man who brings it will kill it before the Lord on the north side of the altar, and Aaron's sons, the priests, will sprinkle its blood back and forth upon the altar." 
Leviticus1:12 Then the man will quarter it, and the priests will lay the pieces, with the head and the fat, on top of the wood on the altar." 
Leviticus1:13 But the internal organs and the legs shall first be washed with water. Then the priests shall burn it all upon the altar as an offering to the Lord; for burnt offerings give much pleasure to the Lord. 
Leviticus1:14 “If anyone wishes to use a bird as his burnt offering, he may choose either turtledoves or young pigeons." 
Leviticus1:15 A priest will take the bird to the altar and wring off its head, and the blood shall be drained out at the side of the altar." 
Leviticus1:16 Then the priest will remove the crop and the feathers and throw them on the east side of the altar with the ashes. 
Leviticus1:17 Then, grasping it by the wings, he shall tear it apart, but not completely. And the priest shall burn it upon the altar, and the Lord will have pleasure in this sacrifice." 
Leviticus2:1 “Anyone who wishes to sacrifice a grain offering to the Lord is to bring fine flour and is to pour olive oil and incense upon it. 
Leviticus2:2 Then he is to take a handful, representing the entire amount, to one of the priests to burn, and the Lord will be fully pleased." 
Leviticus2:3 The remainder of the flour is to be given to Aaron and his sons as their food; but all of it is counted as a holy burnt offering to the Lord. 
Leviticus2:4 “If bread baked in the oven is brought as an offering to the Lord, it must be made from finely ground flour, baked with olive oil but without yeast. Wafers made without yeast and spread with olive oil may also be used as an offering." 
Leviticus2:5 If the offering is something from the griddle, it shall be made of finely ground flour without yeast, and mingled with olive oil." 
Leviticus2:6 Break it into pieces and pour oil upon it—it is a form of grain offering. 
Leviticus2:7 If your offering is cooked in a pan, it too shall be made of fine flour mixed with olive oil." 
Leviticus2:8 “However it is prepared—whether baked, fried, or grilled—you are to bring this grain offering to the priest and he shall take it to the altar to present it to the Lord." 
Leviticus2:9 “The priests are to burn only a representative portion of the offering, but all of it will be fully appreciated by the Lord." 
Leviticus2:10 The remainder belongs to the priests for their own use, but it is all counted as a holy burnt offering to the Lord." 
Leviticus2:11 “Use no yeast with your offerings of flour; for no yeast or honey is permitted in burnt offerings to the Lord. 
Leviticus2:12 You may offer yeast bread and honey as thanksgiving offerings at harvest time, but not as burnt offerings." 
Leviticus2:13 “Every offering must be seasoned with salt, because the salt is a reminder of God's covenant." 
Leviticus2:14 “If you are offering from the first of your harvest, remove the kernels from a fresh ear, crush and roast them, then offer them to the Lord." 
Leviticus2:15 Put olive oil and incense on the offering, for it is a grain offering." 
Leviticus2:16 Then the priests shall burn part of the bruised grain mixed with oil and all of the incense as a representative portion before the Lord. 
Leviticus3:1 “When anyone wants to give an offering of thanksgiving to the Lord, he may use either a bull or a cow, but the animal must be entirely without defect if it is to be offered to the Lord!" 
Leviticus3:2 The man who brings the animal shall lay his hand upon its head and kill it at the door of the Tabernacle. Then Aaron's sons shall throw the blood against the sides of the altar 
Leviticus3:3 and shall burn before the Lord the fat that covers the 
Leviticus3:4 inward parts, the two kidneys and the loin-fat on them, and" 
Leviticus3:5 the gall bladder. And it will give the Lord much pleasure. 
Leviticus3:6 “If a goat or sheep is used as a thank-offering to the Lord, it must have no defect and may be either a male or female." 
Leviticus3:7 “If it is a lamb, the man who brings it" 
Leviticus3:8 shall lay his hand upon its head and kill it at the entrance of the Tabernacle; the priests shall throw the blood against the sides of the altar," 
Leviticus3:9 and shall offer upon the altar the fat, the tail removed close to the backbone, the fat covering the internal organs," 
Leviticus3:10 the two kidneys with the loin-fat on them, and" 
Leviticus3:11 the gall bladder, as a burnt offering to the Lord." 
Leviticus3:12 “If anyone brings a goat as his offering to the Lord," 
Leviticus3:13 he shall lay his hand upon its head and kill it at the entrance of the Tabernacle. The priest shall throw its blood against the sides of the altar," 
Leviticus3:14 and shall offer upon the altar, as a burnt offering to the Lord, the fat that covers the insides," 
Leviticus3:15 the two kidneys and the loin-fat on them, and the gall bladder." 
Leviticus3:16 This burnt offering is very pleasing to the Lord. All the fat is Jehovah's. 
Leviticus3:17 This is a permanent law throughout your land, that you shall eat neither fat nor blood.“" 
Leviticus4:1 Then the Lord gave these further instructions to Moses: 
Leviticus4:2 “Tell the people of Israel that these are the laws concerning anyone who unintentionally breaks any of my commandments. 
Leviticus4:3 If a priest sins unintentionally and so brings guilt upon the people, he must offer a young bull without defect as a sin offering to the Lord." 
Leviticus4:4 He shall bring it to the door of the Tabernacle, and shall lay his hand upon its head and kill it there before Jehovah." 
Leviticus4:5 Then the priest shall take the animal's blood into the Tabernacle," 
Leviticus4:6 and shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle it seven times before the Lord in front of the veil that bars the way to the Holy of Holies. 
Leviticus4:7 Then the priest shall put some of the blood upon the horns of the incense altar before the Lord in the Tabernacle; the remainder of the blood shall be poured out at the base of the altar for burnt offerings, at the entrance to the Tabernacle." 
Leviticus4:8 Then he shall take all the fat on the entrails," 
Leviticus4:9 the two kidneys and the loin-fat on them, and the gall bladder," 
Leviticus4:10 and shall burn them on the altar of burnt offering, just as in the case of a bull or cow sacrificed as a thank-offering." 
Leviticus4:11 But the remainder of the young bull—the skin, meat, head, legs, internal organs, and intestines—" 
Leviticus4:12 shall be carried to a ceremonially clean place outside the camp—a place where the ashes are brought from the altar—and burned there on a wood fire. 
Leviticus4:13 “If the entire nation of Israel sins without realizing it and does something that Jehovah has said not to do, all the people are guilty." 
Leviticus4:14 When they realize it, they shall offer a young bull for a sin offering, bringing it to the Tabernacle" 
Leviticus4:15 where the leaders of the nation shall lay their hands upon the animal's head and kill it before the Lord. 
Leviticus4:16 Then the priest shall bring its blood into the Tabernacle," 
Leviticus4:17 and shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle it seven times before the Lord, in front of the veil." 
Leviticus4:18 Then he shall put blood upon the horns of the altar there in the Tabernacle before the Lord, and all the remainder of the blood shall be poured out at the base of the burnt offering altar, at the entrance to the Tabernacle." 
Leviticus4:19 All the fat shall be removed and burned upon the altar. 
Leviticus4:20 He shall follow the same procedure as for a sin offering; in this way the priest shall make atonement for the nation, and everyone will be forgiven." 
Leviticus4:21 The priest shall then cart the young bull outside the camp and burn it there, just as though it were a sin offering for an individual, only this time it is a sin offering for the entire nation." 
Leviticus4:22 “If one of the leaders sins without realizing it and is guilty of disobeying one of God's laws," 
Leviticus4:23 as soon as it is called to his attention he must bring as his sacrifice a male goat without any physical defect. 
Leviticus4:24 He shall lay his hand upon its head and kill it at the place where the burnt offerings are killed, and present it to the Lord. This is his sin offering." 
Leviticus4:25 Then the priest shall take some of the blood of this sin offering and place it with his finger upon the horns of the altar of burnt offerings, and the rest of the blood shall be poured out at the base of the altar." 
Leviticus4:26 All the fat shall be burned upon the altar, just as if it were the fat of the sacrifice of a thank-offering; thus the priest shall make atonement for the leader concerning his sin, and he shall be forgiven." 
Leviticus4:27 “If any one of the common people sins and doesn't realize it, he is guilty." 
Leviticus4:28 But as soon as he does realize it, he is to bring as his sacrifice a female goat without defect to atone for his sin." 
Leviticus4:29 He shall bring it to the place where the animals for burnt offerings are killed, and there lay his hand upon the head of the sin offering and kill it." 
Leviticus4:30 And the priest shall take some of the blood with his finger and smear it upon the horns of the burnt offering altar. Then the priest shall pour out the remainder of the blood at the base of the altar. 
Leviticus4:31 All the fat shall be taken off, just as in the procedure for the thank-offering sacrifice, and the priest shall burn it upon the altar; and the Lord will appreciate it. Thus the priest shall make atonement for that man, and he shall be forgiven." 
Leviticus4:32 “However, if he chooses to bring a lamb as his sin offering, it must be a female without physical defect." 
Leviticus4:33 He shall bring it to the place where the burnt offerings are killed, and lay his hand upon its head and kill it there as a sin offering." 
Leviticus4:34 The priest shall take some of the blood with his finger and smear it upon the horns of the burnt offering altar, and all the rest of the blood shall be poured out at the base of the altar." 
Leviticus4:35 The fat shall be used just as in the case of a thank-offering lamb—the priest shall burn the fat on the altar as in any other sacrifice made to Jehovah by fire; and the priest shall make atonement for the man, and his sin shall be forgiven." 
Leviticus5:1 “Anyone refusing to give testimony concerning what he knows about a crime is guilty. 
Leviticus5:2 “Anyone touching anything ceremonially unclean—such as the dead body of an animal forbidden for food, wild or domesticated, or the dead body of some forbidden insect—is guilty, even though he wasn't aware of touching it." 
Leviticus5:3 Or if he touches human discharge of any kind, he becomes guilty as soon as he realizes that he has touched it." 
Leviticus5:4 “If anyone makes a rash vow, whether the vow is good or bad, when he realizes what a foolish vow he has taken, he is guilty." 
Leviticus5:5 “In any of these cases, he shall confess his sin" 
Leviticus5:6 and bring his guilt offering to the Lord, a female lamb or goat, and the priest shall make atonement for him, and he shall be freed from his sin, and need not fulfill the vow." 
Leviticus5:7 “If he is too poor to bring a lamb to the Lord, then he shall bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons as his guilt offering; one of the birds shall be his sin offering and the other his burnt offering." 
Leviticus5:8 The priest shall offer as the sin sacrifice whichever bird is handed to him first, breaking its neck, but not severing its head from its body." 
Leviticus5:9 Then he shall sprinkle some of the blood at the side of the altar and the rest shall be drained out at the base of the altar; this is the sin offering. 
Leviticus5:10 He shall offer the second bird as a burnt offering, following the customary procedures that have been set forth; so the priest shall make atonement for him concerning his sin, and he shall be forgiven." 
Leviticus5:11 “If he is too poor to bring turtledoves or young pigeons as his sin offering, then he shall bring a tenth of a bushel of fine flour. He must not mix it with olive oil or put any incense on it because it is a sin offering." 
Leviticus5:12 He shall bring it to the priest, and the priest shall take out a handful as a representative portion and burn it on the altar just as any other offering to Jehovah made by fire; this shall be his sin offering." 
Leviticus5:13 In this way the priest shall make atonement for him for any sin of this kind, and he shall be forgiven. The rest of the flour shall belong to the priest, just as was the case with the grain offering.“" 
Leviticus5:14 And the Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus5:15 “If anyone sins by unintentionally defiling what is holy, then he shall bring a ram without defect, worth whatever fine you charge against him, as his guilt offering to the Lord." 
Leviticus5:16 And he shall make restitution for the holy thing he has defiled, or the tithe omitted, by paying for the loss, plus a 20 percent penalty; he shall bring it to the priest, and the priest shall make atonement for him with the ram of the guilt offering, and he shall be forgiven." 
Leviticus5:17 “Anyone who disobeys some law of God without realizing it is guilty anyway," 
Leviticus5:18 and must bring his sacrifice of a value determined by Moses. This sacrifice shall be a ram without blemish taken to the priest as a guilt offering; with it the priest shall make atonement for him, so that he will be forgiven for whatever it is he has done without realizing it." 
Leviticus5:19 It must be offered as a guilt offering, for he is certainly guilty before the Lord.“" 
Leviticus6:1 And the Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus6:2 “If anyone sins against me by refusing to return a deposit on something borrowed or rented, or by refusing to return something entrusted to him, or by robbery, or by oppressing his neighbor," 
Leviticus6:3 or by finding a lost article and lying about it, swearing that he doesn't have it—" 
Leviticus6:4 on the day he is found guilty of any such sin, he shall restore what he took," 
Leviticus6:5 adding a 20 percent fine, and give it to the one he has harmed; and on the same day he shall bring his guilt offering to the Tabernacle." 
Leviticus6:6 His guilt offering shall be a ram without defect, and must be worth whatever value you demand. He shall bring it to the priest," 
Leviticus6:7 and the priest shall make atonement for him before the Lord, and he shall be forgiven.“" 
Leviticus6:8 Then the Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus6:9 “Give Aaron and his sons these regulations concerning the burnt offering: “The burnt offering shall be left upon the hearth of the altar all night, with the altar fire kept burning." 
Leviticus6:10 (The next morning) the priest shall put on his linen undergarments and his linen outer garments, and clean out the ashes of the burnt offering, and put them beside the altar." 
Leviticus6:11 Then he shall change his clothes and carry the ashes outside the camp to a place that is ceremonially clean. 
Leviticus6:12 Meanwhile, the fire on the altar must be kept burning—it must not go out. The priest shall put on fresh wood each morning, and lay the daily burnt offering on it, and burn the fat of the daily peace offering." 
Leviticus6:13 The fire must be kept burning upon the altar continually. It must never go out. 
Leviticus6:14 “These are the regulations concerning the grain offering: “Aaron's sons shall stand in front of the altar to offer it before the Lord. 
Leviticus6:15 The priest shall then take out a handful of the finely ground flour, with the olive oil and the incense mixed into it, and burn it upon the altar as a representative portion for the Lord; and it will be received with pleasure by the Lord." 
Leviticus6:16 After taking out this handful, the remainder of the flour will belong to Aaron and his sons for their food; it shall be eaten without yeast in the courtyard of the Tabernacle." 
Leviticus6:17 (Stress this instruction, that if it is baked, it must be without yeast.) I have given to the priests this part of the burnt offerings made to me. However, all of it is most holy, just as is the entire sin offering and the entire guilt offering." 
Leviticus6:18 It may be eaten by any male descendant of Aaron, any priest, generation after generation. But only the priests may eat these offerings made by fire to the Lord.“" 
Leviticus6:19 And Jehovah said to Moses," 
Leviticus6:20 “On the day Aaron and his sons are anointed and inducted into the priesthood, they shall bring to the Lord a regular grain offering—a tenth of a bushel of fine flour, half to be offered in the morning and half in the evening." 
Leviticus6:21 It shall be cooked on a griddle, using olive oil, and should be well cooked, then brought to the Lord as an offering that pleases him very much." 
Leviticus6:22 As the sons of the priests replace their fathers, they shall be inducted into office by offering this same sacrifice on the day of their anointing. This is a perpetual law." 
Leviticus6:23 These offerings shall be entirely burned up before the Lord; none of it shall be eaten.“ 
Leviticus6:24 Then the Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus6:25 “Tell Aaron and his sons that these are the instructions concerning the sin offering: “This sacrifice is most holy, and shall be killed before the Lord at the place where the burnt offerings are killed." 
Leviticus6:26 The priest who performs the ceremony shall eat it in the courtyard of the Tabernacle. 
Leviticus6:27 Only those who are sanctified—the priests—may touch this meat; if any blood sprinkles onto their clothing, it must be washed in a holy place." 
Leviticus6:28 Then the clay pot in which the clothing is boiled shall be broken; or if a bronze kettle is used, it must be scoured and rinsed out thoroughly." 
Leviticus6:29 Every male among the priests may eat this offering, but only they, for it is most holy." 
Leviticus6:30 No sin offering may be eaten by the priests if any of its blood is taken into the Tabernacle to make atonement in the Holy Place. That carcass must be entirely burned with fire before the Lord. 
Leviticus7:1 “Here are the instructions concerning the most holy offering for guilt: 
Leviticus7:2 “The sacrificial animal shall be killed at the place where the burnt offering sacrifices are slain, and its blood shall be sprinkled back and forth upon the altar." 
Leviticus7:3 The priest will offer upon the altar all its fat, including the tail, the fat that covers the insides," 
Leviticus7:4 the two kidneys and the loin-fat, and the gall bladder—all shall be set aside for sacrificing." 
Leviticus7:5 The priests will burn them upon the altar as a guilt offering to the Lord. 
Leviticus7:6 Only males among the priests may then eat the carcass, and it must be eaten in a holy place, for this is a most holy sacrifice." 
Leviticus7:7 “The same instructions apply to both the sin offering and the guilt offering—the carcass shall be given to the priest who is in charge of the atonement ceremony, for his food." 
Leviticus7:8 (When the offering is a burnt sacrifice, the priest who is in charge shall also be given the animal's hide.)" 
Leviticus7:9 The priests who present the people's grain offerings to the Lord shall be given whatever remains of the sacrifice after the ceremony is completed. This rule applies whether the sacrifice is baked, fried, or grilled." 
Leviticus7:10 All other grain offerings, whether mixed with olive oil or dry, are the common property of all sons of Aaron." 
Leviticus7:11 “Here are the instructions concerning the sacrifices given to the Lord as special peace offerings: 
Leviticus7:12 “If it is an offering of thanksgiving, unleavened short bread shall be included with the sacrifice, along with unleavened wafers spread with olive oil and loaves from a batter of flour mixed with olive oil." 
Leviticus7:13 This thanksgiving peace offering shall be accompanied with loaves of leavened bread. 
Leviticus7:14 Part of this sacrifice shall be presented to the Lord by a gesture of waving it before the altar, then it shall be given to the assisting priest, the one who sprinkles the blood of the animal presented for the sacrifice." 
Leviticus7:15 After the animal has been sacrificed and presented to the Lord as a peace offering to show special appreciation and thanksgiving to him, its meat is to be eaten that same day, and none left to be eaten the next day." 
Leviticus7:16 “However, if someone brings a sacrifice that is not for thanksgiving, but is because of a vow or is simply a voluntary offering to the Lord, any portion of the sacrifice that is not eaten the day it is sacrificed may be eaten the next day." 
Leviticus7:17 But anything left over until the third day shall be burned. 
Leviticus7:18 For if any of it is eaten on the third day, the Lord will not accept it; it will have no value as a sacrifice, and there will be no credit to the one who brought it to be offered; and the priest who eats it shall be guilty, for it is detestable to the Lord, and the person who eats it must answer for his sin." 
Leviticus7:19 “Any meat that comes into contact with anything that is ceremonially unclean shall not be eaten, but burned; and as for the meat that may be eaten, it may be eaten only by a person who is ceremonially clean." 
Leviticus7:20 Any priest who is ceremonially unclean but eats the thanksgiving offering anyway, shall be cut off from his people, for he has defiled what is sacred." 
Leviticus7:21 Anyone who touches anything that is ceremonially unclean, whether it is uncleanness from man or beast, and then eats the peace offering, shall be cut off from his people, for he has defiled what is holy.“" 
Leviticus7:22 Then the Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus7:23 “Tell the people of Israel never to eat fat, whether from oxen, sheep, or goats." 
Leviticus7:24 The fat of an animal that dies of disease, or is attacked and killed by wild animals, may be used for other purposes, but never eaten." 
Leviticus7:25 Anyone who eats fat from an offering sacrificed by fire to the Lord shall be outlawed from his people. 
Leviticus7:26 “Never eat blood, whether of birds or animals." 
Leviticus7:27 Anyone who does shall be excommunicated from his people.“ 
Leviticus7:28 And the Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus7:29 “Tell the people of Israel that anyone bringing a thanksgiving offering to the Lord must bring it personally with his own hands. 
Leviticus7:30 He shall bring the offering of the fat and breast, which is to be presented to the Lord by waving it before the altar." 
Leviticus7:31 Then the priest shall burn the fat upon the altar, but the breast shall belong to Aaron and his sons," 
Leviticus7:32 while the right thigh shall 
Leviticus7:33 be given to the officiating priest. 
Leviticus7:34 For I have designated the breast and thigh as donations from the people of Israel to the sons of Aaron. Aaron and his sons must always be given this portion of the sacrifice. 
Leviticus7:35 This is their pay! It is to be set apart from the burnt offerings, and given to all who have been appointed to minister to the Lord as priests—to Aaron and to his sons." 
Leviticus7:36 For on the day the Lord anointed them, he commanded that the people of Israel give these portions to them; it is their right forever throughout all their generations.“" 
Leviticus7:37 These were the instructions concerning the burnt offering, grain offering, sin offering, and guilt offering, and concerning the consecration offering and the peace offering;" 
Leviticus7:38 these instructions were given to Moses by the Lord on Mount Sinai, to be passed on to the people of Israel so that they would know how to offer their sacrifices to God in the Sinai desert." 
Leviticus8:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus8:2 “Now bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the Tabernacle, together with their garments, the anointing oil, the young bull for the sin offering, the two rams, and the basket of bread made without yeast;" 
Leviticus8:3 and summon all Israel to a meeting there.“ 
Leviticus8:4 So all the people assembled," 
Leviticus8:5 and Moses said to them, “What I am now going to do has been commanded by Jehovah.“" 
Leviticus8:6 Then he took Aaron and his sons and washed them with water," 
Leviticus8:7 and he clothed Aaron with the special coat, sash, robe, and the ephod-jacket with its beautifully woven belt." 
Leviticus8:8 Then he put on him the chestpiece and deposited the Urim and the Thummim inside its pouch; 
Leviticus8:9 and placed on Aaron's head the turban with the sacred gold plate at its front—the holy crown—as the Lord had commanded Moses. 
Leviticus8:10 Then Moses took the anointing oil and sprinkled it upon the Tabernacle itself and on each item in it, sanctifying them." 
Leviticus8:11 When he came to the altar he sprinkled it seven times, and also sprinkled the utensils of the altar and the washbasin and its pedestal, to sanctify them." 
Leviticus8:12 Then he poured the anointing oil upon Aaron's head, thus setting him apart for his work." 
Leviticus8:13 Next Moses placed the robes on Aaron's sons, with the belts and caps, as the Lord had commanded him." 
Leviticus8:14 Then he took the young bull for the sin offering, and Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon its head" 
Leviticus8:15 as Moses killed it. He smeared some of the blood with his finger upon the four horns of the altar and upon the altar itself, to sanctify it, and poured out the rest of the blood at the base of the altar; thus he sanctified the altar, making atonement for it." 
Leviticus8:16 He took all the fat covering the entrails, the fatty mass above the liver, and the two kidneys and their fat, and burned them all on the altar." 
Leviticus8:17 The carcass of the young bull, with its hide and dung, was burned outside the camp, as the Lord had commanded Moses." 
Leviticus8:18 Then he presented to the Lord the ram for the burnt offering. Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon its head," 
Leviticus8:19 and Moses killed it and sprinkled the blood back and forth upon the altar. 
Leviticus8:20 Next he quartered the ram and burned the pieces, the head and the fat." 
Leviticus8:21 He then washed the insides and the legs with water, and burned them upon the altar, so that the entire ram was consumed before the Lord; it was a burnt offering that pleased the Lord very much, for Jehovah's directions to Moses were followed in every detail." 
Leviticus8:22 Then Moses presented the other ram, the ram of consecration; Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon its head." 
Leviticus8:23 Moses killed it and took some of its blood and smeared it upon the lobe of Aaron's right ear and the thumb of his right hand and upon the big toe of his right foot. 
Leviticus8:24 Next he smeared some of the blood upon Aaron's sons—upon the lobes of their right ears, upon their right thumbs, and upon the big toes of their right feet. The rest of the blood he sprinkled back and forth upon the altar." 
Leviticus8:25 Then he took the fat, the tail, the fat upon the inner organs, the gall bladder, the two kidneys with their fat, and the right shoulder," 
Leviticus8:26 and placed on top of these one unleavened wafer, one wafer spread with olive oil, and a slice of bread, all taken from the basket that had been placed there before the Lord." 
Leviticus8:27 All this was placed in the hands of Aaron and his sons to present to the Lord by a gesture of waving them before the altar. 
Leviticus8:28 Moses then took it all back from them and burned it upon the altar, along with the burnt offering to the Lord; and Jehovah was pleased by the offering." 
Leviticus8:29 Now Moses took the breast and presented it to the Lord by waving it before the altar; this was Moses' portion of the ram of consecration, just as the Lord had instructed him." 
Leviticus8:30 Next he took some of the anointing oil and some of the blood that had been sprinkled upon the altar, and sprinkled it upon Aaron and upon his clothes and upon his sons and upon their clothes, thus consecrating to the Lord's use Aaron and his sons and their clothes." 
Leviticus8:31 Then Moses said to Aaron and his sons, “Boil the meat at the entrance of the Tabernacle, and eat it along with the bread that is in the basket of consecration, just as I instructed you to do." 
Leviticus8:32 Anything left of the meat and bread must be burned.“ 
Leviticus8:33 Next he told them not to leave the Tabernacle entrance for seven days, after which time their consecration would be completed—for it takes seven days." 
Leviticus8:34 Then Moses stated again that all he had done that day had been commanded by the Lord in order to make atonement for them. 
Leviticus8:35 And again he warned Aaron and his sons to stay at the entrance of the Tabernacle day and night for seven days. “If you leave,“ he told them, “you will die—this is what the Lord has said.“" 
Leviticus8:36 So Aaron and his sons did all that the Lord had commanded Moses. 
Leviticus9:1 On the eighth day (of the consecration ceremonies), Moses summoned Aaron and Aaron's sons and the elders of Israel," 
Leviticus9:2 and told Aaron to take a bull calf from the herd for a sin offering, and a ram without bodily defect for a burnt offering, and to offer them before the Lord." 
Leviticus9:3 “And tell the people of Israel,“ Moses instructed, “to select a male goat for their sin offering, also a yearling calf and a yearling lamb, all without bodily defect, for their burnt offering." 
Leviticus9:4 In addition, the people are to bring to the Lord a peace offering sacrifice—an ox and a ram, and a grain offering—flour mingled with olive oil. For today,“ Moses said, “Jehovah will appear to them.“" 
Leviticus9:5 So they brought all these things to the entrance of the Tabernacle, as Moses had commanded, and the people came and stood there before the Lord." 
Leviticus9:6 Moses told them, “When you have followed the Lord's instructions, his glory will appear to you.“" 
Leviticus9:7 Moses then told Aaron to proceed to the altar and to offer the sin offering and the burnt offering, making atonement for himself first, and then for the people, as the Lord had commanded." 
Leviticus9:8 So Aaron went up to the altar and killed the calf as a sacrifice for his own sin; 
Leviticus9:9 his sons caught the blood for him, and he dipped his finger in it and smeared it upon the horns of the altar, and poured out the rest at the base of the altar." 
Leviticus9:10 Then he burned upon the altar the fat, kidneys, and gall bladder from this sin offering, as the Lord had commanded Moses," 
Leviticus9:11 but he burned the meat and hide outside the camp. 
Leviticus9:12 Next he killed the burnt offering animal, and his sons caught the blood, and he sprinkled it back and forth upon the altar;" 
Leviticus9:13 they brought the animal to him piece by piece, including the head, and he burned each part upon the altar." 
Leviticus9:14 Then he washed the insides and the legs, and offered these also upon the altar as a burnt offering." 
Leviticus9:15 Next he sacrificed the people's offering; he killed the goat and offered it in just the same way as he had the sin offering for himself. 
Leviticus9:16 Thus he sacrificed their burnt offering to the Lord, in accordance with the instructions God had given." 
Leviticus9:17 Then he presented the grain offering, taking a handful and burning it upon the altar in addition to the regular morning offering." 
Leviticus9:18 Next he killed the ox and ram—the people's peace offering sacrifice; and Aaron's sons brought the blood to him, and he sprinkled it back and forth upon the altar." 
Leviticus9:19 Then he collected the fat of the ox and the ram—the fat from their tails and the fat covering the inner organs—and the kidneys and gall bladders. 
Leviticus9:20 The fat was placed upon the breasts of these animals, and Aaron burned it upon the altar;" 
Leviticus9:21 but he waved the breasts and right shoulders slowly before the Lord as a gesture of offering it to him, just as Moses had commanded." 
Leviticus9:22 Then, with hands spread out toward the people, Aaron blessed them and came down from the altar." 
Leviticus9:23 Moses and Aaron went into the Tabernacle, and when they came out again they blessed the people; and the glory of the Lord appeared to the whole assembly." 
Leviticus9:24 Then fire came from the Lord and consumed the burnt offering and fat on the altar; and when the people saw it, they all shouted and fell flat upon the ground before the Lord." 
Leviticus10:1 But Nadab and Abihu, the sons of Aaron, placed unholy fire in their censers, laid incense on the fire, and offered the incense before the Lord—contrary to what the Lord had just commanded them!" 
Leviticus10:2 So fire blazed forth from the presence of the Lord and destroyed them. 
Leviticus10:3 Then Moses said to Aaron, “This is what the Lord meant when he said, 'I will show myself holy among those who approach me, and I will be glorified before all the people.' “ And Aaron was speechless." 
Leviticus10:4 Then Moses called for Mishael and Elzaphon, Aaron's cousins, the sons of Uzziel, and told them, “Go and get the charred bodies from before the Tabernacle, and carry them outside the camp.“" 
Leviticus10:5 So they went over and got them, and carried them out in their coats as Moses had told them to." 
Leviticus10:6 Then Moses said to Aaron and his sons Eleazar and Ithamar, “Do not mourn—do not let your hair hang loose as a sign of your mourning, and do not tear your clothes. If you do, God will strike you dead too, and his wrath will come upon all the people of Israel. But the rest of the people of Israel may lament the death of Nadab and Abihu, and mourn because of the terrible fire the Lord has sent." 
Leviticus10:7 But you are not to leave the Tabernacle under penalty of death, for the anointing oil of Jehovah is upon you.“ And they did as Moses commanded." 
Leviticus10:8 Now the Lord instructed Aaron," 
Leviticus10:9 “Never drink wine or strong drink when you go into the Tabernacle, lest you die; and this rule applies to your sons and to all your descendants from generation to generation." 
Leviticus10:10 Your duties will be to arbitrate for the people, to teach them the difference between what is holy and what is ordinary, what is pure and what is impure;" 
Leviticus10:11 and to teach them all the laws Jehovah has given through Moses.“ 
Leviticus10:12 Then Moses said to Aaron and to his sons who were left, Eleazar and Ithamar, “Take the grain offering—the food that remains after the handful has been offered to the Lord by burning it on the altar—make sure there is no leaven in it, and eat it beside the altar. The offering is most holy;" 
Leviticus10:13 therefore, you must eat it in the sanctuary, in a holy place. It belongs to you and to your sons, from the offerings to Jehovah made by fire; for so I am commanded." 
Leviticus10:14 But the breast and the thigh, which have been offered to the Lord by the gesture of waving it before him, may be eaten in any holy place. It belongs to you and to your sons and daughters for your food. It is your portion of the peace offering sacrifices of the people of Israel." 
Leviticus10:15 The people are to bring the thigh that was set aside, along with the breast that was offered when the fat was burned, and they shall be presented before the Lord by the gesture of waving them. And afterwards they shall belong to you and your family, for the Lord has commanded this.“" 
Leviticus10:16 Then Moses searched everywhere for the goat of the sin offering and discovered that it had been burned! He was very angry about this with Eleazar and Ithamar, the remaining sons of Aaron." 
Leviticus10:17 “Why haven't you eaten the sin offering in the sanctuary, since it is most holy, and God has given it to you to take away the iniquity and guilt of the people, to make atonement for them before the Lord?“ he demanded." 
Leviticus10:18 “Since its blood was not taken inside the sanctuary, you should certainly have eaten it there, as I ordered you.“" 
Leviticus10:19 But Aaron interceded with Moses. “They offered their sin offering and burnt offering before the Lord,“ he said, “but if I had eaten the sin offering on such a day as this, would it have pleased the Lord?“" 
Leviticus10:20 And when Moses heard that, he was satisfied." 
Leviticus11:1 Then the Lord said to Moses and Aaron," 
Leviticus11:2 “Tell the people of Israel that the animals which may be used 
Leviticus11:3 for food include any animal with cloven hooves which chews its cud. 
Leviticus11:4 This means that the following may not be eaten: The camel (it chews the cud but does not have cloven hooves); 
Leviticus11:5 The coney, or rock badger (because although it chews the cud, it does not have cloven hooves);" 
Leviticus11:6 The hare (because although it chews the cud, it does not have cloven hooves);" 
Leviticus11:7 The swine (because although it has cloven hooves, it does not chew the cud)." 
Leviticus11:8 You may not eat their meat or even touch their dead bodies; they are forbidden foods for you. 
Leviticus11:9 “As to fish, you may eat whatever has fins and scales, whether taken from rivers or from the sea;" 
Leviticus11:10 but all other water creatures are strictly forbidden to you. 
Leviticus11:11 You mustn't eat their meat or even touch their dead bodies. 
Leviticus11:12 I'll repeat it again—any water creature that does not have fins or scales is forbidden to you. 
Leviticus11:13 “Among the birds, these are the ones you may not eat: The eagle, the metire, the osprey," 
Leviticus11:14 The falcon (all kinds), the kite," 
Leviticus11:15 The raven (all kinds)," 
Leviticus11:16 the ostrich, The nighthawk, the seagull, The hawk (all kinds)," 
Leviticus11:17 the owl, The cormorant, the ibis," 
Leviticus11:18 The marsh hen, The pelican, The vulture," 
Leviticus11:19 the stork, The heron (all kinds), The hoopoe, the bat." 
Leviticus11:20 “No insects may be eaten," 
Leviticus11:21 with the exception of those that jump; locusts of 
Leviticus11:22 all varieties—ordinary locusts, bald locusts, crickets, and grasshoppers—may be eaten." 
Leviticus11:23 All insects that fly and walk or crawl are forbidden to you. 
Leviticus11:24 “Anyone touching their dead bodies shall be defiled until the evening 
Leviticus11:25 and must wash his clothes immediately. He must also quarantine himself until nightfall, as being ceremonially defiled." 
Leviticus11:26 “You are also defiled by touching any animal with only semiparted hoofs, or any animal that does not chew the cud." 
Leviticus11:27 Any animal that walks on paws is forbidden to you as food. Anyone touching the dead body of such an animal shall be defiled until evening. 
Leviticus11:28 Anyone carrying away the carcass shall wash his clothes and be ceremonially defiled until evening; for it is forbidden to you. 
Leviticus11:29 “These are the forbidden small animals which scurry about your feet or crawl upon the ground: The mole, the rat, The great lizard," 
Leviticus11:30 the gecko, The mouse, the lizard, The snail, the chameleon." 
Leviticus11:31 Anyone touching their dead bodies shall be defiled until evening,"
