Numbers20:5 Why did you ever make us leave Egypt and bring us here to this evil place? Where is the fertile land of wonderful crops—the figs, vines, and pomegranates you told us about? Why, there isn't even water enough to drink!“" 
Numbers20:6 Moses and Aaron turned away and went to the entrance of the Tabernacle, where they fell face downward before the Lord; and the glory of Jehovah appeared to them." 
Numbers20:7 And he said to Moses," 
Numbers20:8 “Get Aaron's rod; then you and Aaron must summon the people. As they watch, speak to that rock over there and tell it to pour out its water! You will give them water from a rock, enough for all the people and all their cattle!“" 
Numbers20:9 So Moses did as instructed. He took the rod from the place where it was kept before the Lord; 
Numbers20:10 then Moses and Aaron summoned the people to come and gather at the rock; and he said to them, “Listen, you rebels! Must we bring you water from this rock?“" 
Numbers20:11 Then Moses lifted the rod and struck the rock twice, and water gushed out; and the people and their cattle drank." 
Numbers20:12 But the Lord said to Moses and Aaron, “Because you did not believe me and did not sanctify me in the eyes of the people of Israel, you shall not bring them into the land I have promised them!“" 
Numbers20:13 This place was named Meribah (meaning “Rebel Waters“), because it was where the people of Israel fought against Jehovah, and where he showed himself to be holy before them." 
Numbers20:14 While Moses was at Kadesh he sent messengers to the king of Edom: “We are the descendants of your brother, Israel,“ he declared. “You know our sad history," 
Numbers20:15 how our ancestors went down to visit Egypt and stayed there so long, and became slaves of the Egyptians." 
Numbers20:16 But when we cried to the Lord he heard us and sent an Angel who brought us out of Egypt, and now we are here at Kadesh, encamped on the borders of your land." 
Numbers20:17 Please let us pass through your country. We will be careful not to go through your planted fields, nor through your vineyards; we won't even drink water from your wells, but will stay on the main road and not leave it until we have crossed your border on the other side.“" 
Numbers20:18 But the king of Edom said, “Stay out! If you attempt to enter my land, I will meet you with an army!“" 
Numbers20:19 “But, sir,“ protested the Israeli ambassadors, “we will stay on the main road and will not even drink your water unless we pay whatever you demand for it. We only want to pass through and nothing else.“" 
Numbers20:20 But the king of Edom was adamant. “Stay out!“ he warned, and, mobilizing his army, he marched to the frontier with a great force." 
Numbers20:21 Because Edom refused to allow Israel to pass through their 
Numbers20:22 country, Israel turned back and journeyed from Kadesh to Mount Hor." 
Numbers20:23 Then the Lord said to Moses and Aaron at the border of the land of Edom," 
Numbers20:24 “The time has come for Aaron to die—for he shall not enter the land I have given the people of Israel, for the two of you rebelled against my instructions concerning the water at Meribah." 
Numbers20:25 Now take Aaron and his son Eleazar and lead them up onto Mount Hor. 
Numbers20:26 There you shall remove Aaron's priestly garments from him and put them on Eleazar his son; and Aaron shall die there.“ 
Numbers20:27 So Moses did as the Lord commanded him. The three of them went up together into Mount Hor as all the people watched. 
Numbers20:28 When they reached the summit, Moses removed the priestly garments from Aaron and put them on his son Eleazar; and Aaron died on the top of the mountain. Moses and Eleazar returned," 
Numbers20:29 and when the people were informed of Aaron's death, they mourned for him for thirty days." 
Numbers21:1 When the king of Arad heard that the Israelis were approaching (for they were traveling the same route as the spies), he mobilized his army and attacked Israel, taking some of the men as prisoners." 
Numbers21:2 Then the people of Israel vowed to the Lord that if he would help them conquer the king of Arad and his people, they would completely annihilate all the cities of that area." 
Numbers21:3 The Lord heeded their request and defeated the Canaanites; and the Israelis completely destroyed them and their cities. The name of the region was thereafter called Hormah (meaning “Utterly Destroyed“). 
Numbers21:4 Then the people of Israel returned to Mount Hor, and from there continued southward along the road to the Red Sea in order to go around the land of Edom. The people were very discouraged;" 
Numbers21:5 they began to murmur against God and to complain against Moses. “Why have you brought us out of Egypt to die here in the wilderness?“ they whined. “There is nothing to eat here, and nothing to drink, and we hate this insipid manna.“" 
Numbers21:6 So the Lord sent poisonous snakes among them to punish them, and many of them were bitten and died." 
Numbers21:7 Then the people came to Moses and cried out, “We have sinned, for we have spoken against Jehovah and against you. Pray to him to take away the snakes.“ So Moses prayed for the people." 
Numbers21:8 Then the Lord told him, “Make a bronze replica of one of these snakes and attach it to the top of a pole; anyone who is bitten shall live if he simply looks at it!“" 
Numbers21:9 So Moses made the replica, and whenever anyone who had been bitten looked at the bronze snake, he recovered!" 
Numbers21:10 Israel journeyed next to Oboth and camped there. 
Numbers21:11 Then they went on to Iyeabarim, in the wilderness, a short distance east of Moab," 
Numbers21:12 and from there they traveled to the valley of the brook Zared and set up camp. 
Numbers21:13 Then they moved to the far side of the Arnon River, near the borders of the Amorites. (The Arnon River is the boundary line between the Moabites and the Amorites." 
Numbers21:14 This fact is mentioned in The Book of the Wars of Jehovah, where it is stated that the valley of the Arnon River and the city of Waheb" 
Numbers21:15 lie between the Amorites and the people of Moab.) 
Numbers21:16 Then Israel traveled to Beer (meaning “A Well“). This is the place where the Lord told Moses, “Summon the people, and I will give them water.“" 
Numbers21:17 What happened is described in this song the people sang: Spring up, O well! Sing of the water!" 
Numbers21:18 This is a well The leaders dug. It was hollowed With their staves And shovels. Then they left the desert and proceeded on through Mattanah," 
Numbers21:19 Nahaliel, and Bamoth;" 
Numbers21:20 then to the valley in the plateau of Moab, which overlooks the desert with Mount Pisgah in the distance." 
Numbers21:21 Israel now sent ambassadors to King Sihon of the Amorites. 
Numbers21:22 “Let us travel through your land,“ they requested. “We will not leave the road until we have passed beyond your borders. We won't trample your fields or touch your vineyards or drink your water.“" 
Numbers21:23 But King Sihon refused. Instead he mobilized his army and attacked Israel in the wilderness, battling them at Jahaz." 
Numbers21:24 But Israel slaughtered them and occupied their land from the Arnon River to the Jabbok River, as far as the borders of the Ammonites; but they were stopped there by the rugged terrain." 
Numbers21:25 So Israel captured all the cities of the Amorites and lived in 
Numbers21:26 them, including the city of Heshbon, which had been King Sihon's capital." 
Numbers21:27 The ancient poets had referred to King Sihon in this poem: Come to Heshbon, King Sihon's capital," 
Numbers21:28 For a fire has flamed forth And devoured The city of Ar in Moab, On the heights of the Arnon River." 
Numbers21:29 Woe to Moab! You are finished, O people of Chemosh; Your sons have fled, And your daughters are captured By King Sihon of the Amorites." 
Numbers21:30 He has destroyed The little children And the men and women As far as Dibon, Nophah, and Medeba." 
Numbers21:31 While Israel was there in the Amorite country," 
Numbers21:32 Moses sent spies to look over the Jazer area; he followed up with an armed attack, capturing all of the towns and driving out the Amorites." 
Numbers21:33 They next turned their attention to the city of Bashan, but King Og of Bashan met them with his army at Edrei." 
Numbers21:34 The Lord told Moses not to fear—that the enemy was already conquered! “The same thing will happen to King Og as happened to King Sihon at Heshbon,“ the Lord assured him." 
Numbers21:35 And sure enough, Israel was victorious and killed King Og, his sons, and his subjects, so that not a single survivor remained; and Israel occupied the land." 
Numbers22:1 The people of Israel now traveled to the plains of Moab and camped east of the Jordan River opposite Jericho. 
Numbers22:2 When King Balak of Moab (the son of Zippor) realized how many of them there were," 
Numbers22:3 and when he learned what they had done to the Amorites, he and his people were terrified." 
Numbers22:4 They quickly consulted with the leaders of Midian. “This mob will eat us like an ox eats grass,“ they exclaimed. So King Balak" 
Numbers22:5 sent messengers to Balaam (son of Beor) who was living in his native land of Pethor, near the Euphrates River. He begged Balaam to come and help him. “A vast horde of people has arrived from Egypt, and they cover the face of the earth and are headed toward me,“ he frantically explained." 
Numbers22:6 “Please come and curse them for me, so that I can drive them out of my land; for I know what fantastic blessings fall on those whom you bless, and I also know that those whom you curse are doomed.“" 
Numbers22:7 The messengers he sent were some of the top leaders of Moab and Midian. They went to Balaam with money in hand and urgently explained to him what Balak wanted. 
Numbers22:8 “Stay here overnight,“ Balaam said, “and I'll tell you in the morning whatever the Lord directs me to say.“ So they did." 
Numbers22:9 That night God came to Balaam and asked him, “Who are these men?“" 
Numbers22:10 “They have come from King Balak of Moab,“ he replied." 
Numbers22:11 “The king says that a vast horde of people from Egypt has arrived at his border, and he wants me to go at once and curse them, in the hope that he can battle them successfully.“" 
Numbers22:12 “Don't do it!“ God told him. “You are not to curse them, for I have blessed them!“" 
Numbers22:13 The next morning Balaam told the men, “Go on home! The Lord won't let me do it.“" 
Numbers22:14 So King Balak's ambassadors returned without him and reported his refusal. 
Numbers22:15 Balak tried again. This time he sent a larger number of even more distinguished ambassadors than the former group. 
Numbers22:16 They came to Balaam with this message: “King Balak pleads with you to come. 
Numbers22:17 He promises you great honors plus any payment you ask. Name your own figure! Only come and curse these people for us.“ 
Numbers22:18 But Balaam replied, “If he were to give me a palace filled with silver and gold, I could do nothing contrary to the command of the Lord my God." 
Numbers22:19 However, stay here tonight so that I can find out whether the Lord will add anything to what he said before.“" 
Numbers22:20 That night God told Balaam, “You may get up and go with these men, but be sure to say only what I tell you to.“" 
Numbers22:21 So the next morning he saddled his donkey and started off with them. 
Numbers22:22 But God was angry about Balaam's eager attitude, so he sent an angel to stand in the road to kill him. As Balaam and two servants were riding along," 
Numbers22:23 Balaam's donkey suddenly saw the angel of the Lord standing in the road with a drawn sword. She bolted off the road into a field, but Balaam beat her back onto the road." 
Numbers22:24 Now the angel of the Lord stood at a place where the road went between two vineyard walls. 
Numbers22:25 When the donkey saw him standing there, she squirmed past by pressing against the wall, crushing Balaam's foot in the process. So he beat her again." 
Numbers22:26 Then the angel of the Lord moved farther down the road and stood in a place so narrow that the donkey couldn't get by at all. 
Numbers22:27 So she lay down in the road! In a great fit of temper Balaam beat her again with his staff. 
Numbers22:28 Then the Lord caused the donkey to speak! “What have I done that deserves your beating me these three times?“ she asked. 
Numbers22:29 “Because you have made me look like a fool!“ Balaam shouted. “I wish I had a sword with me, for I would kill you.“" 
Numbers22:30 “Have I ever done anything like this before in my entire life?“ the donkey asked. “No,“ he admitted." 
Numbers22:31 Then the Lord opened Balaam's eyes and he saw the angel standing in the roadway with drawn sword, and he fell flat on the ground before him." 
Numbers22:32 “Why did you beat your donkey those three times?“ the angel demanded. “I have come to stop you because you are headed for destruction. 
Numbers22:33 Three times the donkey saw me and shied away from me; otherwise I would certainly have killed you by now and spared her.“ 
Numbers22:34 Then Balaam confessed, “I have sinned. I didn't realize you were there. I will go back home if you don't want me to go on.“" 
Numbers22:35 But the angel told him, “Go with the men, but say only what I tell you to say.“ So Balaam went on with them." 
Numbers22:36 When King Balak heard that Balaam was on the way, he left the capital and went out to meet him at the Arnon River, at the border of his land." 
Numbers22:37 “Why did you delay so long?“ he asked Balaam. “Didn't you believe me when I said I would give you great honors?“ 
Numbers22:38 Balaam replied, “I have come, but I have no power to say anything except what God tells me to say; and that is what I shall speak.“" 
Numbers22:39 Balaam accompanied the king to Kiriathhuzoth," 
Numbers22:40 where King Balak sacrificed oxen and sheep, and gave animals to Balaam and the ambassadors for their sacrifices." 
Numbers22:41 The next morning Balak took Balaam to the top of Mount Bamoth-baal, from which he could see the people of Israel spread out before him." 
Numbers23:1 Balaam said to the king, “Build seven altars here, and prepare seven young bulls and seven rams for sacrifice.“" 
Numbers23:2 Balak followed his instructions, and a young bull and a ram were sacrificed on each altar." 
Numbers23:3 Then Balaam said to the king, “Stand here by your burnt offerings and I will see if the Lord will meet me; and I will tell you what he says to me.“ So he went up to a barren height," 
Numbers23:4 and God met him there. Balaam told the Lord, “I have prepared seven altars and have sacrificed a young bull and a ram on each.“" 
Numbers23:5 Then the Lord gave Balaam a message for King Balak. 
Numbers23:6 When Balaam returned, the king was standing beside the burnt offerings with all the princes of Moab." 
Numbers23:7 This was Balaam's message: “King Balak, king of Moab, has brought me From the land of Aram, From the eastern mountains. 'Come,' he told me, 'curse Jacob for me! Let your anger rise on Israel.'" 
Numbers23:8 But how can I curse What God has not cursed? How can I denounce A people God has not denounced? 
Numbers23:9 I see them from the cliff tops, I watch them from the hills. They live alone, And prefer to remain distinct From every other nation." 
Numbers23:10 They are as numerous as dust! They are beyond numbering. If only I could die as happy as an Israelite! Oh, that my end might be like theirs!“" 
Numbers23:11 “What have you done to me?“ demanded King Balak. “I told you to curse my enemies, and now you have blessed them!“" 
Numbers23:12 But Balaam replied, “Can I say anything except what Jehovah tells me to?“" 
Numbers23:13 Then Balak told him, “Come with me to another place; there you will see only a portion of the nation of Israel. Curse at least that many!“" 
Numbers23:14 So King Balak took Balaam into the fields of Zophim at the top of Mount Pisgah, and built seven altars there; and he offered up a young bull and a ram on each altar." 
Numbers23:15 Then Balaam said to the king, “Stand here by your burnt offering while I go to meet the Lord.“" 
Numbers23:16 And the Lord met Balaam and told him what to say. 
Numbers23:17 So he returned to where the king and the princes of Moab were standing beside their burnt offerings. “What has Jehovah said?“ the king eagerly inquired. 
Numbers23:18 And he replied, “Rise up, Balak, and hear: Listen to me, you son of Zippor." 
Numbers23:19 God is not a man, that he should lie; He doesn't change his mind like humans do. Has he ever promised, Without doing what he said?" 
Numbers23:20 Look! I have received a command to bless them, For God has blessed them, And I cannot reverse it!" 
Numbers23:21 He has not seen sin in Jacob. He will not trouble Israel! Jehovah their God is with them. He is their king! 
Numbers23:22 God has brought them out of Egypt. Israel has the strength of a wild ox. 
Numbers23:23 No curse can be placed on Jacob, And no magic shall be done against him. For now it shall be said of Israel, 'What wonders God has done for them!'" 
Numbers23:24 These people rise up as a lion; They shall not lie down Until they have eaten what they capture And have drunk the blood of the slain!“ 
Numbers23:25 “If you aren't going to curse them, at least don't bless them!“ the king exclaimed to Balaam." 
Numbers23:26 But Balaam replied, “Didn't I tell you that I must say whatever Jehovah tells me to?“" 
Numbers23:27 Then the king said to Balaam, “I will take you to yet another place. Perhaps it will please God to let you curse them from there.“" 
Numbers23:28 So King Balak took Balaam to the top of Mount Peor, overlooking the desert." 
Numbers23:29 Balaam again told the king to build seven altars, and to prepare seven young bulls and seven rams for the sacrifice." 
Numbers23:30 The king did as Balaam said, and offered a young bull and ram on every altar." 
Numbers24:1 Balaam realized by now that Jehovah planned to bless Israel, so he didn't even go to meet the Lord as he had earlier. Instead, he went at once and looked out toward the camp of Israel" 
Numbers24:2 which stretched away across the plains, divided by tribal areas. Then the Spirit of God came upon him," 
Numbers24:3 and he spoke this prophecy concerning them: “Balaam the son of Beor says—The man whose eyes are open says— 
Numbers24:4 'I have listened to the word of God, I have seen what God Almighty showed me; I fell, and my eyes were opened:" 
Numbers24:5 Oh, the joys awaiting Israel, Joys in the homes of Jacob." 
Numbers24:6 I see them spread before me as green valleys, And fruitful gardens by the riverside; As aloes planted by the Lord himself; As cedar trees beside the waters." 
Numbers24:7 They shall be blessed with an abundance of water, And they shall live in many places. Their king will be greater than Agag; Their kingdom is exalted." 
Numbers24:8 God has brought them from Egypt. Israel has the strength of a wild ox, And shall eat up the nations that oppose him; He shall break their bones in pieces, And shall shoot them with many arrows." 
Numbers24:9 Israel sleeps as a lion or a lioness—Who dares arouse him? Blessed is everyone who blesses you, O Israel, And curses shall fall upon everyone who curses you.' “" 
Numbers24:10 King Balak was livid with rage by now. Striking his hands together in anger and disgust he shouted, “I called you to curse my enemies and instead you have blessed them three times." 
Numbers24:11 Get out of here! Go back home! I had planned to promote you to great honor, but Jehovah has kept you from it!“" 
Numbers24:12 Balaam replied, “Didn't I tell your messengers" 
Numbers24:13 that even if you gave me a palace filled with silver and gold, I could not go beyond the words of Jehovah, and could not say a word of my own? I said that I would say only what Jehovah says!" 
Numbers24:14 Yes, I shall return now to my own people. But first, let me tell you what the Israelites are going to do to your people!“" 
Numbers24:15 So he spoke this prophecy to him: “Balaam the son of Beor is the man Whose eyes are open! 
Numbers24:16 He hears the words of God And has knowledge from the Most High; He sees what Almighty God has shown him; He fell, and his eyes were opened:" 
Numbers24:17 I see in the future of Israel, Far down the distant trail, That there shall come a star from Jacob! This ruler of Israel Shall smite the people of Moab, And destroy the sons of Sheth." 
Numbers24:18 Israel shall possess all Edom and Seir. They shall overcome their enemies. 
Numbers24:19 Jacob shall arise in power And shall destroy many cities.“ 
Numbers24:20 Then Balaam looked over at the homes of the people of Amalek and prophesied: “Amalek was the first of the nations, But its destiny is destruction!“" 
Numbers24:21 Then he looked over at the Kenites: “Yes, you are strongly situated, Your nest is set in the rocks!" 
Numbers24:22 But the Kenites shall be destroyed, And the mighty army of the king of Assyria shall deport you from this land!“" 
Numbers24:23 He concluded his prophecies by saying: “Alas, who can live when God does this?" 
Numbers24:24 Ships shall come from the coasts of Cyprus, And shall oppress both Eber and Assyria. They too must be destroyed.“" 
Numbers24:25 So Balaam and Balak returned to their homes. 
Numbers25:1 While Israel was camped at Acacia, some of the young men began going to wild parties with the local Moabite girls." 
Numbers25:2 These girls also invited them to attend the sacrifices to their gods, and soon the men were not only attending the feasts, but also bowing down and worshiping the idols." 
Numbers25:3 Before long all Israel was joining freely in the worship of Baal, the god of Moab; and the anger of the Lord was hot against his people." 
Numbers25:4 He issued the following command to Moses: “Execute all the tribal leaders of Israel. Hang them up before the Lord in broad daylight, so that his fierce anger will turn away from the people.“" 
Numbers25:5 So Moses ordered the judges to execute all who had worshiped Baal. 
Numbers25:6 But one of the Israeli men insolently brought a Midianite girl into the camp, right before the eyes of Moses and all the people, as they were weeping at the door of the Tabernacle." 
Numbers25:7 When Phinehas (son of Eleazar and grandson of Aaron the priest) saw this, he jumped up, grabbed a spear," 
Numbers25:8 and rushed after the man into his tent, where he had taken the girl. He thrust the spear all the way through the man's body and into her stomach. So the plague was stopped," 
Numbers25:9 but only after 24,000 people had already died." 
Numbers25:10 Then the Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers25:11 “Phinehas (son of Eleazar and grandson of Aaron the priest) has turned away my anger for he was angry with my anger, and would not tolerate the worship of any God but me. So I have stopped destroying all Israel as I had intended." 
Numbers25:12 Now because of what he has done—because of his zeal for his God, and because he has made atonement" 
Numbers25:13 for the people of Israel by what he did—I promise that he and his descendants shall be priests forever.“ 
Numbers25:14 The name of the man who was killed with the Midianite girl was Zimri, son of Salu, a leader of the tribe of Simeon." 
Numbers25:15 The girl's name was Cozbi, daughter of Zur, a Midianite prince." 
Numbers25:16 Then the Lord said 
Numbers25:17 to Moses, “Destroy the Midianites," 
Numbers25:18 for they are destroying you with their wiles. They are causing you to worship Baal, and they are leading you astray, as you have just seen by the death of Cozbi.“" 
Numbers26:1 After the plague had ended, Jehovah said to Moses and to Eleazar (son of Aaron the priest)," 
Numbers26:2 “Take a census of all the men of Israel who are twenty years old or older, to find out how many of each tribe and clan are able to go to war.“" 
Numbers26:3 So Moses and Eleazar issued census instructions to the leaders of Israel. 
Numbers26:4 (The entire nation was camped in the plains of Moab beside the Jordan River, opposite Jericho.) Here are the results of the census:" 
Numbers26:5 The tribe of Reuben: 43,730. (Reuben was Israel's oldest son.) In this tribe were the following clans, named after Reuben's sons:" 
Numbers26:6 The Hanochites, named after their ancestor Hanoch. The Palluites, named after their ancestor Pallu." 
Numbers26:7 (In the subclan of Eliab—who was one of the sons of Pallu— 
Numbers26:8 were the families of Nemuel, Abiram, and Dathan. This Dathan and Abiram were the two leaders who conspired with Korah against Moses and Aaron, and in fact challenged the very authority of God!" 
Numbers26:9 But the earth opened and swallowed them; and 250 men were destroyed by 
Numbers26:10 fire from the Lord that day, as a warning to the entire nation.)" 
Numbers26:11 The Hezronites, named after their ancestor Hezron. The Carmites, named after their ancestor Carmi." 
Numbers26:12 The tribe of Simeon: 22,200." 
Numbers26:13 In this tribe were the following clans, founded by Simeon's sons: The Nemuelites, named after their ancestor Nemuel. The Jaminites, named after their ancestor Jamin. The Jachinites, named after their ancestor Jachin." 
Numbers26:14 The Zerahites, named after their ancestor Zerah. The Shaulites, named after their ancestor Shaul." 
Numbers26:15 The tribe of Gad: 40,500" 
Numbers26:16 In this tribe were the following clans founded by the sons of Gad: The Zephonites, named after their ancestor Zephon. The Haggites, named after their ancestor Haggi. The Shunites, named after their ancestor Shuni." 
Numbers26:17 The Oznites, named after their ancestor Ozni. The Erites, named after their ancestor Eri." 
Numbers26:18 The Arodites, named after their ancestor Arod. The Arelites, named after their ancestor Areli." 
Numbers26:19 The tribe of Judah: 76,500" 
Numbers26:20 In this tribe were the following clans named after 
Numbers26:21 the sons of Judah—but not including Er and Onan who died in the land of Canaan: The Shelanites, named after their ancestor Shelah. The Perezites, named after their ancestor Perez. The Zerahites, named after their ancestor Zerah." 
Numbers26:22 This census also included the subclans of Perez: The Hezronites, named after their ancestor Hezron. The Hamulites, named after their ancestor Hamul." 
Numbers26:23 The tribe of Issachar: 64,300." 
Numbers26:24 In this tribe were the following clans named after the sons of Issachar: The Tolaites, named after their ancestor Tola. The Punites, named after their ancestor Puvah." 
Numbers26:25 The Jashubites, named after their ancestor Jashub. The Shimronites, named after their ancestor Shimron." 
Numbers26:26 The tribe of Zebulun: 60,500." 
Numbers26:27 In this tribe were the following clans named after the sons of Zebulun: The Seredites, named after their ancestor Sered. The Elonites, named after their ancestor Elon. The Jahleelites, named after their ancestor Jahleel." 
Numbers26:28 The tribe of Joseph: 32,500 in the half-tribe of Ephraim; and 52,700 in the half-tribe of Manasseh." 
Numbers26:29 In the half-tribe of Manasseh was the clan of Machirites, named after their ancestor Machir. The subclan of the Machirites was the Gileadites, named after their ancestor Gilead." 
Numbers26:30 The tribes of the Gileadites: The Jezerites, named after their ancestor Jezer. The Helekites, named after their ancestor Helek." 
Numbers26:31 The Asrielites, named after their ancestor Asriel. The Shechemites, named after their ancestor Shechem." 
Numbers26:32 The Shemidaites, named after their ancestor Shemida. The Hepherites, named after their ancestor Hepher. (Hepher's son," 
Numbers26:33 Zelophehad, had no sons. Here are the names of his daughters: Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, Tirzah." 
Numbers26:34 The 32,500 registered in the half-tribe of Ephraim included the following clans, named after the sons of Ephraim:" 
Numbers26:35 The Shuthelahites, named after their ancestor Shuthelah." 
Numbers26:36 (A subclan of the Shuthelahites was the Eranites, named after their ancestor Eran, a son of Shuthelah.)" 
Numbers26:37 The Becherites, named after their ancestor Becher. The Tahanites, named after their ancestor Tahan." 
Numbers26:38 The tribe of Benjamin: 45,600." 
Numbers26:39 In this tribe were the following clans named after the sons of Benjamin: The Belaites, named after their ancestor Bela. Subclans named after sons of Bela were: The Ardites, named after their ancestor Ard. The Naamites, named after their ancestor Naaman." 
Numbers26:40 The Ashbelites, named after their ancestor Ashbel. The Ahiramites, named after their ancestor Ahiram." 
Numbers26:41 The Shuphamites, named after their ancestor Shephupham. The Huphamites, named after their ancestor Hupham." 
Numbers26:42 The tribe of Dan: 64,400." 
Numbers26:43 In this tribe was the clan of the Shuhamites, named after Shuham, the son of Dan." 
Numbers26:44 The tribe of Asher: 53,400." 
Numbers26:45 In this tribe were the following clans named after the sons of Asher: The Imnites, named after their ancestor Imnah. The Ishvites, named after their ancestor Ishvi." 
Numbers26:46 The Beriites, named after their ancestor Beriah. Subclans named after the sons of Beriah were: The Heberites, named after their ancestor Heber. The Malchielites, named after their ancestor Malchiel." 
Numbers26:47 Asher also had a daughter named Serah. 
Numbers26:48 The tribe of Naphtali: 45,400." 
Numbers26:49 In this tribe were the following clans, named after the sons of Naphtali: The Jahzeelites, named after their ancestor Jahzeel. The Gunites, named after their ancestor Guni." 
Numbers26:50 The Jezerites, named after their ancestor Jezer. The Shillemites, named after their ancestor Shillem." 
Numbers26:51 So the total number of the men of draft age throughout Israel was 601,730." 
Numbers26:52 Then the Lord told Moses to divide the land among the 
Numbers26:53 tribes in proportion to their population, as indicated by the census—" 
Numbers26:54 the larger tribes to be given more land, the smaller tribes less land." 
Numbers26:55 “Let the representatives of the larger tribes have a lottery, drawing for the larger" 
Numbers26:56 sections,“ the Lord instructed, “and let the smaller tribes draw for the smaller sections.“" 
Numbers26:57 These are the clans of the Levites numbered in the census: The Gershonites, named after their ancestor Gershon. The Kohathites, named after their ancestor Kohath. The Merarites, named after their ancestor Merari." 
Numbers26:58 These are the families of the tribe of Levi: The Libnites, the Hebronites, The Mahlites, the Mushites, The Korahites." 
Numbers26:59 While Levi was in Egypt, a daughter, Jochebed, was born to him and she became the wife of Amram, son of Kohath. They were the parents of Aaron, Moses, and Miriam." 
Numbers26:60 To Aaron were born Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar." 
Numbers26:61 But Nadab and Abihu died when they offered unauthorized incense before the Lord. 
Numbers26:62 The total number of Levites in the census was 23,000, counting all the males a month old and upward. But the Levites were not included in the total census figure of the people of Israel, for the Levites were given no land when it was divided among the tribes." 
Numbers26:63 So these are the census figures as prepared by Moses and Eleazar the priest, in the plains of Moab beside the Jordan River, across from Jericho." 
Numbers26:64 Not one person in this entire census had been counted in the previous census taken in the wilderness of Sinai! 
Numbers26:65 For all who had been counted then had died, as the Lord had decreed when he said of them, “They shall die in the wilderness.“ The only exceptions were Caleb (son of Jephunneh) and Joshua (son of Nun)." 
Numbers26:65 For all who had been counted then had died, as the Lord had decreed when he said of them, “They shall die in the wilderness.“ The only exceptions were Caleb (son of Jephunneh) and Joshua (son of Nun)." 
Numbers27:1 One day the daughters of Zelophehad came to the entrance of the Tabernacle to give a petition to Moses, Eleazar the priest, the tribal leaders, and others who were there." 
Numbers27:2 The names of these women were Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah. They were members of the half-tribe of Manasseh (a son of Joseph). Their ancestor was Machir, son of Manasseh. Manasseh's son Gilead was their great-grandfather, his son Hepher was their grandfather, and his son Zelophehad was their father." 
Numbers27:3 “Our father died in the wilderness,“ they said, “and he was not one of those who perished in Korah's revolt against the Lord—it was a natural death, but he had no sons." 
Numbers27:4 Why should the name of our father disappear just because he had no son? We feel that we should be given property along with our father's brothers.“ 
Numbers27:5 So Moses brought their case before the Lord. 
Numbers27:6 And the Lord replied to Moses," 
Numbers27:7 “The daughters of Zelophehad are correct. Give them land along with their uncles; give them the property that would have been given to their father if he had lived. 
Numbers27:8 Moreover, this is a general law among you, that if a man dies and has no sons, then his inheritance shall be passed on to his daughters." 
Numbers27:9 And if he has no daughter, it shall belong to his brothers." 
Numbers27:10 And if he has no brother, then it shall go to his uncles." 
Numbers27:11 But if he has no uncles, then it shall go to the nearest relative.“" 
Numbers27:12 One day the Lord said to Moses, “Go up into Mount Abarim and look across the river to the land I have given to the people of Israel." 
Numbers27:13 After you have seen it, you shall die as Aaron your brother did," 
Numbers27:14 for you rebelled against my instructions in the wilderness of Zin. When the people of Israel rebelled, you did not glorify me before them by following my instructions to order water to come out of the rock.“ He was referring to the incident at the waters of Meribah (“Place of Strife“) in Kadesh, in the wilderness of Zin." 
Numbers27:15 Then Moses said to the Lord," 
Numbers27:16 “O Jehovah, the God of the spirits of all mankind, before I am taken away please appoint a new leader for the people," 
Numbers27:17 a man who will lead them into battle and care for them, so that the people of the Lord will not be as sheep without a shepherd.“" 
Numbers27:18 The Lord replied, “Go and get Joshua (son of Nun), who has the Spirit in him," 
Numbers27:18 The Lord replied, “Go and get Joshua (son of Nun), who has the Spirit in him," 
Numbers27:19 and take him to Eleazar the priest, and as all the people watch, charge him with the responsibility of leading the people." 
Numbers27:20 Publicly give him your authority so that all the people of Israel will obey him. 
Numbers27:21 He shall be the one to consult with Eleazar the priest in order to get directions from the Lord. The Lord will speak to Eleazar through the use of the Urim, and Eleazar will pass on these instructions to Joshua and the people. In this way the Lord will continue to give them guidance.“" 
Numbers27:21 He shall be the one to consult with Eleazar the priest in order to get directions from the Lord. The Lord will speak to Eleazar through the use of the Urim, and Eleazar will pass on these instructions to Joshua and the people. In this way the Lord will continue to give them guidance.“" 
Numbers27:22 So Moses did as Jehovah commanded and took Joshua to Eleazar the priest. As the people watched," 
Numbers27:22 So Moses did as Jehovah commanded and took Joshua to Eleazar the priest. As the people watched," 
Numbers27:23 Moses laid his hands upon him and dedicated him to his responsibilities, as the Lord had commanded." 
Numbers28:1 The Lord gave Moses these instructions to give to the people of Israel: 
Numbers28:2 “The offerings which you burn on the altar for me are my food, and are a pleasure to me; so see to it that they are brought regularly and are offered as I have instructed you." 
Numbers28:3 “When you make offerings by fire, you shall use yearling male lambs—each without defect. Two of them shall be offered each day as a regular burnt offering." 
Numbers28:4 One lamb shall be sacrificed in the morning, the other in the evening." 
Numbers28:5 With them shall be offered a grain offering of three quarts of finely ground flour mixed with three pints of oil. 
Numbers28:6 This is the burnt offering ordained at Mount Sinai, to be regularly offered as a fragrant odor, an offering made by fire to the Lord." 
Numbers28:7 Along with it shall be the drink offering, consisting of three pints of strong wine with each lamb, poured out in the holy place before the Lord." 
Numbers28:8 Offer the second lamb in the evening with the same grain offering and drink offering. It too is a fragrant odor to the Lord, an offering made by fire." 
Numbers28:9 “On the Sabbath day, sacrifice two yearling male lambs—both without defect—in addition to the regular offerings." 
Numbers28:10 They are to be accompanied by a grain offering of six quarts of fine flour mixed with oil, and the usual drink offering." 
Numbers28:11 “Also, on the first day of each month there shall be an extra burnt offering to the Lord of two young bulls, one ram, and seven male yearling lambs—all without defect." 
Numbers28:12 Accompany them with nine quarts of finely ground flour mixed with oil as a grain offering with each bull; and six quarts of finely ground flour mixed with oil as a grain offering for the ram; 
Numbers28:13 and for each lamb, three quarts of finely ground flour mixed with oil for a grain offering. This burnt offering shall be presented by fire and will please the Lord very much." 
Numbers28:14 Along with each sacrifice shall be a drink offering—six pints of wine with each bull, four pints for a ram, and three pints for a lamb. This, then, will be the burnt offering each month throughout the year." 
Numbers28:15 “Also on the first day of each month you shall offer one male goat for a sin offering to the Lord. This is in addition to the regular daily burnt offering and its drink offering. 
Numbers28:16 “On April first you shall celebrate the Passover—when the death angel passed over the oldest sons of the Israelites in Egypt, leaving them unharmed." 
Numbers28:17 On the following day a great, joyous seven-day festival will begin, but no leavened bread shall be served." 
Numbers28:18 On the first day of the festival all the people shall be called together before the Lord. No hard work shall be done on that day. 
Numbers28:19 You shall offer as burnt sacrifices to the Lord two young bulls, one ram, and seven yearling male lambs—all without defect." 
Numbers28:20 With each bull there shall be a grain offering of nine quarts of fine flour mixed with oil; with the ram there shall be six quarts; 
Numbers28:21 and with each of the seven lambs there shall be three quarts of fine flour. 
Numbers28:22 You must also offer a male goat as a sin offering, to make atonement for yourselves." 
Numbers28:23 These offerings shall be in addition to the usual daily sacrifices. 
Numbers28:24 This same sacrifice shall be offered on each of the seven days of the feast; they will be very pleasant to the Lord. 
Numbers28:25 On the seventh day there shall again be a holy and solemn assembly of all the people, and during that day you may do no hard work." 
Numbers28:26 “On the first day of the Harvest Festival all the people must come before the Lord for a special, solemn assembly to celebrate the new harvest. On that day you are to present the first of the new crop of grain as a grain offering to the Lord; there is to" 
Numbers28:27 A special burnt offering, very pleasant to the Lord, shall be offered that day. It shall consist of two young bulls, one ram, and seven yearling male lambs." 
Numbers28:28 These shall be accompanied by your grain offering of nine quarts of fine flour mixed with oil 
Numbers28:29 with each bull, six quarts with the ram, and three quarts with each of the seven lambs." 
Numbers28:30 Also offer one male goat to make atonement for yourselves. 
Numbers28:31 These special offerings are in addition to the regular daily burnt offerings and grain offerings and drink offerings. Make sure that the animals you sacrifice are without defect. 
Numbers29:1 “The Festival of Trumpets shall be celebrated on the fifteenth day of September each year; there shall be a solemn assembly of all the people on that day, and no hard work may be done." 
Numbers29:2 On that day you shall offer a burnt sacrifice consisting of one young bull, one ram, and seven yearling male lambs—all without defect. These are sacrifices which the Lord will appreciate and enjoy." 
Numbers29:3 A grain offering of nine quarts of fine flour mingled with oil shall be offered with 
Numbers29:4 the bull, six quarts with the ram, and three quarts with each of the seven lambs." 
Numbers29:5 In addition, there shall be a male goat sacrificed as a sin offering, to make atonement for you." 
Numbers29:6 These special sacrifices are in addition to the regular monthly burnt offering for that day, and also in addition to the regular daily burnt sacrifices, which are to be offered with the respective grain offerings and drink offerings, as specified by the ordinances governing them." 
Numbers29:7 “Ten days later another convocation of all the people shall be held. This will be a day of solemn humility before the Lord, and no work of any kind may be done." 
Numbers29:8 On that day you shall offer a burnt sacrifice to the Lord—it will be very pleasant to him—of one young bull, one ram, seven yearling male lambs—each without defect—" 
Numbers29:9 and their accompanying grain offerings. 
Numbers29:10 Nine quarts of fine flour mixed with oil are to be offered with the bull, six with the ram, and three with each of the seven lambs." 
Numbers29:11 “You are also to sacrifice one male goat for a sin offering. This is in addition to the sin offering of the Day of Atonement offered annually on that day, and in addition to the regular daily burnt sacrifices, grain offerings, and drink offerings." 
Numbers29:12 “Five days later there shall be yet another assembly of all the people, and on that day no hard work shall be done; it is the beginning of a seven-day festival before the Lord." 
Numbers29:13 Your special burnt sacrifice that day, which will give much pleasure to the Lord, shall be thirteen young bulls, two rams, and fourteen male yearling lambs—each without defect—" 
Numbers29:14 accompanied by the usual grain offerings—nine quarts of fine flour mingled with oil for each of the thirteen young bulls; six quarts for each of the two rams; 
Numbers29:15 and three quarts for each of the fourteen lambs. 
Numbers29:16 There must also be a male goat sacrificed for a sin offering, in addition to the regular daily burnt sacrifice with its accompanying grain offerings and drink offerings." 
Numbers29:17 “On the second day of this seven-day festival you shall sacrifice twelve young bulls, two rams, and fourteen male yearling lambs—each without defect—" 
Numbers29:18 accompanied by the usual grain offerings and drink offerings. 
Numbers29:19 Also, in addition to the regular daily burnt sacrifice, you are to sacrifice a male goat with its accompanying grain offering and drink offering for a sin offering." 
Numbers29:20 “On the third day of the festival, offer eleven young bulls, two rams, fourteen male yearling lambs—each without defect—" 
Numbers29:21 and the usual grain offering and drink offering with each sacrifice. 
Numbers29:22 And in addition to the regular daily burnt sacrifices, sacrifice a male goat for a sin offering, with its accompanying grain offering and drink offering." 
Numbers29:23 “On the fourth day of the festival, you are to sacrifice ten young bulls, two rams, and fourteen male yearling lambs—each without defect—" 
Numbers29:24 each with its accompanying grain offering and drink offering; 
Numbers29:25 also a male goat as a sin offering (along with the usual grain and drink offerings) in addition to the regular daily sacrifices. 
Numbers29:26 “On the fifth day of the festival, sacrifice nine young bulls, two rams, and fourteen male yearling lambs—each without defect—" 
Numbers29:27 accompanied by the usual grain offerings and drink offerings; 
Numbers29:28 also sacrifice a male goat with the usual grain and drink offerings, as a special sin offering, in addition to the usual daily sacrifices." 
Numbers29:29 “On the sixth day of the festival, you must sacrifice eight young bulls, two rams, and fourteen male yearling lambs—each without defect—" 
Numbers29:30 along with their usual grain and drink offerings. 
Numbers29:31 In addition to the usual daily sacrifices, sacrifice a male goat and the usual grain and drink offerings as a sin offering." 
Numbers29:32 “On the seventh day of the festival, sacrifice seven young bulls, two rams, and fourteen male yearling lambs—each without defect—" 
Numbers29:33 each with its customary grain and drink offerings; 
Numbers29:34 also sacrifice an extra sin offering of one male goat, with the usual grain and drink offerings, in addition to the regular daily sacrifices." 
Numbers29:35 “On the eighth day summon the people to another solemn assembly; you must do no hard work that day. 
Numbers29:36 Sacrifice a burnt offering—they are very pleasant to the Lord—of one young bull, one ram, seven male yearling lambs—each without defect—" 
Numbers29:37 and the customary grain and drink offerings. 
Numbers29:38 Sacrifice also one male goat with the usual grain and drink offerings for a sin offering, in addition to the regular daily sacrifices." 
Numbers29:39 These offerings are compulsory at the times of your annual feasts, and are in addition to sacrifices and offerings you present in connection with vows, or as free-will offerings, burnt sacrifices, grain offerings, drink offerings, or peace offerings.“" 
Numbers29:40 So Moses gave all of these instructions to the people of Israel. 
Numbers30:1 Now Moses summoned the leaders of the tribes and told them, “The Lord has commanded" 
Numbers30:2 that when anyone makes a promise to the Lord, either to do something or to quit doing something, that vow must not be broken: the person making the vow must do exactly as he has promised." 
Numbers30:3 “If a woman promises the Lord to do or not do something, and she is still a girl at home in her father's home," 
Numbers30:4 and her father hears that she has made a vow with penalties, but says nothing, then her vow shall stand." 
Numbers30:5 But if her father refuses to let her make the vow, or feels that the penalties she has agreed to are too harsh, then her promise will automatically become invalid. Her father must state his disagreement on the first day he hears about it; and then Jehovah will forgive her because her father would not let her do it." 
Numbers30:6 “If she takes a vow or makes a foolish pledge, and later marries," 
Numbers30:7 and her husband learns of her vow and says nothing on the day he hears of it, her vow shall stand." 
Numbers30:8 But if her husband refuses to accept her vow or foolish pledge, his disagreement makes it void, and Jehovah will forgive her." 
Numbers30:9 “But if the woman is a widow or is divorced, she must fulfill her vow." 
Numbers30:10 “If she is married and living in her husband's home when she makes the vow," 
Numbers30:11 and her husband hears of it and does nothing, the vow shall stand;" 
Numbers30:12 but if he refuses to allow it on the first day he hears of it, her vow is void and Jehovah will forgive her." 
Numbers30:13 So her husband may either confirm or nullify her vow," 
Numbers30:14 but if he says nothing for a day, then he has already agreed to it." 
Numbers30:15 If he waits more than a day and then refuses to permit the vow, whatever penalties to which she agreed shall come upon him—he shall be responsible.“" 
Numbers30:16 These, then, are the commandments the Lord gave Moses concerning relationships between a man and his wife and between a father and his daughter who is living at home." 
Numbers31:1 Then the Lord said to Moses, “Take vengeance on the" 
Numbers31:2 Midianites for leading you into idolatry, and then you must die.“" 
Numbers31:3 Moses said to the people, “Some of you must take arms to wage Jehovah's war against Midian." 
Numbers31:4 Conscript 1,000 men from each tribe.“ So this was done; and out of" 
Numbers31:5 the many thousands of Israel, 12,000 armed men were sent to battle by Moses." 
Numbers31:6 Phinehas (son of Eleazar the priest) led them into battle, accompanied by the Ark, with trumpets blaring." 
Numbers31:7 And every man of Midian was killed. 
Numbers31:8 Among those killed were all five of the Midianite kings—Evi, Rekem, Zur, Hur, and Reba. Balaam, the son of Beor, was also killed." 
Numbers31:9 Then the Israeli army took as captives all the women and children," 
Numbers31:10 and seized the cattle and flocks and a lot of miscellaneous booty. 
Numbers31:11 All of the cities, towns, and villages of Midian were then burned." 
Numbers31:12 The captives and other war loot were brought to Moses and Eleazar the priest, and to the rest of the people of Israel who were camped on the plains of Moab beside the Jordan River, across from Jericho." 
Numbers31:13 Moses and Eleazar the priest and all the leaders of the people went out to meet the victorious army," 
Numbers31:14 but Moses was very angry with the army officers and battalion leaders. 
Numbers31:15 “Why have you let all the women live?“ he demanded. 
Numbers31:16 “These are the very ones who followed Balaam's advice and caused the people of Israel to worship idols on Mount Peor, and they are the cause of the plague that destroyed us." 
Numbers31:17 Now kill all the boys and all the women who have had sexual intercourse. 
Numbers31:18 Only the little girls may live; you may keep them for yourselves. 
Numbers31:19 Now stay outside of the camp for seven days, all of you who have killed anyone or touched a dead body. Then purify yourselves and your captives on the third and seventh days." 
Numbers31:20 Remember also to purify all your garments and everything made of leather, goat's hair, or wood.“" 
Numbers31:21 Then Eleazar the priest said to the men who were in the battle, “This is the commandment Jehovah has given Moses:" 
Numbers31:22 'Anything that will stand heat—such as gold, silver, bronze, iron, tin, or lead—" 
Numbers31:23 shall be passed through fire in order to be made ceremonially pure; it must then be further purified with the purification water. But anything that won't stand heat shall be purified by the water alone.' 
Numbers31:24 On the seventh day you must wash your clothes and be purified, and then you may come back into the camp.“" 
Numbers31:25 And the Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers31:26 “You and Eleazar the priest and the leaders of the tribes are to make a list of all the loot, including the people and animals;" 
Numbers31:27 then divide it into two parts. Half of it is for the men who were in the battle, and the other half is to be given to the people of Israel." 
Numbers31:28 But first, the Lord gets a share of all the captives, oxen, donkeys, and flocks kept by the army. His share is one out of every five hundred." 
Numbers31:29 Give this share to Eleazar the priest to be presented to the Lord by the gesture of waving before the altar. 
Numbers31:30 Also levy a 2 percent tribute of all the captives, flocks, and cattle that are given to the people of Israel. Present this to the Levites in charge of the Tabernacle, for it is the Lord's portion.“" 
Numbers31:31 So Moses and Eleazar the priest did as the Lord commanded. 
Numbers31:32 The total booty (besides the jewelry, clothing, etc., which the soldiers kept for themselves) was 675,000 sheep;" 
Numbers31:33 72,000 oxen;" 
Numbers31:34 61,000 donkeys; and" 
Numbers31:35 32,000 young girls." 
Numbers31:36 So the half given to the army totaled: 337,500 sheep" 
Numbers31:37 (of which 675 were given to the Lord); 
Numbers31:38 36,000 oxen (of which 72 were given to the Lord);" 
Numbers31:39 30,500 donkeys (of which 61 were given to the Lord);" 
Numbers31:40 16,000 girls (of whom 32 went to the Levites)." 
Numbers31:41 All of the Lord's portion was given to Eleazar the priest, as the Lord had directed Moses." 
Numbers31:42 The half of the booty assigned to the people of Israel—Moses had separated it from 
Numbers31:43 the half belonging to the warriors—amounted to: 337,500 sheep," 
Numbers31:44 36,000 oxen," 
Numbers31:45 30,500 donkeys," 
Numbers31:46 and 16,000 girls." 
Numbers31:47 In accordance with the Lord's directions, Moses gave 2 percent of these to the Levites." 
Numbers31:48 Then the officers and battalion leaders came to Moses and said, “We have accounted for" 
Numbers31:49 all the men who went out to battle, and not one of us is missing!" 
Numbers31:50 So we have brought a special thank-offering to the Lord from our loot—gold jewelry, bracelets, anklets, rings, earrings, and necklaces. This is to make atonement for our souls before the Lord.“" 
Numbers31:51 Moses and Eleazar the priest received this special offering from the captains and battalion 
Numbers31:52 leaders and company commanders, and found its total value to be more than “300,000." 
Numbers31:53 (The soldiers had also kept personal loot for themselves.) 
Numbers31:54 The offering was taken into the Tabernacle and kept there before the Lord as a memorial of the people of Israel. 
Numbers32:1 When Israel arrived in the land of Jazar and Gilead, the tribes of Reuben and Gad (who had large flocks of sheep) noticed what wonderful sheep country it was." 
Numbers32:2 So they came to Moses and Eleazar the priest and the other tribal leaders and said," 
Numbers32:3 “The Lord has used Israel to destroy the population of this whole countryside—Ataroth, Dibon, Jazer, Nimrah, Heshbon, Elealeh, Sebam, Nebo, and Beon." 
Numbers32:4 And it is all wonderful sheep country, ideal for our flocks." 
Numbers32:5 Please let us have this land as our portion instead of the land on the other side of the Jordan River.“ 
Numbers32:6 “You mean you want to sit here while your brothers go across and do all the fighting?“ Moses demanded. 
Numbers32:7 “Are you trying to discourage the rest of the people from going across to the land that the Lord has given them? 
Numbers32:8 This is the same kind of thing your fathers did! I sent them from Kadesh-barnea to spy out the land," 
Numbers32:9 but when they finished their survey and returned from the valley of Eshcol, they discouraged the people from going on into the Promised Land." 
Numbers32:10 And the Lord's anger was hot against them, and he swore" 
Numbers32:11 that of all those he had rescued from Egypt, no one over twenty years of age would ever see the land he promised Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, for they had refused to do what he wanted them to." 
Numbers32:12 “The only exceptions were Caleb (son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite) and Joshua (son of Nun)—for they wholeheartedly followed the Lord and urged the people to go on into the Promised Land. 
Numbers32:12 “The only exceptions were Caleb (son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite) and Joshua (son of Nun)—for they wholeheartedly followed the Lord and urged the people to go on into the Promised Land. 
Numbers32:13 “The Lord made us wander back and forth in the wilderness for forty years until all that evil generation died. 
Numbers32:14 But here you are, a brood of sinners doing exactly the same thing! Only there are more of you, so Jehovah's anger against Israel will be even fiercer this time." 
Numbers32:15 If you turn away from God like this, he will make the people stay even longer in the wilderness, and you will be responsible for destroying his people and bringing disaster to this entire nation!“" 
Numbers32:16 “Not at all!“ they explained. “We will build sheepfolds for our flocks and cities for our little ones," 
Numbers32:17 but we ourselves will go over armed, ahead of the rest of the people of Israel, until we have brought them safely to their inheritance. But first we will need to build walled cities here for our families, to keep them safe from attack by the local inhabitants." 
Numbers32:18 We will not settle down here until all the people of Israel have received their inheritance. 
Numbers32:19 We don't want land on the other side of the Jordan; we would rather have it on this side, on the east.“" 
Numbers32:20 Then Moses said, “All right, if you will do what you have said and arm yourselves for Jehovah's war," 
Numbers32:21 and keep your troops across the Jordan until the Lord has driven out his enemies," 
Numbers32:22 then, when the land is finally subdued before the Lord, you may return. Then you will have discharged your duty to the Lord and to the rest of the people of Israel. And the land on the eastern side shall be your possession from the Lord." 
Numbers32:23 But if you don't do as you have said, then you will have sinned against the Lord, and you may be sure that your sin will catch up with you." 
Numbers32:24 Go ahead and build cities for your families and sheepfolds for your sheep, and do all you have said.“" 
Numbers32:25 “We will follow your instructions exactly,“ the people of Gad and Reuben replied." 
Numbers32:26 “Our children, wives, flocks, and cattle shall stay here in the cities of Gilead." 
Numbers32:27 But all of us who are conscripted will go over to battle for the Lord, just as you have said.“" 
Numbers32:28 So Moses gave his approval by saying to Eleazar, Joshua, and the tribal leaders of Israel," 
Numbers32:28 So Moses gave his approval by saying to Eleazar, Joshua, and the tribal leaders of Israel," 
Numbers32:29 “If all the men of the tribes of Gad and Reuben who are conscripted for the Lord's battles go with you over Jordan, then, when the land is conquered, you must give them the land of Gilead;" 
Numbers32:30 but if they refuse, then they must accept land among the rest of you in the land of Canaan.“" 
Numbers32:31 The tribes of Gad and Reuben said again, “As the Lord has commanded, so we will do—" 
Numbers32:32 we will follow the Lord fully armed into Canaan, but our own land shall be here on this side of the Jordan.“" 
Numbers32:33 So Moses assigned the territory of King Sihon of the Amorites, and of King Og of Bashan—all the land and cities—to the tribes of Gad, Reuben, and the half-tribe of Manasseh (son of Joseph)." 
Numbers32:34 The people of Gad built these cities: Dibon, Ataroth, Aroer," 
Numbers32:35 Atroth-shophan, Jazer, Jogbehah," 
Numbers32:36 Beth-nimrah, Beth-haran. They were all fortified cities with sheepfolds." 
Numbers32:37 The children of Reuben built the following cities: Heshbon, Elealeh, Kiriathaim," 
Numbers32:38 Nebo, Baal-meon, Sibmah. (The Israelites later changed the names of some of these cities they had conquered and rebuilt.)" 
Numbers32:39 Then the clan of Machir of the tribe of Manasseh went to Gilead and conquered it, and drove out the Amorites who were living there." 
Numbers32:40 So Moses gave Gilead to the Machirites, and they lived there." 
Numbers32:41 The men of Jair, another clan of the tribe of Manasseh, occupied many of the towns in Gilead, and changed the name of their area to Havroth-jair." 
Numbers32:42 Meanwhile, a man named Nobah led an army to Kenath and its surrounding villages, and occupied them, and he called the area Nobah, after his own name." 
Numbers33:1 This is the itinerary of the nation of Israel from the time Moses and Aaron led them out of Egypt. 
Numbers33:2 Moses had written down their movements as the Lord had instructed him. 
Numbers33:3 They left the city of Rameses, Egypt, on the first day of April, the day after the night of the Passover. They left proudly, hurried along by the Egyptians" 
Numbers33:4 who were burying all their eldest sons, killed by the Lord the night before. The Lord had certainly defeated all the gods of Egypt that night!" 
Numbers33:5 After leaving Rameses, they stayed in Succoth," 
Numbers33:6 Etham (at the edge of the wilderness), and" 
Numbers33:7 Pihahiroth (near Baal-zephon, where they camped at the foot of Mount Migdol)." 
Numbers33:8 From there they went through the middle of the Red Sea and on for three days into the Etham wilderness, camping at Marah." 
Numbers33:9 Leaving Marah, they came to Elim, where there are twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees; they stayed there for quite a long time." 
Numbers33:10 Leaving Elim, they camped beside the Red Sea," 
Numbers33:11 and then in the wilderness of Sihn. 
Numbers33:12 Next was Dophkah," 
Numbers33:13 and then Alush; 
Numbers33:14 then on to Rephidim (where there was no water for the people to drink). 
Numbers33:15 From Rephidim they went to the wilderness of Sinai; 
Numbers33:16 from the wilderness of Sinai to Kibroth-hattaavah; 
Numbers33:17 From Kibroth-hattaavah to Hazeroth; 
Numbers33:18 From Hazeroth to Rithmah; 
Numbers33:19 From Rithmah to Rimmon-parez; 
Numbers33:20 From Rimmon-parez to Libnah; 
Numbers33:21 From Libnah to Rissah; 
Numbers33:22 From Rissah to Kehelathah; 
Numbers33:23 From Kehelathah to Mount Shepher; 
Numbers33:24 From Mount Shepher to Haradah; 
Numbers33:25 From Haradah to Makheloth; 
Numbers33:26 From Makheloth to Tahath; 
Numbers33:27 From Tahath to Terah; 
Numbers33:28 From Terah to Mithkah; 
Numbers33:29 From Mithkah to Hashmonah; 
Numbers33:30 From Hashmonah to Moseroth; 
Numbers33:31 From Moseroth to Bene-jaakan; 
Numbers33:32 From Bene-jaakan to Hor-haggidgad; 
Numbers33:33 From Hor-haggidgad to Jotbathah; 
Numbers33:34 From Jotbathah to Abronah; 
Numbers33:35 From Abronah to Ezion-geber; 
Numbers33:36 From Ezion-geber to Kadesh (in the wilderness of Zin); 
Numbers33:37 From Kadesh to Mount Hor (at the edge of the land of Edom). 
Numbers33:38 While they were at the foot of Mount Hor, Aaron the priest was directed by the Lord to go up into the mountain, and there he died. This occurred during the fortieth year after the people of Israel had left Egypt." 
Numbers33:39 The date of his death was July 15, when he was 123 years old." 
Numbers33:40 It was then that the Canaanite king of Arad, who lived in the Negeb, in the land of Canaan, heard that the people of Israel were approaching his land." 
Numbers33:41 After dealing with him, the Israelis journeyed from Mount Hor and camped in Zalmonah," 
Numbers33:42 then at Punon," 
Numbers33:43 then at Oboth," 
Numbers33:44 then Iyeabarim (at the border of Moab). 
Numbers33:45 From there they went to Dibon-gad," 
Numbers33:46 and then to Almon-diblathaim," 
Numbers33:47 and on into the mountains of Abarim, near Mount Nebo," 
Numbers33:48 and finally to the plains of Moab beside the river Jordan, opposite Jericho." 
Numbers33:49 While in that area they camped at various places along the Jordan River, from Bethjeshimoth as far as Abel-shittim, on the plains of Moab." 
Numbers33:50 It was while they were camped there that the Lord told Moses to tell the 
Numbers33:51 people of Israel, “When you pass across the Jordan River into the land of Canaan," 
Numbers33:52 you must drive out all the people living there and destroy all their idols—their carved stones, molten images, and the open-air sanctuaries in the hills where they worship their idols." 
Numbers33:53 I have given the land to you; take it and live there. 
Numbers33:54 You will be given land in proportion to the size of your tribes. The larger sections of land will be divided by lot among the larger tribes, and the smaller sections will be allotted to the smaller tribes." 
Numbers33:55 But if you refuse to drive out the people living there, those who remain will be as cinders in your eyes and thorns in your sides." 
Numbers33:56 And I will destroy you as I had planned for you to destroy them.“ 
Numbers34:1 The Lord told Moses to tell the people of Israel, “When you come into" 
Numbers34:2 the land of Canaan (I am giving you the entire land as your homeland)," 
Numbers34:3 the southern portion of the country will be the wilderness of Zin, along the edge of Edom. The southern boundary will begin at the Dead Sea," 
Numbers34:4 and will continue south past Scorpion Pass in the direction of Zin. Its southernmost point will be Kadesh-barnea, from which it will go to Hazaraddar, and on to Azmon." 
Numbers34:5 From Azmon the boundary will follow the Brook of Egypt down to the Mediterranean Sea. 
Numbers34:6 “Your western boundary will be the coastline of the Mediterranean Sea. 
Numbers34:7 “Your northern border will begin at the Mediterranean Sea 
Numbers34:8 and will proceed eastward to Mount Hor, then to" 
Numbers34:9 Lebo-Hamath, and on through Zedad and Ziphron to Hazar-enan." 
Numbers34:10 “The eastern border will be from Hazar-enan south to Shepham," 
Numbers34:11 then on to Riblah at the east side of Ain. From there it will make a large half-circle, first going south and then westward until it touches the southernmost tip of the Sea of Galilee," 
Numbers34:12 and then along the Jordan River, ending at the Dead Sea.“" 
Numbers34:13 “This is the territory you are to apportion among yourselves by lot,“ Moses said. “It is to be divided up among the nine and one-half tribes," 
Numbers34:14 for the tribes of Reuben and Gad and the half-tribe of Manasseh have 
Numbers34:15 already been assigned land on the east side of the Jordan, opposite Jericho.“" 
Numbers34:16 And the Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers34:17 “These are the names of the men I have appointed to handle the dividing up of the land: Eleazar the priest, Joshua (son of Nun)," 
Numbers34:17 “These are the names of the men I have appointed to handle the dividing up of the land: Eleazar the priest, Joshua (son of Nun)," 
Numbers34:18 and one leader from each tribe, as listed below: Tribe, Leader;" 
Numbers34:19 Judah, Caleb (son of Jephunneh);" 
Numbers34:20 Simeon, Shemuel (son of Ammihud);" 
Numbers34:21 Benjamin, Elidad (son of Chislon);" 
Numbers34:22 Dan, Bukki (son of Jogli);" 
Numbers34:23 Manasseh, Hanniel (son of Ephod);" 
Numbers34:24 Ephraim, Kemuel (son of Shiphtan);" 
Numbers34:25 Zebulun, Elizaphan (son of Parnach);" 
Numbers34:26 Issachar, Paltiel (son of Azzan);" 
Numbers34:27 Asher, Ahihud (son of Shelomi);" 
Numbers34:28 Naphtali, Pedahel (son of Ammihud);" 
Numbers34:29 These are the names of the men I have appointed to oversee the dividing of the land among the tribes.“ 
Numbers35:1 While Israel was camped beside the Jordan on the plains of Moab, opposite Jericho, the Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers35:2 “Instruct the people of Israel to give to the Levites as their inheritance certain cities and surrounding pasture lands. 
Numbers35:3 These cities are for their homes, and the surrounding lands for their cattle, flocks, and other livestock." 
Numbers35:4 Their gardens and vineyards shall extend,500 feet out from the city" 
Numbers35:5 walls in each direction, with an additional,500 feet beyond that for pastureland." 
Numbers35:6 “You shall give the Levites the six Cities of Refuge, where a person who has accidentally killed someone can run and be safe, and forty-two other cities besides." 
Numbers35:7 In all, there shall be forty-eight cities with the surrounding pastureland given to the Levites." 
Numbers35:8 These cities shall be in various parts of the nation; the larger tribes with many cities will give several to the Levites, while the smaller tribes will give fewer.“" 
Numbers35:9 And the Lord said to Moses, “Tell the" 
Numbers35:10 people that when they arrive in the land," 
Numbers35:11 Cities of Refuge shall be designated for anyone to flee into if he has killed someone accidentally. 
Numbers35:12 These cities will be places of protection from the dead man's relatives who want to avenge his death; for the slayer must not be killed unless a fair trial establishes his guilt. 
Numbers35:13 Three of these six Cities of Refuge are to be located in the 
Numbers35:14 land of Canaan, and three on the east side of the Jordan River." 
Numbers35:15 These are not only for the protection of Israelites, but also for foreigners and travelers." 
Numbers35:16 “But if someone is struck and killed by a piece of iron, it must be presumed to be murder, and the murderer must be executed." 
Numbers35:17 Or if the slain man was struck down with a large stone, it is murder, and the murderer shall die." 
Numbers35:18 The same is true if he is killed with a wooden weapon. 
Numbers35:19 The avenger of his death shall personally kill the murderer when he meets him. 
Numbers35:20 So if anyone kills another out of hatred by throwing something at him, or ambushing him," 
Numbers35:21 or angrily striking him with his fist so that he dies, he is a murderer; and the murderer shall be executed by the avenger." 
Numbers35:22 “But if it is an accident—a case in which something is thrown unintentionally," 
Numbers35:23 or in which a stone is thrown without anger, without realizing it will hit anyone, and without wanting to harm an enemy—yet the man dies," 
Numbers35:24 then the people shall judge whether or not it was an accident, and whether or not to hand the killer over to the avenger of the dead man." 
Numbers35:25 If it is decided that it was accidental, then the people shall save the killer from the avenger; the killer shall be permitted to stay in the City of Refuge; and he must live there until the death of the High Priest." 
Numbers35:26 “If the slayer leaves the city," 
Numbers35:27 and the avenger finds him outside and kills him, it is not murder," 
Numbers35:28 for the man should have stayed inside the city until the death of the High Priest. But after the death of the High Priest, the man may return to his own land and home." 
Numbers35:29 These are permanent laws for all Israel from generation to generation. 
Numbers35:30 “All murderers must be executed, but only if there is more than one witness; no man shall die with only one person testifying against him." 
Numbers35:31 Whenever anyone is judged guilty of murder, he must die—no ransom may be accepted for him." 
Numbers35:32 Nor may a payment be accepted from a refugee in a City of Refuge, permitting him to return to his home before the death of the High Priest." 
Numbers35:33 In this way the land will not be polluted, for murder pollutes the land, and no atonement can be made for murder except by the execution of the murderer." 
Numbers35:34 You shall not defile the land where you are going to live, for I, Jehovah, will be living there.“" 
Numbers36:1 Then the heads of the subclan of Gilead (of the clan of Machir, of the tribe of Manasseh, one of the sons of Joseph) came to Moses and the leaders of Israel with a petition:" 
Numbers36:2 “The Lord instructed you to divide the land by lot among the people of Israel,“ they reminded Moses, “and to give the inheritance of our brother Zelophehad to his daughters." 
Numbers36:3 But if they marry into another tribe, their land will go with them to the tribe into which they marry. In this way the total area of our tribe will be reduced" 
Numbers36:4 and will not be returned at the Year of Jubilee.“ 
Numbers36:5 Then Moses replied publicly, giving them these instructions from the Lord: “The men of the tribe of Joseph have a proper complaint." 
Numbers36:6 This is what the Lord has further commanded concerning the daughters of Zelophehad: 'Let them be married to anyone they like, so long as it is within their own tribe." 
Numbers36:7 In this way none of the land of the tribe will shift to any other tribe, for the inheritance of every tribe is to remain permanently as it was first allotted." 
Numbers36:8 The girls throughout the tribes of Israel who are heiresses must marry within their own tribe, so that their land won't leave the tribe." 
Numbers36:9 In this way no inheritance shall move from one tribe to another.' “ 
Numbers36:10 The daughters of Zelophehad did as the Lord commanded Moses. 
Numbers36:11 These girls, Mahlah, Tirzah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Noah, were married to men in their" 
Numbers36:12 own tribe of Manasseh (son of Joseph); so their inheritance remained in their tribe. 
Numbers36:13 These are the commandments and ordinances that the Lord gave to the people of Israel through Moses, while they were camped on the plains of Moab beside the Jordan River, across from Jericho." 
Deuteronomy1:1 This book records Moses' address to the people of Israel when they were camped in the valley of the Arabah in the wilderness of Moab, east of the Jordan River. (Cities in the area included Suph, Paran, Tophel, Laban, Hazeroth, and Dizahab.)" 
Deuteronomy1:2 The speech was given on February 15, forty years after the people of Israel left Mount Horeb-—" 
Deuteronomy1:3 though it takes only eleven days to travel by foot from Mount Horeb to Kadesh-barnea, going by way of Mount Seir!" 
Deuteronomy1:4 At the time of this address, King Sihon of the Amorites had already been defeated at Heshbon, and King Og of Bashan had been defeated at Ashtaroth, near Edre-i." 
Deuteronomy1:5 Here, then, is Moses' address to Israel, stating all the laws God had commanded him to pass on to them:" 
Deuteronomy1:6 “It was forty years ago, at Mount Horeb, that Jehovah our God told us, 'You have stayed here long enough." 
Deuteronomy1:7 Now go and occupy the hill country of the Amorites, the valley of the Arabah, and the Negeb, and all the land of Canaan and Lebanon—the entire area from the shores of the Mediterranean Sea to the Euphrates River." 
Deuteronomy1:8 I am giving all of it to you! Go in and possess it, for it is the land the Lord promised to your ancestors Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, and all of their descendants.'" 
Deuteronomy1:9 “At that time I told the people, 'I need help! You are a great burden for me to carry all by myself," 
Deuteronomy1:10 for the Lord has multiplied you to become as many as the stars! 
Deuteronomy1:11 And may he multiply you a thousand times more and bless you as he promised," 
Deuteronomy1:12 but what can one man do to settle all your quarrels and problems? 
Deuteronomy1:13 So choose some men from each tribe who are wise, experienced, and understanding, and I will appoint them as your leaders.'" 
Deuteronomy1:14 “They agreed to this; 
Deuteronomy1:15 I took the men they selected, some from every tribe, and appointed them as administrative assistants in charge of thousands, hundreds, fifties, and tens to decide their quarrels and assist them in every way." 
Deuteronomy1:16 I instructed them to be perfectly fair at all times, even to foreigners." 
Deuteronomy1:17 'When giving your decisions,' I told them, 'never favor a man because he is rich; be fair to great and small alike. Don't fear their displeasure, for you are judging in the place of God. Bring me any cases too difficult for you, and I will handle them.'" 
Deuteronomy1:18 And I gave them other instructions at that time also. 
Deuteronomy1:19 “Then we left Mount Horeb and traveled through the great and terrible desert, finally arriving among the Amorite hills to which the Lord our God had directed us. We were then at Kadesh-barnea on the border of the Promised Land" 
Deuteronomy1:20 and I said to the people, 'The Lord God has given us this land." 
Deuteronomy1:21 Go and possess it as he told us to. Don't be afraid! Don't even doubt!' 
Deuteronomy1:22 “But they replied, 'First let's send out spies to discover the best route of entry, and to decide which cities we should capture first.'" 
Deuteronomy1:23 “This seemed like a good idea, so I chose twelve spies, one from each tribe." 
Deuteronomy1:24 They crossed into the hills and came to the Valley of Eshcol," 
Deuteronomy1:25 and returned with samples of the local fruit. One look was enough to convince us that it was indeed a good land the Lord our God had given us. 
Deuteronomy1:26 But the people refused to go in and rebelled against the Lord's command. 
Deuteronomy1:27 “They murmured and complained in their tents and said, 'The Lord must hate us, bringing us here from Egypt to be slaughtered by these Amorites." 
Deuteronomy1:28 What are we getting into? Our brothers who spied out the land have frightened us with their report. They say that the people of the land are tall and powerful, and that the walls of their cities rise high into the sky! They have even seen giants there—the descendants of the Anakim!'" 
Deuteronomy1:29 “But I said to them, 'Don't be afraid!" 
Deuteronomy1:30 The Lord God is your leader, and he will fight for you with his mighty miracles, just as you saw him do in Egypt." 
Deuteronomy1:31 And you know how he has cared for you again and again here in the wilderness, just as a father cares for his child!'" 
Deuteronomy1:32 But nothing I said did any good. “They refused to believe the Lord our God 
Deuteronomy1:33 who had led them all the way, and had selected the best places for them to camp, and had guided them by a pillar of fire at night and a pillar of cloud during the day." 
Deuteronomy1:34 “Well, the Lord heard their complaining and was very angry." 
Deuteronomy1:35 He vowed that not one person in that entire generation would live to see the good land he had promised their fathers," 
Deuteronomy1:36 except Caleb (the son of Jephunneh), who, because he had wholly followed the Lord, would receive as his personal inheritance some of the land he had walked over." 
Deuteronomy1:37 “And the Lord was even angry with me because of them and said to me, 'You shall not enter the Promised Land!" 
Deuteronomy1:38 Instead, your assistant, Joshua (the son of Nun), shall lead the people. Encourage him as he prepares to take over the leadership." 
Deuteronomy1:38 Instead, your assistant, Joshua (the son of Nun), shall lead the people. Encourage him as he prepares to take over the leadership." 
Deuteronomy1:39 I will give the land to the children they said would die in the wilderness. 
Deuteronomy1:40 But as for you of the older generation, turn around now and go on back across the desert toward the Red Sea.'" 
Deuteronomy1:41 “Then they confessed, 'We have sinned! We will go into the land and fight for it as the Lord our God has told us to.' So they strapped on their weapons and thought it would be easy to conquer the whole area." 
Deuteronomy1:42 “But the Lord said to me, 'Tell them not to do it, for I will not go with them; they will be struck down before their enemies.'" 
Deuteronomy1:43 “I told them, but they wouldn't listen. Instead, they rebelled again against the Lord's commandment and went on up into the hill country to fight." 
Deuteronomy1:44 But the Amorites who lived there came out against them and chased them like bees and killed them from Seir to Hormah. 
Deuteronomy1:45 Then they returned and wept before the Lord, but he wouldn't listen." 
Deuteronomy1:46 So they stayed there at Kadesh for a long time. 
Deuteronomy2:1 “Then we turned back across the wilderness toward the Red Sea, for so the Lord had instructed me. For many years we wandered around in the area of Mount Seir." 
Deuteronomy2:2 Then at last the Lord said," 
Deuteronomy2:3 'You have stayed here long enough. Turn northward. 
Deuteronomy2:4 Inform the people that they will be passing through the country belonging to their brothers the Edomites, the descendants of Esau who live in Seir; the Edomites will be nervous, so be careful." 
Deuteronomy2:5 Don't start a fight! For I have given them all the Mount Seir hill country as their permanent possession, and I will not give you even a tiny piece of their land." 
Deuteronomy2:6 Pay them for whatever food or water you use. 
Deuteronomy2:7 The Lord your God has watched over you and blessed you every step of the way for all these forty years as you have wandered around in this great wilderness; and you have lacked nothing in all that time.' 
Deuteronomy2:8 “So we passed through Edom where our brothers lived, crossing the Arabah Road that goes south to Elath and Ezion-geber, and traveling northward toward the Moab desert."
