2Kings15:6 The rest of the history of Azariah is recorded in The Annals of the Kings of Judah. 
2Kings15:7 When Azariah died, he was buried with his ancestors in the City of David, and his son Jotham became king." 
2Kings15:8 New king of Israel: Zechariah Father's name: Jeroboam Length of reign: 6 months, in Samaria Reigning in Judah at that time: King Azariah, who had been the king there for 38 years" 
2Kings15:8 New king of Israel: Zechariah Father's name: Jeroboam Length of reign: 6 months, in Samaria Reigning in Judah at that time: King Azariah, who had been the king there for 38 years" 
2Kings15:9 But Zechariah was an evil king in the Lord's sight, just like his ancestors. Like Jeroboam I (the son of Nebat), he encouraged Israel in the sin of worshiping idols." 
2Kings15:9 But Zechariah was an evil king in the Lord's sight, just like his ancestors. Like Jeroboam I (the son of Nebat), he encouraged Israel in the sin of worshiping idols." 
2Kings15:10 Then Shallum (the son of Jabesh) conspired against him and assassinated him at Ibleam and took the crown himself. 
2Kings15:11 The rest of the history of Zechariah's reign is found in The Annals of the Kings of Israel. 
2Kings15:11 The rest of the history of Zechariah's reign is found in The Annals of the Kings of Israel. 
2Kings15:12 (So the Lord's statement to Jehu came true, that Jehu's son, grandson, and great-grandson would be kings of Israel.)" 
2Kings15:13 New king of Israel: Shallum Father's name: Jabesh Length of reign: 1 month, in Samaria Reigning in Judah at that time: King Uzziah, who had been the king there for 39 years" 
2Kings15:14 One month after Shallum became king, Menahem (the son of Gadi) came to Samaria from Tirzah and assassinated him and took the throne." 
2Kings15:15 Additional details about King Shallum and his conspiracy are recorded in The Annals of the Kings of Israel. 
2Kings15:16 Menahem destroyed the city of Tappuah and the surrounding countryside, for its citizens refused to accept him as their king; he killed the entire population and ripped open the pregnant women." 
2Kings15:17 New king of Israel: Menahem Length of reign: 10 years, in Samaria Reigning in Judah at that time: King Azariah, who had been the king there for 39 years" 
2Kings15:18 But Menahem was an evil king. He worshiped idols, as King Jeroboam I had done so long before, and he led the people of Israel into grievous sin." 
2Kings15:19 Then King Pul of Assyria invaded the land; but King Menahem bought him off with a gift of “2,000,000, so he turned around and returned home." 
2Kings15:20 Menahem extorted the money from the rich, assessing each one “2,000 in the form of a special tax." 
2Kings15:21 The rest of the history of King Menahem is written in The Annals of the Kings of Israel. 
2Kings15:22 When he died, his son Pekahiah became the new king." 
2Kings15:23 New king of Israel: Pekahiah Father's name: King Menahem Length of reign: 2 years, in Samaria Reigning in Judah at that time: King Azariah, who had been the king there for 50 years" 
2Kings15:24 But Pekahiah was an evil king, and he continued the idol-worship begun by Jeroboam I (son of Nebat) who led Israel down that evil trail." 
2Kings15:25 Then Pekah (son of Remaliah), the commanding general of his army, conspired against him with fifty men from Gilead and assassinated him in the palace at Samaria (Argob and Arieh were also slain in the revolt). So Pekah became the new king." 
2Kings15:26 The rest of the history of King Pekahiah is recorded in The Annals of the Kings of Israel. 
2Kings15:27 New king of Israel: Pekah Father's name: Remaliah Length of reign: 20 years, in Samaria Reigning in Judah at that time: King Azariah, who had been the king there for 52 years" 
2Kings15:28 Pekah, too, was an evil king, and he continued in the example of Jeroboam I (son of Nebat), who led all of Israel into the sin of worshiping idols." 
2Kings15:29 It was during his reign that King Tiglath-pileser led an attack against Israel. He captured the cities of Ijon, Abel-beth-maacah, Janoah, Kedesh, Hazor, Gilead, Galilee, and all the land of Naphtali; and he took the people away to Assyria as captives." 
2Kings15:30 Then Hoshea (the son of Elah) plotted against Pekah and assassinated him; and he took the throne for himself. New king of Israel: Hoshea Reigning in Judah at that time: King Jotham (son of Uzziah), who had been the king there for 20 years" 
2Kings15:31 The rest of the history of Pekah's reign is recorded in The Annals of the Kings of Israel. 
2Kings15:32 New king of Judah: Jotham Father's name: King Uzziah 
2Kings15:33 His age at the beginning of his reign: 25 years old Length of reign: 16 years, in Jerusalem Mother's name: Jerusha (daughter of Zadok) Reigning in Israel at that time: King Pekah (son of Remaliah), who had been the king there for 2 years" 
2Kings15:34 Generally speaking, Jotham was a good king. Like his father Uzziah, he followed the Lord." 
2Kings15:35 But he didn't destroy the shrines on the hills where the people sacrificed and burned incense. It was during King Jotham's reign that the upper gate of the Temple of the Lord was built. 
2Kings15:36 The rest of Jotham's history is written in The Annals of the Kings of Judah. 
2Kings15:37 In those days the Lord caused King Rezin of Syria and King Pekah of Israel to attack Judah. 
2Kings15:38 When Jotham died he was buried with the other kings of Judah in the royal cemetery, in the City of David section of Jerusalem. Then his son Ahaz became the new king." 
2Kings16:1 New king of Judah: Ahaz Father's name: Jotham His age at the beginning of his reign: 20 years old Length of reign: 16 years, in Jerusalem Character of his reign: evil Reigning in Israel at that time: King Pekah (son of Remaliah), who had been the king there for 17 years" 
2Kings16:2 But he did not follow the Lord as his ancestor David had; 
2Kings16:3 he was as wicked as the kings of Israel. He even killed his own son by offering him as a burnt sacrifice to the gods, following the heathen customs of the nations around Judah—nations that the Lord destroyed when the people of Israel entered the land." 
2Kings16:4 He also sacrificed and burned incense at the shrines on the hills and at the numerous altars in the groves of trees. 
2Kings16:5 Then King Rezin of Syria and King Pekah (son of Remaliah) of Israel declared war on Ahaz and besieged Jerusalem; but they did not conquer it. 
2Kings16:6 However, at that time King Rezin of Syria recovered the city of Elath for Syria; he drove out the Jews and sent Syrians to live there, as they do to this day." 
2Kings16:7 King Ahaz sent a messenger to King Tiglath-pileser of Assyria, begging him to help him fight the attacking armies of Syria and Israel." 
2Kings16:8 Ahaz took the silver and gold from the Temple and from the royal vaults and sent it as a payment to the Assyrian king. 
2Kings16:9 So the Assyrians attacked Damascus, the capital of Syria. They took away the population of the city as captives, resettling them in Kir, and King Rezin of Syria was killed." 
2Kings16:10 King Ahaz now went to Damascus to meet with King Tiglath-pileser, and while he was there he noticed an unusual altar in a heathen temple. He jotted down its dimensions and made a sketch and sent it back to Uriah the priest with a detailed description." 
2Kings16:11 Uriah built one just like it by following these directions and had it ready 
2Kings16:12 for the king, who, upon his return from Damascus, inaugurated it with an offering." 
2Kings16:13 The king presented a burnt offering and a grain offering, poured a drink offering over it, and sprinkled the blood of peace offerings upon it." 
2Kings16:14 Then he removed the old bronze altar from the front of the Temple (it had stood between the Temple entrance and the new altar), and placed it on the north side of the new altar." 
2Kings16:15 He instructed Uriah the priest to use the new altar for the sacrifices of burnt offering, the evening grain offering, the king's burnt offering and grain offering, and the offerings of the people, including their drink offerings. The blood from the burnt offerings and sacrifices was also to be sprinkled over the new altar. So the old altar was used only for purposes of divination. “The old bronze altar,“ he said, “will be only for my personal use.“" 
2Kings16:16 Uriah the priest did as King Ahaz instructed him. 
2Kings16:17 Then the king dismantled the wheeled stands in the Temple, removed their crosspieces and the water vats they supported, and removed the great tank from the backs of the bronze oxen and placed it upon the stone pavement." 
2Kings16:18 In deference to the king of Assyria he also removed the festive passageway he had constructed between the palace and the Temple. 
2Kings16:19 The rest of the history of the reign of King Ahaz is recorded in The Annals of the Kings of Judah. 
2Kings16:20 When Ahaz died he was buried in the royal cemetery, in the City of David sector of Jerusalem, and his son Hezekiah became the new king." 
2Kings17:1 New king of Israel: Hoshea Father's name: Elah Length of reign: 9 years, in Samaria" 
2Kings17:2 Character of his reign: evil—but not as bad as some of the other kings of Israel Reigning in Judah at that time: King Ahaz, who had been the king there for 12 years" 
2Kings17:3 King Shalmaneser of Assyria attacked and defeated King Hoshea, so Israel had to pay heavy annual taxes to Assyria." 
2Kings17:4 Then Hoshea conspired against the king of Assyria by asking King So of Egypt to help him shake free of Assyria's power, but this treachery was discovered. At the same time he refused to pay the annual tribute to Assyria. So the king of Assyria put him in prison and in chains for his rebellion." 
2Kings17:5 Now the land of Israel was filled with Assyrian troops for three years besieging Samaria, the capital city of Israel." 
2Kings17:6 Finally, in the ninth year of King Hoshea's reign, Samaria fell and the people of Israel were exiled to Assyria. They were placed in colonies in the city of Halah and along the banks of the Habor River in Gozan, and among the cities of the Medes." 
2Kings17:7 This disaster came upon the nation of Israel because the people worshiped other gods, thus sinning against the Lord their God who had brought them safely out of their slavery in Egypt." 
2Kings17:8 They had followed the evil customs of the nations which the Lord had cast out from before them. 
2Kings17:9 The people of Israel had also secretly done many things that were wrong, and they had built altars to other gods throughout the land." 
2Kings17:10 They had placed obelisks and idols at the top of every hill and under every green tree; 
2Kings17:11 and they had burned incense to the gods of the very nations which the Lord had cleared out of the land when Israel came in. So the people of Israel had done many evil things, and the Lord was very angry." 
2Kings17:12 Yes, they worshiped idols, despite the Lord's specific and repeated warnings." 
2Kings17:13 Again and again the Lord had sent prophets to warn both Israel and Judah to turn from their evil ways; he had warned them to obey his commandments which he had given to their ancestors through these prophets," 
2Kings17:14 but Israel wouldn't listen. The people were as stubborn as their ancestors and refused to believe in the Lord their God. 
2Kings17:15 They rejected his laws and the covenant he had made with their ancestors, and despised all his warnings. In their foolishness they worshiped heathen idols despite the Lord's stern warnings." 
2Kings17:16 They defied all the commandments of the Lord their God and made two calves from molten gold. They made detestable, shameful idols and worshiped Baal and the sun, moon, and stars." 
2Kings17:17 They even burned their own sons and daughters to death on the altars of Molech; they consulted fortune-tellers and used magic and sold themselves to evil. So the Lord was very angry. 
2Kings17:18 He swept them from his sight until only the tribe of Judah remained in the land. 
2Kings17:19 But even Judah refused to obey the commandments of the Lord their God; they too walked in the same evil paths as Israel had. 
2Kings17:20 So the Lord rejected all the descendants of Jacob. He punished them by delivering them to their attackers until they were destroyed. 
2Kings17:21 For Israel split off from the kingdom of David and chose Jeroboam I (the son of Nebat) as its king. Then Jeroboam drew Israel away from following the Lord. He made them sin a great sin," 
2Kings17:22 and the people of Israel never quit doing the evil things that Jeroboam led them into," 
2Kings17:23 until the Lord finally swept them away, just as all his prophets had warned would happen. So Israel was carried off to the land of Assyria where they remain to this day." 
2Kings17:24 And the king of Assyria transported colonies of people from Babylon, Cuthah, Avva, Hamath, and Sepharvaim and resettled them in the cities of Samaria, replacing the people of Israel. So the Assyrians took over Samaria and the other cities of Israel." 
2Kings17:25 But since these Assyrian colonists did not worship the Lord when they first arrived, the Lord sent lions among them to kill some of them." 
2Kings17:26 Then they sent a message to the king of Assyria: “We colonists here in Israel don't know the laws of the god of the land, and he has sent lions among us to destroy us because we have not worshiped him.“" 
2Kings17:27 The king of Assyria then decreed that one of the exiled priests from Samaria should return to Israel and teach the new residents the laws of the god of the land. 
2Kings17:28 So one of them returned to Bethel and taught the colonists from Babylon how to worship the Lord. 
2Kings17:29 But these foreigners also worshiped their own gods. They placed them in the shrines on the hills near their cities. 
2Kings17:30 Those from Babylon worshiped idols of their god Succoth-benoth; those from Cuth worshiped their god Nergal; and the men of Hamath worshiped Ashima. 
2Kings17:31 The gods Nibhaz and Tartak were worshiped by the Avvites, and the people from Sephar even burned their own children on the altars of their gods Adrammelech and Anammelech." 
2Kings17:32 They also worshiped the Lord, and they appointed from among themselves priests to sacrifice to the Lord on the hilltop altars." 
2Kings17:33 But they continued to follow the religious customs of the nations from which they came. 
2Kings17:34 And this is still going on among them today—they follow their former practices instead of truly worshiping the Lord or obeying the laws he gave to the descendants of Jacob (whose name was later changed to Israel). 
2Kings17:35 For the Lord had made a contract with them—that they were never to worship or make sacrifices to any heathen gods. 
2Kings17:36 They were to worship only the Lord who had brought them out of the land of Egypt with such tremendous miracles and power. 
2Kings17:37 The descendants of Jacob were to obey all of God's laws and never worship other gods. 
2Kings17:38 For God had said, “You must never forget the covenant I made with you; never worship other gods." 
2Kings17:39 You must worship only the Lord; he will save you from all your enemies.“ 
2Kings17:40 But Israel didn't listen, and the people continued to worship other gods." 
2Kings17:41 These colonists from Babylon worshiped the Lord, yes—but they also worshiped their idols. And to this day their descendants do the same thing." 
2Kings18:1 New king of Judah: Hezekiah Father's name: Ahaz 
2Kings18:2 His age at the beginning of his reign: 25 years old Length of reign: 29 years, in Jerusalem Mother's name: Abi (daughter of Zechariah)" 
2Kings18:2 His age at the beginning of his reign: 25 years old Length of reign: 29 years, in Jerusalem Mother's name: Abi (daughter of Zechariah)" 
2Kings18:3 Character of his reign: good (similar to that of his ancestor David) Reigning in Israel at that time: King Hoshea (son of Elah), who had been the king there for 3 years" 
2Kings18:4 He removed the shrines on the hills, broke down the obelisks, knocked down the shameful idols of Asherah, and broke up the bronze serpent that Moses had made, because the people of Israel had begun to worship it by burning incense to it; even though, as King Hezekiah pointed out to them, it was merely a piece of bronze." 
2Kings18:5 He trusted very strongly in the Lord God of Israel. In fact, none of the kings before or after him were as close to God as he was." 
2Kings18:6 For he followed the Lord in everything, and carefully obeyed all of God's commands to Moses." 
2Kings18:7 So the Lord was with him and prospered everything he did. Then he rebelled against the king of Assyria and refused to pay tribute any longer. 
2Kings18:8 He also conquered the Philistines as far distant as Gaza and its suburbs, destroying cities both large and small." 
2Kings18:9 It was during the fourth year of his reign (which was the seventh year of the reign of King Hoshea in Israel) that King Shalmaneser of Assyria attacked Israel and began a siege on the city of Samaria. 
2Kings18:10 Three years later (during the sixth year of the reign of King Hezekiah and the ninth year of the reign of King Hoshea of Israel) Samaria fell. 
2Kings18:11 It was at that time that the king of Assyria transported the Israelis to Assyria and put them in colonies in the city of Halath and along the banks of the Habor River in Gozan, and in the cities of the Medes." 
2Kings18:12 For they had refused to listen to the Lord their God or to do what he wanted them to do. Instead, they had transgressed his covenant and disobeyed all the laws given to them by Moses the servant of the Lord." 
2Kings18:13 Later, during the fourteenth year of the reign of King Hezekiah, King Sennacherib of Assyria besieged and captured all the fortified cities of Judah." 
2Kings18:14 King Hezekiah sued for peace and sent this message to the king of Assyria at Lachish: “I have done wrong. I will pay whatever tribute you demand if you will only go away.“ The king of Assyria then demanded a settlement of “1,500,000." 
2Kings18:15 To gather this amount, King Hezekiah used all the silver stored in the Temple and in the palace treasury." 
2Kings18:16 He even stripped off the gold from the Temple doors, and from the doorposts he had overlaid with gold, and gave it all to the Assyrian king." 
2Kings18:17 Nevertheless the king of Assyria sent his field marshal, his chief treasurer, and his chief of staff from Lachish with a great army; and they camped along the highway beside the field where cloth was bleached, near the conduit of the upper pool." 
2Kings18:18 They demanded that King Hezekiah come out to speak to them, but instead he sent a truce delegation of the following men: Eliakim, his business manager; Shebnah, his secretary; and Joah, his royal historian." 
2Kings18:19 Then the Assyrian general sent this message to King Hezekiah: “The great king of Assyria says, 'No one can save you from my power!" 
2Kings18:20 You need more than mere promises of help before rebelling against me. But which of your allies will give you more than words? Egypt? 
2Kings18:21 If you lean on Egypt, you will find her to be a stick that breaks beneath your weight and pierces your hand. The Egyptian Pharaoh is totally unreliable!" 
2Kings18:22 And if you say, “We're trusting the Lord to rescue us“—just remember that he is the very one whose hilltop altars you've destroyed. For you require everyone to worship at the altar in Jerusalem!'" 
2Kings18:23 I'll tell you what: Make a bet with my master, the king of Assyria! If you have two thousand men left who can ride horses, we'll furnish the horses!" 
2Kings18:24 And with an army as small as yours, you are no threat to even the least lieutenant in charge of the smallest contingent in my master's army. Even if Egypt supplies you with horses and chariots, it will do no good." 
2Kings18:25 And do you think we have come here on our own? No! The Lord sent us and told us, 'Go and destroy this nation!' “" 
2Kings18:26 Then Eliakim, Shebnah, and Joah said to them, “Please speak in Aramaic, for we understand it. Don't use Hebrew, for the people standing on the walls will hear you.“" 
2Kings18:27 But the Assyrian general replied, “Has my master sent me to speak only to you and to your master? Hasn't he sent me to the people on the walls too? For they are doomed with you to eat their own excrement and drink their own urine!“" 
2Kings18:28 Then the Assyrian ambassador shouted in Hebrew to the people on the wall, “Listen to the great king of Assyria!" 
2Kings18:29 'Don't let King Hezekiah fool you. He will never be able to save you from my power. 
2Kings18:30 Don't let him fool you into trusting in the Lord to rescue you. 
2Kings18:31 Don't listen to King Hezekiah. Surrender! You can live in peace here in your own land 
2Kings18:32 until I take you to another land just like this one—with plentiful crops, grain, grapes, olive trees, and honey. All of this instead of death! Don't listen to King Hezekiah when he tries to persuade you that the Lord will deliver you." 
2Kings18:33 Have any of the gods of the other nations ever delivered their people from the king of Assyria? 
2Kings18:34 What happened to the gods of Hamath, Arpad, Sepharvaim, Hena, and Ivvah? Did they rescue Samaria?" 
2Kings18:35 What god has ever been able to save any nation from my power? So what makes you think the Lord can save Jerusalem?' “ 
2Kings18:36 But the people on the wall remained silent, for the king had instructed them to say nothing." 
2Kings18:37 Then Eliakim (son of Hilkiah) the business manager, and Shebnah the king's secretary, and Joah (son of Asaph) the historian went to King Hezekiah with their clothes torn and told him what the Assyrian general had said." 
2Kings19:1 When King Hezekiah heard their report, he tore his clothes and put on sackcloth and went into the Temple to pray." 
2Kings19:2 Then he told Eliakim, Shebnah, and some of the older priests to clothe themselves in sackcloth and to go to Isaiah (son of Amoz), the prophet, with this message:" 
2Kings19:2 Then he told Eliakim, Shebnah, and some of the older priests to clothe themselves in sackcloth and to go to Isaiah (son of Amoz), the prophet, with this message:" 
2Kings19:3 “King Hezekiah says, 'This is a day of trouble, insult, and dishonor. It is as when a child is ready to be born, but the mother has no strength to deliver it." 
2Kings19:4 Yet perhaps the Lord your God has heard the Assyrian general defying the living God and will rebuke him. Oh, pray for the few of us who are left.' “" 
2Kings19:5 Isaiah replied, “The Lord says, 'Tell your master not to" 
2Kings19:5 Isaiah replied, “The Lord says, 'Tell your master not to" 
2Kings19:6 be troubled by the sneers these Assyrians have made against me.' 
2Kings19:7 For the king of Assyria will receive bad news from home and will decide to return; and the Lord will see to it that he is killed when he arrives there.“ 
2Kings19:8 Then the Assyrian general returned to his king at Libnah (for he received word that he had left Lachish). 
2Kings19:9 Soon afterwards news reached the king that King Tirhakah of Ethiopia was coming to attack him. Before leaving to meet the attack, he sent back this message to King Hezekiah:" 
2Kings19:10 “Don't be fooled by that god you trust in. Don't believe it when he says that I won't conquer Jerusalem. 
2Kings19:11 You know perfectly well what the kings of Assyria have done wherever they have gone; they have completely destroyed everything. Why would you be any different? 
2Kings19:12 Have the gods of the other nations delivered them—such nations as Gozan, Haran, Rezeph, and Eden in the land of Telassar? The former kings of Assyria destroyed them all!" 
2Kings19:13 What happened to the king of Hamoth and the king of Arpad? What happened to the kings of Sepharvaim, Hena, and Ivvah?“" 
2Kings19:14 Hezekiah took the letter from the messengers, read it, and went over to the Temple and spread it out before the Lord." 
2Kings19:15 Then he prayed this prayer: “O Lord God of Israel, sitting on your throne high above the angels, you alone are the God of all the kingdoms of the earth. You created the heavens and the earth." 
2Kings19:16 Bend low, O Lord, and listen. Open your eyes, O Lord, and see. Listen to this man's defiance of the living God." 
2Kings19:17 Lord, it is true that the kings of Assyria have destroyed all those nations" 
2Kings19:18 and have burned their idol-gods. But they weren't gods at all; they were destroyed because they were only things that men had made of wood and stone. 
2Kings19:19 O Lord our God, we plead with you to save us from his power; then all the kingdoms of the earth will know that you alone are God.“" 
2Kings19:20 Then Isaiah sent this message to Hezekiah: “The Lord God of Israel says, 'I have heard you!" 
2Kings19:20 Then Isaiah sent this message to Hezekiah: “The Lord God of Israel says, 'I have heard you!" 
2Kings19:21 And this is my reply to King Sennacherib: The virgin daughter of Zion isn't afraid of you! The daughter of Jerusalem scorns and mocks at you. 
2Kings19:22 Whom have you defied and blasphemed? And toward whom have you felt so cocky? It is the Holy One of Israel! 
2Kings19:23 You have boasted, “My chariots have conquered the highest mountains, yes, the peaks of Lebanon. I have cut down the tallest cedars and choicest cypress trees and have conquered the farthest borders." 
2Kings19:24 I have been refreshed at many conquered wells, and I destroyed the strength of Egypt just by walking by!“" 
2Kings19:25 'Why haven't you realized long before this that it is I, the Lord, who lets you do these things? I decreed your conquest of all those fortified cities!" 
2Kings19:26 So of course the nations you conquered had no power against you! They were like grass shriveling beneath the hot sun, and like grain blighted before it is half grown." 
2Kings19:27 I know everything about you. I know all your plans and where you are going next; and I also know the evil things you have said about me. 
2Kings19:28 And because of your arrogance against me, I am going to put a hook in your nose and a bridle in your mouth and turn you back on the road by which you came." 
2Kings19:29 And this is the proof that I will do as I have promised: This year my people will eat the volunteer wheat and use it as seed for next year's crop; and in the third year they will have a bountiful harvest. 
2Kings19:30 'O my people Judah, those of you who have escaped the ravages of the siege shall become a great nation again; you shall be rooted deeply in the soil and bear fruit for God." 
2Kings19:31 A remnant of my people shall become strong in Jerusalem. The Lord is eager to cause this to happen. 
2Kings19:32 'And my command concerning the king of Assyria is that he shall not enter this city. He shall not stand before it with a shield, nor build a ramp against its wall, nor even shoot an arrow into it." 
2Kings19:33 He shall return by the road he came," 
2Kings19:34 for I will defend and save this city for the sake of my own name and for the sake of my servant David.' “ 
2Kings19:35 That very night the angel of the Lord killed 185,000 Assyrian troops, and dead bodies were seen all across the landscape in the morning." 
2Kings19:36 Then King Sennacherib returned to Nineveh; 
2Kings19:37 and as he was worshiping in the temple of his god Nisroch, his sons Adrammelech and Sharezer killed him. They escaped into eastern Turkey—the land of Ararat—and his son Esarhaddon became the new king." 
2Kings20:1 Hezekiah now became deathly sick, and Isaiah the prophet went to visit him. “Set your affairs in order and prepare to die,“ Isaiah told him. “The Lord says you won't recover.“" 
2Kings20:1 Hezekiah now became deathly sick, and Isaiah the prophet went to visit him. “Set your affairs in order and prepare to die,“ Isaiah told him. “The Lord says you won't recover.“" 
2Kings20:2 Hezekiah turned his face to the wall. 
2Kings20:3 “O Lord,“ he pleaded, “remember how I've always tried to obey you and to please you in everything I do. . . . “ Then he broke down and cried." 
2Kings20:4 So before Isaiah had left the courtyard, the Lord spoke to him again." 
2Kings20:4 So before Isaiah had left the courtyard, the Lord spoke to him again." 
2Kings20:5 “Go back to Hezekiah, the leader of my people, and tell him that the Lord God of his ancestor David has heard his prayer and seen his tears. I will heal him, and three days from now he will be out of bed and at the Temple!" 
2Kings20:6 I will add fifteen years to his life and save him and this city from the king of Assyria. And it will all be done for the glory of my own name and for the sake of my servant David.“ 
2Kings20:7 Isaiah then instructed Hezekiah to boil some dried figs and to make a paste of them and spread it on the boil. And he recovered!

2Kings20:8 Meanwhile, King Hezekiah had said to Isaiah, “Do a miracle to prove to me that the Lord will heal me and that I will be able to go to the Temple again three days from now.“" 
2Kings20:8 Meanwhile, King Hezekiah had said to Isaiah, “Do a miracle to prove to me that the Lord will heal me and that I will be able to go to the Temple again three days from now.“" 
2Kings20:9 “All right, the Lord will give you a proof,“ Isaiah told him. “Do you want the shadow on the sundial to go forward ten points or backward ten points?“" 
2Kings20:9 “All right, the Lord will give you a proof,“ Isaiah told him. “Do you want the shadow on the sundial to go forward ten points or backward ten points?“" 
2Kings20:10 “The shadow always moves forward,“ Hezekiah replied; “make it go backward.“" 
2Kings20:11 So Isaiah asked the Lord to do this, and he caused the shadow to move ten points backward on the sundial of Ahaz!" 
2Kings20:11 So Isaiah asked the Lord to do this, and he caused the shadow to move ten points backward on the sundial of Ahaz!" 
2Kings20:12 At that time Merodach-baladan (the son of King Baladan of Babylon) sent ambassadors with greetings and a present to Hezekiah, for he had learned of his sickness." 
2Kings20:13 Hezekiah welcomed them and showed them all his treasures—the silver, gold, spices, aromatic oils, the armory—everything." 
2Kings20:14 Then Isaiah went to King Hezekiah and asked him, “What did these men want? Where are they from?“ “From far away in Babylon,“ Hezekiah replied." 
2Kings20:14 Then Isaiah went to King Hezekiah and asked him, “What did these men want? Where are they from?“ “From far away in Babylon,“ Hezekiah replied." 
2Kings20:15 “What have they seen in your palace?“ Isaiah asked. And Hezekiah replied, “Everything. I showed them all my treasures.“"

2Kings20:16 Then Isaiah said to Hezekiah, “Listen to the word of the Lord:"

2Kings20:17 The time will come when everything in this palace shall be carried to Babylon. All the treasures of your ancestors will be taken—nothing shall be left. 
2Kings20:18 Some of your own sons will be taken away and made into eunuchs who will serve in the palace of the king of Babylon.“ 
2Kings20:19 “All right,“ Hezekiah replied, “if this is what the Lord wants, it is good.“ But he was really thinking, “At least there will be peace and security during the remainder of my own life!“" 
2Kings20:20 The rest of the history of Hezekiah and his great deeds—including the pool and conduit he made and how he brought water into the city—are recorded in The Annals of the Kings of Judah. 
2Kings20:21 When he died, his son Manasseh became the new king." 
2Kings21:1 New king of Judah: Manasseh His age at the beginning of his reign: 12 years old Length of reign: 55 years, in Jerusalem Mother's name: Hephzibah" 
2Kings21:2 Character of his reign: evil; he did the same things the nations had done that were thrown out of the land to make room for the people of Israel 
2Kings21:3 He rebuilt the hilltop shrines that his father Hezekiah had destroyed. 
2Kings21:4 He built altars for Baal and made a shameful Asherah idol, just as Ahab the king of Israel had done." 
2Kings21:5 Heathen altars to the sun god, moon god, and the gods of the stars were placed even in the Temple of the Lord—in the very city and building that the Lord had selected to honor his own name." 
2Kings21:6 And he sacrificed one of his sons as a burnt offering on a heathen altar. He practiced black magic and used fortune-telling, and patronized mediums and wizards. So the Lord was very angry, for Manasseh was an evil man, in God's sight." 
2Kings21:7 Manasseh even set up a shameful Asherah-idol in the Temple—the very place that the Lord had spoken to David and Solomon about when he said, “I will place my name forever in this Temple, and in Jerusalem—the city I have chosen from among all the cities of the tribes of Israel." 
2Kings21:8 If the people of Israel will only follow the instructions I gave them through Moses, I will never again expel them from this land of their fathers.“" 
2Kings21:9 But the people did not listen to the Lord, and Manasseh enticed them to do even more evil than the surrounding nations had done, even though Jehovah had destroyed those nations for their evil ways when the people of Israel entered the land." 
2Kings21:10 Then the Lord declared through the prophets," 
2Kings21:11 “Because King Manasseh has done these evil things and is even more wicked than the Amorites who were in this land long ago, and because he has led the people of Judah into idolatry:" 
2Kings21:12 I will bring such evil upon Jerusalem and Judah that the ears of those who hear about it will tingle with horror. 
2Kings21:13 I will punish Jerusalem as I did Samaria, and as I did King Ahab of Israel and his descendants. I will wipe away the people of Jerusalem as a man wipes a dish and turns it upside down to dry." 
2Kings21:14 Then I will reject even those few of my people who are left, and I will hand them over to their enemies." 
2Kings21:15 For they have done great evil and have angered me ever since I brought their ancestors from Egypt.“ 
2Kings21:16 In addition to the idolatry which God hated and into which Manasseh led the people of Judah, he murdered great numbers of innocent people. And Jerusalem was filled from one end to the other with the bodies of his victims." 
2Kings21:17 The rest of the history of Manasseh's sinful reign is recorded in The Annals of the Kings of Judah. 
2Kings21:18 When he died he was buried in the garden of his palace at Uzza, and his son Amon became the new king." 
2Kings21:19 New king of Judah: Amon His age at the beginning of his reign: 22 years old Length of reign: 2 years, in Jerusalem Mother's name: Meshullemeth (daughter of Haruz, of Jotbah)" 
2Kings21:20 Character of his reign: evil 
2Kings21:21 He did all the evil things his father had done: he worshiped the same idols 
2Kings21:22 and turned his back on the Lord God of his ancestors. He refused to listen to God's instructions. 
2Kings21:23 But his aides conspired against him and killed him in the palace. 
2Kings21:24 Then a posse of civilians killed all the assassins and placed Amon's son Josiah upon the throne. 
2Kings21:25 The rest of Amon's biography is recorded in The Annals of the Kings of Judah. 
2Kings21:26 He was buried in a crypt in the garden of Uzza, and his son Josiah became the new king." 
2Kings22:1 New king of Judah: Josiah His age at the beginning of his reign: 8 years old Length of reign: 31 years, in Jerusalem Mother's name: Jedidah (daughter of Adaiah of Bozkath)" 
2Kings22:2 Character of his reign: good; he followed in the steps of his ancestor King David, obeying the Lord completely" 
2Kings22:3 In the eighteenth year of his reign, King Josiah sent his secretary Shaphan (son of Azaliah, son of Meshullam) to the Temple to give instruction to Hilkiah, the High Priest:" 
2Kings22:4 “Collect the money given to the priests at the door of the Temple when the people come to worship. 
2Kings22:5 Give this money to the building superintendents so that they can hire 
2Kings22:6 carpenters and masons to repair the Temple, and to buy lumber and stone.“" 
2Kings22:7 (The building superintendents were not required to keep account of their expenditures, for they were honest men.)" 
2Kings22:8 One day Hilkiah the High Priest went to Shaphan the secretary and exclaimed, “I have discovered a scroll in the Temple, with God's laws written on it!“ He gave the scroll to Shaphan to read." 
2Kings22:9 When Shaphan reported to the king about the progress of the repairs at the Temple, he also mentioned the scroll found by Hilkiah." 
2Kings22:10 Then Shaphan read it to the king. 
2Kings22:11 When the king heard what was written in it, he tore his clothes in terror." 
2Kings22:12 He commanded Hilkiah the priest, and Shaphan, and Asaiah, the king's assistant, and Ahikam (Shaphan's son), and Achbor (Michaiah's son) to" 
2Kings22:13 ask the Lord, “What shall we do? For we have not been following the instructions of this book: you must be very angry with us, for neither we nor our ancestors have followed your commands.“" 
2Kings22:14 So Hilkiah the priest, and Ahikam, and Achbor, and Shaphan, and Asaiah went to the Mishneh section of Jerusalem to find Huldah the prophetess. (She was the wife of Shallum—son of Tikvah, son of Harhas—who was in charge of the palace tailor shop.)" 
2Kings22:15 She gave them this message from the Lord God of Israel: “Tell the man who sent you to me 
2Kings22:16 that I am going to destroy this city and its people, just as I stated in that book you read." 
2Kings22:17 For the people of Judah have thrown me aside and have worshiped other gods and have made me very angry; and my anger can't be stopped. 
2Kings22:18 But because you were sorry and concerned 
2Kings22:19 and humbled yourself before the Lord when you read the book and its warnings that this land would be cursed and become desolate, and because you have torn your clothing and wept before me in contrition, I will listen to your plea." 
2Kings22:20 The death of this nation will not occur until after you die—you will not see the evil that I will bring upon this place.“ So they took the message to the king. 
2Kings23:1 Then the king sent for the elders and other leaders of Judah and Jerusalem to go to the Temple with him. 
2Kings23:2 So all the priests and prophets and the people, small and great, of Jerusalem and Judah gathered there at the Temple so that the king could read to them the entire book of God's laws which had been discovered in the Temple." 
2Kings23:3 He stood beside the pillar in front of the people, and he and they made a solemn promise to the Lord to obey him at all times and to do everything the book commanded." 
2Kings23:4 Then the king instructed Hilkiah the High Priest and the rest of the priests and the guards of the Temple to destroy all the equipment used in the worship of Baal, Asherah, and the sun, moon, and stars. The king had it all burned in the fields of the Kidron Valley outside Jerusalem, and he carried the ashes to Bethel." 
2Kings23:5 He killed the heathen priests who had been appointed by the previous kings of Judah, for they had burned incense in the shrines on the hills throughout Judah and even in Jerusalem. They had also offered incense to Baal and to the sun, moon, stars, and planets." 
2Kings23:6 He removed the shameful idol of Asherah from the Temple and took it outside Jerusalem to Kidron Brook; there he burned it and beat it to dust and threw the dust on the graves of the common people. 
2Kings23:7 He also tore down the houses of male prostitution around the Temple, where the women wove robes for the Asherah-idol." 
2Kings23:8 He brought back to Jerusalem the priests of the Lord, who were living in other cities of Judah, and tore down all the shrines on the hills where they had burned incense, even those as far away as Geba and Beersheba. He also destroyed the shrines at the entrance of the palace of Joshua, the former mayor of Jerusalem, located on the left side as one enters the city gate." 
2Kings23:8 He brought back to Jerusalem the priests of the Lord, who were living in other cities of Judah, and tore down all the shrines on the hills where they had burned incense, even those as far away as Geba and Beersheba. He also destroyed the shrines at the entrance of the palace of Joshua, the former mayor of Jerusalem, located on the left side as one enters the city gate." 
2Kings23:9 However, these priests did not serve at the altar of the Lord in Jerusalem, even though they ate with the other priests." 
2Kings23:10 Then the king destroyed the altar of Topheth in the Valley of the Sons of Hinnom, so that no one could ever again use it to burn his son or daughter to death as a sacrifice to Molech." 
2Kings23:11 He tore down the statues of horses and chariots located near the entrance of the Temple, next to the quarters of Nathan-melech the eunuch. These had been dedicated by former kings of Judah to the sun god." 
2Kings23:12 Then he tore down the altars that the kings of Judah had built on the palace roof above the Ahaz Room. He also destroyed the altars that Manasseh had built in the two courts of the Temple; he smashed them to bits and scattered the pieces in Kidron Valley. 
2Kings23:13 Next he removed the shrines on the hills east of Jerusalem and south of Destruction Mountain. (Solomon had built these shrines for Ashtoreth, the evil goddess of the Sidonians; and for Chemosh, the evil god of Moab; and for Milcom, the evil god of the Ammonites.)" 
2Kings23:14 He smashed the obelisks and cut down the shameful idols of Asherah; then he defiled these places by scattering human bones over them. 
2Kings23:15 He also tore down the altar and shrine at Bethel that Jeroboam I had made when he led Israel into sin. He crushed the stones to dust and burned the shameful idol of Asherah. 
2Kings23:16 As Josiah was looking around, he noticed several graves in the side of the mountain. He ordered his men to bring out the bones in them and to burn them there upon the altar at Bethel to defile it, just as the Lord's prophet had declared would happen to Jeroboam's altar." 
2Kings23:17 “What is that monument over there?“ he asked. And the men of the city told him, “It is the grave of the prophet who came from Judah and proclaimed that what you have just done would happen here at the altar at Bethel!“" 
2Kings23:18 So King Josiah replied, “Leave it alone. Don't disturb his bones.“ So they didn't burn his bones or those of the prophet from Samaria." 
2Kings23:19 Josiah demolished the shrines on the hills in all of Samaria. They had been built by the various kings of Israel and had made the Lord very angry. But now he crushed them into dust, just as he had done at Bethel." 
2Kings23:20 He executed the priests of the heathen shrines upon their own altars, and he burned human bones upon the altars to defile them. Finally he returned to Jerusalem." 
2Kings23:21 The king then issued orders for his people to observe the Passover ceremonies as recorded by the Lord their God in The Book of the Covenant. 
2Kings23:22 There had not been a Passover celebration like that since the days of the judges of Israel, and there was never another like it in all the years of the kings of Israel and Judah." 
2Kings23:23 This Passover was in the eighteenth year of the reign of King Josiah, and it was celebrated in Jerusalem." 
2Kings23:24 Josiah also exterminated the mediums and wizards, and every kind of idol worship, both in Jerusalem and throughout the land. For Josiah wanted to follow all the laws that were written in the book that Hilkiah the priest had found in the Temple." 
2Kings23:25 There was no other king who so completely turned to the Lord and followed all the laws of Moses; and no king since the time of Josiah has approached his record of obedience. 
2Kings23:26 But the Lord still did not hold back his great anger against Judah, caused by the evils of King Manasseh." 
2Kings23:27 For the Lord had said, “I will destroy Judah just as I have destroyed Israel; and I will discard my chosen city of Jerusalem and the Temple that I said was mine.“" 
2Kings23:28 The rest of the biography of Josiah is written in The Annals of the Kings of Judah. 
2Kings23:29 In those days King Neco of Egypt went out to help the king of Assyria at the Euphrates River. Then King Josiah went out with his troops to fight King Neco; but King Neco withstood him at Megiddo and killed him. 
2Kings23:30 His officers took his body back in a chariot from Megiddo to Jerusalem and buried him in the grave he had selected. And his son Jehoahaz was chosen by the nation as its new king. 
2Kings23:31 New king of Judah: Jehoahaz His age at the beginning of his reign: 23 years old Length of reign: 3 months, in Jerusalem Mother's name: Hamutal (the daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah)" 
2Kings23:31 New king of Judah: Jehoahaz His age at the beginning of his reign: 23 years old Length of reign: 3 months, in Jerusalem Mother's name: Hamutal (the daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah)" 
2Kings23:32 Character of his reign: evil, like the other kings who had preceded him" 
2Kings23:33 Pharaoh-Neco jailed him at Riblah in Hamath to prevent his reigning in Jerusalem, and he levied a tax against Judah totaling “230,000." 
2Kings23:34 The Egyptian king then chose Eliakim, another of Josiah's sons, to reign in Jerusalem; and he changed his name to Jehoiakim. Then he took King Jehoahaz to Egypt, where he died." 
2Kings23:35 Jehoiakim taxed the people to get the money that the Pharaoh had demanded. 
2Kings23:36 New king of Judah: Jehoiakim His age at the beginning of his reign: 25 years old Length of reign: 11 years, in Jerusalem Mother's name: Zebidah (daughter of Pedaiah of Rumah)" 
2Kings23:37 Character of his reign: evil, like the other kings who had preceded him" 
2Kings24:1 During the reign of King Jehoiakim, King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon attacked Jerusalem. Jehoiakim surrendered and paid him tribute for three years, but then rebelled." 
2Kings24:2 And the Lord sent bands of Chaldeans, Syrians, Moabites, and Ammonites against Judah in order to destroy the nation, just as the Lord had warned through his prophets that he would." 
2Kings24:3 It is clear that these disasters befell Judah at the direct command of the Lord. He had decided to wipe Judah out of his sight because of the many sins of Manasseh," 
2Kings24:4 for he had filled Jerusalem with blood, and the Lord would not pardon it." 
2Kings24:5 The rest of the history of the life of Jehoiakim is recorded in The Annals of the Kings of Judah. 
2Kings24:6 When he died, his son Jehoiachin became the new king." 
2Kings24:7 (The Egyptian Pharaoh never returned after that, for the king of Babylon occupied the entire area claimed by Egypt—all of Judah from the Brook of Egypt to the Euphrates River.)" 
2Kings24:8 New king of Judah: Jehoiachin His age at the beginning of his reign: 18 years old 
2Kings24:9 Length of reign: 3 months, in Jerusalem Mother's name: Nehushta (daughter of Elnathan, a citizen of Jerusalem)" 
2Kings24:10 During his reign the armies of King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon besieged the city of Jerusalem. 
2Kings24:11 Nebuchadnezzar himself arrived during the siege," 
2Kings24:12 and King Jehoiachin, all of his officials, and the queen mother surrendered to him. The surrender was accepted, and Jehoiachin was imprisoned in Babylon during the eighth year of Nebuchadnezzar's reign." 
2Kings24:13 The Babylonians carried home all the treasures from the Temple and the royal palace; and they cut apart all the gold bowls which King Solomon of Israel had placed in the Temple at the Lord's directions. 
2Kings24:14 King Nebuchadnezzar took ten thousand captives from Jerusalem, including all the princes and the best of the soldiers, craftsmen, and smiths. So only the poorest and least skilled people were left in the land." 
2Kings24:15 Nebuchadnezzar took King Jehoiachin, his wives and officials, and the queen mother, to Babylon." 
2Kings24:16 He also took seven thousand of the best troops and one thousand craftsmen and smiths, all of whom were strong and fit for war." 
2Kings24:17 Then the king of Babylon appointed King Jehoiachin's great-uncle, Mattaniah, to be the next king; and he changed his name to Zedekiah." 
2Kings24:18 New king of Judah: Zedekiah His age at the beginning of his reign: 21 years old Length of reign: 11 years, in Jerusalem Mother's name: Hamutal (daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah)" 
2Kings24:18 New king of Judah: Zedekiah His age at the beginning of his reign: 21 years old Length of reign: 11 years, in Jerusalem Mother's name: Hamutal (daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah)" 
2Kings24:19 Character of his reign: evil, like that of Jehoiakim" 
2Kings24:20 So the Lord finally, in his anger, destroyed the people of Jerusalem and Judah. But now King Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon." 
2Kings25:1 Then King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon mobilized his entire army and laid siege to Jerusalem, arriving on March 25 of the ninth year of the reign of King Zedekiah of Judah." 
2Kings25:2 The siege continued into the eleventh year of his reign. 
2Kings25:3 The last food in the city was eaten on July 24," 
2Kings25:4 and that night the king and his troops made a hole in the inner wall and fled out toward the Arabah through a gate that lay between the double walls near the king's garden. 
2Kings25:5 The Babylonian troops surrounding the city took out after him and captured him in the plains of Jericho, and all his men scattered." 
2Kings25:6 He was taken to Riblah, where he was tried and sentenced before the king of Babylon." 
2Kings25:7 He was forced to watch as his sons were killed before his eyes; then his eyes were put out, and he was bound with chains and taken away to Babylon." 
2Kings25:8 General Nebuzaradan, the captain of the royal bodyguard, arrived at Jerusalem from Babylon on July 22 of the nineteenth year of the reign of King Nebuchadnezzar." 
2Kings25:9 He burned down the Temple, the palace, and all the other houses of any worth." 
2Kings25:10 He then supervised the Babylonian army in tearing down the walls of Jerusalem. 
2Kings25:11 The remainder of the people in the city and the Jewish deserters who had declared their allegiance to the king of Babylon were all taken as exiles to Babylon. 
2Kings25:12 But the poorest of the people were left to farm the land. 
2Kings25:13 The Babylonians broke up the bronze pillars of the Temple and the bronze tank and its bases and carried all the bronze to Babylon. 
2Kings25:14 They also took all the pots, shovels, firepans, snuffers, spoons, and other bronze instruments used for the sacrifices." 
2Kings25:15 The gold and silver bowls, with all the rest of the gold and silver, were melted down to bullion." 
2Kings25:16 It was impossible to estimate the weight of the two pillars and the great tank and its bases—all made for the Temple by King Solomon—because they were so heavy. 
2Kings25:17 Each pillar was 27 feet high, with an intricate bronze network of pomegranates decorating the 4 1/2-foot capitals at the tops of the pillars." 
2Kings25:18 The general took Seraiah, the chief priest, his assistant Zephaniah, and the three Temple guards to Babylon as captives." 
2Kings25:18 The general took Seraiah, the chief priest, his assistant Zephaniah, and the three Temple guards to Babylon as captives." 
2Kings25:19 A commander of the army of Judah, the chief recruiting officer, five of the king's counselors, and sixty farmers, all of whom were discovered hiding in the city," 
2Kings25:20 were taken by General Nebuzaradan to the king of Babylon at Riblah," 
2Kings25:21 where they were put to the sword and died. So Judah was exiled from its land. 
2Kings25:22 Then King Nebuchadnezzar appointed Gedaliah (the son of Ahikam and grandson of Shaphan) as governor over the people left in Judah. 
2Kings25:23 When the Israeli guerrilla forces learned that the king of Babylon had appointed Gedaliah as governor, some of these underground leaders and their men joined him at Mizpah. These included Ishmael, the son of Nethaniah; Johanan, the son of Kareah; Seraiah, the son of Tanhumeth the Netophathite; and Jaazaniah, son of Maachathite, and their men." 
2Kings25:24 Gedaliah vowed that if they would give themselves up and submit to the Babylonians, they would be allowed to live in the land and would not be exiled." 
2Kings25:25 But seven months later, Ishmael, who was a member of the royal line, went to Mizpah with ten men and killed Gedaliah and his court—both the Jews and the Babylonians." 
2Kings25:26 Then all the men of Judah and the guerrilla leaders fled in panic to Egypt, for they were afraid of what the Babylonians would do to them." 
2Kings25:27 King Jehoiachin was released from prison on the twenty-seventh day of the last month of the thirty-seventh year of his captivity. This occurred during the first year of the reign of King Evil-merodach of Babylon. 
2Kings25:28 He treated Jehoiachin kindly and gave him preferential treatment over all the other kings who were being held as prisoners in Babylon. 
2Kings25:29 Jehoiachin was given civilian clothing to replace his prison garb, and for as long as he lived, he ate regularly at the king's table." 
2Kings25:30 The king also gave him a daily cash allowance for the rest of his life. 
1Chronicles1:1 These are the earliest generations of mankind: Adam, Seth, Enosh," 
1Chronicles1:2 Kenan, Mahalalel, Jared," 
1Chronicles1:3 Enoch, Methuselah, Lamech," 
1Chronicles1:4 Noah, Shem, Ham, and Japheth." 
1Chronicles1:5 The sons of Japheth were: Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras." 
1Chronicles1:6 The sons of Gomer: Ashkenaz, Diphath, and Togarmah." 
1Chronicles1:7 The sons of Javan: Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Rodanim." 
1Chronicles1:8 The sons of Ham: Cush, Mizraim, Canaan, and Put." 
1Chronicles1:9 The sons of Cush were: Seba, Havilah, Sabta, Raama, and Sabteca. The sons of Raama were Sheba and Dedan." 
1Chronicles1:10 Another of the sons of Cush was Nimrod, who became a great hero." 
1Chronicles1:11 The clans named after the sons of Mizraim were: the Ludim, the Anamim, the Lehabim, the Naphtuhim," 
1Chronicles1:12 the Pathrusim, the Caphtorim, and the Casluhim (the ancestors of the Philistines)." 
1Chronicles1:13 Among Canaan's sons were: Sidon (his firstborn) and Heth. Canaan was also the ancestor of the 
1Chronicles1:14 Jebusites, Amorites, Girgashites," 
1Chronicles1:15 Hivites, Arkites, Sinites," 
1Chronicles1:16 Arvadites, Zemarites, and Hamathites." 
1Chronicles1:17 The sons of Shem: Elam, Asshur, Arpachshad, Lud, Aram, Uz, Hul, Gether, and Meshech." 
1Chronicles1:18 Arpachshad's son was Shelah, and Shelah's son was Eber." 
1Chronicles1:19 Eber had two sons: Peleg (which means “Divided,“ for it was during his lifetime that the people of the earth were divided into different language groups) and Joktan." 
1Chronicles1:20 The sons of Joktan: 
1Chronicles1:21 Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah, Hadoram," 
1Chronicles1:22 Uzal, Diklah, Ebal, Abimael," 
1Chronicles1:23 Sheba, Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab." 
1Chronicles1:23 Sheba, Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab." 
1Chronicles1:24 So the son of Shem was Arpachshad, the son of Arpachshad was Shelah," 
1Chronicles1:25 The son of Shelah was Eber, The son of Eber was Peleg, The son of Peleg was Reu," 
1Chronicles1:26 The son of Reu was Serug, The son of Serug was Nahor, The son of Nahor was Terah," 
1Chronicles1:27 The son of Terah was Abram (later known as Abraham). 
1Chronicles1:28 Abraham's sons were Isaac and Ishmael. 
1Chronicles1:29 The sons of Ishmael: Nebaioth (the oldest), Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam," 
1Chronicles1:30 Mishma, Dumah, Massa, Hadad, Tema," 
1Chronicles1:31 Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah." 
1Chronicles1:32 Abraham also had sons by his concubine Keturah: Zimram, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah. Jokshan's sons were Sheba and Dedan." 
1Chronicles1:33 The sons of Midian: Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah. These were the descendants of Abraham by his concubine Keturah." 
1Chronicles1:34 Abraham's son Isaac had two sons, Esau and Israel." 
1Chronicles1:35 The sons of Esau: Eliphaz, Reuel, Jeush, Jalam, and Korah." 
1Chronicles1:36 The sons of Eliphaz: Teman, Omar, Zephi, Gatam, Kenaz, Timna, and Amalek." 
1Chronicles1:37 The sons of Reuel: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah." 
1Chronicles1:38 The sons of Esau also included Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah, Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan; and Esau's daughter was named Timna." 
1Chronicles1:39 Lotan's sons: Hori and Homam. 
1Chronicles1:40 The sons of Shobal: Alian, Manahath, Ebal, Shephi, and Onam. Zibeon's sons were Aiah and Anah." 
1Chronicles1:41 Anah's son was Dishon. The sons of Dishon: Hamran, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran." 
1Chronicles1:42 The sons of Ezer: Bilhan, Zaavan, and Jaakan. Dishan's sons were Uz and Aran." 
1Chronicles1:43 Here is a list of the names of the kings of Edom who reigned before the kingdom of Israel began: Bela (the son of Beor), who lived in the city of Dinhabah." 
1Chronicles1:44 When Bela died, Jobab the son of Zerah from Bozrah became the new king." 
1Chronicles1:44 When Bela died, Jobab the son of Zerah from Bozrah became the new king." 
1Chronicles1:45 When Jobab died, Husham from the country of the Temanites became the king." 
1Chronicles1:45 When Jobab died, Husham from the country of the Temanites became the king." 
1Chronicles1:46 When Husham died, Hadad the son of Bedad—the one who destroyed the army of Midian in the fields of Moab—became king and ruled from the city of Avith." 
1Chronicles1:47 When Hadad died, Samlah from the city of Masrekah came to the throne." 
1Chronicles1:48 When Samlah died, Shaul from the river town of Rehoboth became the new king." 
1Chronicles1:49 When Shaul died, Baal-hanan the son of Achbor became king." 
1Chronicles1:50 When Baal-hanan died, Hadad became king and ruled from the city of Pai (his wife was Mehetabel, the daughter of Matred and granddaughter of Mezahab)." 
1Chronicles1:51 At the time of Hadad's death, the kings of Edom were: Chief Timna, Chief Aliah, Chief Jetheth," 
1Chronicles1:52 Chief Oholibamah, Chief Elah, Chief Pinon," 
1Chronicles1:53 Chief Kenaz, Chief Teman, Chief Mibzar," 
1Chronicles1:54 Chief Magdiel, Chief Iram." 
1Chronicles2:1 The sons of Israel were: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, Zebulun," 
1Chronicles2:2 Dan, Joseph, Benjamin, Naphtali, Gad, Asher." 
1Chronicles2:3 Judah had three sons by Bathshua, a girl from Canaan: Er, Onan, and Shelah. But the oldest son, Er, was so wicked that the Lord killed him." 
1Chronicles2:4 Then Er's widow, Tamar, and her father-in-law, Judah, became the parents of twin sons, Perez and Zerah. So Judah had five sons." 
1Chronicles2:5 The sons of Perez were Hezron and Hamul. 
1Chronicles2:6 The sons of Zerah were: Zimri, Ethan, Heman, Calcol, and Dara." 
1Chronicles2:7 (Achan, the son of Carmi, was the man who robbed God and was such a troublemaker for his nation.)" 
1Chronicles2:8 Ethan's son was Azariah. 
1Chronicles2:9 The sons of Hezron were Jerahmeel, Ram, and Chelubai." 
1Chronicles2:10 Ram was the father of Amminadab, and Amminadab was the father of Nahshon, a leader of Israel." 
1Chronicles2:11 Nahshon was the father of Salma, and Salma was the father of Boaz." 
1Chronicles2:12 Boaz was the father of Obed, and Obed was the father of Jesse." 
1Chronicles2:13 Jesse's first son was Eliab, his second was Abinadab, his third was Shimea," 
1Chronicles2:14 his fourth was Nethanel, his fifth was Raddai," 
1Chronicles2:15 his sixth was Ozem, and his seventh was David." 
1Chronicles2:16 He also had two girls (by the same wife) named Zeruiah and Abigail. Zeruiah's sons were Abishai, Joab, and Asahel." 
1Chronicles2:17 Abigail, whose husband was Jether from the land of Ishmael, had a son named Amasa." 
1Chronicles2:18 Caleb (the son of Hezron) had two wives, Azubah and Jerioth. These are the children of Azubah: Jesher, Shobab, and Ardon." 
1Chronicles2:19 After Azubah's death, Caleb married Ephrath, who presented him with a son, Hur." 
1Chronicles2:20 Hur's son was Uri, and Uri's son was Bezalel." 
1Chronicles2:21 Hezron married Machir's daughter at the age of sixty, and she presented him with a son, Segub. (Machir was also the father of Gilead.)" 
1Chronicles2:22 Segub was the father of Jair, who ruled twenty-three cities in the land of Gilead." 
1Chronicles2:23 But Geshur and Aram wrested these cities from him and also took Kenath and its sixty surrounding villages. 
1Chronicles2:24 Soon after his father Hezron's death, Caleb married Ephrathah, his father's widow, and she gave birth to Ashhur, the father of Tekoa." 
1Chronicles2:25 These are the sons of Jerahmeel (the oldest son of Hezron): Ram (the oldest), Bunah, Oren, Ozem, and Ahijah." 
1Chronicles2:26 Jerahmeel's second wife Atarah was the mother of Onam. 
1Chronicles2:27 The sons of Ram: Maaz, Jamin, and Eker." 
1Chronicles2:28 Onam's sons were Shammai and Jada. Shammai's sons were Nadab and Abishur. 
1Chronicles2:29 The sons of Abishur and his wife Abihail were Ahban and Molid. 
1Chronicles2:30 Nadab's sons were Seled and Appaim. Seled died without children," 
1Chronicles2:31 but Appaim had a son named Ishi; Ishi's son was Sheshan; and Sheshan's son was Ahlai. 
1Chronicles2:32 Shammai's brother Jada had two sons, Jether and Jonathan. Jether died without children," 
1Chronicles2:33 but Jonathan had two sons named Peleth and Zaza. 
1Chronicles2:34 Sheshan had no sons, although he had several daughters." 
1Chronicles2:35 He gave one of his daughters to be the wife of Jarha, his Egyptian servant. And they had a son whom they named Attai." 
1Chronicles2:36 Attai's son was Nathan; Nathan's son was Zabad; 
1Chronicles2:37 Zabad's son was Ephlal; Ephlal's son was Obed; 
1Chronicles2:38 Obed's son was Jehu; Jehu's son was Azariah; 
1Chronicles2:39 Azariah's son was Helez; Helez's son was Eleasah; 
1Chronicles2:40 Eleasah's son was Sismai; Sismai's son was Shallum; 
1Chronicles2:41 Shallum's son was Jekamiah; Jekamiah's son was Elishama. 
1Chronicles2:42 The oldest son of Caleb (Jerahmeel's brother) was Mesha; he was the father of Ziph, who was father of Mareshah, who was the father of Hebron." 
1Chronicles2:43 The sons of Hebron: Korah, Tappuah, Rekem, and Shema." 
1Chronicles2:44 Shema was the father of Raham, who was the father of Jorkeam. Rekem was the father of Shammai." 
1Chronicles2:45 Shammai's son was Maon, the father of Bethzur." 
1Chronicles2:46 Caleb's concubine Ephah bore him Haran, Moza, and Gazez; Haran had a son named Gazez." 
1Chronicles2:47 The sons of Jahdai: Regem, Jotham, Geshan, Pelet, Ephah, and Shaaph." 
1Chronicles2:48 Another of Caleb's concubines, Maacah, bore him Sheber, Tirhanah," 
1Chronicles2:49 Shaaph (the father of Madmannah), and Sheva (the father of Machbenah and of Gibea). Caleb also had a daughter, whose name was Achsah." 
1Chronicles2:50 The sons of Hur (who was the oldest son of Caleb and Ephrathah) were Shobal (the father of Kiriath-jearim)," 
1Chronicles2:51 Salma (the father of Bethlehem), and Hareph (the father of Beth-gader)." 
1Chronicles2:52 Shobal's sons included Kiriath-jearim and Haroeh, the ancestor of half of the Menuhoth tribe." 
1Chronicles2:53 The families of Kiriath-jearim were the Ithrites, the Puthites, the Shumathites, and the Mishraites (from whom descended the Zorathites and Eshtaolites)." 
1Chronicles2:54 The descendants of Salma were his son Bethlehem, the Netophathites, Atrothbeth-joab, half the Manahathites, and the Zorites;" 
1Chronicles2:55 they also included the families of the writers living at Jabez—the Tirathites, Shimeathites, and Sucathites. All these are Kenites who descended from Hammath, the founder of the family of Rechab."

1Chronicles4:1 These are the sons of Judah: Perez, Hezron, Carmi, Hur, Shobal." 
1Chronicles4:2 Shobal's son Reaiah was the father of Jahath, the ancestor of Ahumai and Lahad. These were known as the Zorathite clans." 
1Chronicles4:3 The descendants of Etam: Jezreel, Ishma, Idbash, Hazzelelponi (his daughter)," 
1Chronicles4:4 Penuel (the ancestor of Gedor), Ezer (the ancestor of Hushah), The son of Hur, the oldest son of Ephrathah, who was the father of Bethlehem." 
1Chronicles4:5 Ashhur, the father of Tekoa, had two wives—Helah, and Naarah." 
1Chronicles4:6 Naarah bore him Ahuzzam, Hepher, Temeni, and Haahashtari;" 
1Chronicles4:7 and Helah bore him Zereth, Izhar, and Ethnan." 
1Chronicles4:8 Koz was the father of Anub and Zobebah; he was also the ancestor of the clan named after Aharhel, the son of Harum." 
1Chronicles4:9 Jabez was more distinguished than any of his brothers. His mother named him Jabez because she had such a hard time at his birth (Jabez means “Distress“). 
1Chronicles4:10 He was the one who prayed to the God of Israel, “Oh, that you would wonderfully bless me and help me in my work; please be with me in all that I do, and keep me from all evil and disaster!“ And God granted him his request." 
1Chronicles4:11 The descendants of Recah were: Chelub (the brother of Shuhah), whose son was Mahir, the father of Eshton;" 
1Chronicles4:12 Eshton was the father of Bethrapha, Paseah, and Tehinnah; Tehinnah was the father of Irnahash." 
1Chronicles4:13 The sons of Kenaz were Othniel and Seraiah. Othniel's sons were Hathath and Meonothai; 
1Chronicles4:14 Meonothai was the father of Ophrah; Seraiah was the father of Joab, the ancestor of the inhabitants of Craftsman Valley (called that because many craftsmen lived there)." 
1Chronicles4:15 The sons of Caleb (the son of Jephunneh): Iru, Elah, Naam. The sons of Elah included Kenaz." 
1Chronicles4:16 Jehallelel's sons were: Ziph, Ziphah, Tiria, Asarel." 
1Chronicles4:17 Ezrah's sons were: Jether, Mered, Epher, Jalon. Mered married Bithiah, an Egyptian princess. She was the mother of Miriam, Shammai, and Ishbah—an ancestor of Eshtemoa." 
1Chronicles4:17 Ezrah's sons were: Jether, Mered, Epher, Jalon. Mered married Bithiah, an Egyptian princess. She was the mother of Miriam, Shammai, and Ishbah—an ancestor of Eshtemoa." 
1Chronicles4:18 Eshtemoa's wife was a Jewess; she was the mother of Jered, Heber, and Jekuthiel, who were, respectively, the ancestors of the Gedorites, Socoites, and Zanoahites." 
1Chronicles4:19 Hodiah's wife was the sister of Naham. One of her sons was the father of Keilah the Garmite, and another was the father of Eshtemoa the Maacathite." 
1Chronicles4:20 The sons of Shimon: Amnon, Rinnah, Ben-hanan, Tilon. The sons of Ishi: Zoheth, Ben-zoheth." 
1Chronicles4:21 The sons of Shelah (the son of Judah): Er (the father of Lecah), Laadah (the father of Mareshah), The families of the linen workers who worked at Beth-ashbea," 
1Chronicles4:22 Jokim, The clans of Cozeba, Joash, Saraph (who was a ruler in Moab before he returned to Lehem). These names all come from very ancient records." 
1Chronicles4:23 These clans were noted for their pottery, gardening, and planting; they all worked for the king:" 
1Chronicles4:24 The sons of Simeon: Nemuel, Jamin, Jarib, Zerah, Shaul." 
1Chronicles4:25 Shaul's son was Shallum, his grandson was Mibsam, and his great-grandson was Mishma." 
1Chronicles4:26 Mishma's sons included Hammuel (the father of Zaccur and grandfather of Shimei). 
1Chronicles4:27 Shimei had sixteen sons and six daughters, but none of his brothers had large families—they all had fewer children than was normal in Judah." 
1Chronicles4:28 They lived at Beersheba, Moladah, Hazar-shual," 
1Chronicles4:29 Bilhah, Ezem, Tolad," 
1Chronicles4:30 Bethuel, Hormah, Ziklag," 
1Chronicles4:31 Beth-marcaboth, Hazar-susim, Beth-biri, and Shaaraim. These cities were under their control until the time of David." 
1Chronicles4:32 Their descendants also lived in or near Etam, Ain, Rimmon, Tochen, and Ashan; some were as far away as Baal." 
1Chronicles4:33 (These facts are recorded in their genealogies.) 
1Chronicles4:34 These are the names of some of the princes of 
1Chronicles4:35 wealthy clans who traveled to the east side of Gedor 
1Chronicles4:36 Valley in search of pasture for their flocks: Meshobab, Jamlech, Joshah," 
1Chronicles4:37 Joel, Jehu," 
1Chronicles4:37 Joel, Jehu," 
1Chronicles4:38 Elioenai, Jaakobah, Jeshohaiah, Asaiah, Adiel, Jesimiel, Benaiah," 
1Chronicles4:39 Ziza (the son of Shiphi, son of Allon, son of Jedaiah, son of Shimri, son of Shemaiah)." 
1Chronicles4:40 They found good pastures, and everything was quiet and peaceful; but the land belonged to the descendants of Ham." 
1Chronicles4:41 So during the reign of King Hezekiah of Judah these princes invaded the land and struck down the tents and houses of the descendants of Ham; they killed the inhabitants of the land and took possession of it for themselves. 
1Chronicles4:42 Later, five hundred of these invaders from the tribe of Simeon went to Mount Seir. (Their leaders were Pelatiah, Neariah, Rephaiah, and Uzziel—all sons of Ishi.)" 
1Chronicles4:43 There they destroyed the few surviving members of the tribe of Amalek. And they have lived there ever since. 
1Chronicles5:1 The oldest son of Israel was Reuben, but since he dishonored his father by sleeping with one of his father's wives, his birthright was given to his half brother, Joseph. So the official genealogy doesn't name Reuben as the oldest son." 
1Chronicles5:2 Although Joseph received the birthright, yet Judah was a powerful and influential tribe in Israel, and from Judah came a Prince." 
1Chronicles5:3 The sons of Reuben, Israel's son, were: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, Carmi." 
1Chronicles5:4 Joel's descendants were his son Shemaiah, his grandson Gog, and his great-grandson Shimei." 
1Chronicles5:4 Joel's descendants were his son Shemaiah, his grandson Gog, and his great-grandson Shimei." 
1Chronicles5:5 Shimei's son was Micah; his grandson was Reaiah; and his great-grandson was Baal. 
1Chronicles5:5 Shimei's son was Micah; his grandson was Reaiah; and his great-grandson was Baal. 
1Chronicles5:6 Baal's son was Beerah. He was a prince of the tribe of Reuben and was taken into captivity by King Tilgath-pilneser of Assyria. 
1Chronicles5:7 His relatives became heads of clans and were included in the official genealogy: Jeiel, Zechariah," 
1Chronicles5:7 His relatives became heads of clans and were included in the official genealogy: Jeiel, Zechariah," 
1Chronicles5:8 Bela (the son of Azaz, grandson of Shema, and great-grandson of Joel). These Reubenites lived in Aroer and as far distant as Mount Nebo and Baal-meon." 
1Chronicles5:8 Bela (the son of Azaz, grandson of Shema, and great-grandson of Joel). These Reubenites lived in Aroer and as far distant as Mount Nebo and Baal-meon." 
1Chronicles5:9 Joel was a cattleman, and he pastured his animals eastward to the edge of the desert and to the Euphrates River, for there were many cattle in the land of Gilead." 
1Chronicles5:9 Joel was a cattleman, and he pastured his animals eastward to the edge of the desert and to the Euphrates River, for there were many cattle in the land of Gilead." 
1Chronicles5:10 During the reign of King Saul, the men of Reuben defeated the Hagrites in war and moved into their tents on the eastern edge of Gilead." 
1Chronicles5:11 Across from them, in the land of Bashan, lived the descendants of Gad, who were spread as far as Salecah." 
1Chronicles5:12 Joel was the greatest and was followed by Shapham, also Janai and Shaphat." 
1Chronicles5:12 Joel was the greatest and was followed by Shapham, also Janai and Shaphat." 
1Chronicles5:13 Their relatives, the heads of the seven clans, were Michael, Meshullam, Sheba, Jorai, Jacan, Zia, and Eber." 
1Chronicles5:14 The descendants of Buz, in the order of their generations, were: Jahdo, Jeshishai, Michael, Gilead, Jaroah, Huri, Abihail." 
1Chronicles5:15 Ahi, the son of Abdiel and grandson of Guni, was the leader of the clan." 
1Chronicles5:16 The clan lived in and around Gilead (in the land of Bashan) and throughout the entire pasture country of Sharon. 
1Chronicles5:17 All were included in the official genealogy at the time of King Jotham of Judah and King Jeroboam of Israel. 
1Chronicles5:18 There were 44,760 armed, trained, and brave troops in the army of Reuben, Gad, and the half-tribe of Manasseh." 
1Chronicles5:19 They declared war on the Hagrites, the Jeturites, the Naphishites, and the Nodabites." 
1Chronicles5:20 They cried out to God to help them, and he did, for they trusted in him. So the Hagrites and all their allies were defeated." 
1Chronicles5:21 The booty included 50,000 camels, 250,000 sheep, 2,000 donkeys, and 100,000 captives." 
1Chronicles5:22 A great number of the enemy also died in the battle, for God was fighting against them. So the Reubenites lived in the territory of the Hagrites until the time of the exile." 
1Chronicles5:23 The half-tribe of Manasseh spread through the land from Bashan to Baal-hermon, Senir, and Mount Hermon. They too were very numerous." 
1Chronicles5:24 The chiefs of their clans were the following: Epher, Ishi, Eliel, Azriel, Jeremiah, Hodaviah, Jahdiel. Each of these men had a great reputation as a warrior and leader." 
1Chronicles5:24 The chiefs of their clans were the following: Epher, Ishi, Eliel, Azriel, Jeremiah, Hodaviah, Jahdiel. Each of these men had a great reputation as a warrior and leader." 
1Chronicles5:25 But they were not true to the God of their fathers; instead they worshiped the idols of the people whom God had destroyed. 
1Chronicles5:26 So God caused King Pul of Assyria (also known as Tilgath-pilneser III) to invade the land and deport the men of Reuben, Gad, and the half-tribe of Manasseh. They took them to Halah, Habor, Hara, and the Gozan River, where they remain to this day."

1Chronicles6:49 But only Aaron and his descendants were the priests. 1Chronicles7:1 The sons of Issachar: Tola, Puah, Jashub, Shimron." 
1Chronicles7:2 The sons of Tola, each of whom was the head of a subclan: Uzzi, Rephaiah, Jeriel, Jahmai, Ibsam, Shemuel. At the time of King David, the total number of men of war from these families totaled 22,600." 
1Chronicles7:3 Uzzi's son was Izrahiah among whose five sons were Michael, Obadiah, Joel, and Isshiah, all chiefs of subclans." 
1Chronicles7:3 Uzzi's son was Izrahiah among whose five sons were Michael, Obadiah, Joel, and Isshiah, all chiefs of subclans."

1Chronicles7:4 Their descendants, at the time of King David, numbered 36,000 troops; for all five of them had several wives and many sons." 
1Chronicles7:5 The total number of men available for military service from all the clans of the tribe of Issachar numbered 87,000 stouthearted warriors, all included in the official genealogy." 
1Chronicles7:6 The sons of Benjamin were: Bela, Becher, Jediael." 
1Chronicles7:7 The sons of Bela: Ezbon, Uzzi, Uzziel, Jerimoth, Iri. These five mighty warriors were chiefs of subclans and were the leaders of 22,034 troops (all of whom were recorded in the official genealogies)." 
1Chronicles7:8 The sons of Becher were: Zemirah, Joash, Eliezer, Elioenai, Omri, Jeremoth, Abijah, Anathoth, Alemeth." 
1Chronicles7:9 At the time of David there were 20,200 mighty warriors among their descendants; and they were led by their clan chiefs." 
1Chronicles7:10 The son of Jediael was Bilhan. The sons of Bilhan were: Jeush, Benjamin, Ehud, Chenaanah, Zethan, Tarshish, Ahishahar." 
1Chronicles7:11 They were the chiefs of the subclans of Jediael, and their descendants included 17,200 warriors at the time of King David." 
1Chronicles7:12 The sons of Ir were Shuppim and Huppim. Hushim was one of the sons of Aher. 
1Chronicles7:13 The sons of Naphtali (descendants of Jacob's wife Bilhah) were: Jahziel, Guni, Jezer, Shallum." 
1Chronicles7:14 The sons of Manasseh, born to his Aramaean concubine, were Asriel and Machir (who became the father of Gilead)." 
1Chronicles7:15 It was Machir who found wives for Huppim and Shuppim. Machir's sister was Maacah. Another descendant was Zelophehad, who had only daughters." 
1Chronicles7:16 Machir's wife, also named Maacah, bore him a son whom she named Peresh; his brother's name was Sheresh, and he had sons named Ulam and Rakem." 
1Chronicles7:17 Ulam's son was Bedan. So these were the sons of Gilead, the grandsons of Machir, and the great-grandsons of Manasseh." 
1Chronicles7:18 Hammolecheth, Machir's sister, bore Ishhod, Abiezer, and Mahlah." 
1Chronicles7:19 The sons of Shemida were Ahian, Shechem, Likhi, and Aniam."

1Chronicles7:21 Zabad, Shuthelah, Ezer, Elead. Elead and Ezer attempted to rustle cattle at Gath, but they were killed by the local farmers." 
1Chronicles7:22 Their father Ephraim mourned for them a long time, and his brothers tried to comfort him." 
1Chronicles7:23 Afterwards his wife conceived and bore a son whom he called Beriah (meaning “a tragedy“) because of what had happened. 
1Chronicles7:24 Ephraim's daughter's name was Sheerah. She built Lower and Upper Beth-horon and Uzzen-sheerah.
