Job26:14 “These are some of the minor things he does, merely a whisper of his power. Who then can withstand his thunder?“" 
Job27:1 Job's final defense: 
Job27:2 “I vow by the living God, who has taken away my rights, even the Almighty God who has embittered my soul," 
Job27:3 that as long as I live, while I have breath from God," 
Job27:4 my lips shall speak no evil, my tongue shall speak no lies." 
Job27:5 I will never, never agree that you are right; until I die I will vow my innocence." 
Job27:6 I am not a sinner—I repeat it again and again. My conscience is clear for as long as I live. 
Job27:7 Those who declare otherwise are my wicked enemies. They are evil men. 
Job27:8 “But what hope has the godless when God cuts him off and takes away his life? 
Job27:9 Will God listen to his cry when trouble comes upon him? 
Job27:10 For he does not delight himself in the Almighty or pay any attention to God except in times of crisis. 
Job27:11 “I will teach you about God— 
Job27:12 but really, I don't need to, for you yourselves know as much about him as I do; yet you are saying all these useless things to me." 
Job27:13 “This is the fate awaiting the wicked from the hand of the Almighty: 
Job27:14 If he has a multitude of children, it is so that they will die in war or starve to death." 
Job27:15 Those who survive shall be brought down to the grave by disease and plague, with no one to mourn them, not even their wives." 
Job27:16 “The evil man may accumulate money like dust, with closets jammed full of clothing—" 
Job27:17 yes, he may order them made by his tailor, but the innocent shall wear that clothing and shall divide his silver among them." 
Job27:18 Every house built by the wicked is as fragile as a spider web, as full of cracks as a leafy booth!" 
Job27:19 “He goes to bed rich but wakes up to find that all his wealth is gone. 
Job27:20 Terror overwhelms him, and he is blown away in the storms of the night." 
Job27:21 The east wind carries him away, and he is gone. It sweeps him into eternity." 
Job27:22 For God shall hurl at him unsparingly. He longs to flee from God. 
Job27:23 Everyone will cheer at his death and boo him into eternity. 
Job28:1 “Men know how to mine silver and refine gold," 
Job28:2 to dig iron from the earth and melt copper from stone. 
Job28:3 Men know how to put light into darkness so that a mine shaft can be sunk into the earth, and the earth searched and its deep secrets explored." 
Job28:4 Into the black rock, shadowed by death, men descend on ropes, swinging back and forth." 
Job28:5 “Men know how to obtain food from the surface of the earth, while underneath there is fire." 
Job28:6 “They know how to find sapphires and gold dust— 
Job28:7 treasures that no bird of prey can see, no eagle's eye observe—" 
Job28:8 for they are deep within the mines. No wild animal has ever walked upon those treasures; no lion has set his paw there. 
Job28:9 Men know how to tear apart flinty rocks and how to overturn the roots of mountains. 
Job28:10 They drill tunnels in the rocks and lay bare precious stones. 
Job28:11 They dam up streams of water and pan the gold. 
Job28:12 “But though men can do all these things, they don't know where to find wisdom and understanding." 
Job28:13 They not only don't know how to get it, but, in fact, it is not to be found among the living." 
Job28:14 'It's not here,' the oceans say; and the seas reply, 'Nor is it here.'" 
Job28:15 “It cannot be bought for gold or silver," 
Job28:16 nor for all the gold of Ophir or precious onyx stones or sapphires. 
Job28:17 Wisdom is far more valuable than gold and glass. It cannot be bought for jewels mounted in fine gold. 
Job28:18 Coral or crystal is worthless in trying to get it; its price is far above rubies. 
Job28:19 Topaz from Ethiopia cannot purchase it, nor even the purest gold." 
Job28:20 “Then where can we get it? Where can it be found? 
Job28:21 For it is hid from the eyes of all mankind; even the sharp-eyed birds in the sky cannot discover it. 
Job28:22 “But Destruction and Death speak of knowing something about it! 
Job28:23 And God surely knows where it is to be found," 
Job28:24 for he looks throughout the whole earth, under all the heavens." 
Job28:25 He makes the winds blow and sets the boundaries of the oceans. 
Job28:26 He makes the laws of the rain and a path for the lightning. 
Job28:27 He knows where wisdom is and declares it to all who will listen. He established it and examined it thoroughly. 
Job28:28 And this is what he says to all mankind: 'Look, to fear the Lord is true wisdom; to forsake evil is real understanding.' “" 
Job29:1 Job continues: 
Job29:2 “Oh, for the years gone by when God took care of me," 
Job29:3 when he lighted the way before me and I walked safely through the darkness; 
Job29:4 yes, in my early years, when the friendship of God was felt in my home;" 
Job29:5 when the Almighty was still with me and my children were around me; 
Job29:6 when my projects prospered and even the rock poured out streams of olive oil to me! 
Job29:7 “Those were the days when I went out to the city gate and took my place among the honored elders. 
Job29:8 The young saw me and stepped aside, and even the aged rose and stood up in respect at my coming." 
Job29:9 The princes stood in silence and laid their hands upon their mouths. 
Job29:10 The highest officials of the city stood in quietness. 
Job29:11 All rejoiced in what I said. All who saw me spoke well of me. 
Job29:12 “For I, as an honest judge, helped the poor in their need and the fatherless who had no one to help them." 
Job29:13 I helped those who were ready to perish, and they blessed me. And I caused the widows' hearts to sing for joy." 
Job29:14 All I did was just and honest, for righteousness was my clothing!" 
Job29:15 I served as eyes for the blind and feet for the lame. 
Job29:16 I was as a father to the poor and saw to it that even strangers received a fair trial. 
Job29:17 I knocked out the fangs of the godless oppressors and made them drop their victims. 
Job29:18 “I thought, 'Surely I shall die quietly in my nest after a long, good life.'" 
Job29:19 For everything I did prospered; the dew lay all night upon my fields and watered them. 
Job29:20 Fresh honors were constantly given me, and my abilities were constantly refreshed and renewed." 
Job29:21 Everyone listened to me and valued my advice, and were silent until I spoke." 
Job29:22 And after I spoke, they spoke no more, for my counsel satisfied them." 
Job29:23 They longed for me to speak as those in drought-time long for rain. They waited eagerly with open mouths. 
Job29:24 When they were discouraged, I smiled and that encouraged them and lightened their spirits." 
Job29:25 I told them what they should do and corrected them as their chief, or as a king instructs his army, and as one who comforts those who mourn." 
Job30:1 “But now those younger than I deride me—young men whose fathers are less than my dogs. 
Job30:2 Oh, they have strong backs all right, but they are useless, stupid fools." 
Job30:3 They are gaunt with famine and have been cast out into deserts and the wastelands, desolate and gloomy." 
Job30:4 They eat roots and leaves," 
Job30:5 having been driven from civilization. Men shouted after them as after thieves. 
Job30:6 So now they live in frightening ravines, and in caves, and among the rocks." 
Job30:7 They sound like animals among the bushes, huddling together for shelter beneath the nettles." 
Job30:8 These sons of theirs have also turned out to be fools, yes, children of no name, outcasts of civilization." 
Job30:9 “And now I have become the subject of their ribald song! I am a joke among them! 
Job30:10 They despise me and won't come near me, and don't mind spitting in my face." 
Job30:11 For God has placed my life in jeopardy. These young men, having humbled me, now cast off all restraint before me." 
Job30:12 This rabble trip me and lay traps in my path. 
Job30:13 They block my road and do everything they can to hasten my calamity, knowing full well that I have no one to help me." 
Job30:14 They come at me from all directions. They rush upon me when I am down. 
Job30:15 “I live in terror now. They hold me in contempt, and my prosperity has vanished as a cloud before a strong wind." 
Job30:16 My heart is broken. Depression haunts my days. 
Job30:17 My weary nights are filled with pain as though something were relentlessly gnawing at my bones. 
Job30:18 All night long I toss and turn, and my garments bind about me." 
Job30:19 God has thrown me into the mud. I have become as dust and ashes. 
Job30:20 “I cry to you, O God, but you don't answer me. I stand before you and you don't bother to look." 
Job30:21 You have become cruel toward me and persecute me with great power and effect. 
Job30:22 You throw me into the whirlwind and dissolve me in the storm. 
Job30:23 And I know that your purpose for me is death. 
Job30:24 I expected my fall to be broken, just as one who falls stretches out his hand or cries for help in his calamity." 
Job30:25 “And did I not weep for those in trouble? Wasn't I deeply grieved for the needy? 
Job30:26 I therefore looked for good to come. Evil came instead. I waited for the light. Darkness came. 
Job30:27 My heart is troubled and restless. Waves of affliction have come upon me. 
Job30:28 I am black but not from sunburn. I stand up and cry to the assembly for help. But I might as well save my breath," 
Job30:29 for I am considered a brother to jackals and a companion to ostriches. 
Job30:30 My skin is black and peeling. My bones burn with fever. 
Job30:31 The voice of joy and gladness has turned to mourning. 
Job31:1 “I made a covenant with my eyes not to look with lust upon a girl. 
Job31:2 I know full well that Almighty God 
Job31:3 above sends calamity on those who do. 
Job31:4 He sees everything I do and every step I take. 
Job31:5 “If I have lied and deceived— 
Job31:6 but God knows that I am innocent— 
Job31:7 or if I have stepped off God's pathway, or if my heart has lusted for what my eyes have seen, or if I am guilty of any other sin," 
Job31:8 then let someone else reap the crops I have sown and let all that I have planted be rooted out. 
Job31:9 “Or if I have longed for another man's wife," 
Job31:10 then may I die, and may my wife be in another man's home and someone else become her husband." 
Job31:11 For lust is a shameful sin, a crime that should be punished." 
Job31:12 It is a devastating fire that destroys to hell and would root out all I have planted. 
Job31:13 “If I have been unfair to my servants," 
Job31:14 how could I face God? What could I say when he questioned me about it? 
Job31:15 For God made me and made my servant too. He created us both. 
Job31:16 “If I have hurt the poor, or caused widows to weep," 
Job31:17 or refused food to hungry orphans— 
Job31:18 (but we have always cared for orphans in our home, treating them as our own children)—" 
Job31:19 or if I have seen anyone freezing and not given 
Job31:20 him clothing or fleece from my sheep to keep him warm," 
Job31:21 or if I have taken advantage of an orphan because I thought I could get away with it— 
Job31:22 if I have done any of these things, then let my arm be torn from its socket! Let my shoulder be wrenched out of place!" 
Job31:23 Rather that than face the judgment sent by God; that I dread more than anything else. For if the majesty of God opposes me, what hope is there?" 
Job31:24 “If I have put my trust in money," 
Job31:25 if my happiness depends on wealth," 
Job31:26 or if I have looked at the sun shining in the skies or the moon walking down her silver pathway 
Job31:27 and my heart has been secretly enticed, and I have worshiped them by kissing my hand to them," 
Job31:28 this, too, must be punished by the judges. For if I had done such things, it would mean that I denied the God of heaven." 
Job31:29 “If I have rejoiced at harm to an enemy— 
Job31:30 (but actually I have never cursed anyone nor asked for revenge)— 
Job31:31 or if any of my servants have ever gone hungry— 
Job31:32 (actually I have never turned away even a stranger but have opened my doors to all)— 
Job31:33 or if, like Adam, I have tried to hide my sins," 
Job31:34 fearing the crowd and its contempt so that I refused to acknowledge my sin and do not go out of my way to help others— 
Job31:35 (oh, that there were someone who would listen to me and try to see my side of this argument. Look, I will sign my signature to my defense; now let the Almighty show me that I am wrong; let him approve the indictments made against me by my enemies." 
Job31:36 I would treasure it like a crown. 
Job31:37 Then I would tell him exactly what I have done and why, presenting my defense as one he listens to)." 
Job31:38 “Or if my land accuses me because I stole the fruit it bears," 
Job31:39 or if I have murdered its owners to get their land for myself," 
Job31:40 then let thistles grow on that land instead of wheat, and weeds instead of barley.“ Job's words are ended." 
Job32:1 The three men refused to reply further to Job because he kept insisting on his innocence. 
Job32:2 Then Elihu (son of Barachel, the Buzite, of the Clan of Ram) became angry because Job refused to admit he had sinned and to acknowledge that God had just cause for punishing him." 
Job32:3 But he was also angry with Job's three friends because they had been unable to answer Job's arguments and yet had condemned him. 
Job32:4 Elihu had waited until now to speak because the others were older than he. 
Job32:5 But when he saw that they had no further reply, he spoke out angrily," 
Job32:6 and said, “I am young and you are old, so I held back and did not dare to tell you what I think," 
Job32:7 for those who are older are said to be wiser; 
Job32:8 but it is not mere age that makes men wise. 
Job32:9 Rather, it is the spirit in a man, the breath of the Almighty that makes him intelligent." 
Job32:10 So listen to me awhile and let me express my opinion. 
Job32:11 “I have waited all this time, listening very carefully to your arguments, but not one" 
Job32:12 of them has convinced Job that he is a sinner or has proved that he is. 
Job32:13 And don't give me that line about 'only God can convince the sinner of his sin.' 
Job32:14 If Job had been arguing with me, I would not answer with that kind of logic!" 
Job32:15 “You sit there baffled, with no further replies." 
Job32:16 Shall I then continue to wait when you are silent? 
Job32:17 No, I will give my answer too." 
Job32:18 For I am pent up and full of words, and the spirit within me urges me on." 
Job32:19 I am like a wine cask without a vent! My words are ready to burst out! 
Job32:20 I must speak to find relief, so let me give my answers." 
Job32:21 Don't insist that I be cautious lest I insult someone, and don't make me flatter anyone." 
Job32:22 Let me be frank lest God should strike me dead. 
Job33:1 “Please listen, Job, to what I have to say." 
Job33:2 I have begun to speak; now let me continue. 
Job33:3 I will speak the truth with all sincerity. 
Job33:4 For the Spirit of God has made me, and the breath of the Almighty gives me life." 
Job33:5 Don't hesitate to answer me if you can. 
Job33:6 “Look, I am the one you were wishing for, someone to stand between you and God and to be both his representative and yours." 
Job33:7 You need not be frightened of me. I am not some person of renown to make you nervous and afraid. I, too, am made of common clay." 
Job33:8 “You have said it in my hearing, yes, you've said it again and again—" 
Job33:9 'I am pure, I am innocent; I have not sinned.'" 
Job33:10 You say God is using a fine-toothed comb to try to find a single fault, and so to count you as his enemy." 
Job33:11 'And he puts my feet in the stocks,' you say, 'and watches every move I make.'" 
Job33:12 “All right, here is my reply: In this very thing, you have sinned by speaking of God that way. For God is greater than man." 
Job33:13 Why should you fight against him just because he does not give account to you of what he does? 
Job33:14 “For God speaks again and again," 
Job33:15 in dreams, in visions of the night when deep sleep falls on men as they lie on their beds." 
Job33:16 He opens their ears in times like that and gives them wisdom and instruction," 
Job33:17 causing them to change their minds, and keeping them from pride, and warning" 
Job33:18 them of the penalties of sin, and keeping them from falling into some trap." 
Job33:19 “Or God sends sickness and pain, even though no bone is broken," 
Job33:20 so that a man loses all taste and appetite for food and doesn't care for even the daintiest dessert. 
Job33:21 He becomes thin, mere skin and bones," 
Job33:22 and draws near to death. 
Job33:23 “But if a messenger from heaven is there to intercede for him as a friend, to show him what is right," 
Job33:24 then God pities him and says, 'Set him free. Do not make him die, for I have found a substitute.'" 
Job33:25 Then his body will become as healthy as a child's, firm and youthful again." 
Job33:26 And when he prays to God, God will hear and answer and receive him with joy, and return him to his duties." 
Job33:27 And he will declare to his friends, 'I sinned, but God let me go." 
Job33:28 He did not let me die. I will go on living in the realm of light.' 
Job33:29 “Yes, God often does these things for man—" 
Job33:30 brings back his soul from the pit, so that he may live in the light of the living." 
Job33:31 Mark this well, O Job. Listen to me, and let me say more." 
Job33:32 But if you have anything to say at this point, go ahead. I want to hear it, for I am anxious to justify you." 
Job33:33 But if not, then listen to me. Keep silence and I will teach you wisdom!“" 
Job34:1 Elihu continued: 
Job34:2 “Listen to me, you wise men." 
Job34:3 We can choose the sounds we want to listen to; we can choose the taste we want in food," 
Job34:4 and we should choose to follow what is right. But first of all we must define among ourselves what is good. 
Job34:5 For Job has said, 'I am innocent, but God says I'm not." 
Job34:6 I am called a liar, even though I am innocent. I am horribly punished, even though I have not sinned.'" 
Job34:7 “Who else is as arrogant as Job? 
Job34:8 He must have spent much time with evil men," 
Job34:9 for he said, 'Why waste time trying to please God?'" 
Job34:10 “Listen to me, you with understanding. Surely everyone knows that God doesn't sin!" 
Job34:11 Rather, he punishes the sinners." 
Job34:12 There is no truer statement than this: God is never wicked or unjust. 
Job34:13 He alone has authority over the earth and dispenses justice for the world. 
Job34:14 If God were to withdraw his Spirit," 
Job34:15 all life would disappear and mankind would turn again to dust. 
Job34:16 “Listen now and try to understand. 
Job34:17 Could God govern if he hated justice? Are you going to condemn the Almighty Judge? 
Job34:18 Are you going to condemn this God who says to kings and nobles, 'You are wicked and unjust'?" 
Job34:19 For he doesn't care how great a man may be, and doesn't pay any more attention to the rich than to the poor. He made them all." 
Job34:20 In a moment they die, and at midnight great and small shall suddenly pass away, removed by no human hand." 
Job34:21 “For God carefully watches the goings on of all mankind; he sees them all. 
Job34:22 No darkness is thick enough to hide evil men from his eyes," 
Job34:23 so there is no need to wait for some great crime before a man is called before God in judgment. 
Job34:24 Without making a big issue over it, God simply shatters the greatest of men and puts others in their places." 
Job34:25 He watches what they do and in a single night he overturns them, destroying them," 
Job34:26 or openly strikes them down as wicked men. 
Job34:27 For they turned aside from following him," 
Job34:28 causing the cry of the poor to come to the attention of God. Yes, he hears the cries of those being oppressed." 
Job34:29 Yet when he chooses not to speak, who can criticize? Again," 
Job34:30 he may prevent a vile man from ruling, thus saving a nation from ruin, and he can depose an entire nation just as easily." 
Job34:31 “Why don't people exclaim to their God, 'We have sinned, but we will stop'?" 
Job34:32 Or, 'We know not what evil we have done; only tell us, and we will cease at once.'" 
Job34:33 “Must God tailor his justice to your demands? Must he change the order of the universe to suit your whims? The answer must be obvious even to you! 
Job34:34 Anyone even half bright will agree with me 
Job34:35 that you, Job, are speaking like a fool." 
Job34:36 You should be given the maximum penalty for the wicked way you have talked about God. 
Job34:37 For now you have added rebellion, arrogance, and blasphemy to your other sins.“" 
Job35:1 Elihu continued: 
Job35:2 “Do you think it is right for you to claim, 'I haven't" 
Job35:3 sinned, but I'm no better off before God than if I had'?" 
Job35:4 “I will answer you and all your friends too. 
Job35:5 Look up there into the sky, high above you." 
Job35:6 If you sin, does that shake the heavens and knock God from his throne? Even if you sin again and again, what effect will it have upon him?" 
Job35:7 Or if you are good, is this some great gift to him?" 
Job35:8 Your sins may hurt another man, or your good deeds may profit him." 
Job35:9 The oppressed may shriek beneath their wrongs and groan beneath the power of the rich; yet none 
Job35:10 of them cry to God, asking, 'Where is God my Maker who gives songs in the night" 
Job35:11 and makes us a little wiser than the animals and birds?' 
Job35:12 “But when anyone does cry out this question to him, he never replies by instant punishment of the tyrants." 
Job35:13 But it is false to say he doesn't hear those cries; 
Job35:14 and it is even more false to say that he doesn't see what is going on. He does bring about justice at last if you will only wait. 
Job35:15 But do you cry out against him because he does not instantly respond in anger? 
Job35:16 Job, you have spoken like a fool.“" 
Job36:1 Elihu continued: 
Job36:2 “Let me go on and I will show you the truth of what I am saying. For I have not finished defending God! 
Job36:3 I will give you many illustrations of the righteousness of my Maker. 
Job36:4 I am telling you the honest truth, for I am a man of well-rounded knowledge." 
Job36:5 “God is almighty and yet does not despise anyone! And he is perfect in his understanding. 
Job36:6 He does not reward the wicked with his blessings, but gives them their full share of punishment." 
Job36:7 He does not ignore the good men but honors them by placing them upon eternal, kingly thrones." 
Job36:8 If troubles come upon them and they are enslaved and afflicted," 
Job36:9 then he takes the trouble to point out to them the reason, what they have done that is wrong, or how they have behaved proudly." 
Job36:10 He helps them hear his instruction to turn away from their sin. 
Job36:11 “If they listen and obey him, then they will be blessed with prosperity throughout their lives." 
Job36:12 If they won't listen to him, they shall perish in battle and die because of their lack of good sense." 
Job36:13 But the godless reap his anger. They do not even return to him when he punishes them. 
Job36:14 They die young after lives of dissipation and depravity. 
Job36:15 He delivers by distress! This makes them listen to him! 
Job36:16 “How he wanted to lure you away from danger into a wide and pleasant valley and to prosper you there. 
Job36:17 But you are too preoccupied with your imagined grievances against others. 
Job36:18 Watch out! Don't let your anger at others lead you into scoffing at God! Don't let your suffering embitter you at the only one who can deliver you. 
Job36:19 Do you really think that if you shout loudly enough against God, he will be ashamed and repent? Will this put an end to your chastisement?" 
Job36:20 “Do not desire the nighttime, with its opportunities for crime." 
Job36:21 Turn back from evil, for it was to prevent you from getting into a life of evil that God sent this suffering." 
Job36:22 “Look, God is all-powerful. Who is a teacher like him?" 
Job36:23 Who can say that what he does is absurd or evil? 
Job36:24 Instead, glorify him for his mighty works for which he is so famous." 
Job36:25 Everyone has seen these things from a distance. 
Job36:26 “God is so great that we cannot begin to know him. No one can begin to understand eternity. 
Job36:27 He draws up the water vapor and then distills it into rain," 
Job36:28 which the skies pour down. 
Job36:29 Can anyone really understand the spreading of the clouds and the thunders within? 
Job36:30 See how he spreads the lightning around him, and blankets the tops of the mountains." 
Job36:31 By his fantastic powers in nature he punishes or blesses the people, giving them food in abundance." 
Job36:32 He fills his hands with lightning bolts. He hurls each at its target. 
Job36:33 We feel his presence in the thunder. Even the cattle know when a storm is coming. 
Job37:1 “My heart trembles at this. 
Job37:2 Listen, listen to the thunder of his voice." 
Job37:3 It rolls across the heavens and his lightning flashes out in every direction. 
Job37:4 Afterwards comes the roaring of the thunder—the tremendous voice of his majesty. 
Job37:5 His voice is glorious in the thunder. We cannot comprehend the greatness of his power. 
Job37:6 For he directs the snow, the showers, and storm to fall upon the earth." 
Job37:7 Man's work stops at such a time so that all men everywhere may recognize his power. 
Job37:8 The wild animals hide in the rocks or in their dens. 
Job37:9 “From the south comes the rain; from the north, the cold." 
Job37:10 God blows upon the rivers, and even the widest torrents freeze." 
Job37:11 He loads the clouds with moisture, and they send forth his lightning." 
Job37:12 The lightning bolts are directed by his hand and do whatever he commands throughout the earth. 
Job37:13 He sends the storms as punishment or, in his loving-kindness, to encourage." 
Job37:14 “Listen, O Job, stop and consider the wonderful miracles of God." 
Job37:15 Do you know how God controls all nature and causes the lightning to flash forth from the clouds? 
Job37:16 Do you understand the balancing of the clouds with wonderful perfection and skill? 
Job37:17 Do you know why you become warm when the south wind is blowing and everything is still? 
Job37:18 Can you spread out the gigantic mirror of the skies as he does? 
Job37:19 “You who think you know so much, teach the rest of us how we should approach God. For we are too dull to know!" 
Job37:20 With your wisdom, would we then dare to approach him? Well, does a man wish to be swallowed alive?" 
Job37:21 For as we cannot look at the sun for its brightness when the winds have cleared away the clouds," 
Job37:22 neither can we gaze at the terrible majesty of God breaking forth upon us from heaven, clothed in dazzling splendor." 
Job37:23 We cannot imagine the power of the Almighty, and yet he is so just and merciful that he does not destroy us." 
Job37:24 No wonder men everywhere fear him! For he is not impressed by the world's wisest men!“ 
Job38:1 Then the Lord answered Job from the whirlwind: 
Job38:2 “Why are you using your ignorance to deny my providence? 
Job38:3 Now get ready to fight, for I am going to demand some answers from you, and you must reply." 
Job38:4 “Where were you when I laid the foundations of the earth? Tell me, if you know so much." 
Job38:5 Do you know how its dimensions were determined, and who did the surveying?" 
Job38:6 What supports its foundations, and who laid its cornerstone as the" 
Job38:7 morning stars sang together and all the angels shouted for joy? 
Job38:8 “Who decreed the boundaries of the seas when they gushed from the depths? 
Job38:9 Who clothed them with clouds and thick darkness 
Job38:10 and barred them by limiting their shores," 
Job38:11 and said, 'Thus far and no farther shall you come, and here shall your proud waves stop!'?" 
Job38:12 “Have you ever once commanded the morning to appear and caused the dawn to rise in the east? 
Job38:13 Have you ever told the daylight to spread to the ends of the earth, to end the night's wickedness?" 
Job38:14 Have you ever robed the dawn in red," 
Job38:15 and disturbed the haunts of wicked men, and stopped the arm raised to strike?" 
Job38:16 “Have you explored the springs from which the seas come, or walked in the sources of their depths?" 
Job38:17 Has the location of the gates of Death been revealed to you? Do you realize the extent of the earth? 
Job38:18 Tell me about it if you know! 
Job38:19 Where does the light come from, and how do you get there? Or tell me about the darkness. Where does it come from?" 
Job38:20 Can you find its boundaries, or go to its source?" 
Job38:21 But of course you know all this! For you were born before it was all created, and you are so very experienced!" 
Job38:22 “Have you visited the treasuries of the snow, or seen where hail is made and stored?" 
Job38:23 For I have reserved it for the time when I will need it in war. 
Job38:24 Where is the path to the distribution point of light? Where is the home of the east wind? 
Job38:25 Who dug the valleys for the torrents of rain? Who laid out the path for the lightning," 
Job38:26 causing the rain to fall upon the barren deserts," 
Job38:27 so that the parched and barren ground is satisfied with water and tender grass springs up? 
Job38:28 “Has the rain a father? Where does dew come from? 
Job38:29 Who is the mother of the ice and frost? 
Job38:30 For the water changes and turns to ice as hard as rock. 
Job38:31 “Can you hold back the stars? Can you restrain Orion or Pleiades? 
Job38:32 Can you ensure the proper sequence of the seasons, or guide the constellation of the Bear with her satellites across the heavens?" 
Job38:33 Do you know the laws of the universe and how the heavens influence the earth? 
Job38:34 Can you shout to the clouds and make it rain? 
Job38:35 Can you make lightning appear and cause it to strike as you direct it? 
Job38:36 “Who gives intuition and instinct? 
Job38:37 Who is wise enough to number all the clouds? 
Job38:38 Who can tilt the water jars of heaven, when everything is dust and clods?" 
Job38:39 Can you stalk prey like a lioness, to satisfy the young lions' appetites" 
Job38:40 as they lie in their dens or lie in wait in the jungle? 
Job38:41 Who provides for the ravens when their young cry out to God as they try to struggle up from their nest in hunger? 
Job39:1 “Do you know how mountain goats give birth? Have you ever seen them giving birth to their young? 
Job39:2 Do you know how many months of pregnancy they have before they bow 
Job39:3 themselves to give birth to their young and carry their burden no longer? 
Job39:4 Their young grow up in the open field, then leave their parents and return to them no more." 
Job39:5 “Who makes the wild donkeys wild? 
Job39:6 I have placed them in the wilderness and given them salt plains to live in. 
Job39:7 For they hate the noise of the city and want no drivers shouting at them! 
Job39:8 The mountain ranges are their pastureland; there they search for every blade of grass. 
Job39:9 “Will the wild ox be your happy servant? Will he stay beside your feeding crib? 
Job39:10 Can you use a wild ox to plow with? Will he pull the harrow for you? 
Job39:11 Because he is so strong, will you trust him? Will you let him decide where to work?" 
Job39:12 Can you send him out to bring in the grain from the threshing-floor? 
Job39:13 “The ostrich flaps her wings grandly but has no true motherly love. 
Job39:14 She lays her eggs on top of the earth, to warm them in the dust." 
Job39:15 She forgets that someone may step on them and crush them, or the wild animals destroy them." 
Job39:16 She ignores her young as though they weren't her own and is unconcerned though they die," 
Job39:17 for God has deprived her of wisdom. 
Job39:18 But whenever she jumps up to run, she passes the swiftest horse with its rider." 
Job39:19 “Have you given the horse strength or clothed his neck with a quivering mane? 
Job39:20 Have you made him able to leap forward like a locust? His majestic snorting is something to hear! 
Job39:21 He paws the earth and rejoices in his strength, and when he goes to war," 
Job39:22 he is unafraid and does not run away 
Job39:23 though the arrows rattle against him, or the flashing spear and javelin." 
Job39:24 Fiercely he paws the ground and rushes forward into battle when the trumpet blows. 
Job39:25 At the sound of the bugle he shouts, 'Aha!' He smells the battle when far away. He rejoices at the shouts of battle and the roar of the captain's commands." 
Job39:26 “Do you know how a hawk soars and spreads her wings to the south? 
Job39:27 Is it at your command that the eagle rises high upon the cliffs to make her nest? 
Job39:28 She lives upon the cliffs, making her home in her mountain fortress." 
Job39:29 From there she spies her prey, from a very great distance." 
Job39:30 Her nestlings gulp down blood, for she goes wherever the slain are.“" 
Job40:1 The Lord went on: 
Job40:2 “Do you still want to argue with the Almighty? Or will you yield? Do you—God's critic—have the answers?“ 
Job40:3 Then Job replied to God: 
Job40:4 “I am nothing—how could I ever find the answers? I lay my hand upon my mouth in silence. 
Job40:5 I have said too much already.“ 
Job40:6 Then the Lord spoke to Job again from the whirlwind: 
Job40:7 “Stand up like a man and brace yourself for battle. Let me ask you a question, and give me the answer." 
Job40:8 Are you going to discredit my justice and condemn me so that you can say you are right? 
Job40:9 Are you as strong as God, and can you shout as loudly as he?" 
Job40:10 All right then, put on your robes of state, your majesty and splendor." 
Job40:11 Give vent to your anger. Let it overflow against the proud. 
Job40:12 Humiliate the haughty with a glance; tread down the wicked where they stand. 
Job40:13 Knock them into the dust, stone-faced in death." 
Job40:14 If you can do that, then I'll agree with you that your own strength can save you." 
Job40:15 “Take a look at the hippopotamus! I made him, too, just as I made you! He eats grass like an ox." 
Job40:16 See his powerful loins and the muscles of his belly. 
Job40:17 His tail is as straight as a cedar. The sinews of his thighs are tightly knit together. 
Job40:18 His vertebrae lie straight as a tube of brass. His ribs are like iron bars. 
Job40:19 How ferocious he is among all of God's creation, so let whoever hopes to master him bring a sharp sword!" 
Job40:20 The mountains offer their best food to him—the other wild animals on which he preys. 
Job40:21 He lies down under the lotus plants, hidden by the reeds," 
Job40:22 covered by their shade among the willows there beside the stream. 
Job40:23 He is not disturbed by raging rivers, not even when the swelling Jordan rushes down upon him." 
Job40:24 No one can catch him off guard or put a ring in his nose and lead him away. 
Job41:1 “Can you catch a crocodile with a hook and line? Or put a noose around his tongue? 
Job41:2 Can you tie him with a rope through the nose, or pierce his jaw with a spike?" 
Job41:3 Will he beg you to desist or try to flatter you from your intentions? 
Job41:4 Will he agree to let you make him your slave for life? 
Job41:5 Can you make a pet of him like a bird, or give him to your little girls to play with?" 
Job41:6 Do fishing partners sell him to the fishmongers? 
Job41:7 Will his hide be hurt by darts, or his head with a harpoon?" 
Job41:8 “If you lay your hands upon him, you will long remember the battle that ensues and you will never try it again!" 
Job41:9 No, it's useless to try to capture him. It is frightening even to think about it!" 
Job41:10 No one dares to stir him up, let alone try to conquer him. And if no one can stand before him, who can stand before me?" 
Job41:11 I owe no one anything. Everything under the heaven is mine. 
Job41:12 “I should mention, too, the tremendous strength in his limbs and throughout his enormous frame." 
Job41:13 Who can penetrate his hide, or who dares come within reach of his jaws?" 
Job41:14 For his teeth are terrible. 
Job41:15 His overlapping scales are his pride, making" 
Job41:16 a tight seal so no air can 
Job41:17 get between them, and nothing can penetrate." 
Job41:18 “When he sneezes, the sunlight sparkles like lightning across the vapor droplets. His eyes glow like sparks." 
Job41:19 Fire leaps from his mouth. 
Job41:20 Smoke flows from his nostrils, like steam from a boiling pot that is fired by dry rushes." 
Job41:21 Yes, his breath would kindle coals—flames leap from his mouth." 
Job41:22 “The tremendous strength in his neck strikes terror wherever he goes. 
Job41:23 His flesh is hard and firm, not soft and fat." 
Job41:24 His heart is hard as rock, just like a millstone." 
Job41:25 When he stands up, the strongest are afraid. Terror grips them." 
Job41:26 No sword can stop him, nor spear nor dart nor pointed shaft." 
Job41:27 Iron is nothing but straw to him, and brass is rotten wood." 
Job41:28 Arrows cannot make him flee. Slingstones are as ineffective as straw. 
Job41:29 Clubs do no good, and he laughs at the javelins hurled at him." 
Job41:30 His belly is covered with scales as sharp as shards; they tear up the ground as he drags through the mud. 
Job41:31 “He makes the water boil with his commotion. He churns the depths. 
Job41:32 He leaves a shining wake of froth behind him. One would think the sea was made of frost! 
Job41:33 There is nothing else so fearless anywhere on earth. 
Job41:34 Of all the beasts, he is the proudest—monarch of all that he sees.“" 
Job42:1 Then Job replied to God: 
Job42:2 “I know that you can do anything and that no one can stop you. 
Job42:3 You ask who it is who has so foolishly denied your providence. It is I. I was talking about things I knew nothing about and did not understand, things far too wonderful for me." 
Job42:4 “ You said, 'Listen and I will speak! Let me put the questions to you! See if you can answer them!'" 
Job42:5 “ But now I say, 'I had heard about you before, but now I have seen you," 
Job42:6 and I loathe myself and repent in dust and ashes.' “ 
Job42:7 After the Lord had finished speaking with Job, he said to Eliphaz the Temanite: “I am angry with you and with your two friends, for you have not been right in what you have said about me, as my servant Job was." 
Job42:8 Now take seven young bulls and seven rams and go to my servant Job and offer a burnt offering for yourselves; and my servant Job will pray for you, and I will accept his prayer on your behalf, and won't destroy you as I should because of your sin, your failure to speak rightly concerning my servant Job.“" 
Job42:9 So Eliphaz the Temanite, and Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite did as the Lord commanded them, and the Lord accepted Job's prayer on their behalf." 
Job42:10 Then, when Job prayed for his friends, the Lord restored his wealth and happiness! In fact, the Lord gave him twice as much as before!" 
Job42:11 Then all of his brothers, sisters, and former friends arrived and feasted with him in his home, consoling him for all his sorrow and comforting him because of all the trials the Lord had brought upon him. And each of them brought him a gift of money and a gold ring." 
Job42:12 So the Lord blessed Job at the end of his life more than at the beginning. For now he had 14,000 sheep, 6,000 camels, 1,000 teams of oxen, and 1,000 female donkeys." 
Job42:13 God also gave him seven more sons and three more daughters. 
Job42:14 These were the names of his daughters: Jemima, Kezia, Keren." 
Job42:15 And in all the land there were no other girls as lovely as the daughters of Job; and their father put them into his will along with their brothers. 
Job42:16 Job lived 140 years after that, living to see his grandchildren and great-grandchildren too." 
Job42:17 Then at last he died, an old, old man, after living a long, good life." 
Psalms1:1 Oh, the joys of those who do not follow evil men's advice, who do not hang around with sinners, scoffing at the things of God." 
Psalms1:2 But they delight in doing everything God wants them to, and day and night are always meditating on his laws and thinking about ways to follow him more closely." 
Psalms1:3 They are like trees along a riverbank bearing luscious fruit each season without fail. Their leaves shall never wither, and all they do shall prosper." 
Psalms1:4 But for sinners, what a different story! They blow away like chaff before the wind." 
Psalms1:5 They are not safe on Judgment Day; they shall not stand among the godly. 
Psalms1:6 For the Lord watches over all the plans and paths of godly men, but the paths of the godless lead to doom." 
Psalms2:1 What fools the nations are to rage against the Lord! How strange that men should try to outwit God! 
Psalms2:2 For a summit conference of the nations has been called to plot against the Lord and his Messiah, Christ the King." 
Psalms2:3 “Come, let us break his chains,“ they say, “and free ourselves from all this slavery to God.“" 
Psalms2:4 But God in heaven merely laughs! He is amused by all their puny plans. 
Psalms2:5 And then in fierce fury he rebukes them and fills them with fear. 
Psalms2:6 For the Lord declares, “This is the King of my choice, and I have enthroned him in Jerusalem, my holy city.“" 
Psalms2:7 His chosen one replies, “I will reveal the everlasting purposes of God, for the Lord has said to me, 'You are my Son. This is your Coronation Day. Today I am giving you your glory.' “" 
Psalms2:8 “Only ask and I will give you all the nations of the world. 
Psalms2:9 Rule them with an iron rod; smash them like clay pots!“ 
Psalms2:10 O kings and rulers of the earth, listen while there is time." 
Psalms2:11 Serve the Lord with reverent fear; rejoice with trembling. 
Psalms2:12 Fall down before his Son and kiss his feet before his anger is roused and you perish. I am warning you—his wrath will soon begin. But oh, the joys of those who put their trust in him!" 
Psalms3:1 A Psalm of David when he fled from his son Absalom O Lord, so many are against me. So many seek to harm me. I have so many enemies." 
Psalms3:2 So many say that God will never help me. 
Psalms3:3 But Lord, you are my shield, my glory, and my only hope. You alone can lift my head, now bowed in shame." 
Psalms3:4 I cried out to the Lord, and he heard me from his Temple in Jerusalem." 
Psalms3:5 Then I lay down and slept in peace and woke up safely, for the Lord was watching over me." 
Psalms3:6 And now, although ten thousand enemies surround me on every side, I am not afraid." 
Psalms3:7 I will cry to him, “Arise, O Lord! Save me, O my God!“ And he will slap them in the face, insulting them and breaking off their teeth." 
Psalms3:8 For salvation comes from God. What joys he gives to all his people. 
Psalms4:1 O God, you have declared me perfect in your eyes; you have always cared for me in my distress; now hear me as I call again. Have mercy on me. Hear my prayer." 
Psalms4:2 The Lord God asks, “Sons of men, will you forever turn my glory into shame by worshiping these silly idols, when every claim that's made for them is false?“" 
Psalms4:3 Mark this well: The Lord has set apart the redeemed for himself. Therefore he will listen to me and answer when I call to him. 
Psalms4:4 Stand before the Lord in awe, and do not sin against him. Lie quietly upon your bed in silent meditation." 
Psalms4:5 Put your trust in the Lord, and offer him pleasing sacrifices." 
Psalms4:6 Many say that God will never help us. Prove them wrong, O Lord, by letting the light of your face shine down upon us." 
Psalms4:7 Yes, the gladness you have given me is far greater than their joys at harvest time as they gaze at their bountiful crops." 
Psalms4:8 I will lie down in peace and sleep, for though I am alone, O Lord, you will keep me safe." 
Psalms5:1 O Lord, hear me praying; listen to my plea, O God" 
Psalms5:2 my King, for I will never pray to anyone but you." 
Psalms5:3 Each morning I will look to you in heaven and lay my requests before you, praying earnestly." 
Psalms5:4 I know you get no pleasure from wickedness and cannot tolerate the slightest sin. 
Psalms5:5 Therefore, proud sinners will not survive your searching gaze, for how you hate their evil deeds." 
Psalms5:6 You will destroy them for their lies; how you abhor all murder and deception. 
Psalms5:7 But as for me, I will come into your Temple protected by your mercy and your love; I will worship you with deepest awe." 
Psalms5:8 Lord, lead me as you promised me you would; otherwise my enemies will conquer me. Tell me clearly what to do, which way to turn." 
Psalms5:9 For they cannot speak one truthful word. Their hearts are filled to the brim with wickedness. Their suggestions are full of the stench of sin and death. Their tongues are filled with flatteries to gain their wicked ends. 
Psalms5:10 O God, hold them responsible. Catch them in their own traps; let them fall beneath the weight of their own transgressions, for they rebel against you." 
Psalms5:11 But make everyone rejoice who puts his trust in you. Keep them shouting for joy because you are defending them. Fill all who love you with your happiness. 
Psalms5:12 For you bless the godly man, O Lord; you protect him with your shield of love." 
Psalms6:1 No, Lord! Don't punish me in the heat of your anger." 
Psalms6:2 Pity me, O Lord, for I am weak. Heal me, for my body is sick," 
Psalms6:3 and I am upset and disturbed. My mind is filled with apprehension and with gloom. Oh, restore me soon." 
Psalms6:4 Come, O Lord, and make me well. In your kindness save me." 
Psalms6:5 For if I die, I cannot give you glory by praising you before my friends." 
Psalms6:6 I am worn out with pain; every night my pillow is wet with tears. 
Psalms6:7 My eyes are growing old and dim with grief because of all my enemies. 
Psalms6:8 Go, leave me now, you men of evil deeds, for the Lord has heard my weeping" 
Psalms6:9 and my pleading. He will answer all my prayers. 
Psalms6:10 All my enemies shall be suddenly dishonored, terror-stricken, and disgraced. God will turn them back in shame." 
Psalms7:1 I am depending on you, O Lord my God, to save me from my persecutors." 
Psalms7:2 Don't let them pounce upon me as a lion would and maul me and drag me away with no one to rescue me. 
Psalms7:3 It would be different, Lord, if I were doing evil things—" 
Psalms7:4 if I were paying back evil for good or unjustly attacking those I dislike. 
Psalms7:5 Then it would be right for you to let my enemies destroy me, crush me to the ground, and trample my life in the dust." 
Psalms7:6 But Lord! Arise in anger against the anger of my enemies. Awake! Demand justice for me, Lord!" 
Psalms7:7 Gather all peoples before you; sit high above them, judging their sins." 
Psalms7:8 But justify me publicly; establish my honor and truth before them all. 
Psalms7:9 End all wickedness, O Lord, and bless all who truly worship God; for you, the righteous God, look deep within the hearts of men and examine all their motives and their thoughts." 
Psalms7:10 God is my shield; he will defend me. He saves those whose hearts and lives are true and right. 
Psalms7:11 God is a judge who is perfectly fair, and he is angry with the wicked every day." 
Psalms7:12 Unless they repent, he will sharpen his sword and slay them. He has bent and strung his bow" 
Psalms7:13 and fitted it with deadly arrows made from shafts of fire. 
Psalms7:14 The wicked man conceives an evil plot, labors with its dark details, and brings to birth his treachery and lies;" 
Psalms7:15 let him fall into his own trap. 
Psalms7:16 May the violence he plans for others boomerang upon himself; let him die. 
Psalms7:17 Oh, how grateful and thankful I am to the Lord because he is so good. I will sing praise to the name of the Lord who is above all lords." 
Psalms8:1 O Lord our God, the majesty and glory of your name fills all the earth and overflows the heavens." 
Psalms8:2 You have taught the little children to praise you perfectly. May their example shame and silence your enemies! 
Psalms8:3 When I look up into the night skies and see the work of your fingers—the moon and the stars you have made— 
Psalms8:4 I cannot understand how you can bother with mere puny man, to pay any attention to him!" 
Psalms8:5 And yet you have made him only a little lower than the angels and placed a crown of glory and honor upon his head. 
Psalms8:6 You have put him in charge of everything you made; everything is put under his authority: 
Psalms8:7 all sheep and oxen, and wild animals too," 
Psalms8:8 the birds and fish, and all the life in the sea." 
Psalms8:9 O Jehovah, our Lord, the majesty and glory of your name fills the earth." 
Psalms9:1 O Lord, I will praise you with all my heart and tell everyone about the marvelous things you do." 
Psalms9:2 I will be glad, yes, filled with joy because of you. I will sing your praises, O Lord God above all gods." 
Psalms9:3 My enemies will fall back and perish in your presence; 
Psalms9:4 you have vindicated me; you have endorsed my work, declaring from your throne that it is good." 
Psalms9:5 You have rebuked the nations and destroyed the wicked, blotting out their names forever and ever." 
Psalms9:6 O enemies of mine, you are doomed forever. The Lord will destroy your cities; even the memory of them will disappear." 
Psalms9:7 But the Lord lives on forever; he sits upon 
Psalms9:8 his throne to judge justly the nations of the world. 
Psalms9:9 All who are oppressed may come to him. He is a refuge for them in their times of trouble. 
Psalms9:10 All those who know your mercy, Lord, will count on you for help. For you have never yet forsaken those who trust in you." 
Psalms9:11 Oh, sing out your praises to the God who lives in Jerusalem. Tell the world about his unforgettable deeds." 
Psalms9:12 He who avenges murder has an open ear to those who cry to him for justice. He does not ignore the prayers of men in trouble when they call to him for help. 
Psalms9:13 And now, O Lord, have mercy on me; see how I suffer at the hands of those who hate me. Lord, snatch me back from the jaws of death." 
Psalms9:14 Save me, so that I can praise you publicly before all the people at Jerusalem's gates and rejoice that you have rescued me." 
Psalms9:15 The nations fall into the pitfalls they have dug for others; the trap they set has snapped on them. 
Psalms9:16 The Lord is famous for the way he punishes the wicked in their own snares! 
Psalms9:17 The wicked shall be sent away to hell; this is the fate of all the nations forgetting the Lord. 
Psalms9:18 For the needs of the needy shall not be ignored forever; the hopes of the poor shall not always be crushed. 
Psalms9:19 O Lord, arise and judge and punish the nations; don't let them defy you!" 
Psalms9:20 Make them tremble in fear; put the nations in their place until at last they know they are but puny men. 
Psalms10:1 Lord, why are you standing aloof and far away? Why do you hide when I need you the most?" 
Psalms10:2 Come and deal with all these proud and wicked men who viciously persecute the poor. Pour upon these men the evil they planned for others! 
Psalms10:3 For these men brag of all their evil lusts; they revile God and congratulate those the Lord abhors, whose only goal in life is money." 
Psalms10:4 These wicked men, so proud and haughty, seem to think that God is dead. They wouldn't think of looking for him!" 
Psalms10:5 Yet there is success in everything they do, and their enemies fall before them. They do not see your punishment awaiting them." 
Psalms10:6 They boast that neither God nor man can ever keep them down—somehow they'll find a way! 
Psalms10:7 Their mouths are full of profanity and lies and fraud. They are always boasting of their evil plans. 
Psalms10:8 They lurk in dark alleys of the city and murder passersby. 
Psalms10:9 Like lions they crouch silently, waiting to pounce upon the poor. Like hunters they catch their victims in their traps." 
Psalms10:10 The unfortunate are overwhelmed by their superior strength and fall beneath their blows. 
Psalms10:11 “God isn't watching,“ they say to themselves; “he'll never know!“" 
Psalms10:12 O Lord, arise! O God, crush them! Don't forget the poor or anyone else in need." 
Psalms10:13 Why do you let the wicked get away with this contempt for God? For they think that God will never call them to account. 
Psalms10:14 Lord, you see what they are doing. You have noted each evil act. You know what trouble and grief they have caused. Now punish them. O Lord, the poor man trusts himself to you; you are known as the helper of the helpless." 
Psalms10:15 Break the arms of these wicked men. Go after them until the last of them is destroyed. 
Psalms10:16 The Lord is King forever and forever. Those who follow other gods shall be swept from his land. 
Psalms10:17 Lord, you know the hopes of humble people. Surely you will hear their cries and comfort their hearts by helping them." 
Psalms10:18 You will be with the orphans and all who are oppressed, so that mere earthly man will terrify them no longer." 
Psalms11:1 How dare you tell me, “Flee to the mountains for safety,“ when I am trusting in the Lord?" 
Psalms11:2 For the wicked have strung their bows, drawn their arrows tight against the bowstrings, and aimed from ambush at the people of God." 
Psalms11:3 “Law and order have collapsed,“ we are told. “What can the righteous do but flee?“" 
Psalms11:4 But the Lord is still in his holy temple; he still rules from heaven. He closely watches everything that happens here on earth. 
Psalms11:5 He puts the righteous and the wicked to the test; he hates those loving violence. 
Psalms11:6 He will rain down fire and brimstone on the wicked and scorch them with his burning wind. 
Psalms11:7 For God is good, and he loves goodness; the godly shall see his face." 
Psalms12:1 Lord! Help! Godly men are fast disappearing. Where in all the world can dependable men be found? 
Psalms12:2 Everyone deceives and flatters and lies. There is no sincerity left. 
Psalms12:3 But the Lord will not deal gently with people who act like that; he will destroy those proud liars who say, “We will lie to our hearts' content." 
Psalms12:4 Our lips are our own; who can stop us?“ 
Psalms12:5 The Lord replies, “I will arise and defend the oppressed, the poor, the needy. I will rescue them as they have longed for me to do.“" 
Psalms12:6 The Lord's promise is sure. He speaks no careless word; all he says is purest truth, like silver seven times refined." 
Psalms12:7 O Lord, we know that you will forever preserve your own from the reach of evil men," 
Psalms12:8 although they prowl on every side and vileness is praised throughout the land. 
Psalms13:1 How long will you forget me, Lord? Forever? How long will you look the other way when I am in need?" 
Psalms13:2 How long must I be hiding daily anguish in my heart? How long shall my enemy have the upper hand? 
Psalms13:3 Answer me, O Lord my God; give me light in my darkness lest I die." 
Psalms13:4 Don't let my enemies say, “We have conquered him!“ Don't let them gloat that I am down." 
Psalms13:5 But I will always trust in you and in your mercy and shall rejoice in your salvation. 
Psalms13:6 I will sing to the Lord because he has blessed me so richly. 
Psalms14:1 That man is a fool who says to himself, “There is no God!“ Anyone who talks like that is warped and evil and cannot really be a good person at all." 
Psalms14:2 The Lord looks down from heaven on all mankind to see if there are any who are wise, who want to please God." 
Psalms14:3 But no, all have strayed away; all are rotten with sin. Not one is good, not one!" 
Psalms14:4 They eat my people like bread and wouldn't think of praying! Don't they really know any better? 
Psalms14:5 Terror shall grip them, for God is with those who love him." 
Psalms14:6 He is the refuge of the poor and humble when evildoers are oppressing them. 
Psalms14:7 Oh, that the time of their rescue were already here, that God would come from Zion now to save his people. What gladness when the Lord has rescued Israel!" 
Psalms15:1 Lord, who may go and find refuge and shelter in your tabernacle up on your holy hill?" 
Psalms15:2 Anyone who leads a blameless life and is truly sincere. 
Psalms15:3 Anyone who refuses to slander others, does not listen to gossip, never harms his neighbor," 
Psalms15:4 speaks out against sin, criticizes those committing it, commends the faithful followers of the Lord, keeps a promise even if it ruins him," 
Psalms15:5 does not crush his debtors with high interest rates, and refuses to testify against the innocent despite the bribes offered him—such a man shall stand firm forever." 
Psalms16:1 Save me, O God, because I have come to you for refuge." 
Psalms16:2 I said to him, “You are my Lord; I have no other help but yours.“" 
Psalms16:3 I want the company of the godly men and women in the land; they are the true nobility. 
Psalms16:4 Those choosing other gods shall all be filled with sorrow; I will not offer the sacrifices they do or even speak the names of their gods. 
Psalms16:5 The Lord himself is my inheritance, my prize. He is my food and drink, my highest joy! He guards all that is mine." 
Psalms16:6 He sees that I am given pleasant brooks and meadows as my share! What a wonderful inheritance! 
Psalms16:7 I will bless the Lord who counsels me; he gives me wisdom in the night. He tells me what to do. 
Psalms16:8 I am always thinking of the Lord; and because he is so near, I never need to stumble or to fall." 
Psalms16:9 Heart, body, and soul are filled with joy." 
Psalms16:10 For you will not leave me among the dead; you will not allow your beloved one to rot in the grave. 
Psalms16:11 You have let me experience the joys of life and the exquisite pleasures of your own eternal presence. 
Psalms17:1 I am pleading for your help, O Lord; for I have been honest and have done what is right, and you must listen to my earnest cry!" 
Psalms17:2 Publicly acquit me, Lord, for you are always fair." 
Psalms17:3 You have tested me and seen that I am good. You have come even in the night and found nothing amiss and know that I have told the truth. 
Psalms17:4 I have followed your commands and have not gone along with cruel and evil men. 
Psalms17:5 My feet have not slipped from your paths. 
Psalms17:6 Why am I praying like this? Because I know you will answer me, O God! Yes, listen as I pray." 
Psalms17:7 Show me your strong love in wonderful ways, O Savior of all those seeking your help against their foes." 
Psalms17:8 Protect me as you would the pupil of your eye; hide me in the shadow of your wings as you hover over me. 
Psalms17:9 My enemies encircle me with murder in their eyes. 
Psalms17:10 They are pitiless and arrogant. Listen to their boasting. 
Psalms17:11 They close in upon me and are ready to throw me to the ground. 
Psalms17:12 They are like lions eager to tear me apart, like young lions hiding and waiting their chance." 
Psalms17:13 Lord, arise and stand against them. Push them back! Come and save me" 
Psalms17:14 from these men of the world whose only concern is earthly gain—these men whom you have filled with your treasures so that their children and grandchildren are rich and prosperous. 
Psalms17:15 But as for me, my contentment is not in wealth but in seeing you and knowing all is well between us. And when I awake in heaven, I will be fully satisfied, for I will see you face to face." 
Psalms18:1 This song of David was written at a time when the Lord had delivered him from his many enemies, including Saul. Lord, how I love you! For you have done such tremendous things for me." 
Psalms18:2 The Lord is my fort where I can enter and be safe; no one can follow me in and slay me. He is a rugged mountain where I hide; he is my Savior, a rock where none can reach me, and a tower of safety. He is my shield. He is like the strong horn of a mighty fighting bull." 
Psalms18:3 All I need to do is cry to him—oh, praise the Lord—and I am saved from all my enemies!" 
Psalms18:4 Death bound me with chains, and the floods of ungodliness mounted a massive attack against me." 
Psalms18:5 Trapped and helpless, I struggled against the ropes that drew me on to death." 
Psalms18:6 In my distress I screamed to the Lord for his help. And he heard me from heaven; my cry reached his ears. 
Psalms18:7 Then the earth rocked and reeled, and mountains shook and trembled. How they quaked! For he was angry." 
Psalms18:8 Fierce flames leaped from his mouth, setting fire to the earth; smoke blew from his nostrils." 
Psalms18:9 He bent the heavens down and came to my defense; thick darkness was beneath his feet. 
Psalms18:10 Mounted on a mighty angel, he sped swiftly to my aid with wings of wind." 
Psalms18:11 He enshrouded himself with darkness, veiling his approach with dense clouds dark as murky waters." 
Psalms18:12 Suddenly the brilliance of his presence broke through the clouds with lightning and a mighty storm of hail. 
Psalms18:13 The Lord thundered in the heavens; the God above all gods has spoken—oh, the hailstones; oh, the fire!" 
Psalms18:14 He flashed his fearful arrows of lightning and routed all my enemies. See how they run! 
Psalms18:15 Then at your command, O Lord, the sea receded from the shore. At the blast of your breath the depths were laid bare." 
Psalms18:16 He reached down from heaven and took me and drew me out of my great trials. He rescued me from deep waters. 
Psalms18:17 He delivered me from my strong enemy, from those who hated me—I who was helpless in their hands." 
Psalms18:18 On the day when I was weakest, they attacked. But the Lord held me steady." 
Psalms18:19 He led me to a place of safety, for he delights in me." 
Psalms18:20 The Lord rewarded me for doing right and being pure. 
Psalms18:21 For I have followed his commands and have not sinned by turning back from following him. 
Psalms18:22 I kept close watch on all his laws; I did not refuse a single one. 
Psalms18:23 I did my best to keep them all, holding myself back from doing wrong." 
Psalms18:24 And so the Lord has paid me with his blessings, for I have done what is right, and I am pure of heart. This he knows, for he watches my every step." 
Psalms18:25 Lord, how merciful you are to those who are merciful. And you do not punish those who run from evil." 
Psalms18:26 You give blessings to the pure but pain to those who leave your paths. 
Psalms18:27 You deliver the humble but condemn the proud and haughty ones. 
Psalms18:28 You have turned on my light! The Lord my God has made my darkness turn to light. 
Psalms18:29 Now in your strength I can scale any wall, attack any troop." 
Psalms18:30 What a God he is! How perfect in every way! All his promises prove true. He is a shield for everyone who hides behind him. 
Psalms18:31 For who is God except our Lord? Who but he is as a rock? 
Psalms18:32 He fills me with strength and protects me wherever I go. 
Psalms18:33 He gives me the surefootedness of a mountain goat upon the crags. He leads me safely along the top of the cliffs. 
Psalms18:34 He prepares me for battle and gives me strength to draw an iron bow! 
Psalms18:35 You have given me your salvation as my shield. Your right hand, O Lord, supports me; your gentleness has made me great." 
Psalms18:36 You have made wide steps beneath my feet so that I need never slip. 
Psalms18:37 I chased my enemies; I caught up with them and did not turn back until all were conquered. 
Psalms18:38 I pinned them to the ground; all were helpless before me. I placed my feet upon their necks. 
Psalms18:39 For you have armed me with strong armor for the battle. My enemies quail before me and fall defeated at my feet. 
Psalms18:40 You made them turn and run; I destroyed all who hated me. 
Psalms18:41 They shouted for help, but no one dared to rescue them; they cried to the Lord, but he refused to answer them." 
Psalms18:42 So I crushed them fine as dust and cast them to the wind. I threw them away like sweepings from the floor. 
Psalms18:43 You gave me victory in every battle. The nations came and served me. Even those I didn't know before come now and bow before me. 
Psalms18:44 Foreigners who have never seen me submit instantly. 
Psalms18:45 They come trembling from their strongholds. 
Psalms18:46 God is alive! Praise him who is the great rock of protection. 
Psalms18:47 He is the God who pays back those who harm me and subdues the nations before me. 
Psalms18:48 He rescues me from my enemies; he holds me safely out of their reach and saves me from these powerful opponents. 
Psalms18:49 For this, O Lord, I will praise you among the nations." 
Psalms18:50 Many times you have miraculously rescued me, the king you appointed. You have been loving and kind to me and will be to my descendants." 
Psalms19:1 The heavens are telling the glory of God; they are a marvelous display of his craftsmanship. 
Psalms19:2 Day and night they keep on telling about God. 
Psalms19:3 Without a sound or word, silent in the skies, their message reaches out to all the world." 
Psalms19:4 The sun lives in the heavens where God placed it 
Psalms19:5 and moves out across the skies as radiant as a bridegroom going to his wedding, or as joyous as an athlete looking forward to a race!" 
Psalms19:6 The sun crosses the heavens from end to end, and nothing can hide from its heat." 
Psalms19:7 God's laws are perfect. 
Psalms19:8 They protect us, make us wise, and give us joy and light." 
Psalms19:9 God's laws are pure, eternal, just." 
Psalms19:10 They are more desirable than gold. They are sweeter than honey dripping from a honeycomb. 
Psalms19:11 For they warn us away from harm and give success to those who obey them. 
Psalms19:12 But how can I ever know what sins are lurking in my heart? Cleanse me from these hidden faults. 
Psalms19:13 And keep me from deliberate wrongs; help me to stop doing them. Only then can I be free of guilt and innocent of some great crime. 
Psalms19:14 May my spoken words and unspoken thoughts be pleasing even to you, O Lord my Rock and my Redeemer." 
Psalms20:1 In your day of trouble, may the Lord be with you! May the God of Jacob keep you from all harm." 
Psalms20:2 May he send you aid from his sanctuary in Zion. 
Psalms20:3 May he remember with pleasure the gifts you have given him, your sacrifices and burnt offerings." 
Psalms20:4 May he grant you your heart's desire and fulfill all your plans. 
Psalms20:5 May there be shouts of joy when we hear the news of your victory, flags flying with praise to God for all that he has done for you. May he answer all your prayers!" 
Psalms20:6 “God save the king“—I know he does! He hears me from highest heaven and sends great victories. 
Psalms20:7 Some nations boast of armies and of weaponry, but our boast is in the Lord our God." 
Psalms20:8 Those nations will collapse and perish; we will arise to stand firm and sure! 
Psalms20:9 Give victory to our king, O Lord; oh, hear our prayer." 
Psalms21:1 How the king rejoices in your strength, O Lord! How he exults in your salvation." 
Psalms21:2 For you have given him his heart's desire, everything he asks you for!" 
Psalms21:3 You welcomed him to the throne with success and prosperity. You set a royal crown of solid gold upon his head. 
Psalms21:4 He asked for a long, good life, and you have granted his request; the days of his life stretch on and on forever." 
Psalms21:5 You have given him fame and honor. You have clothed him with splendor and majesty. 
Psalms21:6 You have endowed him with eternal happiness. You have given him the unquenchable joy of your presence. 
Psalms21:7 And because the king trusts in the Lord, he will never stumble, never fall; for he depends upon the steadfast love of the God who is above all gods." 
Psalms21:8 Your hand, O Lord, will find your enemies, all who hate you." 
Psalms21:9 When you appear, they will be destroyed in the fierce fire of your presence." 
Psalms21:10 The Lord will destroy them and their children. 
Psalms21:11 For these men plot against you, Lord, but they cannot possibly succeed." 
Psalms21:12 They will turn and flee when they see your arrows aimed straight at them. 
Psalms21:13 Accept our praise, O Lord, for all your glorious power. We will write songs to celebrate your mighty acts!" 
Psalms22:1 My God, my God, why have you forsaken me? Why do you refuse to help me or even to listen to my groans?" 
Psalms22:2 Day and night I keep on weeping, crying for your help, but there is no reply—" 
Psalms22:3 for you are holy. 
Psalms22:4 The praises of our fathers surrounded your throne; they trusted you and you delivered them. 
Psalms22:5 You heard their cries for help and saved them; they were never disappointed when they sought your aid. 
Psalms22:6 But I am a worm, not a man, scorned and despised by my own people and by all mankind." 
Psalms22:7 Everyone who sees me mocks and sneers and shrugs. 
Psalms22:8 “Is this the one who rolled his burden on the Lord?“ they laugh. “Is this the one who claims the Lord delights in him? We'll believe it when we see God rescue him!“ 
Psalms22:9 Lord, how you have helped me before!" 
Psalms22:10 You took me safely from my mother's womb and brought me through the years of infancy. 
Psalms22:11 I have depended upon you since birth; you have always been my God. Don't leave me now, for trouble is near and no one else can possibly help." 
Psalms22:12 I am surrounded by fearsome enemies, strong as the giant bulls from Bashan." 
Psalms22:13 They come at me with open jaws, like roaring lions attacking their prey." 
Psalms22:14 My strength has drained away like water, and all my bones are out of joint. My heart melts like wax;" 
Psalms22:15 my strength has dried up like sun-baked clay; my tongue sticks to my mouth, for you have laid me in the dust of death." 
Psalms22:16 The enemy, this gang of evil men, circles me like a pack of dogs; they have pierced my hands and feet." 
Psalms22:17 I can count every bone in my body. See these men of evil gloat and stare; 
Psalms22:18 they divide my clothes among themselves by a toss of the dice. 
Psalms22:19 O Lord, don't stay away. O God my Strength, hurry to my aid." 
Psalms22:20 Rescue me from death; spare my precious life from all these evil men. 
Psalms22:21 Save me from these lions' jaws and from the horns of these wild oxen. Yes, God will answer me and rescue me." 
Psalms22:22 I will praise you to all my brothers; I will stand up before the congregation and testify of the wonderful things you have done. 
Psalms22:23 “Praise the Lord, each one of you who fears him,“ I will say. “Each of you must fear and reverence his name. Let all Israel sing his praises," 
Psalms22:24 for he has not despised my cries of deep despair; he has not turned and walked away. When I cried to him, he heard and came.“" 
Psalms22:25 Yes, I will stand and praise you before all the people. I will publicly fulfill my vows in the presence of all who reverence your name." 
Psalms22:26 The poor shall eat and be satisfied; all who seek the Lord shall find him and shall praise his name. Their hearts shall rejoice with everlasting joy. 
Psalms22:27 The whole earth shall see it and return to the Lord; the people of every nation shall worship him. 
Psalms22:28 For the Lord is King and rules the nations. 
Psalms22:29 Both proud and humble together, all who are mortal—born to die—shall worship him." 
Psalms22:30 Our children too shall serve him, for they shall hear from us about the wonders of the Lord;" 
Psalms22:31 generations yet unborn shall hear of all the miracles he did for us. 
Psalms23:1 Because the Lord is my Shepherd, I have everything I need!" 
Psalms23:2 He lets me rest in the meadow grass and leads me beside the quiet streams. 
Psalms23:3 He gives me new strength. He helps me do what honors him the most. 
Psalms23:4 Even when walking through the dark valley of death I will not be afraid, for you are close beside me, guarding, guiding all the way." 
Psalms23:5 You provide delicious food for me in the presence of my enemies. You have welcomed me as your guest; blessings overflow! 
Psalms23:6 Your goodness and unfailing kindness shall be with me all of my life, and afterwards I will live with you forever in your home." 
Psalms24:1 The earth belongs to God! Everything in all the world is his! 
Psalms24:2 He is the one who pushed the oceans back to let dry land appear. 
Psalms24:3 Who may climb the mountain of the Lord and enter where he lives? Who may stand before the Lord? 
Psalms24:4 Only those with pure hands and hearts, who do not practice dishonesty and lying." 
Psalms24:5 They will receive God's own goodness as their blessing from him, planted in their lives by God himself, their Savior." 
Psalms24:6 These are the ones who are allowed to stand before the Lord and worship the God of Jacob. 
Psalms24:7 Open up, O ancient gates, and let the King of Glory in." 
Psalms24:8 Who is this King of Glory? The Lord, strong and mighty, invincible in battle." 
Psalms24:9 Yes, open wide the gates and let the King of Glory in." 
Psalms24:10 Who is this King of Glory? The Commander of all of heaven's armies! 
Psalms25:1 To you, O Lord, I pray." 
Psalms25:2 Don't fail me, Lord, for I am trusting you. Don't let my enemies succeed. Don't give them victory over me." 
Psalms25:3 None of those who have faith in God will ever be disgraced for trusting him. But all who harm the innocent shall be defeated. 
Psalms25:4 Show me the path where I should go, O Lord; point out the right road for me to walk." 
Psalms25:5 Lead me; teach me; for you are the God who gives me salvation. I have no hope except in you. 
Psalms25:6 Overlook my youthful sins, O Lord!" 
Psalms25:7 Look at me instead through eyes of mercy and forgiveness, through eyes of everlasting love and kindness." 
Psalms25:8 The Lord is good and glad to teach the proper path to all who go astray; 
Psalms25:9 he will teach the ways that are right and best to those who humbly turn to him. 
Psalms25:10 And when we obey him, every path he guides us on is fragrant with his loving-kindness and his truth." 
Psalms25:11 But Lord, my sins! How many they are. Oh, pardon them for the honor of your name." 
Psalms25:12 Where is the man who fears the Lord? God will teach him how to choose the best. 
Psalms25:13 He shall live within God's circle of blessing, and his children shall inherit the earth." 
Psalms25:14 Friendship with God is reserved for those who reverence him. With them alone he shares the secrets of his promises. 
Psalms25:15 My eyes are ever looking to the Lord for help, for he alone can rescue me." 
Psalms25:16 Come, Lord, and show me your mercy, for I am helpless, overwhelmed, in deep distress;" 
Psalms25:17 my problems go from bad to worse. Oh, save me from them all!" 
Psalms25:18 See my sorrows; feel my pain; forgive my sins. 
Psalms25:19 See how many enemies I have and how viciously they hate me! 
Psalms25:20 Save me from them! Deliver my life from their power! Oh, let it never be said that I trusted you in vain!" 
Psalms25:21 Assign me Godliness and Integrity as my bodyguards, for I expect you to protect me" 
Psalms25:22 and to ransom Israel from all her troubles. 
Psalms26:1 Dismiss all the charges against me, Lord, for I have tried to keep your laws and have trusted you without wavering." 
Psalms26:2 Cross-examine me, O Lord, and see that this is so; test my motives and affections too." 
Psalms26:3 For I have taken your loving-kindness and your truth as my ideals. 
Psalms26:4 I do not have fellowship with tricky, two-faced men; they are false and hypocritical." 
Psalms26:5 I hate the sinners' hangouts and refuse to enter them. 
Psalms26:6 I wash my hands to prove my innocence and come before your altar," 
Psalms26:7 singing a song of thanksgiving and telling about your miracles. 
Psalms26:8 Lord, I love your home, this shrine where the brilliant, dazzling splendor of your presence lives." 
Psalms26:9 Don't treat me as a common sinner or 
Psalms26:10 murderer who plots against the innocent and demands bribes. 
Psalms26:11 No, I am not like that, O Lord; I try to walk a straight and narrow path of doing what is right; therefore in mercy save me." 
Psalms26:12 I publicly praise the Lord for keeping me from slipping and falling. 
Psalms27:1 The Lord is my light and my salvation; he protects me from danger—whom shall I fear? 
Psalms27:2 When evil men come to destroy me, they will stumble and fall!" 
Psalms27:3 Yes, though a mighty army marches against me, my heart shall know no fear! I am confident that God will save me." 
Psalms27:4 The one thing I want from God, the thing I seek most of all, is the privilege of meditating in his Temple, living in his presence every day of my life, delighting in his incomparable perfections and glory." 
Psalms27:5 There I'll be when troubles come. He will hide me. He will set me on a high rock 
Psalms27:6 out of reach of all my enemies. Then I will bring him sacrifices and sing his praises with much joy. 
Psalms27:7 Listen to my pleading, Lord! Be merciful and send the help I need." 
Psalms27:8 My heart has heard you say, “Come and talk with me, O my people.“ And my heart responds, “Lord, I am coming.“" 
Psalms27:9 Oh, do not hide yourself when I am trying to find you. Do not angrily reject your servant. You have been my help in all my trials before; don't leave me now. Don't forsake me, O God of my salvation." 
Psalms27:10 For if my father and mother should abandon me, you would welcome and comfort me." 
Psalms27:11 Tell me what to do, O Lord, and make it plain because I am surrounded by waiting enemies." 
Psalms27:12 Don't let them get me, Lord! Don't let me fall into their hands! For they accuse me of things I never did, and all the while are plotting cruelty." 
Psalms27:13 I am expecting the Lord to rescue me again, so that once again I will see his goodness to me here in the land of the living." 
Psalms27:14 Don't be impatient. Wait for the Lord, and he will come and save you! Be brave, stouthearted, and courageous. Yes, wait and he will help you." 
Psalms28:1 I plead with you to help me, Lord, for you are my Rock of safety. If you refuse to answer me, I might as well give up and die." 
Psalms28:2 Lord, I lift my hands to heaven and implore your help. Oh, listen to my cry." 
Psalms28:3 Don't punish me with all the wicked ones who speak so sweetly to their neighbors while planning to murder them. 
Psalms28:4 Give them the punishment they so richly deserve! Measure it out to them in proportion to their wickedness; pay them back for all their evil deeds. 
Psalms28:5 They care nothing for God or what he has done or what he has made; therefore God will dismantle them like old buildings, never to be rebuilt again." 
Psalms28:6 Oh, praise the Lord, for he has listened to my pleadings!" 
Psalms28:7 He is my strength, my shield from every danger. I trusted in him, and he helped me. Joy rises in my heart until I burst out in songs of praise to him." 
Psalms28:8 The Lord protects his people and gives victory to his anointed king. 
Psalms28:9 Defend your people, Lord; defend and bless your chosen ones. Lead them like a shepherd and carry them forever in your arms." 
Psalms29:1 Praise the Lord, you angels of his; praise his glory and his strength." 
Psalms29:2 Praise him for his majestic glory, the glory of his name. Come before him clothed in sacred garments." 
Psalms29:3 The voice of the Lord echoes from the clouds. The God of glory thunders through the skies. 
Psalms29:4 So powerful is his voice; so full of majesty. 
Psalms29:5 It breaks down the cedars. It splits the giant trees of Lebanon.
