Psalms29:6 It shakes Mount Lebanon and Mount Sirion. They leap and skip before him like young calves! 
Psalms29:7 The voice of the Lord thunders through the lightning. 
Psalms29:8 It resounds through the deserts and shakes the wilderness of Kadesh. 
Psalms29:9 The voice of the Lord spins and topples the mighty oaks. It strips the forests bare. They whirl and sway beneath the blast. But in his temple all are praising, “Glory, glory to the Lord.“" 
Psalms29:10 At the Flood the Lord showed his control of all creation. Now he continues to unveil his power. 
Psalms29:11 He will give his people strength. He will bless them with peace. 
Psalms30:1 I will praise you, Lord, for you have saved me from my enemies. You refuse to let them triumph over me." 
Psalms30:2 O Lord my God, I pleaded with you, and you gave me my health again." 
Psalms30:3 You brought me back from the brink of the grave, from death itself, and here I am alive!" 
Psalms30:4 Oh, sing to him you saints of his; give thanks to his holy name." 
Psalms30:5 His anger lasts a moment; his favor lasts for life! Weeping may go on all night, but in the morning there is joy." 
Psalms30:6 In my prosperity I said, “This is forever; nothing can stop me now!" 
Psalms30:7 The Lord has shown me his favor. He has made me steady as a mountain.“ Then, Lord, you turned your face away from me and cut off your river of blessings. Suddenly my courage was gone; I was terrified and panic-stricken." 
Psalms30:8 I cried to you, O Lord; oh, how I pled:" 
Psalms30:9 “What will you gain, O Lord, from killing me? How can I praise you then to all my friends? How can my dust in the grave speak out and tell the world about your faithfulness?" 
Psalms30:10 Hear me, Lord; oh, have pity and help me.“" 
Psalms30:11 Then he turned my sorrow into joy! He took away my clothes of mourning and clothed me with joy 
Psalms30:12 so that I might sing glad praises to the Lord instead of lying in silence in the grave. O Lord my God, I will keep on thanking you forever!" 
Psalms31:1 Lord, I trust in you alone. Don't let my enemies defeat me. Rescue me because you are the God who always does what is right." 
Psalms31:2 Answer quickly when I cry to you; bend low and hear my whispered plea. Be for me a great Rock of safety from my foes. 
Psalms31:3 Yes, you are my Rock and my fortress; honor your name by leading me out of this peril." 
Psalms31:4 Pull me from the trap my enemies have set for me. For you alone are strong enough. 
Psalms31:5 Into your hand I commit my spirit. You have rescued me, O God who keeps his promises." 
Psalms31:6 I worship only you; how you hate all those who worship idols, those imitation gods." 
Psalms31:7 I am radiant with joy because of your mercy, for you have listened to my troubles and have seen the crisis in my soul." 
Psalms31:8 You have not handed me over to my enemy but have given me open ground in which to maneuver. 
Psalms31:9 O Lord, have mercy on me in my anguish. My eyes are red from weeping; my health is broken from sorrow. I am pining away with grief;" 
Psalms31:10 my years are shortened, drained away because of sadness. My sins have sapped my strength; I stoop with sorrow and with shame." 
Psalms31:11 I am scorned by all my enemies and even more by my neighbors and friends. They dread meeting me and look the other way when I go by. 
Psalms31:12 I am forgotten like a dead man, like a broken and discarded pot." 
Psalms31:13 I heard the lies about me, the slanders of my enemies. Everywhere I looked I was afraid, for they were plotting against my life." 
Psalms31:14 But I am trusting you, O Lord." 
Psalms31:15 I said, “You alone are my God; my times are in your hands. Rescue me from those who hunt me down relentlessly." 
Psalms31:16 Let your favor shine again upon your servant; save me just because you are so kind! 
Psalms31:17 Don't disgrace me, Lord, by not replying when I call to you for aid. But let the wicked be shamed by what they trust in; let them lie silently in their graves," 
Psalms31:18 their lying lips quieted at last—the lips of these arrogant men who are accusing honest men of evil deeds.“ 
Psalms31:19 Oh, how great is your goodness to those who publicly declare that you will rescue them. For you have stored up great blessings for those who trust and reverence you." 
Psalms31:20 Hide your loved ones in the shelter of your presence, safe beneath your hand, safe from all conspiring men." 
Psalms31:21 Blessed is the Lord, for he has shown me that his never-failing love protects me like the walls of a fort!" 
Psalms31:22 I spoke too hastily when I said, “The Lord has deserted me,“ for you listened to my plea and answered me." 
Psalms31:23 Oh, love the Lord, all of you who are his people; for the Lord protects those who are loyal to him, but harshly punishes all who haughtily reject him." 
Psalms31:24 So cheer up! Take courage if you are depending on the Lord. 
Psalms32:1 What happiness for those whose guilt has been forgiven! What joys when sins are covered over! 
Psalms32:2 What relief for those who have confessed their sins and God has cleared their record. 
Psalms32:3 There was a time when I wouldn't admit what a sinner I was. But my dishonesty made me miserable and filled my days with frustration. 
Psalms32:4 All day and all night your hand was heavy on me. My strength evaporated like water on a sunny day 
Psalms32:5 until I finally admitted all my sins to you and stopped trying to hide them. I said to myself, “I will confess them to the Lord.“ And you forgave me! All my guilt is gone." 
Psalms32:6 Now I say that each believer should confess his sins to God when he is aware of them, while there is time to be forgiven. Judgment will not touch him if he does." 
Psalms32:7 You are my hiding place from every storm of life; you even keep me from getting into trouble! You surround me with songs of victory. 
Psalms32:8 I will instruct you (says the Lord) and guide you along the best pathway for your life; I will advise you and watch your progress. 
Psalms32:9 Don't be like a senseless horse or mule that has to have a bit in its mouth to keep it in line! 
Psalms32:10 Many sorrows come to the wicked, but abiding love surrounds those who trust in the Lord." 
Psalms32:11 So rejoice in him, all those who are his, and shout for joy, all those who try to obey him." 
Psalms33:1 Let all the joys of the godly well up in praise to the Lord, for it is right to praise him." 
Psalms33:2 Play joyous melodies of praise upon the lyre and on the harp. 
Psalms33:3 Compose new songs of praise to him, accompanied skillfully on the harp; sing joyfully." 
Psalms33:4 For all God's words are right, and everything he does is worthy of our trust." 
Psalms33:5 He loves whatever is just and good; the earth is filled with his tender love. 
Psalms33:6 He merely spoke, and the heavens were formed and all the galaxies of stars." 
Psalms33:7 He made the oceans, pouring them into his vast reservoirs." 
Psalms33:8 Let everyone in all the world—men, women and children—fear the Lord and stand in awe of him." 
Psalms33:9 For when he but spoke, the world began! It appeared at his command!" 
Psalms33:10 And with a breath he can scatter the plans of all the nations who oppose him," 
Psalms33:11 but his own plan stands forever. His intentions are the same for every generation. 
Psalms33:12 Blessed is the nation whose God is the Lord, whose people he has chosen as his own." 
Psalms33:13 The Lord gazes down upon mankind from 
Psalms33:14 heaven where he lives. He has made 
Psalms33:15 their hearts and closely watches everything they do. 
Psalms33:16 The best-equipped army cannot save a king—for great strength is not enough to save anyone. 
Psalms33:17 A war horse is a poor risk for winning victories—it is strong, but it cannot save." 
Psalms33:18 But the eyes of the Lord are watching over those who fear him, who rely upon his steady love." 
Psalms33:19 He will keep them from death even in times of famine! 
Psalms33:20 We depend upon the Lord alone to save us. Only he can help us; he protects us like a shield. 
Psalms33:21 No wonder we are happy in the Lord! For we are trusting him. We trust his holy name. 
Psalms33:22 Yes, Lord, let your constant love surround us, for our hopes are in you alone." 
Psalms34:1 I will praise the Lord no matter what happens. I will constantly speak of his glories and grace. 
Psalms34:2 I will boast of all his kindness to me. Let all who are discouraged take heart. 
Psalms34:3 Let us praise the Lord together and exalt his name. 
Psalms34:4 For I cried to him and he answered me! He freed me from all my fears. 
Psalms34:5 Others too were radiant at what he did for them. Theirs was no downcast look of rejection! 
Psalms34:6 This poor man cried to the Lord—and the Lord heard him and saved him out of his troubles. 
Psalms34:7 For the Angel of the Lord guards and rescues all who reverence him. 
Psalms34:8 Oh, put God to the test and see how kind he is! See for yourself the way his mercies shower down on all who trust in him." 
Psalms34:9 If you belong to the Lord, reverence him; for everyone who does this has everything he needs." 
Psalms34:10 Even strong young lions sometimes go hungry, but those of us who reverence the Lord will never lack any good thing." 
Psalms34:11 Sons and daughters, come and listen and let me teach you the importance of trusting and fearing the Lord." 
Psalms34:12 Do you want a long, good life?" 
Psalms34:13 Then watch your tongue! Keep your lips from lying. 
Psalms34:14 Turn from all known sin and spend your time in doing good. Try to live in peace with everyone; work hard at it. 
Psalms34:15 For the eyes of the Lord are intently watching all who live good lives, and he gives attention when they cry to him." 
Psalms34:16 But the Lord has made up his mind to wipe out even the memory of evil men from the earth. 
Psalms34:17 Yes, the Lord hears the good man when he calls to him for help and saves him out of all his troubles." 
Psalms34:18 The Lord is close to those whose hearts are breaking; he rescues those who are humbly sorry for their sins. 
Psalms34:19 The good man does not escape all troubles—he has them too. But the Lord helps him in each and every one. 
Psalms34:20 Not one of his bones is broken. 
Psalms34:21 Calamity will surely overtake the wicked; heavy penalties are meted out to those who hate the good. 
Psalms34:22 But as for those who serve the Lord, he will redeem them; everyone who takes refuge in him will be freely pardoned." 
Psalms35:1 O Lord, fight those fighting me; declare war on them for their attacks on me." 
Psalms35:2 Put on your armor, take your shield and protect me by standing in front." 
Psalms35:3 Lift your spear in my defense, for my pursuers are getting very close. Let me hear you say that you will save me from them." 
Psalms35:4 Dishonor those who are trying to kill me. Turn them back and confuse them. 
Psalms35:5 Blow them away like chaff in the wind—wind sent by the Angel of the Lord. 
Psalms35:6 Make their path dark and slippery before them, with the Angel of the Lord pursuing them." 
Psalms35:7 For though I did them no wrong, yet they laid a trap for me and dug a pitfall in my path." 
Psalms35:8 Let them be overtaken by sudden ruin, caught in their own net and destroyed." 
Psalms35:9 But I will rejoice in the Lord. He shall rescue me! 
Psalms35:10 From the bottom of my heart praise rises to him. Where is his equal in all of heaven and earth? Who else protects the weak and helpless from the strong, and the poor and needy from those who would rob them?" 
Psalms35:11 These evil men swear to a lie. They accuse me of things I have never even heard about. 
Psalms35:12 I do them good, but they return me harm. I am sinking down to death." 
Psalms35:13 When they were ill, I mourned before the Lord in sackcloth, asking him to make them well; I refused to eat; I prayed for them with utmost earnestness, but God did not listen." 
Psalms35:14 I went about sadly as though it were my mother, friend, or brother who was sick and nearing death." 
Psalms35:15 But now that I am in trouble they are glad; they come together in meetings filled with slander against me—I didn't even know some of those who were there. 
Psalms35:16 For they gather with the worthless fellows of the town and spend their time cursing me. 
Psalms35:17 Lord, how long will you stand there, doing nothing? Act now and rescue me, for I have but one life and these young lions are out to get it." 
Psalms35:18 Save me, and I will thank you publicly before the entire congregation, before the largest crowd I can find." 
Psalms35:19 Don't give victory to those who fight me without any reason! Don't let them rejoice at my fall—let them die. 
Psalms35:20 They don't talk of peace and doing good, but of plots against innocent men who are minding their own business." 
Psalms35:21 They shout that they have seen me doing wrong! “Aha!“ they say. “With our own eyes we saw him do it.“ 
Psalms35:22 Lord, you know all about it. Don't stay silent! Don't desert me now!" 
Psalms35:23 Rise up, O Lord my God; vindicate me." 
Psalms35:24 Declare me “not guilty,“ for you are just. Don't let my enemies rejoice over me in my troubles." 
Psalms35:25 Don't let them say, “Aha! Our dearest wish against him will soon be fulfilled!“ and, “At last we have him!“" 
Psalms35:26 Shame them; let these who boast against me and who rejoice at my troubles be themselves overcome by misfortune that strips them bare of everything they own. Bare them to dishonor. 
Psalms35:27 But give great joy to all who wish me well. Let them shout with delight, “Great is the Lord who enjoys helping his child!“" 
Psalms35:28 And I will tell everyone how great and good you are; I will praise you all day long. 
Psalms36:1 Sin lurks deep in the hearts of the wicked, forever urging them on to evil deeds. They have no fear of God to hold them back." 
Psalms36:2 Instead, in their conceit, they think they can hide their evil deeds and not get caught." 
Psalms36:3 Everything they say is crooked and deceitful; they are no longer wise and good. 
Psalms36:4 They lie awake at night to hatch their evil plots instead of planning how to keep away from wrong. 
Psalms36:5 Your steadfast love, O Lord, is as great as all the heavens. Your faithfulness reaches beyond the clouds." 
Psalms36:6 Your justice is as solid as God's mountains. Your decisions are as full of wisdom as the oceans are with water. You are concerned for men and animals alike. 
Psalms36:7 How precious is your constant love, O God! All humanity takes refuge in the shadow of your wings." 
Psalms36:8 You feed them with blessings from your own table and let them drink from your rivers of delight. 
Psalms36:9 For you are the Fountain of life; our light is from your light. 
Psalms36:10 Pour out your unfailing love on those who know you! Never stop giving your blessings to those who long to do your will. 
Psalms36:11 Don't let these proud men trample me. Don't let their wicked hands push me around. 
Psalms36:12 Look! They have fallen. They are thrown down and will not rise again. 
Psalms37:1 Never envy the wicked! 
Psalms37:2 Soon they fade away like grass and disappear. 
Psalms37:3 Trust in the Lord instead. Be kind and good to others; then you will live safely here in the land and prosper, feeding in safety." 
Psalms37:4 Be delighted with the Lord. Then he will give you all your heart's desires. 
Psalms37:5 Commit everything you do to the Lord. Trust him to help you do it, and he will." 
Psalms37:6 Your innocence will be clear to everyone. He will vindicate you with the blazing light of justice shining down as from the noonday sun. 
Psalms37:7 Rest in the Lord; wait patiently for him to act. Don't be envious of evil men who prosper. 
Psalms37:8 Stop your anger! Turn off your wrath. Don't fret and worry—it only leads to harm. 
Psalms37:9 For the wicked shall be destroyed, but those who trust the Lord shall be given every blessing." 
Psalms37:10 Only a little while and the wicked shall disappear. You will look for them in vain. 
Psalms37:11 But all who humble themselves before the Lord shall be given every blessing and shall have wonderful peace. 
Psalms37:12 The Lord is laughing at those who plot against 
Psalms37:13 the godly, for he knows their judgment day is coming." 
Psalms37:14 Evil men take aim to slay the poor; they are ready to butcher those who do right. 
Psalms37:15 But their swords will be plunged into their own hearts, and all their weapons will be broken." 
Psalms37:16 It is better to have little and be godly than to own an evil man's wealth; 
Psalms37:17 for the strength of evil men shall be broken, but the Lord takes care of those he has forgiven." 
Psalms37:18 Day by day the Lord observes the good deeds done by godly men, and gives them eternal rewards." 
Psalms37:19 He cares for them when times are hard; even in famine, they will have enough." 
Psalms37:20 But evil men shall perish. These enemies of God will wither like grass and disappear like smoke. 
Psalms37:21 Evil men borrow and “cannot pay it back“! But the good man returns what he owes with some extra besides. 
Psalms37:22 Those blessed by the Lord shall inherit the earth, but those cursed by him shall die." 
Psalms37:23 The steps of good men are directed by the Lord. He delights in each step they take. 
Psalms37:24 If they fall it isn't fatal, for the Lord holds them with his hand." 
Psalms37:25 I have been young and now I am old. And in all my years I have never seen the Lord forsake a man who loves him; nor have I seen the children of the godly go hungry. 
Psalms37:26 Instead, the godly are able to be generous with their gifts and loans to others, and their children are a blessing." 
Psalms37:27 So if you want an eternal home, leave your evil, low-down ways and live good lives." 
Psalms37:28 For the Lord loves justice and fairness; he will never abandon his people. They will be kept safe forever; but all who love wickedness shall perish. 
Psalms37:29 The godly shall be firmly planted in the land and live there forever. 
Psalms37:30 The godly man is a good counselor because he 
Psalms37:31 is just and fair and knows right from wrong. 
Psalms37:32 Evil men spy on the godly, waiting for an excuse to accuse them and then demanding their death." 
Psalms37:33 But the Lord will not let these evil men succeed, nor let the godly be condemned when they are brought before the judge." 
Psalms37:34 Don't be impatient for the Lord to act! Keep traveling steadily along his pathway and in due season he will honor you with every blessing, and you will see the wicked destroyed." 
Psalms37:35 I myself have seen it happen: a proud and evil man, towering like a cedar of Lebanon, but when I looked again, he was gone!" 
Psalms37:36 I searched but could not find him! 
Psalms37:37 But the good man—what a different story! For the good man—the blameless, the upright, the man of peace—he has a wonderful future ahead of him. For him there is a happy ending." 
Psalms37:38 But evil men shall be destroyed, and their posterity shall be cut off." 
Psalms37:39 The Lord saves the godly! He is their salvation and their refuge when trouble comes. 
Psalms37:40 Because they trust in him, he helps them and delivers them from the plots of evil men." 
Psalms38:1 O Lord, don't punish me while you are angry!" 
Psalms38:2 Your arrows have struck deep; your blows are crushing me. 
Psalms38:3 Because of your anger, my body is sick, my health is broken beneath my sins." 
Psalms38:4 They are like a flood, higher than my head; they are a burden too heavy to bear." 
Psalms38:5 My wounds are festering and full of pus. Because of my sins, I am bent and racked with pain." 
Psalms38:6 My days are filled with anguish. 
Psalms38:7 My loins burn with inflammation, and my whole body is diseased." 
Psalms38:8 I am exhausted and crushed; I groan in despair. 
Psalms38:9 Lord, you know how I long for my health once more. You hear my every sigh." 
Psalms38:10 My heart beats wildly, my strength fails, and I am going blind." 
Psalms38:11 My loved ones and friends stay away, fearing my disease. Even my own family stands at a distance." 
Psalms38:12 Meanwhile my enemies are trying to kill me. They plot my ruin and spend all their waking hours planning treachery. 
Psalms38:13 But I am deaf to all their threats; I am silent before them as a man who cannot speak. 
Psalms38:14 I have nothing to say. 
Psalms38:15 For I am waiting for you, O Lord my God. Come and protect me." 
Psalms38:16 Put an end to their arrogance, these who gloat when I am cast down!" 
Psalms38:17 How constantly I find myself upon the verge of sin; this source of sorrow always stares me in the face. 
Psalms38:18 I confess my sins; I am sorry for what I have done. 
Psalms38:19 But my enemies persecute with vigor and continue to hate me—though I have done nothing against them to deserve it. 
Psalms38:20 They repay me evil for good and hate me for standing for the right. 
Psalms38:21 Don't leave me, Lord; don't go away!" 
Psalms38:22 Come quickly! Help me, O my Savior." 
Psalms39:1 I said to myself, I'm going to quit complaining! I'll keep quiet, especially when the ungodly are around me." 
Psalms39:2 But as I stood there silently the turmoil within me grew to the bursting point. 
Psalms39:3 The more I mused, the hotter the fires inside. Then at last I spoke and pled with God:" 
Psalms39:4 Lord, help me to realize how brief my time on earth will be. Help me to know that I am here for but a moment more." 
Psalms39:5 My life is no longer than my hand! My whole lifetime is but a moment to you. Proud man! Frail as breath! 
Psalms39:6 A shadow! And all his busy rushing ends in nothing. He heaps up riches for someone else to spend. 
Psalms39:7 And so, Lord, my only hope is in you." 
Psalms39:8 Save me from being overpowered by my sins, for even fools will mock me then." 
Psalms39:9 Lord, I am speechless before you. I will not open my mouth to speak one word of complaint, for my punishment is from you." 
Psalms39:10 Lord, don't hit me anymore—I am exhausted beneath your hand." 
Psalms39:11 When you punish a man for his sins, he is destroyed, for he is as fragile as a moth-infested cloth; yes, man is frail as breath." 
Psalms39:12 Hear my prayer, O Lord; listen to my cry! Don't sit back, unmindful of my tears. For I am your guest. I am a traveler passing through the earth, as all my fathers were." 
Psalms39:13 Spare me, Lord! Let me recover and be filled with happiness again before my death." 
Psalms40:1 I waited patiently for God to help me; then he listened and heard my cry. 
Psalms40:2 He lifted me out of the pit of despair, out from the bog and the mire, and set my feet on a hard, firm path, and steadied me as I walked along." 
Psalms40:3 He has given me a new song to sing, of praises to our God. Now many will hear of the glorious things he did for me, and stand in awe before the Lord, and put their trust in him." 
Psalms40:4 Many blessings are given to those who trust the Lord and have no confidence in those who are proud or who trust in idols. 
Psalms40:5 O Lord my God, many and many a time you have done great miracles for us, and we are ever in your thoughts. Who else can do such glorious things? No one else can be compared with you. There isn't time to tell of all your wonderful deeds." 
Psalms40:6 It isn't sacrifices and offerings that you really want from your people. Burnt animals bring no special joy to your heart. But you have accepted the offer of my life-long service. 
Psalms40:7 Then I said, “See, I have come, just as all the prophets foretold." 
Psalms40:8 And I delight to do your will, my God, for your law is written upon my heart!“" 
Psalms40:9 I have told everyone the good news that you forgive people's sins. I have not been timid about it, as you well know, O Lord." 
Psalms40:10 I have not kept this good news hidden in my heart, but have proclaimed your loving-kindness and truth to all the congregation." 
Psalms40:11 O Lord, don't hold back your tender mercies from me! My only hope is in your love and faithfulness." 
Psalms40:12 Otherwise I perish, for problems far too big for me to solve are piled higher than my head. Meanwhile my sins, too many to count, have all caught up with me, and I am ashamed to look up. My heart quails within me." 
Psalms40:13 Please, Lord, rescue me! Quick! Come and help me!" 
Psalms40:14 Confuse them! Turn them around and send them sprawling—all these who are trying to destroy me. 
Psalms40:15 Disgrace these scoffers with their utter failure! 
Psalms40:16 But may the joy of the Lord be given to everyone who loves him and his salvation. May they constantly exclaim, “How great God is!“" 
Psalms40:17 I am poor and weak, yet the Lord is thinking about me right now! O my God, you are my helper. You are my Savior; come quickly, and save me. Please don't delay!" 
Psalms41:1 God blesses those who are kind to the poor. He helps them out of their troubles. 
Psalms41:2 He protects them and keeps them alive; he publicly honors them and destroys the power of their enemies. 
Psalms41:3 He nurses them when they are sick and soothes their pains and worries. 
Psalms41:4 “O Lord,“ I prayed, “be kind and heal me, for I have confessed my sins.“" 
Psalms41:5 But my enemies say, “May he soon die and be forgotten!“" 
Psalms41:6 They act so friendly when they come to visit me while I am sick; but all the time they hate me and are glad that I am lying there upon my bed of pain. And when they leave, they laugh and mock." 
Psalms41:7 They whisper together about what they will do when I am dead. 
Psalms41:8 “It's fatal, whatever it is,“ they say. “He'll never get out of that bed!“" 
Psalms41:9 Even my best friend has turned against me—a man I completely trusted; how often we ate together. 
Psalms41:10 Lord, don't you desert me! Be gracious, Lord, and make me well again so I can pay them back!" 
Psalms41:11 I know you are pleased with me because you haven't let my enemies triumph over me. 
Psalms41:12 You have preserved me because I was honest; you have admitted me forever to your presence. 
Psalms41:13 Bless the Lord, the God of Israel, who exists from everlasting ages past—and on into everlasting eternity ahead. Amen and amen!" 
Psalms42:1 As the deer pants for water, so I long for you, O God." 
Psalms42:2 I thirst for God, the living God. Where can I find him to come and stand before him?" 
Psalms42:3 Day and night I weep for his help, and all the while my enemies taunt me. “Where is this God of yours?“ they scoff." 
Psalms42:4 Take courage, my soul! Do you remember those times (but how could you ever forget them!) when you led a great procession to the Temple on festival days, singing with joy, praising the Lord?" 
Psalms42:5 Why then be downcast? Why be discouraged and sad? Hope in God! I shall yet praise him again. Yes, I shall again praise him for his help." 
Psalms42:6 Yet I am standing here depressed and gloomy, but I will meditate upon your kindness to this lovely land where the Jordan River flows and where Mount Hermon and Mount Mizar stand." 
Psalms42:7 All your waves and billows have gone over me, and floods of sorrow pour upon me like a thundering cataract." 
Psalms42:8 Yet day by day the Lord also pours out his steadfast love upon me, and through the night I sing his songs and pray to God who gives me life." 
Psalms42:9 “O God my Rock,“ I cry, “why have you forsaken me? Why must I suffer these attacks from my enemies?“" 
Psalms42:10 Their taunts pierce me like a fatal wound; again and again they scoff, “Where is that God of yours?“" 
Psalms42:11 But, O my soul, don't be discouraged. Don't be upset. Expect God to act! For I know that I shall again have plenty of reason to praise him for all that he will do. He is my help! He is my God!" 
Psalms43:1 O God, defend me from the charges of these merciless, deceitful men." 
Psalms43:2 For you are God, my only place of refuge. Why have you tossed me aside? Why must I mourn at the oppression of my enemies?" 
Psalms43:3 Oh, send out your light and your truth—let them lead me. Let them lead me to your Temple on your holy mountain, Zion." 
Psalms43:4 There I will go to the altar of God, my exceeding joy, and praise him with my harp. O God—my God!" 
Psalms43:5 O my soul, why be so gloomy and discouraged? Trust in God! I shall again praise him for his wondrous help; he will make me smile again, for he is my God!" 
Psalms44:1 O God, we have heard of the glorious miracles you did in the days of long ago." 
Psalms44:2 Our forefathers have told us how you drove the heathen nations from this land and gave it all to us, spreading Israel from one end of the country to the other." 
Psalms44:3 They did not conquer by their own strength and skill, but by your mighty power and because you smiled upon them and favored them." 
Psalms44:4 You are my King and my God. Decree victories for your people. 
Psalms44:5 For it is only by your power and through your name that we tread down our enemies; 
Psalms44:6 I do not trust my weapons. They could never save me. 
Psalms44:7 Only you can give us the victory over those who hate us. 
Psalms44:8 My constant boast is God. I can never thank you enough! 
Psalms44:9 And yet for a time, O Lord, you have tossed us aside in dishonor and have not helped us in our battles." 
Psalms44:10 You have actually fought against us and defeated us before our foes. Our enemies have invaded our land and pillaged the countryside. 
Psalms44:11 You have treated us like sheep in a slaughter pen and scattered us among the nations. 
Psalms44:12 You sold us for a pittance. You valued us at nothing at all. 
Psalms44:13 The neighboring nations laugh and mock at us because of all the evil you have sent. 
Psalms44:14 You have made the word Jew a byword of contempt and shame among the nations, disliked by all." 
Psalms44:15 I am constantly despised, mocked, taunted," 
Psalms44:16 and cursed by my vengeful enemies. 
Psalms44:17 And all this has happened, Lord, despite our loyalty to you. We have not violated your covenant." 
Psalms44:18 Our hearts have not deserted you! We have not left your path by a single step. 
Psalms44:19 If we had, we could understand your punishing us in the barren wilderness and sending us into darkness and death." 
Psalms44:20 If we had turned away from worshiping our God and were worshiping idols," 
Psalms44:21 would God not know it? Yes, he knows the secrets of every heart." 
Psalms44:22 But that is not our case. For we are facing death threats constantly because of serving you! We are like sheep awaiting slaughter. 
Psalms44:23 Waken! Rouse yourself! Don't sleep, O Lord! Are we cast off forever?" 
Psalms44:24 Why do you look the other way? Why do you ignore our sorrows and oppression? 
Psalms44:25 We lie face downward in the dust. 
Psalms44:26 Rise up, O Lord, and come and help us. Save us by your constant love." 
Psalms45:1 My heart is overflowing with a beautiful thought! I will write a lovely poem to the King, for I am as full of words as the speediest writer pouring out his story." 
Psalms45:2 You are the fairest of all; Your words are filled with grace; God himself is blessing you forever. 
Psalms45:3 Arm yourself, O Mighty One, So glorious, so majestic!" 
Psalms45:4 And in your majesty Go on to victory, Defending truth, humility, and justice. Go forth to awe-inspiring deeds!" 
Psalms45:5 Your arrows are sharp In your enemies' hearts; They fall before you. 
Psalms45:6 Your throne, O God, endures forever. Justice is your royal scepter." 
Psalms45:7 You love what is good And hate what is wrong. Therefore God, your God, Has given you more gladness Than anyone else." 
Psalms45:8 Your robes are perfumed with myrrh, aloes, and cassia. In your palaces of inlaid ivory, lovely music is being played for your enjoyment." 
Psalms45:9 Kings' daughters are among your concubines. Standing beside you is the queen, wearing jewelry of finest gold from Ophir." 
Psalms45:10 “I advise you, O daughter, not to fret about your parents in your homeland far away." 
Psalms45:11 Your royal husband delights in your beauty. Reverence him, for he is your lord." 
Psalms45:12 The people of Tyre, the richest people of our day, will shower you with gifts and entreat your favors.“" 
Psalms45:13 The bride, a princess, waits within her chamber, robed in beautiful clothing woven with gold." 
Psalms45:14 Lovely she is, led beside her maids of honor to the king!" 
Psalms45:15 What a joyful, glad procession as they enter in the palace gates!" 
Psalms45:16 “Your sons will some day be kings like their father. They shall sit on thrones around the world! 
Psalms45:17 “I will cause your name to be honored in all generations; the nations of the earth will praise you forever.“ 
Psalms46:1 God is our refuge and strength, a tested help in times of trouble." 
Psalms46:2 And so we need not fear even if the world blows up and the mountains crumble into the sea. 
Psalms46:3 Let the oceans roar and foam; let the mountains tremble! 
Psalms46:4 There is a river of joy flowing through the city of our God—the sacred home of the God above all gods. 
Psalms46:5 God himself is living in that city; therefore it stands unmoved despite the turmoil everywhere. He will not delay his help. 
Psalms46:6 The nations rant and rave in anger—but when God speaks, the earth melts in submission and kingdoms totter into ruin." 
Psalms46:7 The Commander of the armies of heaven is here among us. He, the God of Jacob, has come to rescue us." 
Psalms46:8 Come, see the glorious things that our God does, how he brings ruin upon the world" 
Psalms46:9 and causes wars to end throughout the earth, breaking and burning every weapon." 
Psalms46:10 “Stand silent! Know that I am God! I will be honored by every nation in the world!“ 
Psalms46:11 The Commander of the heavenly armies is here among us! He, the God of Jacob, has come to rescue us!" 
Psalms47:1 Come, everyone, and clap for joy! Shout triumphant praises to the Lord!" 
Psalms47:2 For the Lord, the God above all gods, is awesome beyond words; he is the great King of all the earth." 
Psalms47:3 He subdues the nations before us 
Psalms47:4 and will personally select his choicest blessings for his Jewish people—the very best for those he loves. 
Psalms47:5 God has ascended with a mighty shout, with trumpets blaring." 
Psalms47:6 Sing out your praises to our God, our King. Yes, sing your highest praises to our King, the King of all the earth." 
Psalms47:7 Sing thoughtful praises! 
Psalms47:8 He reigns above the nations, sitting on his holy throne." 
Psalms47:9 The Gentile rulers of the world have joined with us in praising him—praising the God of Abraham—for the battle shields of all the armies of the world are his trophies. He is highly honored everywhere. 
Psalms48:1 How great is the Lord! How much we should praise him. He lives upon Mount Zion in Jerusalem. 
Psalms48:2 What a glorious sight! See Mount Zion rising north of the city high above the plains for all to see—Mount Zion, joy of all the earth, the residence of the great King." 
Psalms48:3 God himself is the defender of Jerusalem. 
Psalms48:4 The kings of the earth have arrived together to inspect the city. 
Psalms48:5 They marvel at the sight and hurry home again," 
Psalms48:6 afraid of what they have seen; they are filled with panic like a woman in travail! 
Psalms48:7 For God destroys the mightiest warships with a breath of wind. 
Psalms48:8 We have heard of the city's glory—the city of our God, the Commander of the armies of heaven. And now we see it for ourselves! God has established Jerusalem forever." 
Psalms48:9 Lord, here in your Temple we meditate upon your kindness and your love." 
Psalms48:10 Your name is known throughout the earth, O God. You are praised everywhere for the salvation you have scattered throughout the world." 
Psalms48:11 O Jerusalem, rejoice! O people of Judah, rejoice! For God will see to it that you are finally treated fairly." 
Psalms48:12 Go, inspect the city! Walk around and count her many towers!" 
Psalms48:13 Note her walls and tour her palaces so that you can tell your children. 
Psalms48:14 For this great God is our God forever and ever. He will be our guide until we die. 
Psalms49:1 Listen, everyone!" 
Psalms49:2 High and low, rich and poor, all around the world—listen to my words," 
Psalms49:3 for they are wise and filled with insight. 
Psalms49:4 I will tell in song accompanied by harps the answer to one of life's most perplexing problems: 
Psalms49:5 There is no need to fear when times of trouble come, even though surrounded by enemies!" 
Psalms49:6 They trust in their wealth and boast about how rich they are," 
Psalms49:7 yet not one of them, though rich as kings, can ransom his own brother from the penalty of sin! For God's forgiveness does not come that way." 
Psalms49:8 For a soul is far too precious to be ransomed by mere earthly wealth. 
Psalms49:9 There is not enough of it in all the earth to buy eternal life for just one soul, to keep it out of hell." 
Psalms49:10 Rich man! Proud man! Wise man! You must die like all the rest! You have no greater lease on life than foolish, stupid men. You must leave your wealth to others." 
Psalms49:11 You name your estates after yourselves as though your lands could be forever yours and you could live on them eternally. 
Psalms49:12 But man with all his pomp must die like any animal. 
Psalms49:13 Such is the folly of these men, though after they die they will be quoted as having great wisdom." 
Psalms49:14 Death is the shepherd of all mankind. And “in the morning“ those who are evil will be the slaves of those who are good. For the power of their wealth is gone when they die, they cannot take it with them." 
Psalms49:15 But as for me, God will redeem my soul from the power of death, for he will receive me." 
Psalms49:16 So do not be dismayed when evil men grow rich and build their lovely homes. 
Psalms49:17 For when they die, they carry nothing with them! Their honors will not follow them." 
Psalms49:18 Though a man calls himself happy all through his life—and the world loudly applauds success— 
Psalms49:19 yet in the end he dies like everyone else and enters eternal darkness. 
Psalms49:20 For man with all his pomp must die like any animal. 
Psalms50:1 The mighty God, the Lord, has summoned all mankind from east to west!" 
Psalms50:2 God's glory-light shines from the beautiful Temple on Mount Zion. 
Psalms50:3 He comes with the noise of thunder, surrounded by devastating fire; a great storm rages round about him." 
Psalms50:4 He has come to judge his people. To heaven and earth he shouts," 
Psalms50:5 “Gather together my own people who by their sacrifice upon my altar have promised to obey me.“ 
Psalms50:6 God will judge them with complete fairness, for all heaven declares that he is just." 
Psalms50:7 O my people, listen! For I am your God. Listen! Here are my charges against you:" 
Psalms50:8 I have no complaint about the sacrifices you bring to my altar, for you bring them regularly." 
Psalms50:9 But it isn't sacrificial bullocks and goats that I really want from you. 
Psalms50:10 For all the animals of field and forest are mine! The cattle on a thousand hills! 
Psalms50:11 And all the birds upon the mountains! 
Psalms50:12 If I were hungry, I would not mention it to you—for all the world is mine and everything in it." 
Psalms50:13 No, I don't need your sacrifices of flesh and blood." 
Psalms50:14 What I want from you is your true thanks; I want your promises fulfilled. 
Psalms50:15 I want you to trust me in your times of trouble, so I can rescue you and you can give me glory." 
Psalms50:16 But God says to evil men: Recite my laws no longer and stop claiming my promises," 
Psalms50:17 for you have refused my discipline, disregarding my laws." 
Psalms50:18 You see a thief and help him, and spend your time with evil and immoral men." 
Psalms50:19 You curse and lie, and vile language streams from your mouths." 
Psalms50:20 You slander your own brother. 
Psalms50:21 I remained silent—you thought I didn't care—but now your time of punishment has come, and I list all the above charges against you." 
Psalms50:22 This is the last chance for all of you who have forgotten God, before I tear you apart—and no one can help you then." 
Psalms50:23 But true praise is a worthy sacrifice; this really honors me. Those who walk my paths will receive salvation from the Lord. 
Psalms51:1 Written after Nathan the prophet had come to inform David of God's judgment against him because of his adultery with Bathsheba, and his murder of Uriah, her husband. O loving and kind God, have mercy. Have pity upon me and take away the awful stain of my transgressions." 
Psalms51:2 Oh, wash me, cleanse me from this guilt. Let me be pure again." 
Psalms51:3 For I admit my shameful deed—it haunts me day and night. 
Psalms51:4 It is against you and you alone I sinned and did this terrible thing. You saw it all, and your sentence against me is just." 
Psalms51:5 But I was born a sinner, yes, from the moment my mother conceived me." 
Psalms51:6 You deserve honesty from the heart; yes, utter sincerity and truthfulness. Oh, give me this wisdom." 
Psalms51:7 Sprinkle me with the cleansing blood and I shall be clean again. Wash me and I shall be whiter than snow. 
Psalms51:8 And after you have punished me, give me back my joy again." 
Psalms51:9 Don't keep looking at my sins—erase them from your sight. 
Psalms51:10 Create in me a new, clean heart, O God, filled with clean thoughts and right desires." 
Psalms51:11 Don't toss me aside, banished forever from your presence. Don't take your Holy Spirit from me." 
Psalms51:12 Restore to me again the joy of your salvation, and make me willing to obey you." 
Psalms51:13 Then I will teach your ways to other sinners, and they—guilty like me—will repent and return to you." 
Psalms51:14 Don't sentence me to death. O my God, you alone can rescue me." 
Psalms51:15 Then I will sing of your forgiveness, for my lips will be unsealed—oh, how I will praise you." 
Psalms51:16 You don't want penance; if you did, how gladly I would do it! You aren't interested in offerings burned before you on the altar." 
Psalms51:17 It is a broken spirit you want—remorse and penitence. A broken and a contrite heart, O God, you will not ignore." 
Psalms51:18 And Lord, don't punish Israel for my sins—help your people and protect Jerusalem." 
Psalms51:19 And when my heart is right, then you will rejoice in the good that I do and in the bullocks I bring to sacrifice upon your altar." 
Psalms52:1 Written by David to protest against his enemy Doeg (1 Samuel 22), who later slaughtered eighty-five priests and their families. You call yourself a hero, do you? You boast about this evil deed of yours against God's people." 
Psalms52:2 You are sharp as a tack in plotting your evil tricks. 
Psalms52:3 How you love wickedness—far more than good! And lying more than truth! 
Psalms52:4 You love to slander—you love to say anything that will do harm, O man with the lying tongue." 
Psalms52:5 But God will strike you down, pull you from your home, and drag you away from the land of the living." 
Psalms52:6 The followers of God will see it happen. They will watch in awe. Then they will laugh and say," 
Psalms52:7 “See what happens to those who despise God and trust in their wealth, and become ever more bold in their wickedness.“" 
Psalms52:8 But I am like a sheltered olive tree protected by the Lord himself. I trust in the mercy of God forever and ever. 
Psalms52:9 O Lord, I will praise you forever and ever for your punishment. And I will wait for your mercies—for everyone knows what a merciful God you are." 
Psalms53:1 Only a fool would say to himself, “There is no God.“ And why does he say it? Because of his wicked heart, his dark and evil deeds. His life is corroded with sin." 
Psalms53:2 God looks down from heaven, searching among all mankind to see if there is a single one who does right and really seeks for God." 
Psalms53:3 But all have turned their backs on him; they are filthy with sin—corrupt and rotten through and through. Not one is good, not one!" 
Psalms53:4 How can this be? Can't they understand anything? For they devour my people like bread and refuse to come to God. 
Psalms53:5 But soon unheard-of terror will fall on them. God will scatter the bones of these, your enemies. They are doomed, for God has rejected them." 
Psalms53:6 Oh, that God would come from Zion now and save Israel! Only when the Lord himself restores them can they ever be really happy again." 
Psalms54:1 Written by David at the time the men of Ziph tried to betray him to Saul. Come with great power, O God, and save me! Defend me with your might!" 
Psalms54:2 Oh, listen to my prayer." 
Psalms54:3 For violent men have risen against me—ruthless men who care nothing for God are seeking my life. 
Psalms54:4 But God is my helper. He is a friend of mine! 
Psalms54:5 He will cause the evil deeds of my enemies to boomerang upon them. Do as you promised and put an end to these wicked men, O God." 
Psalms54:6 Gladly I bring my sacrifices to you; I will praise your name, O Lord, for it is good." 
Psalms54:7 God has rescued me from all my trouble, and triumphed over my enemies." 
Psalms55:1 Listen to my prayer, O God; don't hide yourself when I cry to you." 
Psalms55:2 Hear me, Lord! Listen to me! For I groan and weep beneath my burden of woe." 
Psalms55:3 My enemies shout against me and threaten me with death. They surround me with terror and plot to kill me. Their fury and hatred rise to engulf me. 
Psalms55:4 My heart is in anguish within me. Stark fear overpowers me. 
Psalms55:5 Trembling and horror overwhelm me. 
Psalms55:6 Oh, for wings like a dove, to fly away and rest!" 
Psalms55:7 I would fly to the far-off deserts and stay there. 
Psalms55:8 I would flee to some refuge from all this storm. 
Psalms55:9 O Lord, make these enemies begin to quarrel among themselves—destroy them with their own violence and strife." 
Psalms55:10 Though they patrol their walls night and day against invaders, their real problem is internal—wickedness and dishonesty are entrenched in the heart of the city." 
Psalms55:11 There is murder and robbery there, and cheating in the markets and wherever you look." 
Psalms55:12 It was not an enemy who taunted me—then I could have borne it; I could have hidden and escaped. 
Psalms55:13 But it was you, a man like myself, my companion and my friend." 
Psalms55:14 What fellowship we had, what wonderful discussions as we walked together to the Temple of the Lord on holy days." 
Psalms55:15 Let death seize them and cut them down in their prime, for there is sin in their homes, and they are polluted to the depths of their souls." 
Psalms55:16 But I will call upon the Lord to save me—and he will. 
Psalms55:17 I will pray morning, noon, and night, pleading aloud with God; and he will hear and answer." 
Psalms55:18 Though the tide of battle runs strongly against me, for so many are fighting me, yet he will rescue me." 
Psalms55:19 God himself—God from everlasting ages past—will answer them! For they refuse to fear him or even honor his commands. 
Psalms55:20 This friend of mine betrayed me—I who was at peace with him. He broke his promises. 
Psalms55:21 His words were oily smooth, but in his heart was war. His words were sweet, but underneath were daggers." 
Psalms55:22 Give your burdens to the Lord. He will carry them. He will not permit the godly to slip or fall. 
Psalms55:23 He will send my enemies to the pit of destruction. Murderers and liars will not live out half their days. But I am trusting you to save me. 
Psalms56:1 Lord, have mercy on me; all day long the enemy troops press in." 
Psalms56:2 So many are proud to fight against me; how they long to conquer me. 
Psalms56:3 But when I am afraid, I will put my confidence in you. Yes, I will trust the promises of God." 
Psalms56:4 And since I am trusting him, what can mere man do to me?" 
Psalms56:5 They are always twisting what I say. All their thoughts are how to harm me. 
Psalms56:6 They meet together to perfect their plans; they hide beside the trail, listening for my steps, waiting to kill me." 
Psalms56:7 They expect to get away with it. Don't let them, Lord. In anger cast them to the ground." 
Psalms56:8 You have seen me tossing and turning through the night. You have collected all my tears and preserved them in your bottle! You have recorded every one in your book. 
Psalms56:9 The very day I call for help, the tide of battle turns. My enemies flee! This one thing I know: God is for me!" 
Psalms56:10 I am trusting God—oh, praise his promises!" 
Psalms56:11 I am not afraid of anything mere man can do to me! Yes, praise his promises." 
Psalms56:12 I will surely do what I have promised, Lord, and thank you for your help." 
Psalms56:13 For you have saved me from death and my feet from slipping, so that I can walk before the Lord in the land of the living." 
Psalms57:1 O God, have pity, for I am trusting you! I will hide beneath the shadow of your wings until this storm is past." 
Psalms57:2 I will cry to the God of heaven who does such wonders for me. 
Psalms57:3 He will send down help from heaven to save me because of his love and his faithfulness. He will rescue me from these liars who are so intent upon destroying me. 
Psalms57:4 I am surrounded by fierce lions—hotheads whose teeth are sharp as spears and arrows. Their tongues are like swords. 
Psalms57:5 Lord, be exalted above the highest heavens! Show your glory high above the earth." 
Psalms57:6 My enemies have set a trap for me. Frantic fear grips me. They have dug a pitfall in my path. But look! They themselves have fallen into it! 
Psalms57:7 O God, my heart is quiet and confident. No wonder I can sing your praises!" 
Psalms57:8 Rouse yourself, my soul! Arise, O harp and lyre! Let us greet the dawn with song!" 
Psalms57:9 I will thank you publicly throughout the land. I will sing your praises among the nations. 
Psalms57:10 Your kindness and love are as vast as the heavens. Your faithfulness is higher than the skies. 
Psalms57:11 Yes, be exalted, O God, above the heavens. May your glory shine throughout the earth." 
Psalms58:1 Justice? You high and mighty politicians don't even know the meaning of the word! 
Psalms58:2 Fairness? Which of you has any left? Not one! All your dealings are crooked: you give “justice“ in exchange for bribes. 
Psalms58:3 These men are born sinners, lying from their earliest words!" 
Psalms58:4 They are poisonous as deadly snakes, cobras that" 
Psalms58:5 close their ears to the most expert of charmers. 
Psalms58:6 O God, break off their fangs. Tear out the teeth of these young lions, Lord." 
Psalms58:7 Let them disappear like water into thirsty ground. Make their weapons useless in their hands. 
Psalms58:8 Let them be as snails that dissolve into slime and as those who die at birth, who never see the sun." 
Psalms58:9 God will sweep away both old and young. He will destroy them more quickly than a cooking pot can feel the blazing fire of thorns beneath it. 
Psalms58:10 The godly shall rejoice in the triumph of right; they shall walk the blood-stained fields of slaughtered, wicked men." 
Psalms58:11 Then at last everyone will know that good is rewarded, and that there is a God who judges justly here on earth." 
Psalms59:1 Written by David at the time King Saul set guards at his home to capture and kill him. (1 Samuel 19:11) O my God, save me from my enemies. Protect me from these who have come to destroy me." 
Psalms59:2 Preserve me from these criminals, these murderers." 
Psalms59:3 They lurk in ambush for my life. Strong men are out there waiting. And not, O Lord, because I've done them wrong." 
Psalms59:4 Yet they prepare to kill me. Lord, waken! See what is happening! Help me!" 
Psalms59:5 (And O Jehovah, God of heaven's armies, God of Israel, arise and punish the heathen nations surrounding us.) Do not spare these evil, treacherous men." 
Psalms59:6 At evening they come to spy, slinking around like dogs that prowl the city." 
Psalms59:7 I hear them shouting insults and cursing God, for “No one will hear us,“ they think." 
Psalms59:8 Lord, laugh at them! (And scoff at these surrounding nations too.)" 
Psalms59:9 O God my Strength! I will sing your praises, for you are my place of safety." 
Psalms59:10 My God is changeless in his love for me, and he will come and help me. He will let me see my wish come true upon my enemies." 
Psalms59:11 Don't kill them—for my people soon forget such lessons—but stagger them with your power and bring them to their knees. Bring them to the dust, O Lord our shield." 
Psalms59:12 They are proud, cursing liars." 
Psalms59:13 Angrily destroy them. Wipe them out. (And let the nations find out, too, that God rules in Israel and will reign throughout the world.)" 
Psalms59:14 Let these evil men slink back at evening and prowl the city 
Psalms59:15 all night before they are satisfied, howling like dogs and searching for food." 
Psalms59:16 But as for me, I will sing each morning about your power and mercy. For you have been my high tower of refuge, a place of safety in the day of my distress." 
Psalms59:17 O my Strength, to you I sing my praises; for you are my high tower of safety, my God of mercy." 
Psalms60:1 Written by David at the time he was at war with Syria, with the outcome still uncertain; this was when Joab, captain of his forces, slaughtered twelve thousand men of Edom in the Valley of Salt. O God, you have rejected us and broken our defenses; you have become angry and deserted us. Lord, restore us again to your favor." 
Psalms60:2 You have caused this nation to tremble in fear; you have torn it apart. Lord, heal it now, for it is shaken to its depths." 
Psalms60:3 You have been very hard on us and made us reel beneath your blows. 
Psalms60:4 But you have given us a banner to rally to; all who love truth will rally to it; then you can deliver your beloved people. 
Psalms60:5 Use your strong right arm to rescue us. 
Psalms60:6 God has promised to help us. He has vowed it by his holiness! No wonder I exult! 
Psalms60:7 “Shechem, Succoth, Gilead, Manasseh—still are mine!“ he says. “Judah shall continue to produce kings, and Ephraim great warriors." 
Psalms60:8 Moab shall become my lowly servant, and Edom my slave. And I will shout in triumph over the Philistines.“" 
Psalms60:9 Who will bring me in triumph into Edom's strong cities? God will! He who cast us off! 
Psalms60:10 He who abandoned us to our foes! 
Psalms60:11 Yes, Lord, help us against our enemies, for man's help is useless." 
Psalms60:12 With God's help we shall do mighty things, for he will trample down our foes." 
Psalms61:1 O God, listen to me! Hear my prayer!" 
Psalms61:2 For wherever I am, though far away at the ends of the earth, I will cry to you for help. When my heart is faint and overwhelmed, lead me to the mighty, towering Rock of safety." 
Psalms61:3 For you are my refuge, a high tower where my enemies can never reach me." 
Psalms61:4 I shall live forever in your tabernacle; oh, to be safe beneath the shelter of your wings!" 
Psalms61:5 For you have heard my vows, O God, to praise you every day, and you have given me the blessings you reserve for those who reverence your name." 
Psalms61:6 You will give me added years of life, as rich and full as those of many generations, all packed into one." 
Psalms61:7 And I shall live before the Lord forever. Oh, send your loving-kindness and truth to guard and watch over me," 
Psalms61:8 and I will praise your name continually, fulfilling my vow of praising you each day." 
Psalms62:1 I stand silently before the Lord, waiting for him to rescue me. For salvation comes from him alone." 
Psalms62:2 Yes, he alone is my Rock, my rescuer, defense and fortress. Why then should I be tense with fear when troubles come?" 
Psalms62:3 But what is this? They pick on me at a time when my throne is tottering; 
Psalms62:4 they plot my death and use lies and deceit to try to force me from the throne. They are so friendly to my face while cursing in their hearts! 
Psalms62:5 But I stand silently before the Lord, waiting for him to rescue me. For salvation comes from him alone." 
Psalms62:6 Yes, he alone is my Rock, my rescuer, defense, and fortress—why then should I be tense with fear when troubles come?" 
Psalms62:7 My protection and success come from God alone. He is my refuge, a Rock where no enemy can reach me." 
Psalms62:8 O my people, trust him all the time. Pour out your longings before him, for he can help!" 
Psalms62:9 The greatest of men or the lowest—both alike are nothing in his sight. They weigh less than air on scales. 
Psalms62:10 Don't become rich by extortion and robbery; if your riches increase, don't be proud." 
Psalms62:11 God has said it many times, that power belongs to him (and also, O Lord, steadfast love belongs to you)." 
Psalms62:12 He rewards each one of us according to what our works deserve. 
Psalms63:1 A Psalm of David when he was hiding in the wilderness of Judea. O God, my God! How I search for you! How I thirst for you in this parched and weary land where there is no water. How I long to find you!" 
Psalms63:2 How I wish I could go into your sanctuary to see your strength and glory," 
Psalms63:3 for your love and kindness are better to me than life itself. How I praise you! 
Psalms63:4 I will bless you as long as I live, lifting up my hands to you in prayer." 
Psalms63:5 At last I shall be fully satisfied; I will praise you with great joy. 
Psalms63:6 I lie awake at night thinking of you— 
Psalms63:7 of how much you have helped me—and how I rejoice through the night beneath the protecting shadow of your wings. 
Psalms63:8 I follow close behind you, protected by your strong right arm." 
Psalms63:9 But those plotting to destroy me shall go down to the depths of hell. 
Psalms63:10 They are doomed to die by the sword, to become the food of jackals." 
Psalms63:11 But I will rejoice in God. All who trust in him exult, while liars shall be silenced." 
Psalms64:1 Lord, listen to my complaint: Oh, preserve my life from" 
Psalms64:2 the conspiracy of these wicked men, these gangs of criminals." 
Psalms64:3 They cut me down with sharpened tongues; they aim their bitter words like arrows straight at my heart. 
Psalms64:4 They shoot from ambush at the innocent. Suddenly the deed is done, yet they are not afraid." 
Psalms64:5 They encourage each other to do evil. They meet in secret to set their traps. “He will never notice them here,“ they say." 
Psalms64:6 They keep a sharp lookout for opportunities of crime. They spend long hours with all their endless evil thoughts and plans. 
Psalms64:7 But God himself will shoot them down. Suddenly his arrow will pierce them. 
Psalms64:8 They will stagger backward, destroyed by those they spoke against. All who see it happening will scoff at them." 
Psalms64:9 Then everyone shall stand in awe and confess the greatness of the miracles of God; at last they will realize what amazing things he does. 
Psalms64:10 And the godly shall rejoice in the Lord, and trust and praise him." 
Psalms65:1 O God in Zion, we wait before you in silent praise, and thus fulfill our vow." 
Psalms65:2 And because you answer prayer, all mankind will come to you with their requests." 
Psalms65:3 Though sins fill our hearts, you forgive them all." 
Psalms65:4 How greatly to be envied are those you have chosen to come and live with you within the holy tabernacle courts! What joys await us among all the good things there. 
Psalms65:5 With dread deeds and awesome power you will defend us from our enemies, O God who saves us. You are the only hope of all mankind throughout the world and far away upon the sea." 
Psalms65:6 He formed the mountains by his mighty strength. 
Psalms65:7 He quiets the raging oceans and all the world's clamor. 
Psalms65:8 In the farthest corners of the earth the glorious acts of God shall startle everyone. The dawn and sunset shout for joy! 
Psalms65:9 He waters the earth to make it fertile. The rivers of God will not run dry! He prepares the earth for his people and sends them rich harvests of grain. 
Psalms65:10 He waters the furrows with abundant rain. Showers soften the earth, melting the clods and causing seeds to sprout across the land." 
Psalms65:11 Then he crowns it all with green, lush" 
Psalms65:12 pastures in the wilderness; hillsides blossom with joy. 
Psalms65:13 The pastures are filled with flocks of sheep, and the valleys are carpeted with grain. All the world shouts with joy and sings." 
Psalms66:1 Sing to the Lord, all the earth!" 
Psalms66:2 Sing of his glorious name! Tell the world how wonderful he is. 
Psalms66:3 How awe-inspiring are your deeds, O God! How great your power! No wonder your enemies surrender!" 
Psalms66:4 All the earth shall worship you and sing of your glories. 
Psalms66:5 Come, see the glorious things God has done. What marvelous miracles happen to his people!" 
Psalms66:6 He made a dry road through the sea for them. They went across on foot. What excitement and joy there was that day! 
Psalms66:7 Because of his great power he rules forever. He watches every movement of the nations. O rebel lands, he will deflate your pride." 
Psalms66:8 Let everyone bless God and sing his praises; 
Psalms66:9 for he holds our lives in his hands, and he holds our feet to the path." 
Psalms66:10 You have purified us with fire, O Lord, like silver in a crucible." 
Psalms66:11 You captured us in your net and laid great burdens on our backs. 
Psalms66:12 You sent troops to ride across our broken bodies. We went through fire and flood. But in the end, you brought us into wealth and great abundance." 
Psalms66:13 Now I have come to your Temple with burnt offerings to pay my vows. 
Psalms66:14 For when I was in trouble, I promised you many offerings." 
Psalms66:15 That is why I am bringing you these fat male goats, rams, and calves. The smoke of their sacrifice shall rise before you." 
Psalms66:16 Come and hear, all of you who reverence the Lord, and I will tell you what he did for me:" 
Psalms66:17 For I cried to him for help with praises ready on my tongue. 
Psalms66:18 He would not have listened if I had not confessed my sins. 
Psalms66:19 But he listened! He heard my prayer! He paid attention to it! 
Psalms66:20 Blessed be God, who didn't turn away when I was praying and didn't refuse me his kindness and love." 
Psalms67:1 O God, in mercy bless us; let your face beam with joy as you look down at us." 
Psalms67:2 Send us around the world with the news of your saving power and your eternal plan for all mankind. 
Psalms67:3 How everyone throughout the earth will praise the Lord! 
Psalms67:4 How glad the nations will be, singing for joy because you are their King and will give true justice to their people!" 
Psalms67:5 Praise God, O world! May all the peoples of the earth give thanks to you." 
Psalms67:6 For the earth has yielded abundant harvests. God, even our own God, will bless us." 
Psalms67:7 And peoples from remotest lands will worship him. 
Psalms68:1 Arise, O God, and scatter all your enemies! Chase them away!" 
Psalms68:2 Drive them off like smoke before the wind; melt them like wax in fire! So let the wicked perish at the presence of God. 
Psalms68:3 But may the godly man exult. May he rejoice and be merry. 
Psalms68:4 Sing praises to the Lord! Raise your voice in song to him who rides upon the clouds! Jehovah is his name—oh, rejoice in his presence." 
Psalms68:5 He is a father to the fatherless; he gives justice to the widows, for he is holy." 
Psalms68:6 He gives families to the lonely, and releases prisoners from jail, singing with joy! But for rebels there is famine and distress." 
Psalms68:7 O God, when you led your people through the wilderness," 
Psalms68:8 the earth trembled and the heavens shook. Mount Sinai quailed before you—the God of Israel. 
Psalms68:9 You sent abundant rain upon your land, O God, to refresh it in its weariness!" 
Psalms68:10 There your people lived, for you gave them this home when they were destitute." 
Psalms68:11 The Lord speaks. The enemy flees. The women at home cry out the happy news: 
Psalms68:12 “The armies that came to destroy us have fled!“ Now all the women of Israel are dividing the booty. 
Psalms68:13 See them sparkle with jewels of silver and gold, covered all over as wings cover doves!" 
Psalms68:14 God scattered their enemies like snowflakes melting in the forests of Zalmon. 
Psalms68:15 O mighty mountains in Bashan! O splendid many-peaked ranges! 
Psalms68:16 Well may you look with envy at Mount Zion, the mount where God has chosen to live forever." 
Psalms68:17 Surrounded by unnumbered chariots, the Lord moves on from Mount Sinai and comes to his holy temple high upon Mount Zion." 
Psalms68:18 He ascends the heights, leading many captives in his train. He receives gifts for men, even those who once were rebels. God will live among us here." 
Psalms68:19 What a glorious Lord! He who daily bears our burdens also gives us our salvation. 
Psalms68:20 He frees us! He rescues us from death. 
Psalms68:21 But he will crush his enemies, for they refuse to leave their guilty, stubborn ways." 
Psalms68:22 The Lord says, “Come,“ to all his people's enemies; they are hiding on Mount Hermon's highest slopes and deep within the sea!" 
Psalms68:23 His people must destroy them. Cover your feet with their blood; dogs will eat them. 
Psalms68:24 The procession of God my King moves onward to the sanctuary— 
Psalms68:25 singers in front, musicians behind, girls playing the timbrels in between." 
Psalms68:26 Let all the people of Israel praise the Lord, who is Israel's fountain." 
Psalms68:27 The little tribe of Benjamin leads the way. The princes and elders of Judah, and the princes of Zebulun and Naphtali are right behind." 
Psalms68:28 Summon your might; display your strength, O God, for you have done such mighty things for us." 
Psalms68:29 The kings of the earth are bringing their gifts to your temple in Jerusalem. 
Psalms68:30 Rebuke our enemies, O Lord. Bring them—submissive, tax in hand. Scatter all who delight in war." 
Psalms68:31 Egypt will send gifts of precious metals. Ethiopia will stretch out her hands to God in adoration. 
Psalms68:32 Sing to the Lord, O kingdoms of the earth—sing praises to the Lord," 
Psalms68:33 to him who rides upon the ancient heavens, whose mighty voice thunders from the sky." 
Psalms68:34 Power belongs to God! His majesty shines down on Israel; his strength is mighty in the heavens. 
Psalms68:35 What awe we feel, kneeling here before him in the sanctuary. The God of Israel gives strength and mighty power to his people. Blessed be God!" 
Psalms69:1 Save me, O my God. The floods have risen." 
Psalms69:2 Deeper and deeper I sink in the mire; the waters rise around me. 
Psalms69:3 I have wept until I am exhausted; my throat is dry and hoarse; my eyes are swollen with weeping, waiting for my God to act." 
Psalms69:4 I cannot even count all those who hate me without cause. They are influential men, these who plot to kill me though I am innocent. They demand that I be punished for what I didn't do." 
Psalms69:5 O God, you know so well how stupid I am, and you know all my sins." 
Psalms69:6 O Lord God of the armies of heaven, don't let me be a stumbling block to those who trust in you. O God of Israel, don't let me cause them to be confused," 
Psalms69:7 though I am mocked and cursed and shamed for your sake. 
Psalms69:8 Even my own brothers pretend they don't know me! 
Psalms69:9 My zeal for God and his work burns hot within me. And because I advocate your cause, your enemies insult me even as they insult you." 
Psalms69:10 How they scoff and mock me when I mourn and fast before the Lord! 
Psalms69:11 How they talk about me when I wear sackcloth to show my humiliation and sorrow for my sins! 
Psalms69:12 I am the talk of the town and the song of the drunkards. 
Psalms69:13 But I keep right on praying to you, Lord. For now is the time—you are bending down to hear! You are ready with a plentiful supply of love and kindness. Now answer my prayer and rescue me as you promised." 
Psalms69:14 Pull me out of this mire. Don't let me sink in. Rescue me from those who hate me, and from these deep waters I am in." 
Psalms69:15 Don't let the floods overwhelm me or the ocean swallow me; save me from the pit that threatens me. 
Psalms69:16 O Jehovah, answer my prayers, for your loving-kindness is wonderful; your mercy is so plentiful, so tender and so kind." 
Psalms69:17 Don't hide from me, for I am in deep trouble. Quick! Come and save me." 
Psalms69:18 Come, Lord, and rescue me. Ransom me from all my enemies." 
Psalms69:19 You know how they talk about me, and how they so shamefully dishonor me. You see them all and know what each has said." 
Psalms69:20 Their contempt has broken my heart; my spirit is heavy within me. If even one would show some pity, if even one would comfort me!" 
Psalms69:21 For food they gave me gall; for my awful thirst they offered vinegar. 
Psalms69:22 Let their joys turn to ashes and their peace disappear; 
Psalms69:23 let darkness, blindness, and great feebleness be theirs." 
Psalms69:24 Pour out your fury upon them; consume them with the fierceness of your anger. 
Psalms69:25 Let their homes be desolate and abandoned. 
Psalms69:26 For they persecute the one you have smitten and scoff at the pain of the one you have pierced. 
Psalms69:27 Pile their sins high and do not overlook them. 
Psalms69:28 Let these men be blotted from the list of the living; do not give them the joys of life with the righteous. 
Psalms69:29 But rescue me, O God, from my poverty and pain." 
Psalms69:30 Then I will praise God with my singing! My thanks will be his praise— 
Psalms69:31 that will please him more than sacrificing a bullock or an ox. 
Psalms69:32 The humble shall see their God at work for them. No wonder they will be so glad! All who seek for God shall live in joy. 
Psalms69:33 For Jehovah hears the cries of his needy ones and does not look the other way. 
Psalms69:34 Praise him, all heaven and earth! Praise him, all the seas and everything in them!" 
Psalms69:35 For God will save Jerusalem; he rebuilds the cities of Judah. His people shall live in them and not be dispossessed. 
Psalms69:36 Their children shall inherit the land; all who love his name shall live there safely. 
Psalms70:1 Rescue me, O God! Lord, hurry to my aid!" 
Psalms70:2 They are after my life and delight in hurting me. Confuse them! Shame them! Stop them! 
Psalms70:3 Don't let them keep on mocking me! 
Psalms70:4 But fill the followers of God with joy. Let those who love your salvation exclaim, “What a wonderful God he is!“" 
Psalms70:5 But I am in deep trouble. Rush to my aid, for only you can help and save me. O Lord, don't delay." 
Psalms71:1 Lord, you are my refuge! Don't let me down!" 
Psalms71:2 Save me from my enemies, for you are just! Rescue me! Bend down your ear and listen to my plea and save me." 
Psalms71:3 Be to me a great protecting Rock, where I am always welcome, safe from all attacks. For you have issued the order to save me." 
Psalms71:4 Rescue me, O God, from these unjust and cruel men." 
Psalms71:5 O Lord, you alone are my hope; I've trusted you from childhood." 
Psalms71:6 Yes, you have been with me from birth and have helped me constantly—no wonder I am always praising you!" 
Psalms71:7 My success—at which so many stand amazed—is because you are my mighty protector. 
Psalms71:8 All day long I'll praise and honor you, O God, for all that you have done for me." 
Psalms71:9 And now, in my old age, don't set me aside. Don't forsake me now when my strength is failing." 
Psalms71:10 My enemies are whispering," 
Psalms71:11 “God has forsaken him! Now we can get him. There is no one to help him now!“ 
Psalms71:12 O God, don't stay away! Come quickly! Help!" 
Psalms71:13 Destroy them! Cover them with failure and disgrace—these enemies of mine. 
Psalms71:14 I will keep on expecting you to help me. I praise you more and more. 
Psalms71:15 I cannot count the times when you have faithfully rescued me from danger. I will tell everyone how good you are, and of your constant, daily care." 
Psalms71:16 I walk in the strength of the Lord God. I tell everyone that you alone are just and good. 
Psalms71:17 O God, you have helped me from my earliest childhood—and I have constantly testified to others of the wonderful things you do." 
Psalms71:18 And now that I am old and gray, don't forsake me. Give me time to tell this new generation (and their children too) about all your mighty miracles." 
Psalms71:19 Your power and goodness, Lord, reach to the highest heavens. You have done such wonderful things. Where is there another God like you?" 
Psalms71:20 You have let me sink down deep in desperate problems. But you will bring me back to life again, up from the depths of the earth." 
Psalms71:21 You will give me greater honor than before and turn again and comfort me. 
Psalms71:22 I will praise you with music, telling of your faithfulness to all your promises, O Holy One of Israel." 
Psalms71:23 I will shout and sing your praises for redeeming me. 
Psalms71:24 I will talk to others all day long about your justice and your goodness. For all who tried to hurt me have been disgraced and dishonored. 
Psalms72:1 O God, help the king to judge as you would, and help his son to walk in godliness." 
Psalms72:2 Help him to give justice to your people, even to the poor." 
Psalms72:3 May the mountains and hills flourish in prosperity because of his good reign. 
Psalms72:4 Help him to defend the poor and needy and to crush their oppressors. 
Psalms72:5 May the poor and needy revere you constantly, as long as sun and moon continue in the skies! Yes, forever!" 
Psalms72:6 May the reign of this son of mine be as gentle and fruitful as the springtime rains upon the grass—like showers that water the earth! 
Psalms72:7 May all good men flourish in his reign with abundance of peace to the end of time. 
Psalms72:8 Let him reign from sea to sea and from the Euphrates River to the ends of the earth. 
Psalms72:9 The desert nomads shall bow before him; his enemies shall fall face downward in the dust. 
Psalms72:10 Kings along the Mediterranean coast—the kings of Tarshish and the islands—and those from Sheba and from Seba—all will bring their gifts. 
Psalms72:11 Yes, kings from everywhere! All will bow before him! All will serve him!" 
Psalms72:12 He will take care of the helpless and poor when they cry to him; for they have no one else to defend them. 
Psalms72:13 He feels pity for the weak and needy and will rescue them. 
Psalms72:14 He will save them from oppression and from violence, for their lives are precious to him." 
Psalms72:15 And he shall live; and to him will be given the gold of Sheba, and there will be constant praise for him. His people will bless him all day long." 
Psalms72:16 Bless us with abundant crops throughout the land, even on the highland plains; may there be fruit like that of Lebanon; may the cities be as full of people as the fields are of grass." 
Psalms72:17 His name will be honored forever; it will continue as the sun; and all will be blessed in him; all nations will praise him. 
Psalms72:18 Blessed be Jehovah God, the God of Israel, who only does wonderful things!" 
Psalms72:19 Blessed be his glorious name forever! Let the whole earth be filled with his glory. Amen and amen! 
Psalms72:20 (This ends the psalms of David, son of Jesse.)" 
Psalms73:1 How good God is to Israel—to those whose hearts are pure. 
Psalms73:2 But as for me, I came so close to the edge of the cliff! My feet were slipping and I was almost gone." 
Psalms73:3 For I was envious of the prosperity of the proud and wicked. 
Psalms73:4 Yes, all through life their road is smooth! They grow sleek and fat." 
Psalms73:5 They aren't always in trouble and plagued with problems like everyone else," 
Psalms73:6 so their pride sparkles like a jeweled necklace, and their clothing is woven of cruelty!" 
Psalms73:7 These fat cats have everything their hearts could ever wish for! 
Psalms73:8 They scoff at God and threaten his people. How proudly they speak! 
Psalms73:9 They boast against the very heavens, and their words strut through the earth." 
Psalms73:10 And so God's people are dismayed and confused and drink it all in. 
Psalms73:11 “Does God realize what is going on?“ they ask. 
Psalms73:12 “Look at these men of arrogance; they never have to lift a finger—theirs is a life of ease; and all the time their riches multiply.“ 
Psalms73:13 Have I been wasting my time? Why take the trouble to be pure? 
Psalms73:14 All I get out of it is trouble and woe—every day and all day long! 
Psalms73:15 If I had really said that, I would have been a traitor to your people." 
Psalms73:16 Yet it is so hard to explain it—this prosperity of those who hate the Lord. 
Psalms73:17 Then one day I went into God's sanctuary to meditate and thought about the future of these evil men. 
Psalms73:18 What a slippery path they are on—suddenly God will send them sliding over the edge of the cliff and down to their destruction: 
Psalms73:19 an instant end to all their happiness, an eternity of terror." 
Psalms73:20 Their present life is only a dream! They will awaken to the truth as one awakens from a dream of things that never really were! 
Psalms73:21 When I saw this, what turmoil filled my heart!" 
Psalms73:22 I saw myself so stupid and so ignorant; I must seem like an animal to you, O God." 
Psalms73:23 But even so, you love me! You are holding my right hand!" 
Psalms73:24 You will keep on guiding me all my life with your wisdom and counsel, and afterwards receive me into the glories of heaven!" 
Psalms73:25 Whom have I in heaven but you? And I desire no one on earth as much as you! 
Psalms73:26 My health fails; my spirits droop, yet God remains! He is the strength of my heart; he is mine forever!" 
Psalms73:27 But those refusing to worship God will perish, for he destroys those serving other gods." 
Psalms73:28 But as for me, I get as close to him as I can! I have chosen him, and I will tell everyone about the wonderful ways he rescues me." 
Psalms74:1 O God, why have you cast us away forever? Why is your anger hot against us—the sheep of your own pasture?" 
Psalms74:2 Remember that we are your people—the ones you chose in ancient times from slavery and made the choicest of your possessions. You chose Jerusalem as your home on earth! 
Psalms74:3 Walk through the awful ruins of the city and see what the enemy has done to your sanctuary. 
Psalms74:4 There they shouted their battle cry and erected their idols to flaunt their victory. 
Psalms74:5 Everything lies in shambles like a forest chopped to the ground. 
Psalms74:6 They came with their axes and sledgehammers and smashed and chopped the carved paneling; 
Psalms74:7 they set the sanctuary on fire, and razed it to the ground—your sanctuary, Lord." 
Psalms74:8 “Let's wipe out every trace of God,“ they said, and went through the entire country burning down the assembly places where we worshiped you." 
Psalms74:9 There is nothing left to show that we are your people. The prophets are gone, and who can say when it all will end?" 
Psalms74:10 How long, O God, will you allow our enemies to dishonor your name? Will you let them get away with this forever?" 
Psalms74:11 Why do you delay? Why hold back your power? Unleash your fist and give them a final blow. 
Psalms74:12 God is my King from ages past; you have been actively helping me everywhere throughout the land. 
Psalms74:13 You divided the Red Sea with your strength; you crushed the sea-god's heads! 
Psalms74:14 You gave him to the desert tribes to eat! 
Psalms74:15 At your command the springs burst forth to give your people water; and then you dried a path for them across the ever-flowing Jordan.
