Psalms74:16 Day and night alike belong to you; you made the starlight and the sun. 
Psalms74:17 All nature is within your hands; you make the summer and the winter too. 
Psalms74:18 Lord, see how these enemies scoff at you. O Jehovah, an arrogant nation has blasphemed your name." 
Psalms74:19 O Lord, save me! Protect your turtledove from the hawks. Save your beloved people from these beasts." 
Psalms74:20 Remember your promise! For the land is full of darkness and cruel men. 
Psalms74:21 O Lord, don't let your downtrodden people be constantly insulted. Give cause for these poor and needy ones to praise your name!" 
Psalms74:22 Arise, O God, and state your case against our enemies. Remember the insults these rebels have hurled against you all day long." 
Psalms74:23 Don't overlook the cursing of these enemies of yours; it grows louder and louder. 
Psalms75:1 How we thank you, Lord! Your mighty miracles give proof that you care." 
Psalms75:2 “Yes,“ the Lord replies, “and when I am ready, I will punish the wicked!" 
Psalms75:3 Though the earth shakes and all its people live in turmoil, yet its pillars are firm, for I have set them in place!“" 
Psalms75:4 I warned the proud to cease their arrogance! I told the wicked to lower their insolent gaze 
Psalms75:5 and to stop being stubborn and proud. 
Psalms75:6 For promotion and power come from nowhere on earth, but only from God." 
Psalms75:7 He promotes one and deposes another. 
Psalms75:8 In Jehovah's hand there is a cup of pale and sparkling wine. It is his judgment, poured out upon the wicked of the earth. They must drain that cup to the dregs." 
Psalms75:9 But as for me, I shall forever declare the praises of the God of Jacob." 
Psalms75:10 “I will cut off the strength of evil men,“ says the Lord, “and increase the power of good men in their place.“" 
Psalms76:1 God's reputation is very great in Judah and in Israel. 
Psalms76:2 His home is in Jerusalem. He lives upon Mount Zion. 
Psalms76:3 There he breaks the weapons of our enemies. 
Psalms76:4 The everlasting mountains cannot compare with you in glory! 
Psalms76:5 The mightiest of our enemies are conquered. They lie before us in the sleep of death; not one can lift a hand against us. 
Psalms76:6 When you rebuked them, God of Jacob, steeds and riders fell." 
Psalms76:7 No wonder you are greatly feared! Who can stand before an angry God? 
Psalms76:8 You pronounce sentence on them from heaven; the earth trembles and stands silently before you. 
Psalms76:9 You stand up to punish the evil-doers and to defend the meek of the earth. 
Psalms76:10 Man's futile wrath will bring you glory. You will use it as an ornament! 
Psalms76:11 Fulfill all your vows that you have made to Jehovah your God. Let everyone bring him presents. He should be reverenced and feared," 
Psalms76:12 for he cuts down princes and does awesome things to the kings of the earth. 
Psalms77:1 I cry to the Lord; I call and call to him. Oh, that he would listen." 
Psalms77:2 I am in deep trouble and I need his help so much. All night long I pray, lifting my hands to heaven, pleading. There can be no joy for me until he acts." 
Psalms77:3 I think of God and moan, overwhelmed with longing for his help." 
Psalms77:4 I cannot sleep until you act. I am too distressed even to pray! 
Psalms77:5 I keep thinking of the good old days of the past, long since ended." 
Psalms77:6 Then my nights were filled with joyous songs. I search my soul and meditate upon the difference now. 
Psalms77:7 Has the Lord rejected me forever? Will he never again be favorable? 
Psalms77:8 Is his loving-kindness gone forever? Has his promise failed? 
Psalms77:9 Has he forgotten to be kind to one so undeserving? Has he slammed the door in anger on his love? 
Psalms77:10 And I said: This is my fate, that the blessings of God have changed to hate." 
Psalms77:11 I recall the many miracles he did for me so long ago. 
Psalms77:12 Those wonderful deeds are constantly in my thoughts. I cannot stop thinking about them. 
Psalms77:13 O God, your ways are holy. Where is there any other as mighty as you?" 
Psalms77:14 You are the God of miracles and wonders! You still demonstrate your awesome power. 
Psalms77:15 You have redeemed us who are the sons of Jacob and of Joseph by your might. 
Psalms77:16 When the Red Sea saw you, how it feared! It trembled to its depths!" 
Psalms77:17 The clouds poured down their rain, the thunder rolled and crackled in the sky. Your lightning flashed." 
Psalms77:18 There was thunder in the whirlwind; the lightning lighted up the world! The earth trembled and shook. 
Psalms77:19 Your road led by a pathway through the sea—a pathway no one knew was there! 
Psalms77:20 You led your people along that road like a flock of sheep, with Moses and Aaron as their shepherds." 
Psalms78:1 O my people, listen to my teaching. Open your ears to what I am saying." 
Psalms78:2 For I will show you lessons from our 
Psalms78:3 history, stories handed down to us from former generations." 
Psalms78:4 I will reveal these truths to you so that you can describe these glorious deeds of Jehovah to your children and tell them about the mighty miracles he did. 
Psalms78:5 For he gave his laws to Israel and commanded our fathers to teach them to their children," 
Psalms78:6 so that they in turn could teach their children too. Thus his laws pass down from generation to generation. 
Psalms78:7 In this way each generation has been able to obey his laws and to set its hope anew on God and not forget his glorious miracles. 
Psalms78:8 Thus they did not need to be as their fathers were—stubborn, rebellious, unfaithful, refusing to give their hearts to God." 
Psalms78:9 The people of Ephraim, though fully armed, turned their backs and fled when the day of battle came" 
Psalms78:10 because they didn't obey his laws. They refused to follow his ways. 
Psalms78:11 And they forgot about the wonderful miracles God had 
Psalms78:12 done for them and for their fathers in Egypt. 
Psalms78:13 For he divided the sea before them and led them through! The water stood banked up along both sides of them! 
Psalms78:14 In the daytime he led them by a cloud, and at night by a pillar of fire." 
Psalms78:15 He split open the rocks in the wilderness to give them plenty of water, as though gushing from a spring." 
Psalms78:16 Streams poured from the rock, flowing like a river!" 
Psalms78:17 Yet they kept on with their rebellion, sinning against the God who is above all gods." 
Psalms78:18 They murmured and complained, demanding other food than God was giving them." 
Psalms78:19 They even spoke against God himself. 
Psalms78:20 “Why can't he give us decent food as well as water?“ they grumbled. 
Psalms78:21 Jehovah heard them and was angry; the fire of his wrath burned against Israel 
Psalms78:22 because they didn't believe in God or trust in him to care for them," 
Psalms78:23 even though he commanded the skies to open—he opened the windows of heaven— 
Psalms78:24 and rained down manna for their food. He gave them bread from heaven! 
Psalms78:25 They ate angels' food! He gave them all they could hold. 
Psalms78:26 And he led forth the east wind and guided the south wind by his mighty power. 
Psalms78:27 He rained down birds as thick as dust, clouds of them like sands along the shore!" 
Psalms78:28 He caused the birds to fall to the ground among the tents. 
Psalms78:29 The people ate their fill. He gave them what they asked for. 
Psalms78:30 But they had hardly finished eating, and the meat was yet in their mouths," 
Psalms78:31 when the anger of the Lord rose against them and killed the finest of Israel's young men. 
Psalms78:32 Yet even so the people kept on sinning and refused to believe in miracles. 
Psalms78:33 So he cut their lives short and gave them years of terror and disaster. 
Psalms78:34 Then at last, when he had ruined them, they walked awhile behind him; how earnestly they turned around and followed him!" 
Psalms78:35 Then they remembered that God was their Rock—that their Savior was the God above all gods. 
Psalms78:36 But it was only with their words they followed him, not with their hearts;" 
Psalms78:37 their hearts were far away. They did not keep their promises. 
Psalms78:38 Yet he was merciful and forgave their sins and didn't destroy them all. Many and many a time he held back his anger. 
Psalms78:39 For he remembered that they were merely mortal men, gone in a moment like a breath of wind." 
Psalms78:40 Oh, how often they rebelled against him in those desert years and grieved his heart." 
Psalms78:41 Again and again they turned away and tempted God to kill them, and limited the Holy One of Israel from giving them his blessings." 
Psalms78:42 They forgot his power and love and how he had rescued them from their enemies; 
Psalms78:43 they forgot the plagues he sent upon the Egyptians in Tanis— 
Psalms78:44 how he turned their rivers into blood so that no one could drink," 
Psalms78:45 how he sent vast swarms of flies to fill the land, and how the frogs had covered all of Egypt!" 
Psalms78:46 He gave their crops to caterpillars. Their harvest was consumed by locusts. 
Psalms78:47 He destroyed their grapevines and their sycamores with hail. 
Psalms78:48 Their cattle died in the fields, mortally wounded by huge hailstones from heaven. Their sheep were killed by lightning." 
Psalms78:49 He loosed on them the fierceness of his anger, sending sorrow and trouble. He dispatched against them a band of destroying angels." 
Psalms78:50 He gave free course to his anger and did not spare the Egyptians' lives, but handed them over to plagues and sickness." 
Psalms78:51 Then he killed the eldest son in each Egyptian family—he who was the beginning of its strength and joy. 
Psalms78:52 But he led forth his own people like a flock, guiding them safely through the wilderness." 
Psalms78:53 He kept them safe, so they were not afraid. But the sea closed in upon their enemies and overwhelmed them." 
Psalms78:54 He brought them to the border of his land of blessing, to this land of hills he made for them." 
Psalms78:55 He drove out the nations occupying the land and gave each tribe of Israel its apportioned place as its home. 
Psalms78:56 Yet though he did all this for them, they still rebelled against the God above all gods and refused to follow his commands." 
Psalms78:57 They turned back from entering the Promised Land and disobeyed as their fathers had. Like a crooked arrow, they missed the target of God's will." 
Psalms78:58 They made him angry by erecting idols and altars to other gods. 
Psalms78:59 When God saw their deeds, his wrath was strong and he despised his people." 
Psalms78:60 Then he abandoned his Tabernacle at Shiloh, where he had lived among mankind," 
Psalms78:61 and allowed his Ark to be captured; he surrendered his glory into enemy hands. 
Psalms78:62 He caused his people to be butchered because his anger was intense. 
Psalms78:63 Their young men were killed by fire, and their girls died before they were old enough to sing their wedding songs." 
Psalms78:64 The priests were slaughtered, and their widows died before they could even begin their lament." 
Psalms78:65 Then the Lord rose up as though awakening from sleep, and like a mighty man aroused by wine," 
Psalms78:66 he routed his enemies; he drove them back and sent them to eternal shame. 
Psalms78:67 But he rejected Joseph's family, the tribe of Ephraim," 
Psalms78:68 and chose the tribe of Judah—and Mount Zion, which he loved." 
Psalms78:69 There he built his towering temple, solid and enduring as the heavens and the earth." 
Psalms78:70 He chose his servant David, taking him from feeding sheep" 
Psalms78:71 and from following the ewes with lambs; God presented David to his people as their shepherd," 
Psalms78:72 and he cared for them with a true heart and skillful hands. 
Psalms79:1 O God, your land has been conquered by the heathen nations. Your Temple is defiled, and Jerusalem is a heap of ruins." 
Psalms79:2 The bodies of your people lie exposed—food for birds and animals. 
Psalms79:3 The enemy has butchered the entire population of Jerusalem; blood has flowed like water. No one is left even to bury them. 
Psalms79:4 The nations all around us scoff. They heap contempt on us. 
Psalms79:5 O Jehovah, how long will you be angry with us? Forever? Will your jealousy burn till every hope is gone?" 
Psalms79:6 Pour out your wrath upon the godless nations—not on us—on kingdoms that refuse to pray, that will not call upon your name!" 
Psalms79:7 For they have destroyed your people Israel, invading every home." 
Psalms79:8 Oh, do not hold us guilty for our former sins! Let your tenderhearted mercies meet our needs, for we are brought low to the dust." 
Psalms79:9 Help us, God of our salvation! Help us for the honor of your name. Oh, save us and forgive our sins." 
Psalms79:10 Why should the heathen nations be allowed to scoff, “Where is their God?“ Publicly avenge this slaughter of your people!" 
Psalms79:11 Listen to the sighing of the prisoners and those condemned to die. Demonstrate the greatness of your power by saving them. 
Psalms79:12 O Lord, take sevenfold vengeance on these nations scorning you." 
Psalms79:13 Then we your people, the sheep of your pasture, will thank you forever and forever, praising your greatness from generation to generation." 
Psalms80:1 O Shepherd of Israel who leads Israel like a flock; O God enthroned above the Guardian Angels, bend down your ear and listen as I plead. Display your power and radiant glory." 
Psalms80:2 Let Ephraim, Benjamin, and Manasseh see you rouse yourself and use your mighty power to rescue us." 
Psalms80:3 Turn us again to yourself, O God. Look down on us in joy and love; only then shall we be saved." 
Psalms80:4 O Jehovah, God of heaven's armies, how long will you be angry and reject our prayers?" 
Psalms80:5 You have fed us with sorrow and tears 
Psalms80:6 and have made us the scorn of the neighboring nations. They laugh among themselves. 
Psalms80:7 Turn us again to yourself, O God of Hosts. Look down on us in joy and love; only then shall we be saved." 
Psalms80:8 You brought us from Egypt as though we were a tender vine and drove away the heathen from your land and planted us. 
Psalms80:9 You cleared the ground and tilled the soil, and we took root and filled the land." 
Psalms80:10 The mountains were covered with our shadow; we were like the mighty cedar trees," 
Psalms80:11 covering the entire land from the Mediterranean Sea to the Euphrates River. 
Psalms80:12 But now you have broken down our walls, leaving us without protection." 
Psalms80:13 The boar from the forest roots around us, and the wild animals feed on us." 
Psalms80:14 Come back, we beg of you, O God of the armies of heaven, and bless us. Look down from heaven and see our plight and care for this your vine!" 
Psalms80:15 Protect what you yourself have planted, this son you have raised for yourself." 
Psalms80:16 For we are chopped and burned by our enemies. May they perish at your frown. 
Psalms80:17 Strengthen the man you love, the son of your choice," 
Psalms80:18 and we will never forsake you again. Revive us to trust in you. 
Psalms80:19 Turn us again to yourself, O God of the armies of heaven. Look down on us, your face aglow with joy and love—only then shall we be saved." 
Psalms81:1 The Lord makes us strong! Sing praises! Sing to Israel's God! 
Psalms81:2 Sing, accompanied by drums; pluck the sweet lyre and harp." 
Psalms81:3 Sound the trumpet! Come to the joyous celebrations at full moon, new moon, and all the other holidays." 
Psalms81:4 For God has given us these times of joy; they are scheduled in the laws of Israel. 
Psalms81:5 He gave them as reminders of his war against Egypt where we were slaves on foreign soil. I heard an unknown voice that said," 
Psalms81:6 “Now I will relieve your shoulder of its burden; I will free your hands from their heavy tasks.“ 
Psalms81:7 He said, “You cried to me in trouble, and I saved you; I answered from Mount Sinai where the thunder hides. I tested your faith at Meribah, when you complained there was no water." 
Psalms81:8 Listen to me, O my people, while I give you stern warnings. O Israel, if you will only listen!" 
Psalms81:9 You must never worship any other god nor ever have an idol in your home. 
Psalms81:10 For it was I, Jehovah your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt. Only test me! Open your mouth wide and see if I won't fill it. You will receive every blessing you can use!" 
Psalms81:11 “But no, my people won't listen. Israel doesn't want me around." 
Psalms81:12 So I am letting them go their blind and stubborn way, living according to their own desires." 
Psalms81:13 “But oh, that my people would listen to me! Oh, that Israel would follow me, walking in my paths!" 
Psalms81:14 How quickly then I would subdue her enemies! How soon my hands would be upon her foes! 
Psalms81:15 Those who hate the Lord would cringe before him; their desolation would last forever. 
Psalms81:16 But he would feed you with the choicest foods. He would satisfy you with honey for the taking.“ 
Psalms82:1 God stands up to open heaven's court. He pronounces judgment on the judges. 
Psalms82:2 How long will you judges refuse to listen to the evidence? How long will you shower special favors on the wicked? 
Psalms82:3 Give fair judgment to the poor man, the afflicted, the fatherless, the destitute." 
Psalms82:4 Rescue the poor and helpless from the grasp of evil men. 
Psalms82:5 But you are so foolish and so ignorant! Because you are in darkness, all the foundations of society are shaken to the core." 
Psalms82:6 I have called you all “gods“ and “sons of the Most High.“ 
Psalms82:7 But in death you are mere men. You will fall as any prince—for all must die. 
Psalms82:8 Stand up, O God, and judge the earth. For all of it belongs to you. All nations are in your hands." 
Psalms83:1 O God, don't sit idly by, silent and inactive when we pray. Answer us! Deliver us!" 
Psalms83:2 Don't you hear the tumult and commotion of your enemies? Don't you see what they are doing, these proud men who hate the Lord?" 
Psalms83:3 They are full of craftiness and plot against your people, laying plans to slay your precious ones." 
Psalms83:4 “Come,“ they say, “and let us wipe out Israel as a nation—we will destroy the very memory of her existence.“" 
Psalms83:5 This was their unanimous decision at their summit conference—they signed a treaty to ally themselves against Almighty God— 
Psalms83:6 these Ishmaelites and Edomites and Moabites and Hagrites; 
Psalms83:7 people from the lands of Gebal, Ammon, Amalek, Philistia and Tyre;" 
Psalms83:8 Assyria has joined them too, and is allied with the descendants of Lot." 
Psalms83:9 Do to them as once you did to Midian, or as you did to Sisera and Jabin at the river Kishon," 
Psalms83:10 and as you did to your enemies at Endor, whose decaying corpses fertilized the soil." 
Psalms83:11 Make their mighty nobles die as Oreb did, and Zeeb; let all their princes die like Zebah and Zalmunna," 
Psalms83:12 who said, “Let us seize for our own use these pasturelands of God!“" 
Psalms83:13 O my God, blow them away like dust; like chaff before the wind—" 
Psalms83:14 as a forest fire that roars across a mountain. 
Psalms83:15 Chase them with your fiery storms, tempests, and tornados." 
Psalms83:16 Utterly disgrace them until they recognize your power and name, O Lord." 
Psalms83:17 Make them failures in everything they do; let them be ashamed and terrified 
Psalms83:18 until they learn that you alone, Jehovah, are the God above all gods in supreme charge of all the earth." 
Psalms84:1 How lovely is your Temple, O Lord of the armies of heaven." 
Psalms84:2 I long, yes, faint with longing to be able to enter your courtyard and come near to the Living God." 
Psalms84:3 Even the sparrows and swallows are welcome to come and nest among your altars and there have their young, O Lord of heaven's armies, my King and my God!" 
Psalms84:4 How happy are those who can live in your Temple, singing your praises." 
Psalms84:5 Happy are those who are strong in the Lord, who want above all else to follow your steps." 
Psalms84:6 When they walk through the Valley of Weeping, it will become a place of springs where pools of blessing and refreshment collect after rains!" 
Psalms84:7 They will grow constantly in strength, and each of them is invited to meet with the Lord in Zion." 
Psalms84:8 O Jehovah, God of the heavenly armies, hear my prayer! Listen, God of Israel." 
Psalms84:9 O God, our Defender and our Shield, have mercy on the one you have anointed as your king." 
Psalms84:10 A single day spent in your Temple is better than a thousand anywhere else! I would rather be a doorman of the Temple of my God than live in palaces of wickedness. 
Psalms84:11 For Jehovah God is our Light and our Protector. He gives us grace and glory. No good thing will he withhold from those who walk along his paths. 
Psalms84:12 O Lord of the armies of heaven, blessed are those who trust in you." 
Psalms85:1 Lord, you have poured out amazing blessings on this land! You have restored the fortunes of Israel," 
Psalms85:2 and forgiven the sins of your people—yes, covered over each one," 
Psalms85:3 so that all your wrath, your blazing anger, is now ended." 
Psalms85:4 Now bring us back to loving you, O Lord, so that your anger will never need rise against us again." 
Psalms85:5 (Or will you be always angry—on and on to distant generations?) 
Psalms85:6 Oh, revive us! Then your people can rejoice in you again." 
Psalms85:7 Pour out your love and kindness on us, Lord, and grant us your salvation." 
Psalms85:8 I am listening carefully to all the Lord is saying—for he speaks peace to his people, his saints, if they will only stop their sinning." 
Psalms85:9 Surely his salvation is near to those who reverence him; our land will be filled with his glory. 
Psalms85:10 Mercy and truth have met together. Grim justice and peace have kissed! 
Psalms85:11 Truth rises from the earth, and righteousness smiles down from heaven." 
Psalms85:12 Yes, the Lord pours down his blessings on the land, and it yields its bountiful crops." 
Psalms85:13 Justice goes before him to make a pathway for his steps. 
Psalms86:1 Bend down and hear my prayer, O Lord, and answer me, for I am deep in trouble." 
Psalms86:2 Protect me from death, for I try to follow all your laws. Save me, for I am serving you and trusting you." 
Psalms86:3 Be merciful, O Lord, for I am looking up to you in constant hope." 
Psalms86:4 Give me happiness, O Lord, for I worship only you." 
Psalms86:5 O Lord, you are so good and kind, so ready to forgive, so full of mercy for all who ask your aid." 
Psalms86:6 Listen closely to my prayer, O God. Hear my urgent cry." 
Psalms86:7 I will call to you whenever trouble strikes, and you will help me." 
Psalms86:8 Where among the heathen gods is there a god like you? Where are their miracles? 
Psalms86:9 All the nations—and you made each one—will come and bow before you, Lord, and praise your great and holy name." 
Psalms86:10 For you are great and do great miracles. You alone are God. 
Psalms86:11 Tell me where you want me to go and I will go there. May every fiber of my being unite in reverence to your name. 
Psalms86:12 With all my heart I will praise you. I will give glory to your name forever," 
Psalms86:13 for you love me so much! You are constantly so kind! You have rescued me from deepest hell. 
Psalms86:14 O God, proud and insolent men defy me; violent, godless men are trying to kill me." 
Psalms86:15 But you are merciful and gentle, Lord, slow in getting angry, full of constant loving-kindness and of truth;" 
Psalms86:16 so look down in pity and grant strength to your servant and save me. 
Psalms86:17 Send me a sign of your favor. When those who hate me see it, they will lose face because you help and comfort me." 
Psalms87:1 High on his holy mountain stands Jerusalem, the city" 
Psalms87:2 of God, the city he loves more than any other!" 
Psalms87:3 O city of God, what wondrous tales are told of you!" 
Psalms87:4 Nowadays when I mention among my friends the names of Egypt and Babylonia, Philistia and Tyre, or even distant Ethiopia, someone boasts that he was born in one or another of those countries." 
Psalms87:5 But someday the highest honor will be to be a native of Jerusalem! For the God above all gods will personally bless this city. 
Psalms87:6 When he registers her citizens, he will place a checkmark beside the names of those who were born here." 
Psalms87:7 And in the festivals they'll sing, “All my heart is in Jerusalem.“" 
Psalms88:1 O Jehovah, God of my salvation, I have wept before you day and night." 
Psalms88:2 Now hear my prayers; oh, listen to my cry," 
Psalms88:3 for my life is full of troubles, and death draws near." 
Psalms88:4 They say my life is ebbing out—a hopeless case. 
Psalms88:5 They have left me here to die, like those slain on battlefields from whom your mercies are removed." 
Psalms88:6 You have thrust me down to the darkest depths. 
Psalms88:7 Your wrath lies heavy on me; wave after wave engulfs me. 
Psalms88:8 You have made my friends to loathe me, and they have gone away. I am in a trap with no way out." 
Psalms88:9 My eyes grow dim with weeping. Each day I beg your help; O Lord, I reach my pleading hands to you for mercy." 
Psalms88:10 Soon it will be too late! Of what use are your miracles when I am in the grave? How can I praise you then? 
Psalms88:11 Can those in the grave declare your loving-kindness? Can they proclaim your faithfulness? 
Psalms88:12 Can the darkness speak of your miracles? Can anyone in the Land of Forgetfulness talk about your help? 
Psalms88:13 O Lord, I plead for my life and will keep on pleading day by day." 
Psalms88:14 O Jehovah, why have you thrown my life away? Why are you turning your face from me and looking the other way?" 
Psalms88:15 From my youth I have been sickly and ready to die. I stand helpless before your terrors. 
Psalms88:16 Your fierce wrath has overwhelmed me. Your terrors have cut me off. 
Psalms88:17 They flow around me all day long. 
Psalms88:18 Lover, friend, acquaintance—all are gone. There is only darkness everywhere." 
Psalms89:1 Forever and ever I will sing about the tender kindness of the Lord! Young and old shall hear about your blessings. 
Psalms89:2 Your love and kindness are forever; your truth is as enduring as the heavens. 
Psalms89:3 The Lord God says, “I have made a solemn agreement with my chosen servant David." 
Psalms89:4 I have taken an oath to establish his descendants as kings forever on his throne, from now until eternity!“" 
Psalms89:5 All heaven shall praise your miracles, O Lord; myriads of angels will praise you for your faithfulness." 
Psalms89:6 For who in all of heaven can be compared with God? What mightiest angel is anything like him? 
Psalms89:7 The highest of angelic powers stand in dread and awe of him. Who is as revered as he by those surrounding him? 
Psalms89:8 O Jehovah, Commander of the heavenly armies, where is there any other Mighty One like you? Faithfulness is your very character." 
Psalms89:9 You rule the oceans when their waves arise in fearful storms; you speak, and they lie still." 
Psalms89:10 You have cut haughty Egypt to pieces. Your enemies are scattered by your awesome power. 
Psalms89:11 The heavens are yours, the world, everything—for you created them all." 
Psalms89:12 You created north and south! Mount Tabor and Mount Hermon rejoice to be signed by your name as their maker! 
Psalms89:13 Strong is your arm! Strong is your hand! Your right hand is lifted high in glorious strength. 
Psalms89:14 Your throne is founded on two strong pillars—the one is Justice and the other Righteousness. Mercy and Truth walk before you as your attendants. 
Psalms89:15 Blessed are those who hear the joyful blast of the trumpet, for they shall walk in the light of your presence." 
Psalms89:16 They rejoice all day long in your wonderful reputation and in your perfect righteousness. 
Psalms89:17 You are their strength. What glory! Our power is based on your favor! 
Psalms89:18 Yes, our protection is from the Lord himself and he, the Holy One of Israel, has given us our king." 
Psalms89:19 In a vision you spoke to your prophet and said, “I have chosen a splendid young man from the common people to be the king—" 
Psalms89:20 he is my servant David! I have anointed him with my holy oil. 
Psalms89:21 I will steady him and make him strong. 
Psalms89:22 His enemies shall not outwit him, nor shall the wicked overpower him." 
Psalms89:23 I will beat down his adversaries before him and destroy those who hate him. 
Psalms89:24 I will protect and bless him constantly and surround him with my love; he will be great because of me. 
Psalms89:25 He will hold sway from the Euphrates River to the Mediterranean Sea. 
Psalms89:26 And he will cry to me, 'You are my Father, my God, and my Rock of Salvation.'" 
Psalms89:27 “I will treat him as my firstborn son and make him the mightiest king in all the earth. 
Psalms89:28 I will love him forever and be kind to him always; my covenant with him will never end. 
Psalms89:29 He will always have an heir; his throne will be as endless as the days of heaven. 
Psalms89:30 If his children forsake my 
Psalms89:31 laws and don't obey them," 
Psalms89:32 then I will punish them," 
Psalms89:33 but I will never completely take away my loving-kindness from them, nor let my promise fail." 
Psalms89:34 No, I will not break my covenant; I will not take back one word of what I said." 
Psalms89:35 For I have sworn to David (and a holy God can never lie) that his 
Psalms89:36 dynasty will go on forever, and his throne will continue to the end of time." 
Psalms89:37 It shall be eternal as the moon, my faithful witness in the sky!“" 
Psalms89:38 Then why cast me off, rejected? Why be so angry with the one you chose as king?" 
Psalms89:39 Have you renounced your covenant with him? For you have thrown his crown in the dust. 
Psalms89:40 You have broken down the walls protecting him and laid in ruins every fort defending him. 
Psalms89:41 Everyone who comes along has robbed him while his neighbors mock. 
Psalms89:42 You have strengthened his enemies against him and made them rejoice. 
Psalms89:43 You have struck down his sword and refused to help him in battle. 
Psalms89:44 You have ended his splendor and overturned his throne. 
Psalms89:45 You have made him old before his time and publicly disgraced him. 
Psalms89:46 O Jehovah, how long will this go on? Will you hide yourself from me forever? How long will your wrath burn like fire?" 
Psalms89:47 Oh, remember how short you have made man's lifespan. Is it an empty, futile life you give the sons of men?" 
Psalms89:48 No man can live forever. All will die. Who can rescue his life from the power of the grave? 
Psalms89:49 Lord, where is the love you used to have for me? Where is your kindness that you promised to David with a faithful pledge?" 
Psalms89:50 Lord, see how all the people are despising me." 
Psalms89:51 Your enemies joke about me, the one you anointed as their king." 
Psalms89:52 And yet—blessed be the Lord forever! Amen and amen! 
Psalms90:1 A prayer of Moses, the man of God. Lord, through all the generations you have been our home!" 
Psalms90:2 Before the mountains were created, before the earth was formed, you are God without beginning or end." 
Psalms90:3 You speak, and man turns back to dust." 
Psalms90:4 A thousand years are but as yesterday to you! They are like a single hour! 
Psalms90:5 We glide along the tides of time as swiftly as a racing river and vanish as quickly as a dream. 
Psalms90:6 We are like grass that is green in the morning but mowed down and withered before the evening shadows fall. 
Psalms90:7 We die beneath your anger; we are overwhelmed by your wrath. 
Psalms90:8 You spread out our sins before you—our secret sins—and see them all. 
Psalms90:9 No wonder the years are long and heavy here beneath your wrath. All our days are filled with sighing. 
Psalms90:10 Seventy years are given us! And some may even live to eighty. But even the best of these years are often emptiness and pain; soon they disappear, and we are gone." 
Psalms90:11 Who can realize the terrors of your anger? Which of us can fear you as he should? 
Psalms90:12 Teach us to number our days and recognize how few they are; help us to spend them as we should. 
Psalms90:13 O Jehovah, come and bless us! How long will you delay? Turn away your anger from us." 
Psalms90:14 Satisfy us in our earliest youth with your loving-kindness, giving us constant joy to the end of our lives." 
Psalms90:15 Give us gladness in proportion to our former misery! Replace the evil years with good. 
Psalms90:16 Let us see your miracles again; let our children see glorious things, the kind you used to do," 
Psalms90:17 and let the Lord our God favor us and give us success. May he give permanence to all we do. 
Psalms91:1 We live within the shadow of the Almighty, sheltered by the God who is above all gods." 
Psalms91:2 This I declare, that he alone is my refuge, my place of safety; he is my God, and I am trusting him." 
Psalms91:3 For he rescues you from every trap and protects you from the fatal plague. 
Psalms91:4 He will shield you with his wings! They will shelter you. His faithful promises are your armor. 
Psalms91:5 Now you don't need to be afraid of the dark any more, nor fear the dangers of the day;" 
Psalms91:6 nor dread the plagues of darkness, nor disasters in the morning." 
Psalms91:7 Though a thousand fall at my side, though ten thousand are dying around me, the evil will not touch me." 
Psalms91:8 I will see how the wicked are punished, but I will not share it." 
Psalms91:9 For Jehovah is my refuge! I choose the God above all gods to shelter me. 
Psalms91:10 How then can evil overtake me or any plague come near? 
Psalms91:11 For he orders his angels to protect you wherever you go. 
Psalms91:12 They will steady you with their hands to keep you from stumbling against the rocks on the trail. 
Psalms91:13 You can safely meet a lion or step on poisonous snakes, yes, even trample them beneath your feet!" 
Psalms91:14 For the Lord says, “Because he loves me, I will rescue him; I will make him great because he trusts in my name." 
Psalms91:15 When he calls on me, I will answer; I will be with him in trouble and rescue him and honor him." 
Psalms91:16 I will satisfy him with a full life and give him my salvation.“

Psalms92:2 Every morning tell him, “Thank you for your kindness,“ and every evening rejoice in all his faithfulness." 
Psalms92:3 Sing his praises, accompanied by music from the harp and lute and lyre." 
Psalms92:4 You have done so much for me, O Lord. No wonder I am glad! I sing for joy." 
Psalms92:5 O Lord, what miracles you do! And how deep are your thoughts!" 
Psalms92:6 Unthinking people do not understand them! No fool can comprehend this: 
Psalms92:7 that although the wicked flourish like weeds, there is only eternal destruction ahead of them." 
Psalms92:8 But the Lord continues forever, exalted in the heavens," 
Psalms92:9 while his enemies—all evil-doers—shall be scattered. 
Psalms92:10 But you have made me as strong as a wild bull. How refreshed I am by your blessings! 
Psalms92:11 I have heard the doom of my enemies announced and seen them destroyed. 
Psalms92:12 But the godly shall flourish like palm trees and grow tall as the cedars of Lebanon. 
Psalms92:13 For they are transplanted into the Lord's own garden and are under his personal care. 
Psalms92:14 Even in old age they will still produce fruit and be vital and green. 
Psalms92:15 This honors the Lord and exhibits his faithful care. He is my shelter. There is nothing but goodness in him!

Psalms93:4 You are mightier than all the breakers pounding on the seashores of the world! 
Psalms93:5 Your royal decrees cannot be changed. Holiness is forever the keynote of your reign.

Psalms94:7 isn't looking,“ they say, “and besides, he doesn't care.“" 
Psalms94:8 Fools! 
Psalms94:9 Is God deaf and blind—he who makes ears and eyes? 
Psalms94:10 He punishes the nations—won't he also punish you? He knows everything—doesn't he also know what you are doing? 
Psalms94:11 The Lord is fully aware of how limited and futile the thoughts of mankind are," 
Psalms94:12 so he helps us by punishing us. 
Psalms94:13 This makes us follow his paths and gives us respite from our enemies while God traps them and destroys them. 
Psalms94:14 The Lord will not forsake his people, for they are his prize." 
Psalms94:15 Judgment will again be just, and all the upright will rejoice." 
Psalms94:16 Who will protect me from the wicked? Who will be my shield? 
Psalms94:17 I would have died unless the Lord had helped me.

Psalms94:19 Lord, when doubts fill my mind, when my heart is in turmoil, quiet me and give me renewed hope and cheer." 
Psalms94:20 Will you permit a corrupt government to rule under your protection—a government permitting wrong to defeat right? 
Psalms94:21 Do you approve of those who condemn the innocent to death? No! 
Psalms94:22 The Lord my God is my fortress—the mighty Rock where I can hide. 
Psalms94:23 God has made the sins of evil men to boomerang upon them! He will destroy them by their own plans. Jehovah our God will cut them off.

Psalms95:4 He controls the formation of the depths of the earth and the mightiest mountains; all are his. 
Psalms95:5 He made the sea and formed the land; they too are his. 
Psalms95:6 Come, kneel before the Lord our Maker," 
Psalms95:7 for he is our God. We are his sheep, and he is our Shepherd. Oh, that you would hear him calling you today and come to him!"

Psalms95:9 For there your fathers doubted me, though they had seen so many of my miracles before. My patience was severely tried by their complaints." 
Psalms95:10 “For forty years I watched them in disgust,“ the Lord God says. “They were a nation whose thoughts and heart were far away from me. They refused to accept my laws." 
Psalms95:11 Therefore, in mighty wrath I swore that they would never enter the Promised Land, the place of rest I planned for them.“"

Psalms96:5 For the gods of other nations are merely idols, but our God made the heavens!"

Psalms101:4 I will reject all selfishness and stay away from every evil. 
Psalms101:5 I will not tolerate anyone who secretly slanders his neighbors; I will not permit conceit and pride.

Psalms101:7 But I will not allow those who deceive and lie to stay in my house.

Psalms102:1 A prayer when overwhelmed with trouble. Lord, hear my prayer! Listen to my plea!" 
Psalms102:2 Don't turn away from me in this time of my distress. Bend down your ear and give me speedy answers," 
Psalms102:3 for my days disappear like smoke. 
Psalms102:4 My health is broken, and my heart is sick; it is trampled like grass and is withered. My food is tasteless, and I have lost my appetite." 
Psalms102:5 I am reduced to skin and bones because of all my groaning and despair. 
Psalms102:6 I am like a vulture in a far-off wilderness or like an owl alone in the desert. 
Psalms102:7 I lie awake, lonely as a solitary sparrow on the roof." 
Psalms102:8 My enemies taunt me day after day and curse at me. 
Psalms102:9 I eat ashes instead of bread. My tears run down into my drink because of your anger against me, because of your wrath." 
Psalms102:10 For you have rejected me and thrown me out. 
Psalms102:11 My life is passing swiftly as the evening shadows. I am withering like grass," 
Psalms102:12 while you, Lord, are a famous King forever. Your fame will endure to every generation." 
Psalms102:13 I know that you will come and have mercy on Jerusalem—and now is the time to pity her—the time you promised help. 
Psalms102:14 For your people love every stone in her walls and feel sympathy for every grain of dust in her streets. 
Psalms102:15 Now let the nations and their rulers tremble before the Lord, before his glory." 
Psalms102:16 For Jehovah will rebuild Jerusalem! He will appear in his glory! 
Psalms102:17 He will listen to the prayers of the destitute, for he is never too busy to heed their requests." 
Psalms102:18 I am recording this so that future generations will also praise the Lord for all that he has done. And a people that shall be created shall praise the Lord. 
Psalms102:19 Tell them that God looked down from his temple in heaven 
Psalms102:20 and heard the groans of his people in slavery—they were children of death—and released them," 
Psalms102:21 so that multitudes would stream to the Temple in Jerusalem to praise him, and his" 
Psalms102:22 praises were sung throughout the city; and many rulers throughout the earth came to worship him. 
Psalms102:23 He has cut me down in middle life, shortening my days." 
Psalms102:24 But I cried to him, “O God, you live forever and forever! Don't let me die half through my years!" 
Psalms102:25 In ages past you laid the foundations of the earth and made the heavens with your hands! 
Psalms102:26 They shall perish, but you go on forever. They will grow old like worn-out clothing, and you will change them like a man putting on a new shirt and throwing away the old one!" 
Psalms102:27 But you yourself never grow old. You are forever, and your years never end." 
Psalms102:28 “But our families will continue; generation after generation will be preserved by your protection.“

Psalms103:2 Yes, I will bless the Lord and not forget the glorious things he does for me." 
Psalms103:3 He forgives all my sins. He heals me. 
Psalms103:4 He ransoms me from hell. He surrounds me with loving-kindness and tender mercies. 
Psalms103:5 He fills my life with good things! My youth is renewed like the eagle's! 
Psalms103:6 He gives justice to all who are treated unfairly. 
Psalms103:7 He revealed his will and nature to Moses and the people of Israel. 
Psalms103:8 He is merciful and tender toward those who don't deserve it; he is slow to get angry and full of kindness and love. 
Psalms103:9 He never bears a grudge, nor remains angry forever." 
Psalms103:10 He has not punished us as we deserve for all our sins," 
Psalms103:11 for his mercy toward those who fear and honor him is as great as the height of the heavens above the earth. 
Psalms103:12 He has removed our sins as far away from us as the east is from the west. 
Psalms103:13 He is like a father to us, tender and sympathetic to those who reverence him." 
Psalms103:14 For he knows we are but dust 
Psalms103:15 and that our days are few and brief, like grass, like flowers," 
Psalms103:16 blown by the wind and gone forever.

Psalms103:18 his salvation is to children's children of those who are faithful to his covenant and remember to obey him!

Psalms103:21 Yes, bless the Lord, you armies of his angels who serve him constantly."
