Psalms104:1 I bless the Lord: O Lord my God, how great you are! You are robed with honor and with majesty and light!" 
Psalms104:2 You stretched out the starry curtain of the heavens," 
Psalms104:3 and hollowed out the surface of the earth to form the seas. The clouds are his chariots. He rides upon the wings of the wind. 
Psalms104:4 The angels are his messengers—his servants of fire! 
Psalms104:5 You bound the world together so that it would never fall apart. 
Psalms104:6 You clothed the earth with floods of waters covering up the mountains. 
Psalms104:7 You spoke, and at the sound of your shout the water collected into its" 
Psalms104:8 vast ocean beds, and mountains rose and valleys sank to the levels you decreed." 
Psalms104:9 And then you set a boundary for the seas so that they would never again cover the earth. 
Psalms104:10 He placed springs in the valleys and streams that gush from the mountains. 
Psalms104:11 They give water for all the animals to drink. There the wild donkeys quench their thirst," 
Psalms104:12 and the birds nest beside the streams and sing among the branches of the trees. 
Psalms104:13 He sends rain upon the mountains and fills the earth with fruit. 
Psalms104:14 The tender grass grows up at his command to feed the cattle, and there are fruit trees, vegetables, and grain for man to cultivate," 
Psalms104:15 and wine to make him glad, and olive oil as lotion for his skin, and bread to give him strength." 
Psalms104:16 The Lord planted the cedars of Lebanon. They are tall and flourishing. 
Psalms104:17 There the birds make their nests, the storks in the firs." 
Psalms104:18 High in the mountains are pastures for the wild goats, and rock-badgers burrow in among the rocks and find protection there." 
Psalms104:19 He assigned the moon to mark the months and the sun to mark the days. 
Psalms104:20 He sends the night and darkness, when all the forest folk come out." 
Psalms104:21 Then the young lions roar for their food, but they are dependent on the Lord." 
Psalms104:22 At dawn they slink back into their dens to rest," 
Psalms104:23 and men go off to work until the evening shadows fall again. 
Psalms104:24 O Lord, what a variety you have made! And in wisdom you have made them all! The earth is full of your riches." 
Psalms104:25 There before me lies the mighty ocean, teeming with life of every kind, both great and small." 
Psalms104:26 And look! See the ships! And over there, the whale you made to play in the sea." 
Psalms104:27 Every one of these depends on you to give them daily food. 
Psalms104:28 You supply it, and they gather it. You open wide your hand to feed them, and they are satisfied with all your bountiful provision." 
Psalms104:29 But if you turn away from them, then all is lost. And when you gather up their breath, they die and turn again to dust." 
Psalms104:30 Then you send your Spirit, and new life is born to replenish all the living of the earth." 
Psalms104:31 Praise God forever! How he must rejoice in all his work! 
Psalms104:32 The earth trembles at his glance; the mountains burst into flame at his touch. 
Psalms104:33 I will sing to the Lord as long as I live. I will praise God to my last breath! 
Psalms104:34 May he be pleased by all these thoughts about him, for he is the source of all my joy." 
Psalms104:35 Let all sinners perish—all who refuse to praise him. But I will praise him. Hallelujah! 
Psalms105:1 Thank the Lord for all the glorious things he does; proclaim them to the nations. 
Psalms105:2 Sing his praises and tell everyone about his miracles. 
Psalms105:3 Glory in the Lord; O worshipers of God, rejoice." 
Psalms105:4 Search for him and for his strength, and keep on searching!" 
Psalms105:5 Think of the mighty deeds he did for us, his chosen ones—descendants of God's servant Abraham, and of Jacob." 
Psalms105:6 Remember how he destroyed our enemies. 
Psalms105:7 He is the Lord our God. His goodness is seen everywhere throughout the land. 
Psalms105:8 Though a thousand generations pass he never forgets 
Psalms105:9 his promise, his covenant with Abraham and Isaac" 
Psalms105:10 and confirmed with Jacob. 
Psalms105:11 This is his never-ending treaty with the people of Israel: “I will give you the land of Canaan as your inheritance.“ 
Psalms105:12 He said this when they were but few in number, very few, and were only visitors in Canaan." 
Psalms105:13 Later they were dispersed among the nations and were driven from one kingdom to another; 
Psalms105:14 but through it all he would not let one thing be done to them apart from his decision. He destroyed many a king who tried! 
Psalms105:15 “Touch not these chosen ones of mine,“ he warned, “and do not hurt my prophets.“" 
Psalms105:16 He called for a famine on the land of Canaan, cutting off its food supply." 
Psalms105:17 Then he sent Joseph as a slave to Egypt to save his people from starvation. 
Psalms105:18 There in prison they hurt his feet with fetters and placed his neck in an iron collar 
Psalms105:19 until God's time finally came—how God tested his patience! 
Psalms105:20 Then the king sent for him and set him free. 
Psalms105:21 He was put in charge of all the king's possessions. 
Psalms105:22 At his pleasure he could imprison the king's aides and teach the king's advisors. 
Psalms105:23 Then Jacob (Israel) arrived in Egypt and lived there with his sons. 
Psalms105:24 In the years that followed, the people of Israel multiplied explosively until they were a greater nation than their rulers." 
Psalms105:25 At that point God turned the Egyptians against the Israelis; they hated and enslaved them. 
Psalms105:26 But God sent Moses as his representative, and Aaron with him," 
Psalms105:27 to call down miracles of terror upon the land of Egypt. 
Psalms105:28 They followed his instructions. He sent thick darkness through the land 
Psalms105:29 and turned the nation's water into blood, poisoning the fish." 
Psalms105:30 Then frogs invaded in enormous numbers; they were found even in the king's private rooms. 
Psalms105:31 When Moses spoke, the flies and other insects swarmed in vast clouds from one end of Egypt to the other." 
Psalms105:32 Instead of rain he sent down murderous hail, and lightning flashes overwhelmed the nation." 
Psalms105:33 Their grape vines and fig trees were ruined; all the trees lay broken on the ground. 
Psalms105:34 He spoke, and hordes of locusts came" 
Psalms105:35 and ate up everything green, destroying all the crops." 
Psalms105:36 Then he killed the oldest child in each Egyptian home, their pride and joy—" 
Psalms105:37 and brought his people safely out from Egypt, loaded with silver and gold; there were no sick and feeble folk among them then." 
Psalms105:38 Egypt was glad when they were gone, for the dread of them was great." 
Psalms105:39 He spread out a cloud above them to shield them from the burning sun and gave them a pillar of flame at night to give them light. 
Psalms105:40 They asked for meat, and he sent them quail and gave them manna—bread from heaven." 
Psalms105:41 He opened up a rock, and water gushed out to form a river through the dry and barren land;" 
Psalms105:42 for he remembered his sacred promises to Abraham his servant. 
Psalms105:43 So he brought his chosen ones singing into the Promised Land. 
Psalms105:44 He gave them the lands of the Gentiles, complete with their growing crops; they ate what others planted." 
Psalms105:45 This was done to make them faithful and obedient to his laws. Hallelujah! 
Psalms106:1 Hallelujah! Thank you, Lord! How good you are! Your love for us continues on forever." 
Psalms106:2 Who can ever list the glorious miracles of God? Who can ever praise him half enough? 
Psalms106:3 Happiness comes to those who are fair to others and are always just and good. 
Psalms106:4 Remember me too, O Lord, while you are blessing and saving your people." 
Psalms106:5 Let me share in your chosen ones' prosperity and rejoice in all their joys, and receive the glory you give to them." 
Psalms106:6 Both we and our fathers have sinned so much. 
Psalms106:7 They weren't impressed by the wonder of your miracles in Egypt and soon forgot your many acts of kindness to them. Instead they rebelled against you at the Red Sea. 
Psalms106:8 Even so you saved them—to defend the honor of your name and demonstrate your power to all the world. 
Psalms106:9 You commanded the Red Sea to divide, forming a dry road across its bottom. Yes, as dry as any desert!" 
Psalms106:10 Thus you rescued them from their enemies. 
Psalms106:11 Then the water returned and covered the road and drowned their foes; not one survived. 
Psalms106:12 Then at last his people believed him. Then they finally sang his praise. 
Psalms106:13 Yet how quickly they forgot again! They wouldn't wait for him to act 
Psalms106:14 but demanded better food, testing God's patience to the breaking point." 
Psalms106:15 So he gave them their demands but sent them leanness in their souls. 
Psalms106:16 They were envious of Moses, yes, and Aaron too, the man anointed by God as his priest." 
Psalms106:17 Because of this, the earth opened and swallowed Dathan, Abiram, and his friends;" 
Psalms106:18 and fire fell from heaven to consume these wicked men. 
Psalms106:19 For they preferred a statue of an ox that 
Psalms106:20 eats grass to the glorious presence of God himself. 
Psalms106:21 Thus they despised their Savior who had done 
Psalms106:22 such mighty miracles in Egypt and at the Sea. 
Psalms106:23 So the Lord declared he would destroy them. But Moses, his chosen one, stepped into the breach between the people and their God and begged him to turn from his wrath and not destroy them." 
Psalms106:24 They refused to enter the Promised Land, for they wouldn't believe his solemn oath to care for them." 
Psalms106:25 Instead, they pouted in their tents and mourned and despised his command." 
Psalms106:26 Therefore he swore that he would kill them in the wilderness 
Psalms106:27 and send their children away to distant lands as exiles. 
Psalms106:28 Then our fathers joined the worshipers of Baal at Peor and even offered sacrifices to the dead! 
Psalms106:29 With all these things they angered him—and so a plague broke out upon them 
Psalms106:30 and continued until Phineas executed those whose sins had caused the plague to start. 
Psalms106:31 (For this good deed Phineas will be remembered forever.) 
Psalms106:32 At Meribah, too, Israel angered God, causing Moses serious trouble," 
Psalms106:33 for he became angry and spoke foolishly. 
Psalms106:34 Nor did Israel destroy the nations in the land as God had told them to," 
Psalms106:35 but mingled in among the heathen and learned their evil ways," 
Psalms106:36 sacrificing to their idols, and were led away from God." 
Psalms106:37 They even sacrificed their little children to the demons—the idols 
Psalms106:38 of Canaan—shedding innocent blood and polluting the land with murder. 
Psalms106:39 Their evil deeds defiled them, for their love of idols was adultery in the sight of God." 
Psalms106:40 That is why Jehovah's anger burned against his people, and he abhorred them." 
Psalms106:41 That is why he let the heathen nations crush them. 
Psalms106:42 They were ruled by those who hated them and oppressed by their enemies. 
Psalms106:43 Again and again he delivered them from their slavery, but they continued to rebel against him and were finally destroyed by their sin." 
Psalms106:44 Yet, even so, he listened to their cries and heeded their distress;" 
Psalms106:45 he remembered his promises to them and relented because of his great love," 
Psalms106:46 and caused even their enemies who captured them to pity them. 
Psalms106:47 O Lord God, save us! Regather us from the nations so we can thank your holy name and rejoice and praise you." 
Psalms106:48 Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting. Let all the people say, “Amen!“ Hallelujah!" 
Psalms107:1 Say thank you to the Lord for being so good, for always being so loving and kind." 
Psalms107:2 Has the Lord redeemed you? Then speak out! Tell others he has saved you from your enemies. 
Psalms107:3 He brought the exiles back from the farthest corners of the earth. 
Psalms107:4 They were wandering homeless in the desert," 
Psalms107:5 hungry and thirsty and faint. 
Psalms107:6 “Lord, help!“ they cried, and he did!" 
Psalms107:7 He led them straight to safety and a place to live. 
Psalms107:8 Oh, that these men would praise the Lord for his loving-kindness, and for all of his wonderful deeds!" 
Psalms107:9 For he satisfies the thirsty soul and fills the hungry soul with good. 
Psalms107:10 Who are these who sit in darkness, in the shadow of death, crushed by misery and slavery?" 
Psalms107:11 They rebelled against the Lord, scorning him who is the God above all gods." 
Psalms107:12 That is why he broke them with hard labor; they fell and none could help them rise again. 
Psalms107:13 Then they cried to the Lord in their troubles, and he rescued them!" 
Psalms107:14 He led them from the darkness and shadow of death and snapped their chains. 
Psalms107:15 Oh, that these men would praise the Lord for his loving-kindness and for all of his wonderful deeds!" 
Psalms107:16 For he broke down their prison gates of brass and cut apart their iron bars. 
Psalms107:17 Others, the fools, were ill because of their sinful ways." 
Psalms107:18 Their appetites were gone, and death was near." 
Psalms107:19 Then they cried to the Lord in their troubles, and he helped them and delivered them." 
Psalms107:20 He spoke, and they were healed—snatched from the door of death." 
Psalms107:21 Oh, that these men would praise the Lord for his loving-kindness and for all of his wonderful deeds!" 
Psalms107:22 Let them tell him thank you as their sacrifice and sing about his glorious deeds. 
Psalms107:23 And then there are the sailors sailing the seven seas, plying the trade routes of the world." 
Psalms107:24 They, too, observe the power of God in action." 
Psalms107:25 He calls to the storm winds; the waves rise high. 
Psalms107:26 Their ships are tossed to the heavens and sink again to the depths; the sailors cringe in terror. 
Psalms107:27 They reel and stagger like drunkards and are at their wit's end. 
Psalms107:28 Then they cry to the Lord in their trouble, and he saves them." 
Psalms107:29 He calms the storm and stills the waves. 
Psalms107:30 What a blessing is that stillness as he brings them safely into harbor! 
Psalms107:31 Oh, that these men would praise the Lord for his loving-kindness and for all of his wonderful deeds!" 
Psalms107:32 Let them praise him publicly before the congregation and before the leaders of the nation. 
Psalms107:33 He dries up rivers 
Psalms107:34 and turns the good land of the wicked into deserts of salt. 
Psalms107:35 Again, he turns deserts into fertile, watered valleys." 
Psalms107:36 He brings the hungry to settle there and build their cities," 
Psalms107:37 to sow their fields and plant their vineyards, and reap their bumper crops!" 
Psalms107:38 How he blesses them! They raise big families there and many cattle. 
Psalms107:39 But others become poor through oppression, trouble, and sorrow." 
Psalms107:40 For God pours contempt upon the haughty and causes princes to wander among ruins; 
Psalms107:41 but he rescues the poor who are godly and gives them many children and much prosperity. 
Psalms107:42 Good men everywhere will see it and be glad, while evil men are stricken silent." 
Psalms107:43 Listen, if you are wise, to what I am saying. Think about the loving-kindness of the Lord!" 
Psalms108:1 O God, my heart is ready to praise you! I will sing and rejoice before you." 
Psalms108:2 Wake up, O harp and lyre! We will meet the dawn with song." 
Psalms108:3 I will praise you everywhere around the world, in every nation." 
Psalms108:4 For your loving-kindness is great beyond measure, high as the heavens. Your faithfulness reaches the skies." 
Psalms108:5 His glory is far more vast than the heavens. It towers above the earth. 
Psalms108:6 Hear the cry of your beloved child—come with mighty power and rescue me. 
Psalms108:7 God has given sacred promises; no wonder I exult! He has promised to give us all the land of Shechem and also Succoth Valley. 
Psalms108:8 “Gilead is mine to give to you,“ he says, “and Manasseh as well; the land of Ephraim is the helmet on my head. Judah is my scepter." 
Psalms108:9 But Moab and Edom are despised; and I will shout in triumph over the Philistines.“ 
Psalms108:10 Who but God can give me strength to conquer these fortified cities? Who else can lead me into Edom? 
Psalms108:11 Lord, have you thrown us away? Have you deserted our army?" 
Psalms108:12 Oh, help us fight against our enemies, for men are useless allies." 
Psalms108:13 But with the help of God we shall do mighty acts of valor. For he treads down our foes. 
Psalms109:1 O God of my praise, don't stand silent and aloof" 
Psalms109:2 while the wicked slander me and tell their lies. 
Psalms109:3 They have no reason to hate and fight me, yet they do!" 
Psalms109:4 I love them, but even while I am praying for them, they are trying to destroy me." 
Psalms109:5 They return evil for good, and hatred for love." 
Psalms109:6 Show him how it feels! Let lies be told about him, and bring him to court before an unfair judge." 
Psalms109:7 When his case is called for judgment, let him be pronounced guilty. Count his prayers as sins." 
Psalms109:8 Let his years be few and brief; let others step forward to replace him. 
Psalms109:9 May his children become fatherless and his wife a widow; 
Psalms109:10 may they be evicted from the ruins of their home. 
Psalms109:11 May creditors seize his entire estate and strangers take all he has earned. 
Psalms109:12 Let no one be kind to him; let no one pity his fatherless children. May they die. 
Psalms109:13 May his family name be blotted out in a single generation. 
Psalms109:14 Punish the sins of his father and mother. Don't overlook them. 
Psalms109:15 Think constantly about the evil things he has done, and cut off his name from the memory of man." 
Psalms109:16 For he refused all kindness to others, and persecuted those in need, and hounded brokenhearted ones to death." 
Psalms109:17 He loved to curse others; now you curse him. He never blessed others; now don't you bless him. 
Psalms109:18 Cursing is as much a part of him as his clothing, or as the water he drinks, or the rich food he eats." 
Psalms109:19 Now may those curses return and cling to him like his clothing or his belt. 
Psalms109:20 This is the Lord's punishment upon my enemies who tell lies about me and threaten me with death. 
Psalms109:21 But as for me, O Lord, deal with me as your child, as one who bears your name! Because you are so kind, O Lord, deliver me." 
Psalms109:22 I am slipping down the hill to death; I am shaken off 
Psalms109:23 from life as easily as a man brushes a grasshopper from his arm. 
Psalms109:24 My knees are weak from fasting, and I am skin and bones." 
Psalms109:25 I am a symbol of failure to all mankind; when they see me they shake their heads. 
Psalms109:26 Help me, O Lord my God! Save me because you are loving and kind." 
Psalms109:27 Do it publicly, so all will see that you yourself have done it." 
Psalms109:28 Then let them curse me if they like—I won't mind that if you are blessing me! For then all their efforts to destroy me will fail, and I shall go right on rejoicing!" 
Psalms109:29 Make them fail in everything they do. Clothe them with disgrace. 
Psalms109:30 But I will give repeated thanks to the Lord, praising him to everyone." 
Psalms109:31 For he stands beside the poor and hungry to save them from their enemies. 
Psalms110:1 Jehovah said to my Lord the Messiah, “Rule as my regent—I will subdue your enemies and make them bow low before you.“" 
Psalms110:2 Jehovah has established your throne in Jerusalem to rule over your enemies. 
Psalms110:3 In that day of your power your people shall come to you willingly, dressed in holy altar robes. And your strength shall be renewed day by day like morning dew." 
Psalms110:4 Jehovah has taken oath and will not rescind his vow that you are a priest forever like Melchizedek. 
Psalms110:5 God stands beside you to protect you. He will strike down many kings in the day of his anger. 
Psalms110:6 He will punish the nations and fill them with their dead. He will crush many heads. 
Psalms110:7 But he himself shall be refreshed from springs along the way. 
Psalms111:1 Hallelujah! I want to express publicly before his people my heartfelt thanks to God for his mighty miracles. 
Psalms111:2 All who are thankful should ponder them with me. 
Psalms111:3 For his miracles demonstrate his honor, majesty, and eternal goodness." 
Psalms111:4 Who can forget the wonders he performs—deeds of mercy and of grace? 
Psalms111:5 He gives food to those who trust him; he never forgets his promises. 
Psalms111:6 He has shown his great power to his people by giving them the land of Israel, though it was the home of many nations living there." 
Psalms111:7 All he does is just and good, and all his laws are right," 
Psalms111:8 for they are formed from truth and goodness and stand firm forever. 
Psalms111:9 He has paid a full ransom for his people; now they are always free to come to Jehovah (what a holy, awe-inspiring name that is)." 
Psalms111:10 How can men be wise? The only way to begin is by reverence for God. For growth in wisdom comes from obeying his laws. Praise his name forever. 
Psalms112:1 Praise the Lord! For all who fear God and trust in him are blessed beyond expression. Yes, happy is the man who delights in doing his commands." 
Psalms112:2 His children shall be honored everywhere, for good men's sons have a special heritage." 
Psalms112:3 He himself shall be wealthy, and his good deeds will never be forgotten." 
Psalms112:4 When darkness overtakes him, light will come bursting in. He is kind and merciful—" 
Psalms112:5 and all goes well for the generous man who conducts his business fairly. 
Psalms112:6 Such a man will not be overthrown by evil circumstances. God's constant care of him will make a deep impression on all who see it. 
Psalms112:7 He does not fear bad news, nor live in dread of what may happen. For he is settled in his mind that Jehovah will take care of him." 
Psalms112:8 That is why he is not afraid but can calmly face his foes. 
Psalms112:9 He gives generously to those in need. His deeds will never be forgotten. He shall have influence and honor. 
Psalms112:10 Evil-minded men will be infuriated when they see all this; they will gnash their teeth in anger and slink away, their hopes thwarted." 
Psalms113:1 Hallelujah! O servants of Jehovah, praise his name." 
Psalms113:2 Blessed is his name forever and forever. 
Psalms113:3 Praise him from sunrise to sunset! 
Psalms113:4 For he is high above the nations; his glory is far greater than the heavens. 
Psalms113:5 Who can be compared with God enthroned on high? 
Psalms113:6 Far below him are the heavens and the earth; he stoops to look," 
Psalms113:7 and lifts the poor from the dirt and the hungry from the garbage dump," 
Psalms113:8 and sets them among princes! 
Psalms113:9 He gives children to the childless wife, so that she becomes a happy mother. Hallelujah! Praise the Lord." 
Psalms114:1 Long ago when the Israelis escaped from Egypt, from that land of foreign tongue," 
Psalms114:2 then the lands of Judah and of Israel became God's new home and kingdom. 
Psalms114:3 The Red Sea saw them coming and quickly broke apart before them. The Jordan River opened up a path for them to cross. 
Psalms114:4 The mountains skipped like rams, the little hills like lambs!" 
Psalms114:5 What's wrong, Red Sea, that made you cut yourself in two? What happened, Jordan River, to your waters? Why were they held back?" 
Psalms114:6 Why, mountains, did you skip like rams? Why, little hills, like lambs?" 
Psalms114:7 Tremble, O earth, at the presence of the Lord, the God of Jacob." 
Psalms114:8 For he caused gushing streams to burst from flinty rock. 
Psalms115:1 Glorify your name, not ours, O Lord! Cause everyone to praise your loving-kindness and your truth." 
Psalms115:2 Why let the nations say, “Their God is dead!“" 
Psalms115:3 For he is in the heavens and does as he wishes. 
Psalms115:4 Their gods are merely manmade things of silver and of gold. 
Psalms115:5 They can't talk or see, despite their eyes and mouths!" 
Psalms115:6 Nor can they hear, nor smell," 
Psalms115:7 nor use their hands or feet, nor speak!" 
Psalms115:8 And those who make and worship them are just as foolish as their idols are. 
Psalms115:9 O Israel, trust the Lord! He is your helper. He is your shield." 
Psalms115:10 O priests of Aaron, trust the Lord! He is your helper; he is your shield." 
Psalms115:11 All of you, his people, trust in him. He is your helper; he is your shield." 
Psalms115:12 Jehovah is constantly thinking about us, and he will surely bless us. He will bless the people of Israel and the priests of Aaron," 
Psalms115:13 and all, both great and small, who reverence him." 
Psalms115:14 May the Lord richly bless both you and your children. 
Psalms115:15 Yes, Jehovah who made heaven and earth will personally bless you!" 
Psalms115:16 The heavens belong to the Lord, but he has given the earth to all mankind." 
Psalms115:17 The dead cannot sing praises to Jehovah here on earth," 
Psalms115:18 but we can! We praise him forever! Hallelujah! Praise the Lord! 
Psalms116:1 I love the Lord because he hears my prayers and answers them. 
Psalms116:2 Because he bends down and listens, I will pray as long as I breathe!" 
Psalms116:3 Death stared me in the face—I was frightened and sad. 
Psalms116:4 Then I cried, “Lord, save me!“" 
Psalms116:5 How kind he is! How good he is! So merciful, this God of ours!" 
Psalms116:6 The Lord protects the simple and the childlike; I was facing death, and then he saved me." 
Psalms116:7 Now I can relax. For the Lord has done this wonderful miracle for me. 
Psalms116:8 He has saved me from death, my eyes from tears, my feet from stumbling." 
Psalms116:9 I shall live! Yes, in his presence—here on earth!" 
Psalms116:10 In my discouragement I thought, “They are" 
Psalms116:11 lying when they say I will recover.“ 
Psalms116:12 But now what can I offer Jehovah for all he has done for me? 
Psalms116:13 I will bring him an offering of wine and praise his name for saving me. 
Psalms116:14 I will publicly bring him the sacrifice I vowed I would. 
Psalms116:15 His loved ones are very precious to him, and he does not lightly let them die." 
Psalms116:16 O Lord, you have freed me from my bonds, and I will serve you forever." 
Psalms116:17 I will worship you and offer you a sacrifice of thanksgiving. 
Psalms116:18 Here in the courts of the Temple in Jerusalem, before all the people, I will pay everything I vowed to the Lord." 
Psalms116:19 Praise the Lord. 
Psalms117:1 Praise the Lord, all nations everywhere. Praise him, all the peoples of the earth." 
Psalms117:2 For he loves us very dearly, and his truth endures. Praise the Lord." 
Psalms118:1 Oh, thank the Lord, for he's so good! His loving-kindness is forever." 
Psalms118:2 Let the congregation of Israel praise him with these same words: “His loving-kindness is forever.“ 
Psalms118:3 And let the priests of Aaron chant, “His loving-kindness is forever.“" 
Psalms118:4 Let the Gentile converts chant, “His loving-kindness is forever.“" 
Psalms118:5 In my distress I prayed to the Lord, and he answered me and rescued me." 
Psalms118:6 He is for me! How can I be afraid? What can mere man do to me? 
Psalms118:7 The Lord is on my side; he will help me. Let those who hate me beware. 
Psalms118:8 It is better to trust the Lord than to put confidence in men. 
Psalms118:9 It is better to take refuge in him than in the mightiest king! 
Psalms118:10 Though all the nations of the world attack me, I will march out behind his banner and destroy them." 
Psalms118:11 Yes, they surround and attack me; but with his flag flying above me I will cut them off." 
Psalms118:12 They swarm around me like bees; they blaze against me like a roaring flame. Yet beneath his flag I shall destroy them. 
Psalms118:13 You did your best to kill me, O my enemy, but the Lord helped me." 
Psalms118:14 He is my strength and song in the heat of battle, and now he has given me the victory." 
Psalms118:15 Songs of joy at the news of our rescue are sung in the homes of the godly. 
Psalms118:16 The strong arm of the Lord has done glorious things! 
Psalms118:17 I shall not die but live to tell of all his deeds. 
Psalms118:18 The Lord has punished me but not handed me over to death. 
Psalms118:19 Open the gates of the Temple—I will go in and give him my thanks. 
Psalms118:20 Those gates are the way into the presence of the Lord, and the godly enter there." 
Psalms118:21 O Lord, thank you so much for answering my prayer and saving me." 
Psalms118:22 The stone rejected by the builders has now become the capstone of the arch! 
Psalms118:23 This is the Lord's doing, and it is marvelous to see!" 
Psalms118:24 This is the day the Lord has made. We will rejoice and be glad in it. 
Psalms118:25 O Lord, please help us. Save us. Give us success." 
Psalms118:26 Blessed is the one who is coming, the one sent by the Lord. We bless you from the Temple." 
Psalms118:27 Jehovah God is our light. 
Psalms118:28 I present to him my sacrifice upon the altar, for you are my God, and I shall give you this thanks and this praise." 
Psalms118:29 Oh, give thanks to the Lord, for he is so good! For his loving-kindness is forever." 
Psalms119:1 Happy are all who perfectly follow the laws of God. 
Psalms119:2 Happy are all who search for God and always do his will," 
Psalms119:3 rejecting compromise with evil and walking only in his paths. 
Psalms119:4 You have given us your laws to obey— 
Psalms119:5 oh, how I want to follow them consistently." 
Psalms119:6 Then I will not be disgraced, for I will have a clean record." 
Psalms119:7 After you have corrected me, I will thank you by living as I should!" 
Psalms119:8 I will obey! Oh, don't forsake me and let me slip back into sin again." 
Psalms119:9 How can a young man stay pure? By reading your Word and following its rules. 
Psalms119:10 I have tried my best to find you—don't let me wander off from your instructions. 
Psalms119:11 I have thought much about your words and stored them in my heart so that they would hold me back from sin. 
Psalms119:12 Blessed Lord, teach me your rules." 
Psalms119:13 I have recited your laws 
Psalms119:14 and rejoiced in them more than in riches. 
Psalms119:15 I will meditate upon them and give them my full respect. 
Psalms119:16 I will delight in them and not forget them. 
Psalms119:17 Bless me with life so that I can continue to obey you. 
Psalms119:18 Open my eyes to see wonderful things in your Word. 
Psalms119:19 I am but a pilgrim here on earth: how I need a map—and your commands are my chart and guide. 
Psalms119:20 I long for your instructions more than I can tell. 
Psalms119:21 You rebuke those cursed proud ones who refuse your commands— 
Psalms119:22 don't let them scorn me for obeying you. 
Psalms119:23 For even princes sit and talk against me, but I will continue in your plans." 
Psalms119:24 Your laws are both my light and my counselors. 
Psalms119:25 I am completely discouraged—I lie in the dust. Revive me by your Word. 
Psalms119:26 I told you my plans and you replied. Now give me your instructions. 
Psalms119:27 Make me understand what you want; for then I shall see your miracles. 
Psalms119:28 I weep with grief; my heart is heavy with sorrow; encourage and cheer me with your words. 
Psalms119:29 Keep me far from every wrong; help me, undeserving as I" 
Psalms119:30 am, to obey your laws, for I have chosen to do right." 
Psalms119:31 I cling to your commands and follow them as closely as I can. Lord, don't let me make a mess of things." 
Psalms119:32 If you will only help me to want your will, then I will follow your laws even more closely." 
Psalms119:33 Just tell me what to do and I will do it, Lord." 
Psalms119:34 As long as I live I'll wholeheartedly obey. 
Psalms119:35 Make me walk along the right paths, for I know how delightful they really are." 
Psalms119:36 Help me to prefer obedience to making money! 
Psalms119:37 Turn me away from wanting any other plan than yours. Revive my heart toward you. 
Psalms119:38 Reassure me that your promises are for me, for I trust and revere you." 
Psalms119:39 How I dread being mocked for obeying, for your laws are right and good." 
Psalms119:40 I long to obey them! 
Psalms119:41 Therefore in fairness renew my life, for this was your promise—yes, Lord, to save me! Now spare me by your kindness and your love." 
Psalms119:42 Then I will have an answer for those who taunt me, for I trust your promises." 
Psalms119:43 May I never forget your words, for they are my only hope." 
Psalms119:44 Therefore I will keep on obeying you forever and forever, free" 
Psalms119:45 within the limits of your laws. I will speak to kings 
Psalms119:46 about their value, and they will listen with interest and respect." 
Psalms119:47 How I love your laws! How I enjoy your commands! 
Psalms119:48 “Come, come to me,“ I call to them, for I love them and will let them fill my life." 
Psalms119:49 Never forget your promises to me your servant, for they are my only hope." 
Psalms119:50 They give me strength in all my troubles; how they refresh and revive me! 
Psalms119:51 Proud men hold me in contempt for obedience to God, but I stand unmoved." 
Psalms119:52 From my earliest youth I have tried to obey you; your Word has been my comfort. 
Psalms119:53 I am very angry with those who spurn your commands. 
Psalms119:54 For these laws of yours have been my source of joy and singing through all these years of my earthly pilgrimage. 
Psalms119:55 I obey them even at night and keep my thoughts, O Lord, on you." 
Psalms119:56 What a blessing this has been to me—to constantly obey. 
Psalms119:57 Jehovah is mine! And I promise to obey! 
Psalms119:58 With all my heart I want your blessings. Be merciful just as you promised. 
Psalms119:59 I thought about the wrong direction in which I was 
Psalms119:60 headed, and turned around and came running back to you." 
Psalms119:61 Evil men have tried to drag me into sin, but I am firmly anchored to your laws." 
Psalms119:62 At midnight I will rise to give my thanks to you for your good laws. 
Psalms119:63 Anyone is my brother who fears and trusts the Lord and obeys him. 
Psalms119:64 O Lord, the earth is full of your loving-kindness! Teach me your good paths." 
Psalms119:65 Lord, I am overflowing with your blessings, just as you promised." 
Psalms119:66 Now teach me good judgment as well as knowledge. For your laws are my guide. 
Psalms119:67 I used to wander off until you punished me; now I closely follow all you say. 
Psalms119:68 You are good and do only good; make me follow your lead. 
Psalms119:69 Proud men have made up lies about me, but the truth is that I obey your laws with all my heart." 
Psalms119:70 Their minds are dull and stupid, but I have sense enough to follow you." 
Psalms119:71 The punishment you gave me was the best thing that could have happened to me," 
Psalms119:72 for it taught me to pay attention to your laws. They are more valuable to me than millions in silver and gold! 
Psalms119:73 You made my body, Lord; now give me sense to heed your laws." 
Psalms119:74 All those who fear and trust in you will welcome me because I too am trusting in your Word. 
Psalms119:75 I know, O Lord, that your decisions are right and that your punishment was right and did me good." 
Psalms119:76 Now let your loving-kindness comfort me, just as you promised." 
Psalms119:77 Surround me with your tender mercies that I may live. For your law is my delight. 
Psalms119:78 Let the proud be disgraced, for they have cut me down with all their lies. But I will concentrate my thoughts upon your laws." 
Psalms119:79 Let all others join me who trust and fear you, and we will discuss your laws." 
Psalms119:80 Help me to love your every wish; then I will never have to be ashamed of myself. 
Psalms119:81 I faint for your salvation; but I expect your help, for you have promised it." 
Psalms119:82 My eyes are straining to see your promises come true. When will you comfort me with your help? 
Psalms119:83 I am shriveled like a wineskin in the smoke, exhausted with waiting. But still I cling to your laws and obey them." 
Psalms119:84 How long must I wait before you punish those who persecute me? 
Psalms119:85 These proud men who hate your truth and laws have dug deep pits for me to fall in. Their lies have brought me into deep trouble. 
Psalms119:86 Help me, for you love only truth." 
Psalms119:87 They had almost finished me off, yet I refused to yield and disobey your laws." 
Psalms119:88 In your kindness, spare my life; then I can continue to obey you." 
Psalms119:89 Forever, O Lord, your Word stands firm in heaven." 
Psalms119:90 Your faithfulness extends to every generation, like the earth you" 
Psalms119:91 created; it endures by your decree, for everything serves your plans." 
Psalms119:92 I would have despaired and perished unless your laws had been my deepest delight. 
Psalms119:93 I will never lay aside your laws, for you have used them to restore my joy and health." 
Psalms119:94 I am yours! Save me! For I have tried to live according to your desires. 
Psalms119:95 Though the wicked hide along the way to kill me, I will quietly keep my mind upon your promises." 
Psalms119:96 Nothing is perfect except your words. 
Psalms119:97 Oh, how I love them. I think about them all day long." 
Psalms119:98 They make me wiser than my enemies because they are my constant guide. 
Psalms119:99 Yes, wiser than my teachers, for I am ever thinking of your rules." 
Psalms119:100 They make me even wiser than the aged. 
Psalms119:101 I have refused to walk the paths of evil, for I will remain obedient to your Word." 
Psalms119:102 No, I haven't turned away from what you" 
Psalms119:103 taught me; your words are sweeter than honey. 
Psalms119:104 And since only your rules can give me wisdom and understanding, no wonder I hate every false teaching." 
Psalms119:105 Your words are a flashlight to light the path ahead of me and keep me from stumbling. 
Psalms119:106 I've said it once and I'll say it again and again: I will obey these wonderful laws of yours. 
Psalms119:107 I am close to death at the hands of my enemies; oh, give me back my life again, just as you promised me." 
Psalms119:108 Accept my grateful thanks and teach me your desires. 
Psalms119:109 My life hangs in the balance, but I will not give up obedience to your laws." 
Psalms119:110 The wicked have set their traps for me along your path, but I will not turn aside." 
Psalms119:111 Your laws are my joyous treasure forever. 
Psalms119:112 I am determined to obey you until I die. 
Psalms119:113 I hate those who are undecided whether or not to obey you; but my choice is clear—I love your law. 
Psalms119:114 You are my refuge and my shield, and your promises are my only source of hope." 
Psalms119:115 Begone, you evil-minded men! Don't try to stop me from obeying God's commands." 
Psalms119:116 Lord, you promised to let me live! Never let it be said that God failed me." 
Psalms119:117 Hold me safe above the heads of all my enemies; then I can continue to obey your laws. 
Psalms119:118 But you have rejected all who reject your laws. They are only fooling themselves. 
Psalms119:119 The wicked are the scum you skim off and throw away; no wonder I love to obey your laws! 
Psalms119:120 I tremble in fear of you; I fear your punishments. 
Psalms119:121 Don't leave me to the mercy of my enemies, for I have done what is right; I've been perfectly fair." 
Psalms119:122 Commit yourself to bless me! Don't let the proud oppress me! 
Psalms119:123 My eyes grow dim with longing for you to fulfill your wonderful promise to rescue me. 
Psalms119:124 Lord, deal with me in loving-kindness, and teach me, your servant, to obey;" 
Psalms119:125 for I am your servant; therefore give me common sense to apply your rules to everything I do. 
Psalms119:126 Lord, it is time for you to act. For these evil men have violated your laws," 
Psalms119:127 while I love your commandments more than the finest gold. 
Psalms119:128 Every law of God is right, whatever it concerns. I hate every other way." 
Psalms119:129 Your laws are wonderful; no wonder I obey them. 
Psalms119:130 As your plan unfolds, even the simple can understand it." 
Psalms119:131 No wonder I wait expectantly for each of your commands. 
Psalms119:132 Come and have mercy on me as is your way with those who love you. 
Psalms119:133 Guide me with your laws so that I will not be overcome by evil. 
Psalms119:134 Rescue me from the oppression of evil men; then I can obey you. 
Psalms119:135 Look down in love upon me and teach me all your laws. 
Psalms119:136 I weep because your laws are disobeyed. 
Psalms119:137 O Lord, you are just and your punishments are fair." 
Psalms119:138 Your demands are just and right. 
Psalms119:139 I am indignant and angry because of the way my enemies have disregarded your laws. 
Psalms119:140 I have thoroughly tested your promises, and that is why I love them so much." 
Psalms119:141 I am worthless and despised, but I don't despise your laws." 
Psalms119:142 Your justice is eternal for your laws are perfectly fair. 
Psalms119:143 In my distress and anguish your commandments comfort me. 
Psalms119:144 Your laws are always fair; help me to understand them, and I shall live." 
Psalms119:145 I am praying with great earnestness; answer me, O Lord, and I will obey your laws." 
Psalms119:146 “Save me,“ I cry, “for I am obeying.“" 
Psalms119:147 Early in the morning before the sun is up, I am praying and pointing out how much I trust in you." 
Psalms119:148 I stay awake through the night to think about your promises. 
Psalms119:149 Because you are so loving and kind, listen to me and make me well again." 
Psalms119:150 Here come these lawless men to attack me," 
Psalms119:151 but you are near, O Lord; all your commandments are based on truth." 
Psalms119:152 I have known from earliest days that your will never changes. 
Psalms119:153 Look down upon my sorrows and rescue me, for I am obeying your commands." 
Psalms119:154 Yes, rescue me and give me back my life again just as you have promised." 
Psalms119:155 The wicked are far from salvation, for they do not care for your laws." 
Psalms119:156 Lord, how great is your mercy; oh, give me back my life again." 
Psalms119:157 My enemies are so many. They try to make me disobey, but I have not swerved from your will." 
Psalms119:158 I loathed these traitors because they care nothing for your laws. 
Psalms119:159 Lord, see how much I really love your demands. Now give me back my life and health because you are so kind." 
Psalms119:160 There is utter truth in all your laws; your decrees are eternal. 
Psalms119:161 Great men have persecuted me, though they have no reason to, but I stand in awe of only your words." 
Psalms119:162 I rejoice in your laws like one who finds a great treasure. 
Psalms119:163 How I hate all falsehood, but how I love your laws." 
Psalms119:164 I will praise you seven times a day because of your wonderful laws. 
Psalms119:165 Those who love your laws have great peace of heart and mind and do not stumble. 
Psalms119:166 I long for your salvation, Lord, and so I have obeyed your laws." 
Psalms119:167 I have looked for your commandments, and I love them very much;" 
Psalms119:168 yes, I have searched for them. You know this because everything I do is known to you." 
Psalms119:169 O Lord, listen to my prayers; give me the common sense you promised." 
Psalms119:170 Hear my prayers; rescue me as you said you would. 
Psalms119:171 I praise you for letting me learn your laws. 
Psalms119:172 I will sing about their wonder, for each of them is just." 
Psalms119:173 Stand ready to help me because I have chosen to follow your will. 
Psalms119:174 O Lord, I have longed for your salvation, and your law is my delight." 
Psalms119:175 If you will let me live, I will praise you; let your laws assist me." 
Psalms119:176 I have wandered away like a lost sheep; come and find me, for I have not turned away from your commandments." 
Psalms120:1 In my troubles I pled with God to help me and he did! 
Psalms120:2 Deliver me, O Lord, from liars." 
Psalms120:3 O lying tongue, what shall be your fate?" 
Psalms120:4 You shall be pierced with sharp arrows and burned with glowing coals. 
Psalms120:5 My troubles pile high among these haters of the Lord, these men of Meshech and Kedar." 
Psalms120:6 I am tired of being here among these men who hate peace. 
Psalms120:7 I am for peace, but they are for war, and my voice goes unheeded in their councils." 
Psalms121:1 Shall I look to the mountain gods for help? 
Psalms121:2 No! My help is from Jehovah who made the mountains! And the heavens too! 
Psalms121:3 He will never let me stumble, slip, or fall." 
Psalms121:4 For he is always watching, never sleeping." 
Psalms121:5 Jehovah himself is caring for you! He is your defender. 
Psalms121:6 He protects you day and night. 
Psalms121:7 He keeps you from all evil and preserves your life. 
Psalms121:8 He keeps his eye upon you as you come and go and always guards you. 
Psalms122:1 I was glad for the suggestion of going to Jerusalem, to the Temple of the Lord." 
Psalms122:2 Now we are standing 
Psalms122:3 here inside the crowded city. 
Psalms122:4 All Israel—Jehovah's people—have come to worship as the law requires, to thank and praise the Lord." 
Psalms122:5 Look! There are the judges holding court beside the city gates, deciding all the people's arguments." 
Psalms122:6 Pray for the peace of Jerusalem. May all who love this city prosper. 
Psalms122:7 O Jerusalem, may there be peace within your walls and prosperity in your palaces." 
Psalms122:8 This I ask for the sake of all my brothers and my friends who live here; 
Psalms122:9 and may there be peace as a protection to the Temple of the Lord. 
Psalms123:1 O God enthroned in heaven, I lift my eyes to you." 
Psalms123:2 We look to Jehovah our God for his mercy and kindness just as a servant keeps his eyes upon his master or a slave girl watches her mistress for the slightest signal. 
Psalms123:3 Have mercy on us, Lord, have mercy." 
Psalms123:4 For we have had our fill of contempt and of the scoffing of the rich and proud. 
Psalms124:1 If the Lord had not been on our side (let all Israel admit it), if the Lord had not been on our side," 
Psalms124:2 we would have been swallowed alive 
Psalms124:3 by our enemies, destroyed by their anger." 
Psalms124:4 We would have drowned beneath the 
Psalms124:5 flood of these men's fury and pride. 
Psalms124:6 Blessed be Jehovah who has not let them devour us. 
Psalms124:7 We have escaped with our lives as a bird from a hunter's snare. The snare is broken and we are free! 
Psalms124:8 Our help is from the Lord who made heaven and earth. 
Psalms125:1 Those who trust in the Lord are steady as Mount Zion, unmoved by any circumstance." 
Psalms125:2 Just as the mountains surround and protect Jerusalem, so the Lord surrounds and protects his people." 
Psalms125:3 For the wicked shall not rule the godly, lest the godly be forced to do wrong." 
Psalms125:4 O Lord, do good to those who are good, whose hearts are right with the Lord;" 
Psalms125:5 but lead evil men to execution. And let Israel have quietness and peace. 
Psalms126:1 When Jehovah brought back his exiles to Jerusalem, it was like a dream!" 
Psalms126:2 How we laughed and sang for joy. And the other nations said, “What amazing things the Lord has done for them.“" 
Psalms126:3 Yes, glorious things! What wonder! What joy!" 
Psalms126:4 May we be refreshed as by streams in the desert. 
Psalms126:5 Those who sow tears shall reap joy. 
Psalms126:6 Yes, they go out weeping, carrying seed for sowing, and return singing, carrying their sheaves." 
Psalms127:1 Unless the Lord builds a house, the builders' work is useless. Unless the Lord protects a city, sentries do no good." 
Psalms127:2 It is senseless for you to work so hard from early morning until late at night, fearing you will starve to death; for God wants his loved ones to get their proper rest." 
Psalms127:3 Children are a gift from God; they are his reward. 
Psalms127:4 Children born to a young man are like sharp arrows to defend him. 
Psalms127:5 Happy is the man who has his quiver full of them. That man shall have the help he needs when arguing with his enemies. 
Psalms128:1 Blessings on all who reverence and trust the Lord—on all who obey him! 
Psalms128:2 Their reward shall be prosperity and happiness. 
Psalms128:3 Your wife shall be contented in your home. And look at all those children! There they sit around the dinner table as vigorous and healthy as young olive trees. 
Psalms128:4 That is God's reward to those who reverence and trust him. 
Psalms128:5 May the Lord continually bless you with heaven's blessings as well as with human joys. 
Psalms128:6 May you live to enjoy your grandchildren! And may God bless Israel! 
Psalms129:1 Persecuted from my earliest youth (Israel is speaking)," 
Psalms129:2 and faced with never-ending discrimination—but not destroyed! My enemies have never been able to finish me off! 
Psalms129:3 Though my back is cut to ribbons with their whips, the Lord is good." 
Psalms129:4 For he has snapped the chains that evil men had bound me with. 
Psalms129:5 May all who hate the Jews be brought to ignominious defeat. 
Psalms129:6 May they be as grass in shallow soil, turning sere and" 
Psalms129:7 yellow when half grown, ignored by the reaper, despised by the binder." 
Psalms129:8 And may those passing by refuse to bless them by saying, “Jehovah's blessings be upon you; we bless you in Jehovah's name.“" 
Psalms130:1 O Lord, from the depths of despair I cry for your help:" 
Psalms130:2 “Hear me! Answer! Help me!“ 
Psalms130:3 Lord, if you keep in mind our sins, then who can ever get an answer to his prayers?" 
Psalms130:4 But you forgive! What an awesome thing this is! 
Psalms130:5 That is why I wait expectantly, trusting God to help, for he has promised." 
Psalms130:6 I long for him more than sentinels long for the dawn. 
Psalms130:7 O Israel, hope in the Lord; for he is loving and kind and comes to us with armloads of salvation." 
Psalms130:8 He himself shall ransom Israel from her slavery to sin. 
Psalms131:1 Lord, I am not proud and haughty. I don't think myself better than others. I don't pretend to “know it all.“" 
Psalms131:2 I am quiet now before the Lord, just as a child who is weaned from the breast. Yes, my begging has been stilled." 
Psalms131:3 O Israel, you too should quietly trust in the Lord—now, and always." 
Psalms132:1 Lord, do you remember that time when my heart was so filled with turmoil?" 
Psalms132:2 I couldn't rest," 
Psalms132:3 I couldn't sleep," 
Psalms132:4 thinking how I ought to build a permanent home for the Ark of the Lord, a Temple for the mighty one of Israel." 
Psalms132:5 Then I vowed that I would do it; I made a solemn promise to the Lord. 
Psalms132:6 First the Ark was in Ephrathah, then in the distant countryside of Jaar." 
Psalms132:7 But now it will be settled in the Temple, in God's permanent home here on earth. That is where we will go to worship him." 
Psalms132:8 Arise, O Lord, and enter your Temple with the Ark, the symbol of your power." 
Psalms132:9 We will clothe the priests in white, the symbol of all purity. May our nation shout for joy." 
Psalms132:10 Do not reject your servant David—the king you chose for your people. 
Psalms132:11 For you promised me that my son would sit on my throne and succeed me. And surely you will never go back on a promise! 
Psalms132:12 You also promised that if my descendants will obey the terms of your contract with me, then the dynasty of David shall never end." 
Psalms132:13 O Lord, you have chosen Jerusalem as your home:" 
Psalms132:14 “This is my permanent home where I shall live,“ you said, “for I have always wanted it this way." 
Psalms132:15 I will make this city prosperous and satisfy her poor with food. 
Psalms132:16 I will clothe her priests with salvation; her saints shall shout for joy. 
Psalms132:17 David's power shall grow, for I have decreed for him a mighty Son." 
Psalms132:18 I'll clothe his enemies with shame, but he shall be a glorious King.“" 
Psalms133:1 How wonderful it is, how pleasant, when brothers live in harmony!" 
Psalms133:2 For harmony is as precious as the fragrant anointing oil that was poured over Aaron's head and ran down onto his beard and onto the border of his robe. 
Psalms133:3 Harmony is as refreshing as the dew on Mount Hermon, on the mountains of Israel. And God has pronounced this eternal blessing on Jerusalem, even life forevermore." 
Psalms134:1 Oh, bless the Lord, you who serve him as watchmen in the Temple every night." 
Psalms134:2 Lift your hands in holiness and bless the Lord. 
Psalms134:3 The Lord bless you from Zion—the Lord who made heaven and earth. 
Psalms135:1 Hallelujah! 
Psalms135:2 Yes, let his people praise him as they stand in his Temple courts." 
Psalms135:3 Praise the Lord because he is so good; sing to his wonderful name. 
Psalms135:4 For the Lord has chosen Israel as his personal possession. 
Psalms135:5 I know the greatness of the Lord—that he is greater far than any other god. 
Psalms135:6 He does whatever pleases him throughout all of heaven and earth and in the deepest seas. 
Psalms135:7 He makes mists rise throughout the earth; he sends the lightning to bring down the rain and sends the winds from his treasuries. 
Psalms135:8 He destroyed the eldest child in each Egyptian home, along with the firstborn of the flocks." 
Psalms135:9 He did great miracles in Egypt before Pharaoh and all his people. 
Psalms135:10 He smote great nations, slaying mighty kings—" 
Psalms135:11 Sihon, king of Amorites; and Og, the king of Bashan; and the kings of Canaan—" 
Psalms135:12 and gave their land as an eternal gift to his people Israel. 
Psalms135:13 O Jehovah, your name endures forever; your fame is known to every generation." 
Psalms135:14 For Jehovah will vindicate his people and have compassion on his servants. 
Psalms135:15 The heathen worship idols of gold and silver made by men— 
Psalms135:16 idols with speechless mouths, sightless eyes," 
Psalms135:17 and ears that cannot hear; they cannot even breathe. 
Psalms135:18 Those who make them become like them! And so do all who trust in them! 
Psalms135:19 O Israel, bless Jehovah! High priests of Aaron, bless his name." 
Psalms135:20 O Levite priests, bless the Lord Jehovah! Oh, bless his name, all of you who trust and reverence him." 
Psalms135:21 All people of Jerusalem, praise the Lord, for he lives here in Jerusalem. Hallelujah!" 
Psalms136:1 Oh, give thanks to the Lord, for he is good; his loving-kindness continues forever." 
Psalms136:2 Give thanks to the God of gods, for his loving-kindness continues forever." 
Psalms136:3 Give thanks to the Lord of lords, for his loving-kindness continues forever." 
Psalms136:4 Praise him who alone does mighty miracles, for his loving-kindness continues forever." 
Psalms136:5 Praise him who made the heavens, for his loving-kindness continues forever." 
Psalms136:6 Praise him who planted the water within the earth, for his loving-kindness continues forever." 
Psalms136:7 Praise him who made the heavenly lights, for his loving-kindness continues forever:" 
Psalms136:8 the sun to rule the day, for his loving-kindness continues forever;" 
Psalms136:9 and the moon and stars at night, for his loving-kindness continues forever." 
Psalms136:10 Praise the God who smote the firstborn of Egypt, for his loving-kindness to Israel continues forever." 
Psalms136:11 He brought them out with mighty power and upraised fist 
Psalms136:12 to strike their enemies, for his loving-kindness to Israel continues forever." 
Psalms136:13 Praise the Lord who opened the Red Sea to make a path before them, for his loving-kindness continues forever," 
Psalms136:14 and led them safely through, for his loving-kindness continues forever—" 
Psalms136:15 but drowned Pharaoh's army in the sea, for his loving-kindness to Israel continues forever." 
Psalms136:16 Praise him who led his people through the wilderness, for his loving-kindness continues forever." 
Psalms136:17 Praise him who saved his people from the power of mighty kings, for his loving-kindness continues forever," 
Psalms136:18 and killed famous kings who were their enemies, for his loving-kindness to Israel continues forever:" 
Psalms136:19 Sihon, king of Amorites—for God's loving-kindness to Israel continues forever—" 
Psalms136:20 and Og, king of Bashan—for his loving-kindness to Israel continues forever." 
Psalms136:21 God gave the land of these kings to Israel as a gift forever, for his loving-kindness to Israel continues forever;" 
Psalms136:22 yes, a permanent gift to his servant Israel, for his loving-kindness continues forever." 
Psalms136:23 He remembered our utter weakness, for his loving-kindness continues forever." 
Psalms136:24 And saved us from our foes, for his loving-kindness continues forever." 
Psalms136:25 He gives food to every living thing, for his loving-kindness continues forever." 
Psalms136:26 Oh, give thanks to the God of heaven, for his loving-kindness continues forever." 
Psalms137:1 Weeping, we sat beside the rivers of Babylon thinking of Jerusalem." 
Psalms137:2 We have put away our lyres, hanging them upon the branches of the willow trees," 
Psalms137:3 for how can we sing? 
Psalms137:4 Yet our captors, our tormentors, demand that we sing for them the happy songs of Zion!" 
Psalms137:5 If I forget you, O Jerusalem, let my right hand forget its skill upon the harp." 
Psalms137:6 If I fail to love her more than my highest joy, let me never sing again." 
Psalms137:7 O Jehovah, do not forget what these Edomites did on that day when the armies of Babylon captured Jerusalem. “Raze her to the ground!“ they yelled." 
Psalms137:8 O Babylon, evil beast, you shall be destroyed. Blessed is the man who destroys you as you have destroyed us." 
Psalms137:9 Blessed is the man who takes your babies and smashes them against the rocks! 
Psalms138:1 Lord, with all my heart I thank you. I will sing your praises before the armies of angels." 
Psalms138:2 I face your Temple as I worship, giving thanks to you for all your loving-kindness and your faithfulness, for your promises are backed by all the honor of your name." 
Psalms138:3 When I pray, you answer me and encourage me by giving me the strength I need." 
Psalms138:4 Every king in all the earth shall give you thanks, O Lord, for all of them shall hear your voice." 
Psalms138:5 Yes, they shall sing about Jehovah's glorious ways, for his glory is very great." 
Psalms138:6 Yet though he is so great, he respects the humble, but proud men must keep their distance." 
Psalms138:7 Though I am surrounded by troubles, you will bring me safely through them. You will clench your fist against my angry enemies! Your power will save me." 
Psalms138:8 The Lord will work out his plans for my life—for your loving-kindness, Lord, continues forever. Don't abandon me—for you made me." 
Psalms139:1 O Lord, you have examined my heart and know everything about me." 
Psalms139:2 You know when I sit or stand. When far away you know my every thought. 
Psalms139:3 You chart the path ahead of me and tell me where to stop and rest. Every moment you know where I am. 
Psalms139:4 You know what I am going to say before I even say it. 
Psalms139:5 You both precede and follow me and place your hand of blessing on my head. 
Psalms139:6 This is too glorious, too wonderful to believe!" 
Psalms139:7 I can never be lost to your Spirit! I can never get away from my God! 
Psalms139:8 If I go up to heaven, you are there; if I go down to the place of the dead, you are there." 
Psalms139:9 If I ride the morning winds to the farthest oceans," 
Psalms139:10 even there your hand will guide me, your strength will support me." 
Psalms139:11 If I try to hide in the darkness, the night becomes light around me." 
Psalms139:12 For even darkness cannot hide from God; to you the night shines as bright as day. Darkness and light are both alike to you. 
Psalms139:13 You made all the delicate, inner parts of my body and knit them together in my mother's womb." 
Psalms139:14 Thank you for making me so wonderfully complex! It is amazing to think about. Your workmanship is marvelous—and how well I know it. 
Psalms139:15 You were there while I was being formed in utter seclusion! 
Psalms139:16 You saw me before I was born and scheduled each day of my life before I began to breathe. Every day was recorded in your book! 
Psalms139:17 How precious it is, Lord, to realize that you are thinking about me constantly!" 
Psalms139:18 I can't even count how many times a day your thoughts turn toward me. And when I waken in the morning, you are still thinking of me!" 
Psalms139:19 Surely you will slay the wicked, Lord! Away, bloodthirsty men! Begone!" 
Psalms139:20 They blaspheme your name and stand in arrogance against you—how silly can they be? 
Psalms139:21 O Lord, shouldn't I hate those who hate you? Shouldn't I be grieved with them?" 
Psalms139:22 Yes, I hate them, for your enemies are my enemies too." 
Psalms139:23 Search me, O God, and know my heart; test my thoughts." 
Psalms139:24 Point out anything you find in me that makes you sad, and lead me along the path of everlasting life." 
Psalms140:1 O Lord, deliver me from evil men. Preserve me from the violent," 
Psalms140:2 who plot and stir up trouble all day long. 
Psalms140:3 Their words sting like poisonous snakes. 
Psalms140:4 Keep me out of their power. Preserve me from their violence, for they are plotting against me." 
Psalms140:5 These proud men have set a trap to catch me, a noose to yank me up and leave me dangling in the air; they wait in ambush with a net to throw over and hold me helpless in its meshes." 
Psalms140:6 O Jehovah, my Lord and Savior, my God and" 
Psalms140:7 my shield—hear me as I pray! Don't let these 
Psalms140:8 wicked men succeed; don't let them prosper and be proud. 
Psalms140:9 Let their plots boomerang! Let them be destroyed by the very evil they have planned for me. 
Psalms140:10 Let burning coals fall down upon their heads, or throw them into the fire or into deep pits from which they can't escape." 
Psalms140:11 Don't let liars prosper here in our land; quickly punish them. 
Psalms140:12 But the Lord will surely help those they persecute; he will maintain the rights of the poor. 
Psalms140:13 Surely the godly are thanking you, for they shall live in your presence." 
Psalms141:1 Quick, Lord, answer me—for I have prayed. Listen when I cry to you for help!" 
Psalms141:2 Regard my prayer as my evening sacrifice and as incense wafting up to you. 
Psalms141:3 Help me, Lord, to keep my mouth shut and my lips sealed." 
Psalms141:4 Take away my lust for evil things; don't let me want to be with sinners, doing what they do, sharing their delicacies." 
Psalms141:5 Let the godly smite me! It will be a kindness! If they reprove me, it is medicine! Don't let me refuse it. But I am in constant prayer against the wicked and their deeds." 
Psalms141:6 When their leaders are condemned, and their bones are strewn across the ground, then these" 
Psalms141:7 men will finally listen to me and know that I am trying to help them. 
Psalms141:8 I look to you for help, O Lord God. You are my refuge. Don't let them slay me." 
Psalms141:9 Keep me out of their traps. 
Psalms141:10 Let them fall into their own snares, while I escape." 
Psalms142:1 How I plead with God, how I implore" 
Psalms142:2 his mercy, pouring out my troubles before him." 
Psalms142:3 For I am overwhelmed and desperate, and you alone know which way I ought to turn to miss the traps my enemies have set for me." 
Psalms142:4 (There's one—just over there to the right!) No one gives me a passing thought. No one will help me; no one cares a bit what happens to me. 
Psalms142:5 Then I prayed to Jehovah. “Lord,“ I pled, “you are my only place of refuge. Only you can keep me safe." 
Psalms142:6 “Hear my cry, for I am very low. Rescue me from my persecutors, for they are too strong for me." 
Psalms142:7 Bring me out of prison so that I can thank you. The godly will rejoice with me for all your help.“ 
Psalms143:1 Hear my prayer, O Lord; answer my plea because you are faithful to your promises." 
Psalms143:2 Don't bring me to trial! For as compared with you, no one is perfect." 
Psalms143:3 My enemies chased and caught me. They have knocked me to the ground. They force me to live in the darkness like those in the grave. 
Psalms143:4 I am losing all hope; I am paralyzed with fear. 
Psalms143:5 I remember the glorious miracles you did in days of long ago. 
Psalms143:6 I reach out for you. I thirst for you as parched land thirsts for rain. 
Psalms143:7 Come quickly, Lord, and answer me, for my depression deepens; don't turn away from me or I shall die." 
Psalms143:8 Let me see your kindness to me in the morning, for I am trusting you. Show me where to walk, for my prayer is sincere." 
Psalms143:9 Save me from my enemies. O Lord, I run to you to hide me." 
Psalms143:10 Help me to do your will, for you are my God. Lead me in good paths, for your Spirit is good." 
Psalms143:11 Lord, saving me will bring glory to your name. Bring me out of all this trouble because you are true to your promises." 
Psalms143:12 And because you are loving and kind to me, cut off all my enemies and destroy those who are trying to harm me; for I am your servant." 
Psalms144:1 Bless the Lord who is my immovable Rock. He gives me strength and skill in battle. 
Psalms144:2 He is always kind and loving to me; he is my fortress, my tower of strength and safety, my deliverer. He stands before me as a shield. He subdues my people under me." 
Psalms144:3 O Lord, what is man that you even notice him? Why bother at all with the human race?" 
Psalms144:4 For man is but a breath; his days are like a passing shadow. 
Psalms144:5 Bend down the heavens, Lord, and come. The mountains smoke beneath your touch." 
Psalms144:6 Let loose your lightning bolts, your arrows, Lord, upon your enemies, and scatter them." 
Psalms144:7 Reach down from heaven and rescue me; deliver me from deep waters, from the power of my enemies." 
Psalms144:8 Their mouths are filled with lies; they swear to the truth of what is false. 
Psalms144:9 I will sing you a new song, O God, with a ten-stringed harp." 
Psalms144:10 For you grant victory to kings! You are the one who will rescue your servant David from the fatal sword. 
Psalms144:11 Save me! Deliver me from these enemies, these liars, these treacherous men." 
Psalms144:12 Here is my description of a truly happy land where Jehovah is God: Sons vigorous and tall as growing plants. Daughters of graceful beauty like the pillars of a palace wall. 
Psalms144:13 Barns full to the brim with crops of every kind. Sheep by the thousands out in our fields. 
Psalms144:14 Oxen loaded down with produce. No enemy attacking the walls, but peace everywhere. No crime in our streets." 
Psalms144:15 Yes, happy are those whose God is Jehovah." 
Psalms145:1 I will praise you, my God and King," 
Psalms145:2 and bless your name each day and forever. 
Psalms145:3 Great is Jehovah! Greatly praise him! His greatness is beyond discovery! 
Psalms145:4 Let each generation tell its children what glorious things he does. 
Psalms145:5 I will meditate about your glory, splendor, majesty, and miracles." 
Psalms145:6 Your awe-inspiring deeds shall be on every tongue; I will proclaim your greatness. 
Psalms145:7 Everyone will tell about how good you are and sing about your righteousness. 
Psalms145:8 Jehovah is kind and merciful, slow to get angry, full of love." 
Psalms145:9 He is good to everyone, and his compassion is intertwined with everything he does." 
Psalms145:10 All living things shall thank you, Lord, and your people will bless you." 
Psalms145:11 They will talk together about the glory of your kingdom and mention examples of your power. 
Psalms145:12 They will tell about your miracles and about the majesty and glory of your reign. 
Psalms145:13 For your kingdom never ends. You rule generation after generation. 
Psalms145:14 The Lord lifts the fallen and those bent beneath their loads. 
Psalms145:15 The eyes of all mankind look up to you for help; you give them their food as they need it. 
Psalms145:16 You constantly satisfy the hunger and thirst of every living thing. 
Psalms145:17 The Lord is fair in everything he does and full of kindness. 
Psalms145:18 He is close to all who call on him sincerely. 
Psalms145:19 He fulfills the desires of those who reverence and trust him; he hears their cries for help and rescues them. 
Psalms145:20 He protects all those who love him, but destroys the wicked." 
Psalms145:21 I will praise the Lord and call on all men everywhere to bless his holy name forever and forever. 
Psalms146:1 Praise the Lord! Yes, really praise him!" 
Psalms146:2 I will praise him as long as I live, yes, even with my dying breath." 
Psalms146:3 Don't look to men for help; their greatest leaders fail; 
Psalms146:4 for every man must die. His breathing stops, life ends, and in a moment all he planned for himself is ended." 
Psalms146:5 But happy is the man who has the God of Jacob as his helper, whose hope is in the Lord his God—" 
Psalms146:6 the God who made both earth and heaven, the seas and everything in them. He is the God who keeps every promise," 
Psalms146:7 who gives justice to the poor and oppressed and food to the hungry. He frees the prisoners 
Psalms146:8 and opens the eyes of the blind; he lifts the burdens from those bent down beneath their loads. For the Lord loves good men. 
Psalms146:9 He protects the immigrants and cares for the orphans and widows. But he turns topsy-turvy the plans of the wicked. 
Psalms146:10 The Lord will reign forever. O Jerusalem, your God is King in every generation! Hallelujah! Praise the Lord!" 
Psalms147:1 Hallelujah! Yes, praise the Lord! How good it is to sing his praises! How delightful, and how right!" 
Psalms147:2 He is rebuilding Jerusalem and bringing back the exiles. 
Psalms147:3 He heals the brokenhearted, binding up their wounds." 
Psalms147:4 He counts the stars and calls them all by name. 
Psalms147:5 How great he is! His power is absolute! His understanding is unlimited. 
Psalms147:6 The Lord supports the humble, but brings the wicked into the dust." 
Psalms147:7 Sing out your thanks to him; sing praises to our God, accompanied by harps." 
Psalms147:8 He covers the heavens with clouds, sends down the showers, and makes the green grass grow in mountain pastures." 
Psalms147:9 He feeds the wild animals, and the young ravens cry to him for food." 
Psalms147:10 The speed of a horse is nothing to him. How puny in his sight is the strength of a man. 
Psalms147:11 But his joy is in those who reverence him, those who expect him to be loving and kind." 
Psalms147:12 Praise him, O Jerusalem! Praise your God, O Zion!" 
Psalms147:13 For he has fortified your gates against all enemies and blessed your children. 
Psalms147:14 He sends peace across your nation and fills your barns with plenty of the finest wheat. 
Psalms147:15 He sends his orders to the world. How swiftly his word flies. 
Psalms147:16 He sends the snow in all its lovely whiteness, scatters the frost upon the ground," 
Psalms147:17 and hurls the hail upon the earth. Who can stand before his freezing cold? 
Psalms147:18 But then he calls for warmer weather, and the spring winds blow and all the river ice is broken." 
Psalms147:19 He has made known his laws and ceremonies of worship to Israel— 
Psalms147:20 something he has not done with any other nation; they have not known his commands. Hallelujah! Yes, praise the Lord!"

Psalms148:6 he established them forever and forever. His orders will never be revoked.

Psalms148:13 all praise the Lord together. For he alone is worthy. His glory is far greater than all of earth and heaven.

Proverbs1:1 These are the proverbs of King Solomon of Israel, David's son:" 
Proverbs1:2 He wrote them to teach his people how to live—how to act in every circumstance," 
Proverbs1:3 for he wanted them to be understanding, just and fair in everything they did." 
Proverbs1:4 “I want to make the simpleminded wise!“ he said. “I want to warn young men about some problems they will face. 
Proverbs1:5 I want those already wise to become the wiser and become 
Proverbs1:6 leaders by exploring the depths of meaning in these nuggets of truth.“ 
Proverbs1:7 How does a man become wise? The first step is to trust and reverence the Lord! Only fools refuse to be taught. 
Proverbs1:8 Listen to your father and mother. What you learn from them 
Proverbs1:9 will stand you in good stead; it will gain you many honors. 
Proverbs1:10 If young toughs tell you, “Come and join us“—turn your back on them!" 
Proverbs1:11 “We'll hide and rob and kill,“ they say." 
Proverbs1:12 “Good or bad, we'll treat them all alike." 
Proverbs1:13 And the loot we'll get! All kinds of stuff! 
Proverbs1:14 Come on, throw in your lot with us; we'll split with you in equal shares.“" 
Proverbs1:15 Don't do it, son! Stay far from men like that," 
Proverbs1:16 for crime is their way of life, and murder is their specialty." 
Proverbs1:17 When a bird sees a trap being set, it stays away," 
Proverbs1:18 but not these men; they trap themselves! They lay a booby trap for their own lives. 
Proverbs1:19 Such is the fate of all who live by violence and murder. They will die a violent death. 
Proverbs1:20 Wisdom shouts in the streets for a hearing. 
Proverbs1:21 She calls out to the crowds along Main Street, and to the judges in their courts, and to everyone in all the land:" 
Proverbs1:22 “You simpletons!“ she cries. “How long will you go on being fools? How long will you scoff at wisdom and fight the facts? 
Proverbs1:23 Come here and listen to me! I'll pour out the spirit of wisdom upon you and make you wise. 
Proverbs1:24 I have called you so often, but still you won't come. I have pleaded, but all in vain." 
Proverbs1:25 For you have spurned my counsel and reproof. 
Proverbs1:26 Some day you'll be in trouble, and I'll laugh! Mock me, will you?—I'll mock you!" 
Proverbs1:27 When a storm of terror surrounds you, and when you are engulfed by anguish and distress," 
Proverbs1:28 then I will not answer your cry for help. It will be too late though you search for me ever so anxiously. 
Proverbs1:29 “For you closed your eyes to the facts and did not choose to reverence and trust the Lord," 
Proverbs1:30 and you turned your back on me, spurning my advice." 
Proverbs1:31 That is why you must eat the bitter fruit of having your own way and experience the full terrors of the pathway you have chosen. 
Proverbs1:32 For you turned away from me—to death; your own complacency will kill you. Fools! 
Proverbs1:33 But all who listen to me shall live in peace and safety, unafraid.“" 
Proverbs2:1 Every young man who listens to me and obeys 
Proverbs2:2 my instructions will be given wisdom and good sense. 
Proverbs2:3 Yes, if you want better insight and discernment," 
Proverbs2:4 and are searching for them as you would for lost money or hidden treasure, then wisdom will be given you and knowledge of God himself;" 
Proverbs2:5 you will soon learn the importance of reverence for the Lord and of trusting him. 
Proverbs2:6 For the Lord grants wisdom! His every word is a treasure of knowledge and understanding. 
Proverbs2:7 He grants good sense to the godly—his saints. 
Proverbs2:8 He is their shield, protecting them and guarding their pathway." 
Proverbs2:9 He shows how to distinguish right from wrong, how to find the right decision every time." 
Proverbs2:10 For wisdom and truth will enter the very center of your being, filling your life with joy." 
Proverbs2:11 You will be given the sense to stay away from evil 
Proverbs2:12 men who want you to be their partners in crime—men who 
Proverbs2:13 turn from God's ways to walk down dark and evil paths 
Proverbs2:14 and exult in doing wrong, for they thoroughly enjoy their sins." 
Proverbs2:15 Everything they do is crooked and wrong. 
Proverbs2:16 Only wisdom from the Lord can save a man from the flattery of 
Proverbs2:17 prostitutes; these girls have abandoned their husbands and flouted the laws of God. 
Proverbs2:18 Their houses lie along the road to death and hell. 
Proverbs2:19 The men who enter them are doomed. None of these men will ever be the same again. 
Proverbs2:20 Follow the steps of the godly instead, and stay on the right path," 
Proverbs2:21 for only good men enjoy life to the full; 
Proverbs2:22 evil men lose the good things they might have had, and they themselves shall be destroyed." 
Proverbs3:1 My son, never forget the things I've taught you." 
Proverbs3:2 If you want a long and satisfying life, closely follow my instructions." 
Proverbs3:3 Never tire of loyalty and kindness. Hold these virtues tightly. Write them deep within your heart. 
Proverbs3:4 If you want favor with both God and man, and a reputation for" 
Proverbs3:5 good judgment and common sense, then trust the Lord completely; don't ever trust yourself." 
Proverbs3:6 In everything you do, put God first, and he will direct you and crown your efforts with success." 
Proverbs3:7 Don't be conceited, sure of your own wisdom." 
Proverbs3:8 Instead, trust and reverence the Lord, and turn your back on evil; when you do that, then you will be given renewed health and vitality." 
Proverbs3:9 Honor the Lord by giving him the first part of all your income, and he will" 
Proverbs3:10 fill your barns with wheat and barley and overflow your wine vats with the finest wines. 
Proverbs3:11 Young man, do not resent it when God chastens and corrects you, for his punishment is proof of his love." 
Proverbs3:12 Just as a father punishes a son he delights in to make him better, so the Lord corrects you." 
Proverbs3:13 The man who knows right from wrong and has good judgment and common sense 
Proverbs3:14 is happier than the man who is immensely rich! 
Proverbs3:15 For such wisdom is far more valuable than precious jewels. Nothing else compares with it. 
Proverbs3:16 Wisdom gives: A long, good" 
Proverbs3:17 life, Riches, Honor, Pleasure, Peace." 
Proverbs3:18 Wisdom is a tree of life to those who eat her fruit; happy is the man who keeps on eating it. 
Proverbs3:19 The Lord's wisdom founded the earth; his understanding established all the universe and space. 
Proverbs3:20 The deep fountains of the earth were broken open by his knowledge, and the skies poured down rain." 
Proverbs3:21 Have two goals: wisdom—that is, knowing and doing right—and common sense. Don't let them slip away," 
Proverbs3:22 for they fill you with living energy and bring you honor and respect. 
Proverbs3:23 They keep you safe from defeat and disaster and from stumbling off the trail. 
Proverbs3:24 With them on guard you can sleep without fear; you 
Proverbs3:25 need not be afraid of disaster or the plots of 
Proverbs3:26 wicked men, for the Lord is with you; he protects you." 
Proverbs3:27 Don't withhold repayment of your debts. 
Proverbs3:28 Don't say “some other time,“ if you can pay now." 
Proverbs3:29 Don't plot against your neighbor; he is trusting you. 
Proverbs3:30 Don't get into needless fights. 
Proverbs3:31 Don't envy violent men. Don't copy their ways.
