Jeremiah41:17 them all those they had rescued—soldiers, women, children, and eunuchs, to prepare to leave for Egypt." 
Jeremiah41:18 For they were afraid of what the Babylonians would do when the news reached them that Ishmael had killed Gedaliah the governor, for he had been chosen and appointed by the Babylonian emperor." 
Jeremiah42:1 Then Johanan and the army captains and all the people, great and small, came to Jeremiah" 
Jeremiah42:2 and said, “Please pray for us to the Lord your God, for as you know so well, we are only a tiny remnant of what we were before." 
Jeremiah42:3 Beg the Lord your God to show us what to do and where to go.“ 
Jeremiah42:4 “All right,“ Jeremiah replied. “I will ask him and I will tell you what he says. I will hide nothing from you.“" 
Jeremiah42:5 Then they said to Jeremiah, “May the curse of God be on us if we refuse to obey whatever he says we should do!" 
Jeremiah42:6 Whether we like it or not, we will obey the Lord our God, to whom we send you with our plea. For if we obey him, everything will turn out well for us.“" 
Jeremiah42:7 Ten days later the Lord gave his reply to Jeremiah. 
Jeremiah42:8 So he called for Johanan and the captains of his forces, and for all the people, great and small," 
Jeremiah42:9 and said to them: “You sent me to the Lord, the God of Israel, with your request, and this is his reply:" 
Jeremiah42:10 'Stay here in this land. If you do, I will bless you, and no one will harm you. For I am sorry for all the punishment I have had to give to you." 
Jeremiah42:11 Don't fear the king of Babylon any more, for I am with you to save you and to deliver you from his hand." 
Jeremiah42:12 And I will be merciful to you by making him kind so that he will not kill you or make slaves of you but will let you stay here in your land.' 
Jeremiah42:13 “But if you refuse to obey the Lord and say, 'We will not stay here,'—and insist" 
Jeremiah42:14 on going to Egypt where you think you will be free from war and hunger and alarms," 
Jeremiah42:15 then this is what the Lord replies, O remnant of Judah: 'The Lord Almighty, the God of Israel, says: If you insist on going to Egypt," 
Jeremiah42:16 the war and famine you fear will follow close behind you, and you will perish there." 
Jeremiah42:17 That is the fate awaiting every one of you who insists on going to live in Egypt. Yes, you will die from sword, famine, and disease. None of you will escape from the evil I will bring upon you there.'" 
Jeremiah42:18 “For the Lord, the God of Israel, says: 'Just as my anger and fury were poured out upon the people of Jerusalem, so it will be poured out on you when you enter Egypt. You will be received with disgust and with hatred—you will be cursed and reviled. And" 
Jeremiah42:19 For the Lord has said: 'O remnant of Judah, do not go to Egypt!' “ Jeremiah concluded: “Never forget the warning I have given you today." 
Jeremiah42:20 If you go, it will be at the cost of your lives. For you were deceitful when you sent me to pray for you and said, 'Just tell us what God says and we will do it!'" 
Jeremiah42:21 And today I have told you exactly what he said, but you will not obey any more now than you did the other times." 
Jeremiah42:22 Therefore know for a certainty that you will die by sword, famine, and disease in Egypt, where you insist on going.“" 
Jeremiah43:1 When Jeremiah had finished giving this message from God to all the people," 
Jeremiah43:2 Azariah (son of Hoshaiah) and Johanan (son of Kareah) and all the other proud men, said to Jeremiah, “You lie! The Lord our God hasn't told you to tell us not to go to Egypt!" 
Jeremiah43:3 Baruch (son of Neriah) has plotted against us and told you to say this so that we will stay here and be killed by the Babylonians or carried off to Babylon as slaves.“ 
Jeremiah43:4 So Johanan and all the guerrilla leaders and all the people refused to obey the Lord and stay in Judah. 
Jeremiah43:5 All of them, including all those who had returned from the nearby countries where they had fled, now started off for Egypt with Johanan and the other captains in command." 
Jeremiah43:6 In the crowd were men, women and children, the king's daughters, and all those whom Nebuzaradan, the captain of the guard, had left with Gedaliah. They even forced Jeremiah and Baruch to go with them too." 
Jeremiah43:7 And so they arrived in Egypt at the city of Tahpanhes, for they would not obey the Lord." 
Jeremiah43:8 Then at Tahpanhes, the Lord spoke to Jeremiah again and said:" 
Jeremiah43:9 “Call together the men of Judah and, as they watch you, bury large rocks between the pavement stones at the entrance of Pharaoh's palace here in Tahpanhes," 
Jeremiah43:10 and tell the men of Judah this: 'The Lord Almighty, the God of Israel, says: I will surely bring Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, here to Egypt, for he is my servant. I will set his throne upon these stones that I have hidden. He shall spread his royal canopy over them." 
Jeremiah43:11 And when he comes, he shall destroy the land of Egypt, killing all those I want killed and capturing those I want captured, and many shall die of plague." 
Jeremiah43:12 He will set fire to the temples of the gods of Egypt and burn the idols and carry off the people as his captives. And he shall plunder the land of Egypt as a shepherd picks fleas from his cloak! And he himself shall leave unharmed. 
Jeremiah43:13 And he shall break down the obelisks standing in the city of Heliopolis and burn down the temples of the gods of Egypt.' “ 
Jeremiah44:1 This is the message God gave to Jeremiah concerning all the Jews who were living in the north of Egypt in the cities of Migdol, Tahpanhes, and Memphis, and throughout southern Egypt as well:" 
Jeremiah44:2 “The Lord Almighty, the God of Israel, says: 'You saw what I did to Jerusalem and to all the cities of Judah." 
Jeremiah44:3 Because of all their wickedness they lie in heaps and ashes, without a living soul. For my anger rose high against them for worshiping other gods—“gods“ that neither they nor you nor any of your fathers have ever known." 
Jeremiah44:4 'I sent my servants, the prophets, to protest over and over again and to plead with them not to do this horrible thing I hate," 
Jeremiah44:5 but they wouldn't listen and wouldn't turn back from their wicked ways; they have kept right on with their sacrifices to these “gods.“ 
Jeremiah44:6 And so my fury and anger boiled over and fell as fire upon the cities of Judah and into the streets of Jerusalem, and there is desolation until this day.'" 
Jeremiah44:7 “And now the Lord, the Lord Almighty, the God of Israel, asks you: 'Why are you destroying yourselves? For not one of you shall live—not a man, woman or child among you who has come here from Judah, not even the babies in arms." 
Jeremiah44:8 For you are rousing my anger with the idols you have made and worshiped here in Egypt, burning incense to them, and causing me to destroy you completely and to make you a curse and a stench in the nostrils of all the nations of the earth." 
Jeremiah44:9 Have you forgotten the sins of your fathers, the sins of the kings and queens of Judah, your own sins, and the sins of your wives in Judah and Jerusalem?" 
Jeremiah44:10 And even until this very hour there has been no apology; no one has wanted to return to me or follow the laws I gave you and your fathers before you.' 
Jeremiah44:11 “Therefore the Lord, the God of Israel, says: 'There is fury in my face and I will destroy every one of you!" 
Jeremiah44:12 I will take this remnant of Judah that insisted on coming here to Egypt, and I will consume them. They shall fall here in Egypt, killed by famine and sword; all shall die, from the least important to the greatest. They shall be despised and loathed, cursed and hated." 
Jeremiah44:13 I will punish them in Egypt just as I punished them in Jerusalem, by sword, famine, and disease." 
Jeremiah44:14 Not one of them shall escape from my wrath except those who repent of their coming and escape from the others by returning again to their own land.' “ 
Jeremiah44:15 Then all the women present and all the men who knew that their wives had burned incense to idols (it was a great crowd of all the Jews in southern Egypt) answered Jeremiah: 
Jeremiah44:16 “We will not listen to your false 'Messages from God'! 
Jeremiah44:17 We will do whatever we want to. We will burn incense to the Queen of Heaven and sacrifice to her just as much as we like—just as we and our fathers before us, and our kings and princes have always done in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem; for in those days we had plenty to eat, and we were well off and happy!" 
Jeremiah44:18 But ever since we quit burning incense to the Queen of Heaven and stopped worshiping her, we have been in great trouble and have been destroyed by sword and famine.“" 
Jeremiah44:19 “And,“ the women added, “do you suppose that we were worshiping the Queen of Heaven and pouring out our libations to her and making cakes for her with her image on them, without our husbands knowing it and helping us? Of course not!“" 
Jeremiah44:20 Then Jeremiah said to all of them, men and women alike, who had given him that answer:" 
Jeremiah44:21 “Do you think the Lord didn't know that you and your fathers, your kings and princes, and all the people were burning incense to idols in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem?" 
Jeremiah44:22 It was because he could no longer bear all the evil things you were doing that he made your land desolate, an incredible ruin, cursed, without an inhabitant, as it is today." 
Jeremiah44:23 The very reason all these terrible things have befallen you is because you have burned incense and sinned against the Lord and refused to obey him.“ 
Jeremiah44:24 Then Jeremiah said to them all, including the women: “Listen to the word of the Lord, all you citizens of Judah who are here in Egypt!" 
Jeremiah44:25 The Lord, the God of Israel, says: 'Both you and your wives have said that you will never give up your devotion and sacrifices to the Queen of Heaven, and you have proved it by your actions. Then go ahead and carry out your promises and vows to her!'" 
Jeremiah44:26 But listen to the word of the Lord, all you Jews who are living in the land of Egypt: 'I have sworn by my great name,' says the Lord, 'that it will do you no good to seek my help and blessing any more, saying, “O Lord our God, help us!“" 
Jeremiah44:27 For I will watch over you, but not for good! I will see to it that evil befalls you, and you shall be destroyed by war and famine until all of you are dead." 
Jeremiah44:28 'Only those who return to Judah (it will be but a tiny remnant) shall escape my wrath, but all who refuse to go back—who insist on living in Egypt—shall find out who tells the truth, I or they!" 
Jeremiah44:29 And this is the proof I give you that all I have threatened will happen to you and that I will punish you here: 
Jeremiah44:30 I will turn Pharaoh Hophra, king of Egypt, over to those who seek his life, just as I turned Zedekiah, king of Judah, over to Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon.' “" 
Jeremiah45:1 This is the message Jeremiah gave to Baruch in the fourth year of the reign of King Jehoiakim (son of Josiah), after Baruch had written down all God's messages as Jeremiah was dictating them to him:" 
Jeremiah45:2 “O Baruch, the Lord God of Israel says this to you:" 
Jeremiah45:3 You have said, “Woe is me! Don't I have troubles enough already? And now the Lord has added more! I am weary of my own sighing and I find no rest.'" 
Jeremiah45:4 But tell Baruch this, 'The Lord says: “I will destroy this nation that I built; I will wipe out what I established." 
Jeremiah45:5 Are you seeking great things for yourself? Don't do it! For though I will bring great evil upon all these people, I will protect you wherever you go, as your reward.' “" 
Jeremiah46:1 Here are the messages given to Jeremiah concerning foreign nations: The Egyptians 
Jeremiah46:2 This message was given against Egypt at the occasion of the battle of Carchemish when Pharaoh Necho, king of Egypt, and his army were defeated beside the Euphrates River by Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, in the fourth year of the reign of Jehoiakim (son of Josiah), king of Judah:" 
Jeremiah46:3 “Buckle on your armor, you Egyptians and advance to battle!" 
Jeremiah46:4 Harness the horses and prepare to mount them—don your helmets, sharpen your spears, put on your armor." 
Jeremiah46:5 But look! The Egyptian army flees in terror; the mightiest of its soldiers run without a backward glance. Yes, terror shall surround them on every side,“ says the Lord." 
Jeremiah46:6 “The swift will not escape, nor the mightiest of warriors. In the north, by the river Euphrates, they have stumbled and fallen." 
Jeremiah46:7 “What is this mighty army, rising like the Nile at flood time, overflowing all the land?" 
Jeremiah46:8 It is the Egyptian army, boasting that it will cover the earth like a flood, destroying every foe." 
Jeremiah46:9 Then come, O horses and chariots and mighty soldiers of Egypt! Come, all of you from Cush and Put and Lud who handle the shield and bend the bow!" 
Jeremiah46:10 For this is the day of the Lord, the Lord Almighty, a day of vengeance upon his enemies. The sword shall devour until it is sated, yes, drunk with your blood, for the Lord, the Lord Almighty will receive a sacrifice today in the north country beside the river Euphrates!" 
Jeremiah46:11 Go up to Gilead for medicine, O virgin daughter of Egypt! Yet there is no cure for your wounds. Though you have used many medicines, there is no healing for you." 
Jeremiah46:12 The nations have heard of your shame. The earth is filled with your cry of despair and defeat; your mightiest soldiers will stumble across each other and fall together.“ 
Jeremiah46:13 Then God gave Jeremiah this message concerning the coming of Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, to attack Egypt:" 
Jeremiah46:14 “Shout it out in Egypt; publish it in the cities of Migdol, Memphis, and Tahpanhes! Mobilize for battle, for the sword of destruction shall devour all around you." 
Jeremiah46:15 Why has Apis, your bull god, fled in terror? Because the Lord knocked him down before your enemies." 
Jeremiah46:16 Vast multitudes fall in heaps. (Then the remnant of the Jews will say, 'Come, let us return again to Judah where we were born and get away from all this slaughter here!')" 
Jeremiah46:17 “Rename Pharaoh Hophra and call him 'The Man with No Power But with Plenty of Noise!' 
Jeremiah46:18 “As I live,“ says the King, the Lord of Hosts, “one is coming against Egypt who is as tall as Mount Tabor or Mount Carmel by the sea!" 
Jeremiah46:19 Pack up; get ready to leave for exile, you citizens of Egypt, for the city of Memphis shall be utterly destroyed and left without a soul alive." 
Jeremiah46:20 Egypt is sleek as a heifer, but a gadfly sends her running—a gadfly from the north!" 
Jeremiah46:21 Even her famed mercenaries have become like frightened calves. They turn and run, for it is the day of great calamity for Egypt, a time of great punishment." 
Jeremiah46:22 Silent as a serpent gliding away, Egypt flees; the invading army marches in." 
Jeremiah46:23 The numberless soldiers cut down your people like woodsmen who clear a forest of its trees. 
Jeremiah46:24 Egypt is as helpless as a girl before these men from the north.“ 
Jeremiah46:25 The Lord, the God of Israel, says: “I will punish Amon, god of Thebes, and all the other gods of Egypt. I will punish Pharaoh too, and all who trust in him." 
Jeremiah46:26 I will deliver them into the hands of those who want them killed—into the hands of Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, and his army. But afterwards the land shall recover from the ravages of war." 
Jeremiah46:27 “But don't you be afraid, O my people who return to your own land, don't be dismayed; for I will save you from far away and bring your children from a distant land. Yes, Israel shall return and be at rest, and nothing shall make her afraid." 
Jeremiah46:28 Fear not, O Jacob, my servant,“ says the Lord, “for I am with you. I will destroy all the nations to which I have exiled you, but I will not destroy you. I will punish you, but only enough to correct you.“" 
Jeremiah47:1 The Philistines This is God's message to Jeremiah concerning the Philistines of Gaza, before the city was captured by the Egyptian army:" 
Jeremiah47:2 “The Lord says: 'A flood is coming from the north to overflow the land of the Philistines; it will destroy their cities and everything in them. Strong men will scream in terror, and all the land will weep." 
Jeremiah47:3 Hear the clattering hoofs and rumbling wheels as the chariots go rushing by; fathers flee without a backward glance at their helpless children," 
Jeremiah47:4 for the time has come when all the Philistines and their allies from Tyre and Sidon will be destroyed. For the Lord is destroying the Philistines, those colonists from Caphtor." 
Jeremiah47:5 The cities of Gaza and Ashkelon will be razed to the ground and lie in ruins. O descendants of the Anakim, how you will lament and mourn!" 
Jeremiah47:6 'O sword of the Lord, when will you be at rest again? Go back into your scabbard; rest and be still!" 
Jeremiah47:7 But how can it be still when the Lord has sent it on an errand? For the city of Ashkelon and those living along the sea must be destroyed.' “ 
Jeremiah48:1 The Moabites This is the message of the Lord of Hosts, the God of Israel, against Moab: “Woe to the city of Nebo, for it shall lie in ruins. The city of Kiriathaim and its forts are overwhelmed and captured." 
Jeremiah48:2 No one will ever brag about Moab any more, for there is a plot against her life. In Heshbon plans have been completed to destroy her. 'Come,' they say, 'we will cut her off from being a nation.' In Madmen all is silent." 
Jeremiah48:3 And then the roar of battle will surge against Horonaim," 
Jeremiah48:4 for all Moab is being destroyed. Her crying will be heard as far away as Zoar. 
Jeremiah48:5 Her refugees will climb the hills of Luhith, weeping bitterly, while cries of terror rise from the city below." 
Jeremiah48:6 Flee for your lives; hide in the wilderness! 
Jeremiah48:7 For you trusted in your wealth and skill; therefore, you shall perish. Your god Chemosh, with his priests and princes, shall be taken away to distant lands!" 
Jeremiah48:8 “All the villages and cities, whether they be on the plateaus or in the valleys, shall be destroyed, for the Lord has said it." 
Jeremiah48:9 Oh, for wings for Moab that she could fly away, for her cities shall be left without a living soul." 
Jeremiah48:10 Cursed be those withholding their swords from your blood, refusing to do the work that God has given them!" 
Jeremiah48:11 “From her earliest history Moab has lived there undisturbed from all invasions. She is like wine that has not been poured from flask to flask and is fragrant and smooth. But now she shall have the pouring out of exile! 
Jeremiah48:12 The time is coming soon,“ the Lord has said, “when he will send troublemakers to spill her out from jar to jar and then shatter the jars!" 
Jeremiah48:13 Then at last Moab shall be ashamed of her idol Chemosh, as Israel was of her calf-idol at Bethel." 
Jeremiah48:14 “Do you remember that boast of yours: 'We are heroes, mighty men of war'?" 
Jeremiah48:15 But now Moab is to be destroyed; her destroyer is on the way; her choicest youth are doomed to slaughter,“ says the King, the Lord Almighty." 
Jeremiah48:16 “Calamity is coming fast to Moab. 
Jeremiah48:17 “O friends of Moab, weep for her and cry! See how the strong, the beautiful is shattered!" 
Jeremiah48:18 Come down from your glory and sit in the dust, O people of Dibon, for those destroying Moab shall shatter Dibon too, and tear down all her towers." 
Jeremiah48:19 Those in Aroer stand anxiously beside the road to watch, and shout to those who flee from Moab, 'What has happened there?'" 
Jeremiah48:20 “And they reply, 'Moab lies in ruins; weep and wail. Tell it by the banks of the Arnon, that Moab is destroyed.'" 
Jeremiah48:21 “All the cities of the tableland lie in ruins too, for God's judgment has been poured out upon them all—on Holon and Jahzah and Mephaath," 
Jeremiah48:22 and Dibon and Nebo and Beth-diblathaim," 
Jeremiah48:23 and Kiriathaim and Beth-gamul and Beth-meon," 
Jeremiah48:24 and Kerioth and Bozrah—and all the cities of the land of Moab, far and near." 
Jeremiah48:25 “The strength of Moab is ended—her horns are cut off; her arms are broken. 
Jeremiah48:26 Let her stagger and fall like a drunkard, for she has rebelled against the Lord. Moab shall wallow in her vomit, scorned by all." 
Jeremiah48:27 For you scorned Israel and robbed her and were happy at her fall. 
Jeremiah48:28 “O people of Moab, flee from your cities and live in the caves like doves that nest in the clefts of the rocks." 
Jeremiah48:29 We have all heard of the pride of Moab, for it is very great. We know your loftiness, your arrogance, and your haughty heart." 
Jeremiah48:30 I know her insolence,“ the Lord has said, “but her boasts are false—her helplessness is great." 
Jeremiah48:31 Yes, I wail for Moab, my heart is broken for the men of Kir-heres." 
Jeremiah48:32 “O men of Sibmah, rich in vineyards, I weep for you even more than for Jazer. For the destroyer has cut off your spreading tendrils and harvested your grapes and summer fruits. He has plucked you bare!" 
Jeremiah48:33 Joy and gladness are gone from fruitful Moab. The presses yield no wine; no one treads the grapes with shouts of joy. There is shouting, yes, but not the shouting of joy." 
Jeremiah48:34 Instead the awful cries of terror and pain rise from all over the land—from Heshbon clear across to Elealeh and to Jahaz; from Zoar to Horonaim and to Eglath-shelishiyah. The pastures of Nimrim are deserted now.“ 
Jeremiah48:35 For the Lord says: “I have put a stop to Moab's worshiping false gods and burning incense to idols. 
Jeremiah48:36 Sad sings my heart for Moab and Kir-heres, for all their wealth has disappeared." 
Jeremiah48:37 They shave their heads and beards in anguish; they slash their hands and put on clothes of sackcloth. 
Jeremiah48:38 Crying and sorrow will be in every Moabite home and on the streets; for I have smashed and shattered Moab like an old, unwanted bottle." 
Jeremiah48:39 How it is broken! Hear the wails! See the shame of Moab! For she is a sign of horror and of scoffing to her neighbors now. 
Jeremiah48:40 “A vulture circles ominously above the land of Moab,“ says the Lord." 
Jeremiah48:41 “Her cities are fallen; her strongholds are seized. The hearts of her mightiest warriors fail with fear like women in the pains of giving birth. 
Jeremiah48:42 Moab shall no longer be a nation, for she has boasted against the Lord." 
Jeremiah48:43 Fear and traps and treachery shall be your lot, O Moab,“ says the Lord." 
Jeremiah48:44 “He who flees shall fall in a trap, and he who escapes from the trap shall run into a snare. I will see to it that you do not get away, for the time of your judgment has come." 
Jeremiah48:45 They flee to Heshbon, unable to go farther. But a fire comes from Heshbon—Sihon's ancestral home—and devours the land from end to end with all its rebellious people." 
Jeremiah48:46 “Woe to you, O Moab; the people of the god Chemosh are destroyed, and your sons and daughters are taken away as slaves." 
Jeremiah48:47 But in the latter days,“ says the Lord, “I will reestablish Moab.“ (Here the prophecy concerning Moab ends.)" 
Jeremiah49:1 The Ammonites “What is this you are doing? Why are you living in the cities of the Jews? Aren't there Jews enough to fill them up? Didn't they inherit them from me? Why then have you, who worship Milcom, taken over Gad and all its cities?" 
Jeremiah49:2 I will punish you for this,“ the Lord declares, “by destroying your city of Rabbah. It shall become a desolate heap, and the neighboring towns shall be burned. Then Israel shall come and take back her land from you again. She shall dispossess those who dispossessed her,“ says the Lord." 
Jeremiah49:3 “Cry out, O Heshbon, for Ai is destroyed! Weep, daughter of Rabbah! Put on garments of mourning; weep and wail, hiding in the hedges, for your god Milcom shall be exiled along with his princes and priests." 
Jeremiah49:4 You are proud of your fertile valleys, but they will soon be ruined. O wicked daughter, you trusted in your wealth and thought no one could ever harm you." 
Jeremiah49:5 But see, I will bring terror upon you,“ says the Lord, the Lord Almighty. “For all your neighbors shall drive you from your land and none shall help your exiles as they flee." 
Jeremiah49:6 But afterward I will restore the fortunes of the Ammonites,“ says the Lord. The Edomites" 
Jeremiah49:7 The Lord says: “Where are all your wise men of days gone by? Is there not one left in all of Teman? 
Jeremiah49:8 Flee to the remotest parts of the desert, O people of Dedan; for when I punish Edom, I will punish you!" 
Jeremiah49:9 Those who gather grapes leave a few for the poor, and even thieves don't take everything," 
Jeremiah49:10 but I will strip bare the land of Esau, and there will be no place to hide. Her children, her brothers, her neighbors—all will be destroyed—and she herself will perish too." 
Jeremiah49:11 (But I will preserve your fatherless children who remain, and let your widows depend upon me.)“" 
Jeremiah49:12 The Lord says to Edom: “If the innocent must suffer, how much more must you! You shall not go unpunished! You must drink this cup of judgment!" 
Jeremiah49:13 For I have sworn by my own name,“ says the Lord, “that Bozrah shall become heaps of ruins, cursed and mocked; and her cities shall be eternal wastes.“" 
Jeremiah49:14 I have heard this message from the Lord: “I have sent a messenger to call the nations to form a coalition against Edom and destroy her. 
Jeremiah49:15 I will make her weak among the nations and despised by all,“ says the Lord." 
Jeremiah49:16 “You have been fooled by your fame and your pride, living there in the mountains of Petra, in the clefts of the rocks. But though you live among the peaks with the eagles, I will bring you down,“ says the Lord." 
Jeremiah49:17 “The fate of Edom will be horrible; all who go by will be appalled and gasp at the sight. 
Jeremiah49:18 Your cities will become as silent as Sodom and Gomorrah and their neighboring towns,“ says the Lord. “No one will live there anymore." 
Jeremiah49:19 I will send against them one who will come like a lion from the wilds of Jordan stalking the sheep in the fold. Suddenly Edom shall be destroyed, and I will appoint over the Edomites the person of my choice. For who is like me, and who can call me to account? What shepherd can defy me?“" 
Jeremiah49:20 Take note: The Lord will certainly do this to Edom and also the people of Teman—even little children will be dragged away as slaves! It will be a shocking thing to see. 
Jeremiah49:21 The earth shakes with the noise of Edom's fall; the cry of the people is heard as far away as the Red Sea. 
Jeremiah49:22 The one who will come will fly as swift as a vulture and will spread his wings against Bozrah. Then the courage of the mightiest warriors will disappear like that of women in labor. Damascus 
Jeremiah49:23 “The cities of Hamath and Arpad are stricken with fear, for they have heard the news of their doom. Their hearts are troubled like a wild sea in a raging storm." 
Jeremiah49:24 Damascus has become feeble, and all her people turn to flee. Fear, anguish, and sorrow have gripped her as they do women in labor." 
Jeremiah49:25 O famous city, city of joy, how you are forsaken now!" 
Jeremiah49:26 Your young men lie dead in the streets; your entire army shall be destroyed in one day,“ says the Lord Almighty." 
Jeremiah49:27 “And I will start a fire at the edge of Damascus that shall burn up the palaces of Benhadad.“ Kedar and Hazor 
Jeremiah49:28 This prophecy is about Kedar and the kingdoms of Hazor that are going to be destroyed by Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, for the Lord will send him to destroy them:" 
Jeremiah49:29 “Their flocks and their tents will be captured,“ says the Lord, “with all their household goods. Their camels will be taken away, and all around will be the shouts of panic, 'We are surrounded and doomed!'" 
Jeremiah49:30 Flee for your lives,“ says the Lord. “Go deep into the deserts, O people of Hazor, for Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, has plotted against you and is preparing to destroy you." 
Jeremiah49:31 “Go,“ said the Lord to King Nebuchadnezzar. “Attack those wealthy Bedouin tribes living alone in the desert without a care in the world, boasting that they are self-sufficient—that they need neither walls nor gates." 
Jeremiah49:32 Their camels and cattle shall all be yours, and I will scatter these heathen to the winds. From all directions I will bring calamity upon them." 
Jeremiah49:33 “Hazor shall be a home for wild animals of the desert. No one shall ever live there again. It shall be desolate forever.“ Elam 
Jeremiah49:34 God's message against Elam came to Jeremiah in the beginning of the reign of Zedekiah, king of Judah:" 
Jeremiah49:35 “The Lord says: 'I will destroy the army of Elam," 
Jeremiah49:36 and I will scatter the people of Elam to the four winds; they shall be exiled to countries throughout the world. 
Jeremiah49:37 My fierce anger will bring great evil upon Elam,' says the Lord, 'and I will cause her enemies to wipe her out." 
Jeremiah49:38 And I will set my throne in Elam,' says the Lord. 'I will destroy her king and princes." 
Jeremiah49:39 But in the latter days I will bring the people back,' says the Lord.“" 
Jeremiah50:1 Babylon This is the message from the Lord against Babylon and the Chaldeans, spoken by Jeremiah the prophet:" 
Jeremiah50:2 “Tell all the world that Babylon will be destroyed; her god Marduk will be utterly disgraced! 
Jeremiah50:3 For a nation shall come down upon her from the north with such destruction that no one shall live in her again; all shall be gone—both men and animals shall flee. 
Jeremiah50:4 “Then the people of Israel and Judah shall join together, weeping and seeking the Lord their God." 
Jeremiah50:5 They shall ask the way to Zion and start back home again. 'Come,' they will say, 'let us be united to the Lord with an eternal pledge that will never be broken again.'" 
Jeremiah50:6 “My people have been lost sheep. Their shepherds led them astray and then turned them loose in the mountains. They lost their way and didn't remember how to get back to the fold. 
Jeremiah50:7 All who found them devoured them and said, 'We are permitted to attack them freely, for they have sinned against the Lord, the God of justice, the hope of their fathers.'" 
Jeremiah50:8 “But now, flee from Babylon, the land of the Chaldeans; lead my people home again." 
Jeremiah50:9 For see, I am raising up an army of great nations from the north, and I will bring them against Babylon to attack her, and she shall be destroyed. The enemies' arrows go straight to the mark; they do not miss!" 
Jeremiah50:10 And Babylon shall be sacked until everyone is sated with loot,“ says the Lord." 
Jeremiah50:11 “Though you were glad, O Chaldeans, plunderers of my people, and are fat as cows that feed in lush pastures, and neigh like stallions," 
Jeremiah50:12 yet your mother shall be overwhelmed with shame, for you shall become the least of the nations—a wilderness, a dry and desert land." 
Jeremiah50:13 Because of the anger of the Lord, Babylon shall become deserted wasteland, and all who pass by shall be appalled and shall mock at her for all her wounds." 
Jeremiah50:14 “Yes, prepare to fight with Babylon, all you nations round about; let the archers shoot at her; spare no arrows, for she has sinned against the Lord." 
Jeremiah50:15 Shout against her from every side. Look! She surrenders! Her walls have fallen. The Lord has taken vengeance. Do to her as she has done! 
Jeremiah50:16 Let the farmhands all depart. Let them rush back to their own lands as the enemies advance. 
Jeremiah50:17 “The Israelites are like sheep the lions chase. First the king of Assyria ate them up; then Nebuchadnezzar, the king of Babylon, crunched their bones.“" 
Jeremiah50:18 Therefore the Lord, the God of Israel, says: “Now I will punish the king of Babylon and his land as I punished the king of Assyria." 
Jeremiah50:19 And I will bring Israel home again to her own land, to feed in the fields of Carmel and Bashan and to be happy once more on Mount Ephraim and Mount Gilead." 
Jeremiah50:20 In those days,“ says the Lord, “no sin shall be found in Israel or in Judah, for I will pardon the remnant I preserve." 
Jeremiah50:21 “Go up, O my warriors, against the land of Merathaim and against the people of Pekod. Yes, march against Babylon, the land of rebels, a land that I will judge! Annihilate them, as I have commanded you." 
Jeremiah50:22 Let there be the shout of battle in the land, a shout of great destruction." 
Jeremiah50:23 Babylon, the mightiest hammer in all the earth, lies broken and shattered. Babylon is desolate among the nations!" 
Jeremiah50:24 O Babylon, I have set a trap for you and you are caught, for you have fought against the Lord." 
Jeremiah50:25 “The Lord has opened his armory and brought out weapons to explode his wrath upon his enemies. The terror that befalls Babylon will be the work of the Lord God. 
Jeremiah50:26 Yes, come against her from distant lands; break open her granaries; knock down her walls and houses into heaps of ruins and utterly destroy her; let nothing be left." 
Jeremiah50:27 Not even her cattle—woe to them too! Kill them all! For the time has come for Babylon to be devastated. 
Jeremiah50:28 “But my people will flee; they will escape back to their own country to tell how the Lord their God has broken forth in fury upon those who destroyed his Temple. 
Jeremiah50:29 “Send out a call for archers to come to Babylon; surround the city so that none can escape. Do to her as she has done to others, for she has haughtily defied the Lord, the Holy One of Israel." 
Jeremiah50:30 Her young men will fall in the streets and die; her warriors will all be killed. 
Jeremiah50:31 For see, I am against you, O people so proud; and now your day of reckoning has come." 
Jeremiah50:32 Land of pride, you will stumble and fall, and no one will raise you up; for the Lord will light a fire in the cities of Babylon that will burn everything around them.“" 
Jeremiah50:33 “The Lord says: The people of Israel and Judah have been wronged. Their captors hold them and refuse to let them go. 
Jeremiah50:34 But their Redeemer is strong. His name is the Lord Almighty. He will plead for them and see that they are freed to live again in quietness in Israel. “As for the people of Babylon—there is no rest for them! 
Jeremiah50:35 The sword of destruction shall smite the Chaldeans,“ says the Lord. “It shall smite the people of Babylon—her princes and wise men too." 
Jeremiah50:36 All her wise counselors shall become fools! Panic shall seize her mightiest warriors! 
Jeremiah50:37 War shall devour her horses and chariots, and her allies from other lands shall become as weak as women. Her treasures shall all be robbed;" 
Jeremiah50:38 even her water supply will fail. And why? Because the whole land is full of images, and the people are madly in love with their idols." 
Jeremiah50:39 “Therefore this city of Babylon shall become inhabited by ostriches and jackals; it shall be a home for the wild animals of the desert. Never again shall it be lived in by human beings; it shall lie desolate forever. 
Jeremiah50:40 The Lord declares that he will destroy Babylon just as he destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah and their neighboring towns. No one has lived in them since, and no one will live again in Babylon." 
Jeremiah50:41 “See them coming! A great army from the north! It is accompanied by many kings called by God from many lands. 
Jeremiah50:42 They are fully armed for slaughter; they are cruel and show no mercy; their battle cry roars like the surf against the shoreline. O Babylon, they ride against you fully ready for the battle.“" 
Jeremiah50:43 When the king of Babylon received the dispatch, his hands fell helpless at his sides; pangs of terror gripped him like the pangs of a woman in labor." 
Jeremiah50:44 “I will send against them an invader who will come upon them suddenly, like a lion from the jungles of Jordan that leaps upon the grazing sheep. I will put her defenders to flight and appoint over them whomsoever I please. For who is like me? What ruler c" 
Jeremiah50:45 Listen to the plan of the Lord against Babylon, the land of the Chaldeans. For even little children shall be dragged away as slaves; oh, the horror; oh, the terror." 
Jeremiah50:46 The whole earth shall shake at Babylon's fall, and her cry of despair shall be heard around the world." 
Jeremiah51:1 The Lord says: “I will stir up a destroyer against Babylon, against that whole land of the Chaldeans, and destroy it." 
Jeremiah51:2 Winnowers shall come and winnow her and blow her away; they shall come from every side to rise against her in her day of trouble. 
Jeremiah51:3 The arrows of the enemy shall strike down the bowmen of Babylon and pierce her warriors in their coats of mail. No one shall be spared; both young and old alike shall be destroyed. 
Jeremiah51:4 They shall fall down slain in the land of the Chaldeans, slashed to death in her streets." 
Jeremiah51:5 For the Lord Almighty has not forsaken Israel and Judah. He is still their God, but the land of the Chaldeans is filled with sin against the Holy One of Israel.“" 
Jeremiah51:6 Flee from Babylon! Save yourselves! Don't get trapped! If you stay, you will be destroyed when God takes his vengeance on all of Babylon's sins." 
Jeremiah51:7 Babylon has been as a gold cup in the Lord's hands, a cup from which he made the whole earth drink and go mad." 
Jeremiah51:8 But now, suddenly Babylon too has fallen. Weep for her; give her medicine; perhaps she can yet be healed." 
Jeremiah51:9 We would help her if we could, but nothing can save her now. Let her go. Abandon her and return to your own land, for God is judging her from heaven." 
Jeremiah51:10 The Lord has vindicated us. Come, let us declare in Jerusalem all the Lord our God has done." 
Jeremiah51:11 Sharpen the arrows! Lift up the shields! For the Lord has stirred up the spirit of the kings of the Medes to march on Babylon and destroy her. This is his vengeance on those who wronged his people and desecrated his Temple. 
Jeremiah51:12 Prepare your defenses, Babylon! Set many watchmen on your walls; send out an ambush, for the Lord will do all he has said he would concerning Babylon." 
Jeremiah51:13 O wealthy port, great center of commerce, your end has come; the thread of your life is cut." 
Jeremiah51:14 The Lord Almighty has taken this vow and sworn to it in his own name: “Your cities shall be filled with enemies, like fields filled with locusts in a plague, and they shall lift to the skies their mighty shouts of victory.“" 
Jeremiah51:15 God made the earth by his power and wisdom. He stretched out the heavens by his understanding. 
Jeremiah51:16 When he speaks there is thunder in the heavens, and he causes the vapors to rise around the world; he brings the lightning with the rain and the winds from his treasuries." 
Jeremiah51:17 Compared to him, all men are stupid beasts. They have no wisdom—none at all! The silversmith is dulled by the images he makes, for in making them he lies; for he calls them gods when there is not a breath of life in them at all!" 
Jeremiah51:18 Idols are nothing! They are lies! And the time is coming when God will come and see, and shall destroy them all." 
Jeremiah51:19 But the God of Israel is no idol! For he made everything there is, and Israel is his nation; the Lord Almighty is his name." 
Jeremiah51:20 “Cyrus is God's battleaxe and sword. I will use you,“ says the Lord, “to break nations in pieces and to destroy many kingdoms." 
Jeremiah51:21 With you I will crush armies, destroying the horse and his rider, the chariot and the charioteer—" 
Jeremiah51:22 yes, and the civilians too, both old and young, young men and maidens," 
Jeremiah51:23 shepherds and flocks, farmers and oxen, captains and rulers;" 
Jeremiah51:24 before your eyes I will repay Babylon and all the Chaldeans for all the evil they have done to my people,“ says the Lord." 
Jeremiah51:25 “For see, I am against you, O mighty mountain, Babylon, destroyer of the earth! I will lift my hand against you, roll you down from your heights, and leave you, a burnt-out mountain." 
Jeremiah51:26 You shall be desolate forever; even your stones shall never be used for building again. You shall be completely wiped out.“ 
Jeremiah51:27 Signal many nations to mobilize for war on Babylon. Sound the battle cry; bring out the armies of Ararat, Minni, and Ashkenaz. Appoint a leader; bring a multitude of horses!" 
Jeremiah51:28 Bring against her the armies of the kings of the Medes and their generals, and the armies of all the countries they rule." 
Jeremiah51:29 Babylon trembles and writhes in pain, for all that the Lord has planned against her stands unchanged. Babylon will be left desolate without a living soul." 
Jeremiah51:30 Her mightiest soldiers no longer fight; they stay in their barracks. Their courage is gone; they have become as women. The invaders have burned the houses and broken down the city gates. 
Jeremiah51:31 Messengers from every side come running to the king to tell him all is lost! 
Jeremiah51:32 All the escape routes are blocked; the fortifications are burning, and the army is in panic." 
Jeremiah51:33 “For the Lord, the God of Israel, says: Babylon is like the wheat upon a threshing floor; in just a little while the flailing will begin.“" 
Jeremiah51:34 The Jews in Babylon say, “Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, has eaten and crushed us and emptied out our strength; he has swallowed us like a great monster and filled his belly with our riches; he has cast us out of our own country." 
Jeremiah51:35 May Babylon be repaid for all she did to us! May she be paid in full for all our blood she spilled!“ 
Jeremiah51:36 And the Lord replies: “I will be your lawyer; I will plead your case; I will avenge you. I will dry up her river, her water supply," 
Jeremiah51:37 and Babylon shall become a heap of ruins, haunted by jackals, a land horrible to see, incredible, without a living soul." 
Jeremiah51:38 In their drunken feasts, the men of Babylon roar like lions." 
Jeremiah51:39 And while they lie inflamed with all their wine, I will prepare a different kind of feast for them and make them drink until they fall unconscious to the floor, to sleep forever, never to waken again,“ says the Lord." 
Jeremiah51:40 “I will bring them like lambs to the slaughter, like rams and goats." 
Jeremiah51:41 “How Babylon is fallen—great Babylon, lauded by all the earth! The world can scarcely believe its eyes at Babylon's fall!" 
Jeremiah51:42 The sea has risen upon Babylon; she is covered by its waves. 
Jeremiah51:43 Her cities lie in ruins—she is a dry wilderness where no one lives nor even travelers pass by. 
Jeremiah51:44 And I will punish Bel, the god of Babylon, and pull from his mouth what he has taken. The nations shall no longer come and worship him; the wall of Babylon has fallen." 
Jeremiah51:45 “O my people, flee from Babylon; save yourselves from the fierce anger of the Lord." 
Jeremiah51:46 But don't panic when you hear the first rumor of approaching forces. For rumors will keep coming year by year. Then there will be a time of civil war as the governors of Babylon fight against each other. 
Jeremiah51:47 For the time is surely coming when I will punish this great city and all her idols; her dead shall lie in the streets. 
Jeremiah51:48 Heaven and earth shall rejoice, for out of the north shall come destroying armies against Babylon,“ says the Lord." 
Jeremiah51:49 “Just as Babylon killed the people of Israel, so must she be killed." 
Jeremiah51:50 Go, you who escaped the sword! Don't stand and watch—flee while you can! Remember the Lord and return to Jerusalem far away!“" 
Jeremiah51:51 “We are ashamed because the Temple of the Lord has been defiled by foreigners from Babylon.“ 
Jeremiah51:52 “Yes,“ says the Lord. “But the time is coming for the destruction of the idols of Babylon. All through the land will be heard the groans of the wounded." 
Jeremiah51:53 Though Babylon be as powerful as heaven, though she increase her strength immeasurably, she shall die,“ says the Lord." 
Jeremiah51:54 “Listen! Hear the cry of great destruction out of Babylon, the land the Chaldeans rule!" 
Jeremiah51:55 For the Lord is destroying Babylon; her mighty voice is stilled as the waves roar in upon her. 
Jeremiah51:56 Destroying armies come and slay her mighty men; all her weapons break in her hands, for the Lord God gives just punishment and is giving Babylon all her due." 
Jeremiah51:57 I will make drunk her princes, wise men, rulers, captains, warriors. They shall sleep and not wake up again!“ So says the King, the Lord Almighty." 
Jeremiah51:58 “For the wide walls of Babylon shall be leveled to the ground, and her high gates shall be burned; the builders from many lands have worked in vain—their work shall be destroyed by fire!“" 
Jeremiah51:59 During the fourth year of Zedekiah's reign, this message came to Jeremiah to give to Seraiah (son of Neriah, son of Mahseiah), concerning Seraiah's capture and exile to Babylon along with Zedekiah, king of Judah. (Seraiah was quartermaster of Zedekiah's army.)" 
Jeremiah51:60 Jeremiah wrote on a scroll all the terrible things God had scheduled against Babylon—all the words written above— 
Jeremiah51:61 and gave the scroll to Seraiah and said to him, “When you get to Babylon, read what I have written" 
Jeremiah51:62 and say, 'Lord, you have said that you will destroy Babylon so that not a living creature will remain, and it will be abandoned forever.'" 
Jeremiah51:63 Then, when you have finished reading the scroll, tie a rock to it, and throw it into the Euphrates River," 
Jeremiah51:64 and say, 'So shall Babylon sink, never more to rise, because of the evil I am bringing upon her.' “ (This ends Jeremiah's messages.)" 
Jeremiah52:1 (Events told about in chapter 39.) Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king, and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Hamutal (daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah)." 
Jeremiah52:2 But he was a wicked king, just as Jehoiakim had been." 
Jeremiah52:3 Things became so bad at last that the Lord, in his anger, saw to it that Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon until he and the people of Israel were ejected from the Lord's presence in Jerusalem and Judah, and were taken away as captives to Babylon." 
Jeremiah52:4 In the ninth year of Zedekiah's reign, on the tenth day of the tenth month, Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, came with all his army against Jerusalem and built forts around it," 
Jeremiah52:5 and laid siege to the city for two years. 
Jeremiah52:6 Then finally, on the ninth day of the fourth month, when the famine in the city was very serious, with the last of the food entirely gone," 
Jeremiah52:7 the people in the city tore a hole in the city wall and all the soldiers fled from the city during the night, going out by the gate between the two walls near the king's gardens (for the city was surrounded by the Chaldeans), and made a dash for it across the fields, toward Arabah." 
Jeremiah52:8 But the Chaldean soldiers chased them and caught King Zedekiah in some fields near Jericho—for all his army was scattered from him. 
Jeremiah52:9 They brought him to the king of Babylon who was staying in the city of Riblah in the kingdom of Hamath, and there judgment was passed upon him." 
Jeremiah52:10 He made Zedekiah watch while his sons and all the princes of Judah were killed before his eyes. 
Jeremiah52:11 Then his eyes were gouged out, and he was taken in chains to Babylon and put in prison for the rest of his life." 
Jeremiah52:12 On the tenth day of the fifth month during the nineteenth year of the reign of Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, Nebuzaradan, captain of the guard, arrived in Jerusalem," 
Jeremiah52:13 and burned the Temple and the palace and all the larger homes," 
Jeremiah52:14 and set the Chaldean army to work tearing down the walls of the city. 
Jeremiah52:15 Then he took to Babylon, as captives, some of the poorest of the people—along with those who survived the city's destruction, and those who had deserted Zedekiah and had come over to the Babylonian army, and the tradesmen who were left." 
Jeremiah52:16 But he left some of the poorest people to care for the crops as vinedressers and plowmen. 
Jeremiah52:17 The Babylonians dismantled the two large bronze pillars that stood at the entrance of the Temple, and the bronze laver and bronze bulls on which it stood, and carted them off to Babylon." 
Jeremiah52:18 And he took along all the bronze pots and kettles, the ash shovels used at the altar, the snuffers, spoons, bowls, and all the other items used in the Temple." 
Jeremiah52:19 He also took the firepans, the solid gold and silver candlesticks, and the cups and bowls." 
Jeremiah52:20 The weight of the two enormous pillars, the laver, and twelve bulls was tremendous. They had no way of estimating it. (They had been made in the days of King Solomon.)" 
Jeremiah52:21 For the pillars were each 27 feet high and 18 feet in circumference, hollow, with 3-inch walls." 
Jeremiah52:22 The top 7 1/2 feet of each column had bronze carvings, a network of bronze pomegranates." 
Jeremiah52:23 There were 96 pomegranates on the sides, and on the network round about there were a hundred more." 
Jeremiah52:24 The captain of the guard took along with him as his prisoners: Seraiah the chief priest, Zephaniah his assistant, the three chief Temple guards," 
Jeremiah52:24 The captain of the guard took along with him as his prisoners: Seraiah the chief priest, Zephaniah his assistant, the three chief Temple guards," 
Jeremiah52:25 one of the commanding officers of the army, seven of the king's special counselors discovered in the city, the secretary of the general-in-chief of the Jewish army (who was in charge of recruitment), and sixty other men of importance found hiding." 
Jeremiah52:26 He took them to the king of Babylon at Riblah," 
Jeremiah52:27 where the king killed them all. So it was that Judah's exile was accomplished. 
Jeremiah52:28 The number of captives taken to Babylon in the seventh year of Nebuchadnezzar's reign was 3,023." 
Jeremiah52:29 Then, eleven years later, he took 832 more;" 
Jeremiah52:30 five years after that he sent Nebuzaradan, his captain of the guard, and took 745—a total of 4,600 captives in all." 
Jeremiah52:31 On February 25 of the thirty-seventh year of the imprisonment in Babylon of Jehoiachin, king of Judah, Evil-merodach, who became king of Babylon that year, was kind to King Jehoiachin and brought him out of prison." 
Jeremiah52:32 He spoke pleasantly to him and gave him preference over all the other kings in Babylon; 
Jeremiah52:33 he gave him new clothes and fed him from the king's kitchen as long as he lived. 
Jeremiah52:34 And he was given a regular allowance to cover his daily needs until the day of his death. 
Lamentations1:1 Jerusalem's streets, once thronged with people, are silent now. Like a widow broken with grief, she sits alone in her mourning. She, once queen of nations, is now a slave." 
Lamentations1:2 She sobs through the night; tears run down her cheeks. Among all her lovers, there is none to help her. All her friends are now her enemies." 
Lamentations1:3 Why is Judah led away, a slave? Because of all the wrong she did to others, making them her slaves. Now she sits in exile far away. There is no rest, for those she persecuted have turned and conquered her." 
Lamentations1:4 The roads to Zion mourn, no longer filled with joyous throngs who come to celebrate the Temple feasts; the city gates are silent, her priests groan, her virgins have been dragged away. Bitterly she weeps." 
Lamentations1:5 Her enemies prosper, for the Lord has punished Jerusalem for all her many sins; her young children are captured and taken far away as slaves." 
Lamentations1:6 All her beauty and her majesty are gone; her princes are like starving deer that search for pasture—helpless game too weak to keep on running from their foes. 
Lamentations1:7 And now in the midst of all Jerusalem's sadness she remembers happy bygone days. She thinks of all the precious joys she had before her mocking enemy struck her down—and there was no one to give her aid. 
Lamentations1:8 For Jerusalem sinned so horribly; therefore, she is tossed away like dirty rags. All who honored her despise her now, for they have seen her stripped naked and humiliated. She groans and hides her face." 
Lamentations1:9 She indulged herself in immorality and refused to face the fact that punishment was sure to come. Now she lies in the gutter with no one left to lift her out. “O Lord,“ she cries, “see my plight. The enemy has triumphed.“" 
Lamentations1:10 Her enemies have plundered her completely, taking everything precious she owns. She has seen foreign nations violate her sacred Temple—foreigners you had forbidden even to enter." 
Lamentations1:11 Her people groan and cry for bread; they have sold all they have for food to give a little strength. “Look, O Lord,“ she prays, “and see how I'm despised.“" 
Lamentations1:12 Is it nothing to you, all you who pass by? Look and see if there is any sorrow like my sorrow because of all the Lord has done to me in the day of his fierce wrath." 
Lamentations1:13 He has sent fire from heaven that burns within my bones; he has placed a pitfall in my path and turned me back. He has left me sick and desolate the whole day through. 
Lamentations1:14 He wove my sins into ropes to hitch me to a yoke of slavery. He sapped my strength and gave me to my enemies; I am helpless in their hands. 
Lamentations1:15 The Lord has trampled all my mighty men. A great army has come at his command to crush the noblest youth. The Lord has trampled his beloved city as grapes in a winepress. 
Lamentations1:16 For all these things I weep; tears flow down my cheeks. My Comforter is far away—he who alone could help me. My children have no future; we are a conquered land. 
Lamentations1:17 Jerusalem pleads for help, but no one comforts her. For the Lord has spoken: “Let her neighbors be her foes! Let her be thrown out like filthy rags!“" 
Lamentations1:18 And the Lord is right, for we rebelled. And yet, O people everywhere, behold and see my anguish and despair, for my sons and daughters are taken far away as slaves to distant lands." 
Lamentations1:19 I begged my allies for their help. False hope—they could not help at all. Nor could my priests and elders—they were starving in the streets while searching through the garbage dumps for bread. 
Lamentations1:20 See, O Lord, my anguish; my heart is broken and my soul despairs, for I have terribly rebelled. In the streets the sword awaits me; at home, disease and death." 
Lamentations1:21 Hear my groans! And there is no one anywhere to help. All my enemies have heard my troubles, and they are glad to see what you have done. And yet, O Lord, the time will surely come—for you have promised it—when you will do to them as you have done to me." 
Lamentations1:22 Look also on their sins, O Lord, and punish them as you have punished me, for my sighs are many and my heart is faint." 
Lamentations2:1 A cloud of anger from the Lord has overcast Jerusalem; the fairest city of Israel lies in the dust of the earth, cast from the heights of heaven at his command. In his day of awesome fury he has shown no mercy even to his Temple." 
Lamentations2:2 The Lord without mercy has destroyed every home in Israel. In his wrath he has broken every fortress, every wall. He has brought the kingdom to dust, with all its rulers." 
Lamentations2:3 All the strength of Israel vanishes beneath his wrath. He has withdrawn his protection as the enemy attacks. God burns across the land of Israel like a raging fire. 
Lamentations2:4 He bends his bow against his people as though he were an enemy. His strength is used against them to kill their finest youth. His fury is poured out like fire upon them. 
Lamentations2:5 Yes, the Lord has vanquished Israel like an enemy. He has destroyed her forts and palaces. Sorrows and tears are his portion for Jerusalem." 
Lamentations2:6 He has violently broken down his Temple as though it were a booth of leaves and branches in a garden! No longer can the people celebrate their holy feasts and Sabbaths. Kings and priests together fall before his wrath. 
Lamentations2:7 The Lord has rejected his own altar, for he despises the false “worship“ of his people; he has given their palaces to their enemies, who carouse in the Temple as Israel used to do on days of holy feasts!" 
Lamentations2:8 The Lord determined to destroy Jerusalem. He laid out an unalterable line of destruction. Therefore the ramparts and walls fell down before him. 
Lamentations2:9 Jerusalem's gates are useless. All their locks and bars are broken, for he has crushed them. Her kings and princes are enslaved in far-off lands, without a temple, without a divine law to govern them or prophetic vision to guide them." 
Lamentations2:10 The elders of Jerusalem sit upon the ground in silence, clothed in sackcloth; they throw dust upon their heads in sorrow and despair. The virgins of Jerusalem hang their heads in shame." 
Lamentations2:11 I have cried until the tears no longer come; my heart is broken, my spirit poured out, as I see what has happened to my people; little children and tiny babies are fainting and dying in the streets." 
Lamentations2:12 “Mama, Mama, we want food,“ they cry, and then collapse upon their mothers' shrunken breasts. Their lives ebb away like those wounded in battle." 
Lamentations2:13 In all the world has there ever been such sorrow? O Jerusalem, what can I compare your anguish to? How can I comfort you? For your wound is deep as the sea. Who can heal you?" 
Lamentations2:14 Your “prophets“ have said so many foolish things, false to the core. They have not tried to hold you back from slavery by pointing out your sins. They lied and said that all was well." 
Lamentations2:15 All who pass by scoff and shake their heads and say, “Is this the city called 'Most Beautiful in All the World,' and 'Joy of All the Earth'?“" 
Lamentations2:16 All your enemies deride you. They hiss and grind their teeth and say, “We have destroyed her at last! Long have we waited for this hour, and it is finally here! With our own eyes we've seen her fall.“" 
Lamentations2:17 But it is the Lord who did it, just as he had warned. He has fulfilled the promises of doom he made so long ago. He has destroyed Jerusalem without mercy and caused her enemies to rejoice over her and boast of their power." 
Lamentations2:18 Then the people wept before the Lord. O walls of Jerusalem, let tears fall down upon you like a river; give yourselves no rest from weeping day or night." 
Lamentations2:19 Rise in the night and cry to your God. Pour out your hearts like water to the Lord; lift up your hands to him; plead for your children as they faint with hunger in the streets. 
Lamentations2:20 O Lord, think! These are your own people to whom you are doing this. Shall mothers eat their little children, those they bounced upon their knees? Shall priests and prophets die within the Temple of the Lord?" 
Lamentations2:21 See them lying in the streets—old and young, boys and girls, killed by the enemies' swords. You have killed them, Lord, in your anger; you have killed them without mercy." 
Lamentations2:22 You have deliberately called for this destruction; in the day of your anger none escaped or remained. All my little children lie dead upon the streets before the enemy. 
Lamentations3:1 I am the man who has seen the afflictions that come from the rod of God's wrath. 
Lamentations3:2 He has brought me into deepest darkness, shutting out all light." 
Lamentations3:3 He has turned against me. Day and night his hand is heavy on me. 
Lamentations3:4 He has made me old and has broken my bones. 
Lamentations3:5 He has built forts against me and surrounded me with anguish and distress. 
Lamentations3:6 He buried me in dark places, like those long dead." 
Lamentations3:7 He has walled me in; I cannot escape; he has fastened me with heavy chains. 
Lamentations3:8 And though I cry and shout, he will not hear my prayers!" 
Lamentations3:9 He has shut me into a place of high, smooth walls; he has filled my path with detours." 
Lamentations3:10 He lurks like a bear, like a lion, waiting to attack me." 
Lamentations3:11 He has dragged me into the underbrush and torn me with his claws, leaving me bleeding and desolate." 
Lamentations3:12 He has bent his bow and aimed it squarely at me," 
Lamentations3:13 and sent his arrows deep within my heart. 
Lamentations3:14 My own people laugh at me; all day long they sing their ribald songs. 
Lamentations3:15 He has filled me with bitterness and given me a cup of deepest sorrows to drink. 
Lamentations3:16 He has made me eat gravel and broken my teeth; he has rolled me in ashes and dirt. 
Lamentations3:17 O Lord, all peace and all prosperity have long since gone, for you have taken them away. I have forgotten what enjoyment is." 
Lamentations3:18 All hope is gone; my strength has turned to water, for the Lord has left me." 
Lamentations3:19 Oh, remember the bitterness and suffering you have dealt to me!" 
Lamentations3:20 For I can never forget these awful years; always my soul will live in utter shame. 
Lamentations3:21 Yet there is one ray of hope: 
Lamentations3:22 his compassion never ends. It is only the Lord's mercies that have kept us from complete destruction. 
Lamentations3:23 Great is his faithfulness; his loving-kindness begins afresh each day. 
Lamentations3:24 My soul claims the Lord as my inheritance; therefore I will hope in him. 
Lamentations3:25 The Lord is wonderfully good to those who wait for him, to those who seek for him." 
Lamentations3:26 It is good both to hope and wait quietly for the salvation of the Lord. 
Lamentations3:27 It is good for a young man to be under discipline," 
Lamentations3:28 for it causes him to sit apart in silence beneath the Lord's demands," 
Lamentations3:29 to lie face downward in the dust; then at last there is hope for him. 
Lamentations3:30 Let him turn the other cheek to those who strike him and accept their awful insults," 
Lamentations3:31 for the Lord will not abandon him forever. 
Lamentations3:32 Although God gives him grief, yet he will show compassion too, according to the greatness of his loving-kindness." 
Lamentations3:33 For he does not enjoy afflicting men and causing sorrow. 
Lamentations3:34 But you have trampled and crushed beneath your feet the lowly of the world," 
Lamentations3:35 and deprived men of their God-given rights," 
Lamentations3:36 and refused them justice. No wonder the Lord has had to deal with you! 
Lamentations3:37 For who can act against you without the Lord's permission? 
Lamentations3:38 It is the Lord who helps one and harms another. 
Lamentations3:39 Why then should we, mere humans as we are, murmur and complain when punished for our sins?" 
Lamentations3:40 Let us examine ourselves instead, and let us repent and turn again to the Lord." 
Lamentations3:41 Let us lift our hearts and hands to him in heaven," 
Lamentations3:42 for we have sinned; we have rebelled against the Lord, and he has not forgotten it." 
Lamentations3:43 You have engulfed us by your anger, Lord, and slain us without mercy." 
Lamentations3:44 You have veiled yourself as with a cloud so that our prayers do not reach through. 
Lamentations3:45 You have made us as refuse and garbage among the nations. 
Lamentations3:46 All our enemies have spoken out against us. 
Lamentations3:47 We are filled with fear, for we are trapped and desolate, destroyed." 
Lamentations3:48 My eyes flow day and night with never-ending streams 
Lamentations3:49 of tears because of the destruction of my people. 
Lamentations3:50 Oh, that the Lord might look down from heaven and respond to my cry!" 
Lamentations3:51 My heart is breaking over what is happening to the young girls of Jerusalem. 
Lamentations3:52 My enemies, whom I have never harmed, chased me as though I were a bird." 
Lamentations3:53 They threw me in a well and capped it with a rock. 
Lamentations3:54 The water flowed above my head. I thought, This is the end!" 
Lamentations3:55 But I called upon your name, O Lord, from deep within the well," 
Lamentations3:56 and you heard me! You listened to my pleading; you heard my weeping! 
Lamentations3:57 Yes, you came at my despairing cry and told me not to fear." 
Lamentations3:58 O Lord, you are my lawyer! Plead my case! For you have redeemed my life." 
Lamentations3:59 You have seen the wrong they did to me; be my Judge, to prove me right." 
Lamentations3:60 You have seen the plots my foes have laid against me. 
Lamentations3:61 You have heard the vile names they have called me," 
Lamentations3:62 and all they say about me and their whispered plans. 
Lamentations3:63 See how they laugh and sing with glee, preparing my doom." 
Lamentations3:64 O Lord, repay them well for all the evil they have done." 
Lamentations3:65 Harden their hearts and curse them, Lord." 
Lamentations3:66 Go after them in fierce pursuit and wipe them off the earth, beneath the heavens of the Lord." 
Lamentations4:1 How the finest gold has lost its luster! For the inlaid Temple walls are scattered in the streets! 
Lamentations4:2 The cream of our youth—the finest of the gold—are treated as earthenware pots. 
Lamentations4:3 Even the jackals feed their young, but not my people, Israel. They are like cruel desert ostriches, heedless of their babies' cries." 
Lamentations4:4 The children's tongues stick to the roofs of their mouths for thirst, for there is not a drop of water left. Babies cry for bread, but no one can give them any." 
Lamentations4:5 Those who used to eat fastidiously are begging in the streets for anything at all. Those brought up in palaces now scratch in garbage pits for food. 
Lamentations4:6 For the sin of my people is greater than that of Sodom, where utter disaster struck in a moment without the hand of man." 
Lamentations4:7 Our princes were lean and tanned, the finest specimens of men;" 
Lamentations4:8 but now their faces are as black as soot. No one can recognize them. Their skin sticks to their bones; it is dry and hard and withered. 
Lamentations4:9 Those killed by the sword are far better off than those who die of slow starvation. 
Lamentations4:10 Tenderhearted women have cooked and eaten their own children; thus they survived the siege. 
Lamentations4:11 But now at last the anger of the Lord is satisfied; his fiercest anger has been poured out. He started a fire in Jerusalem that burned it down to its foundations. 
Lamentations4:12 Not a king in all the earth—no one in all the world—would have believed an enemy could enter through Jerusalem's gates! 
Lamentations4:13 Yet God permitted it because of the sins of her prophets and priests, who defiled the city by shedding innocent blood." 
Lamentations4:14 Now these same men are blindly staggering through the streets, covered with blood, defiling everything they touch." 
Lamentations4:15 “Get away!“ the people shout at them. “You are defiled!“ They flee to distant lands and wander there among the foreigners; but none will let them stay. 
Lamentations4:16 The Lord himself has dealt with them; he no longer helps them, for they persecuted the priests and elders who stayed true to God." 
Lamentations4:17 We look for our allies to come and save us, but we look in vain. The nation we expected most to help us makes no move at all." 
Lamentations4:18 We can't go into the streets without danger to our lives. Our end is near—our days are numbered. We are doomed. 
Lamentations4:19 Our enemies are swifter than the eagles; if we flee to the mountains they find us. If we hide in the wilderness, they are waiting for us there." 
Lamentations4:20 Our king—the life of our life, the Lord's anointed—was captured in their snares. Yes, even our mighty king, about whom we had boasted that under his protection we could hold our own against any nation on earth!" 
Lamentations4:21 Do you rejoice, O people of Edom, in the land of Uz? But you, too, will feel the awful anger of the Lord." 
Lamentations4:22 Israel's exile for her sins will end at last, but Edom's never." 
Lamentations5:1 O Lord, remember all that has befallen us; see what sorrows we must bear!" 
Lamentations5:2 Our homes, our nation, now are filled with foreigners." 
Lamentations5:3 We are orphans—our fathers dead, our mothers widowed." 
Lamentations5:4 We must even pay for water to drink; our fuel is sold to us at the highest of prices. 
Lamentations5:5 We bow our necks beneath the victors' feet; unending work is now our lot. 
Lamentations5:6 We beg for bread from Egypt, and Assyria too." 
Lamentations5:7 Our fathers sinned but died before the hand of judgment fell. We have borne the blow that they deserved! 
Lamentations5:8 Our former servants have become our masters; there is no one left to save us. 
Lamentations5:9 We went into the wilderness to hunt for food, risking death from enemies." 
Lamentations5:10 Our skin was black from famine. 
Lamentations5:11 They rape the women of Jerusalem and the girls in Judah's cities. 
Lamentations5:12 Our princes are hanged by their thumbs. Even aged men are treated with contempt. 
Lamentations5:13 They take away the young men to grind their grain, and the little children stagger beneath their heavy loads." 
Lamentations5:14 The old men sit no longer in the city gates; the young no longer dance and sing. 
Lamentations5:15 The joy of our hearts has ended; our dance has turned to death. 
Lamentations5:16 Our glory is gone. The crown is fallen from our head. Woe upon us for our sins. 
Lamentations5:17 Our hearts are faint and weary; our eyes grow dim. 
Lamentations5:18 Jerusalem and the Temple of the Lord are desolate, deserted by all but wild animals lurking in the ruins." 
Lamentations5:19 O Lord, forever you remain the same! Your throne continues from generation to generation." 
Lamentations5:20 Why do you forget us forever? Why do you forsake us for so long? 
Lamentations5:21 Turn us around and bring us back to you again! That is our only hope! Give us back the joys we used to have! 
Lamentations5:22 Or have you utterly rejected us? Are you angry with us still? 
Ezekiel1:1 Ezekiel was a priest (the son of Buzi) who lived with the Jewish exiles beside the Chebar Canal in Babylon. 
Ezekiel1:2 One day late in June, when I was thirty years old, the" 
Ezekiel1:3 heavens were suddenly opened to me and I saw visions from God. 
Ezekiel1:4 I saw, in this vision, a great storm coming toward me from the north, driving before it a huge cloud glowing with fire, with a mass of fire inside that flashed continually; and in the fire there was something that shone like polished brass." 
Ezekiel1:5 Then from the center of the cloud, four strange forms appeared that looked like men," 
Ezekiel1:6 except that each had four faces and two pairs of wings! 
Ezekiel1:7 Their legs were like those of men, but their feet were cloven like calves' feet, and shone like burnished brass." 
Ezekiel1:8 And beneath each of their wings I could see human hands. 
Ezekiel1:9 The four living beings were joined wing to wing, and they flew straight forward without turning." 
Ezekiel1:10 Each had the face of a man in front, with a lion's face on the right side of his head, and the face of an ox on the left side, and the face of an eagle at the back!" 
Ezekiel1:11 Each had two pairs of wings spreading out from the middle of his back. One pair stretched out to attach to the wings of the living beings on each side, and the other pair covered his body." 
Ezekiel1:12 Wherever their spirit went they went, going straight forward without turning." 
Ezekiel1:13 Going up and down among them were other forms that glowed like bright coals of fire or brilliant torches, and it was from these the lightning flashed." 
Ezekiel1:14 The living beings darted to and fro, swift as lightning." 
Ezekiel1:15 As I stared at all of this, I saw four wheels on the ground beneath them, one wheel belonging to each." 
Ezekiel1:16 The wheels looked as if they were made of polished amber, and each wheel was constructed with a second wheel crosswise inside." 
Ezekiel1:17 They could go in any of the four directions without having to face around. 
Ezekiel1:18 The four wheels had rims and spokes, and the rims were filled with eyes around their edges." 
Ezekiel1:19 When the four living beings flew forward, the wheels moved forward with them. When they flew upwards, the wheels went up too." 
Ezekiel1:20 When the living beings stopped, the wheels stopped." 
Ezekiel1:21 For the spirit of the four living beings was in the wheels; so wherever their spirit went, the wheels and the living beings went there too." 
Ezekiel1:22 The sky spreading out above them looked as though it were made of crystal; it was inexpressibly beautiful. 
Ezekiel1:23 The wings of each stretched straight out to touch the others' wings, and each had two wings covering his body." 
Ezekiel1:24 And as they flew, their wings roared like waves against the shore, or like the voice of God, or like the shouting of a mighty army. When they stopped, they let down their wings." 
Ezekiel1:25 And every time they stopped, there came a voice from the crystal sky above them." 
Ezekiel1:26 For high in the sky above them was what looked like a throne made of beautiful blue sapphire stones, and upon it sat someone who appeared to be a Man." 
Ezekiel1:27 From his waist up, he seemed to be all glowing bronze, dazzling like fire; and from his waist down he seemed to be entirely flame, and there was a glowing halo" 
Ezekiel1:28 like a rainbow all around him. That was the way the glory of the Lord appeared to me. And when I saw it, I fell face downward on the ground and heard the voice of someone speaking to me:" 
Ezekiel2:1 And he said to me: “Stand up, son of dust, and I will talk to you.“" 
Ezekiel2:2 And the Spirit entered into me as he spoke, and set me on my feet." 
Ezekiel2:3 “Son of dust,“ he said, “I am sending you to the nation of Israel, to a nation rebelling against me. They and their fathers have kept on sinning against me until this very hour." 
Ezekiel2:4 For they are a hardhearted, stiff-necked people. But I am sending you to give them my messages—the messages of the Lord God." 
Ezekiel2:5 And whether they listen or not (for remember, they are rebels), they will at least know they have had a prophet among them." 
Ezekiel2:6 “Son of dust, don't be afraid of them; don't be frightened even though their threats are sharp and barbed and sting like scorpions. Don't be dismayed by their dark scowls. For remember, they are rebels!" 
Ezekiel2:7 You must give them my messages whether they listen or not (but they won't, for they are utter rebels)." 
Ezekiel2:8 Listen, son of dust, to what I say to you. Don't you be a rebel too! Open your mouth and eat what I give you.“" 
Ezekiel2:9 Then I looked and saw a hand holding out to me a scroll," 
Ezekiel2:10 with writing on both sides. He unrolled it, and I saw that it was full of warnings and sorrows and pronouncements of doom." 
Ezekiel3:1 And he said to me: “Son of dust, eat what I am giving you—eat this scroll! Then go and give its message to the people of Israel.“" 
Ezekiel3:2 So I took the scroll. 
Ezekiel3:3 “Eat it all,“ he said. And when I ate it, it tasted sweet as honey." 
Ezekiel3:4 Then he said: “Son of dust, I am sending you to the people of Israel with my messages." 
Ezekiel3:5 I am not sending you to some far-off foreign land where you can't understand the language— 
Ezekiel3:6 no, not to tribes with strange, difficult tongues. (If I did, they would listen!)" 
Ezekiel3:7 I am sending you to the people of Israel, and they won't listen to you any more than they listen to me! For the whole lot of them are hard, impudent, and stubborn." 
Ezekiel3:8 But see, I have made you hard and stubborn too—as tough as they are." 
Ezekiel3:9 I have made your forehead as hard as rock. So don't be afraid of them, or fear their sullen, angry looks, even though they are such rebels.“" 
Ezekiel3:10 Then he added: “Son of dust, let all my words sink deep into your own heart first; listen to them carefully for yourself." 
Ezekiel3:11 Then, afterward, go to your people in exile, and whether or not they will listen, tell them: 'This is what the Lord God says!' “" 
Ezekiel3:12 Then the Spirit lifted me up, and the glory of the Lord began to move away, accompanied by the sound of a great earthquake." 
Ezekiel3:13 It was the noise of the wings of the living beings as they touched against each other, and the sound of their wheels beside them." 
Ezekiel3:14 The Spirit lifted me up, and took me away to Tel Abib, another colony of Jewish exiles beside the Chebar River. I went in bitterness and anger, but the hand of the Lord was strong upon me." 
Ezekiel3:15 And I sat among them, overwhelmed, for seven days." 
Ezekiel3:16 At the end of the seven days, the Lord said to me:" 
Ezekiel3:17 “Son of dust, I have appointed you as a watchman for Israel; whenever I send my people a warning, pass it on to them at once." 
Ezekiel3:18 If you refuse to warn the wicked when I want you to tell them, “You are under the penalty of death; therefore repent and save your life,“ they will die in their sins, but I will punish you. I will demand your blood for theirs." 
Ezekiel3:19 But if you warn them, and they keep on sinning and refuse to repent, they will die in their sins, but you are blameless—you have done all you could." 
Ezekiel3:20 And if a good man becomes bad, and you refuse to warn him of the consequences, and the Lord destroys him, his previous good deeds won't help him—he shall die in his sin. But I will hold you responsible for his death and punish you." 
Ezekiel3:21 But if you warn him and he repents, he shall live, and you have saved your own life too.“" 
Ezekiel3:22 I was helpless in the hand of God, and when he said to me, “Go out into the valley and I will talk to you there“—" 
Ezekiel3:23 I arose and went, and oh, I saw the glory of the Lord there, just as in my first vision! And I fell to the ground on my face." 
Ezekiel3:24 Then the Spirit entered into me and set me on my feet. He talked to me and said: “Go, imprison yourself in your house," 
Ezekiel3:25 and I will paralyze you so you can't leave; 
Ezekiel3:26 and I will make your tongue stick to the roof of your mouth so that you can't reprove them; for they are rebels. 
Ezekiel3:27 But whenever I give you a message, then I will loosen your tongue and let you speak, and you shall say to them: 'The Lord God says.' Let anyone listen who wants to, and let anyone refuse who wants to, for they are rebels." 
Ezekiel4:1 “And now, son of dust, take a large brick and lay it before you and draw a map of the city of Jerusalem on it." 
Ezekiel4:2 Draw a picture of siege mounds being built against the city, put enemy camps around it and battering rams surrounding the walls." 
Ezekiel4:3 And put an iron plate between you and the city, like a wall of iron. Demonstrate how an enemy army will capture Jerusalem! “There is special meaning in each detail of what I have told you to do. For it is a warning to the people of Israel."
